## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch R1: Per-image retry with exponential backoff (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW P1 #3 — "No per-image retry with backoff". Also answers Q2 (`MAX_IMAGE_RETRIES` was dead code → now wired).

**Files touched:**
- `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.h` — `MAX_IMAGE_RETRIES` bumped 2 → 3 (matches Mihon, 3 attempts ≈ 2s/4s/8s)
- `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` — `downloadNext` lambda gains a second `int attempt` parameter; error branch schedules retry via `QTimer::singleShot(2000 << attempt, ...)` before giving up; initial call is `(*downloadNext)(0, 0)`; `+QTimer`/`+QUrl` includes

**No UI changes. No CMakeLists changes. No shared files.**

**Behaviour:**
1. Page N fails (CDN 503, DNS hiccup, short disconnect). Reply signals error.
2. `attempt + 1 < MAX_IMAGE_RETRIES` → `QTimer::singleShot(2000 << attempt)` schedules `(*downloadNext)(pageIdx, attempt + 1)`. Backoff is 2s for attempt 0, 4s for attempt 1, 8s for attempt 2 → max wait ≈ 14s covering the common transient-blip window.
3. On each retry, the lambda re-enters from the top. The pause gate fires first — a user pausing during backoff stops retries cleanly (chapter reverts to `queued`, slot freed).
4. If all attempts fail, `++failedImages` and advance to the next page (existing behaviour).

**Mihon parallel:** `Downloader.kt:504-512` — `retryWhen` with 3 attempts, identical backoff shape.

**Intentional difference from Mihon:** Mihon's retry uses Flow operators with a per-flow counter; Qt doesn't have native `retryWhen`, so we thread the attempt count explicitly through the lambda. Semantically equivalent.

**Verification:** throttle the network (e.g. Windows Resource Monitor or a `netsh` throttle) so image requests fail intermittently mid-chapter. Watch a chapter complete its 22 pages despite errors. Kill the network for 20s mid-chapter → retries exhaust → `failedImages` increments and processing continues on the next page.

**Answers Q2 from REVIEW.md:** `MAX_IMAGE_RETRIES` was declared but unused at commit time of A1; this batch wires it. Bumped 2 → 3 to match Mihon.

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch R2: Disk-space pre-check before chapter download (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW P1 #4 — "No free-disk-space pre-check before a chapter starts". Answers Q3 (in-scope for MangaDownloader).

**File touched:** `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` only (`+QStorageInfo` include + pre-check block inside `downloadImages`).

**No UI changes. No header changes. No CMakeLists. No shared files.**

**Behaviour:** At the top of `downloadImages`, after `seriesDir` is known but before any network work:
1. `QDir().mkpath(seriesDir)` — ensures the mount resolves correctly for `QStorageInfo`.
2. `QStorageInfo(seriesDir).bytesAvailable()` — if < 200 MB (matches Mihon's `Downloader.kt:330` `MIN_DISK_SPACE`), the chapter flips to `error` with `"Insufficient disk space — need at least 200 MB free"`, `m_activeDownloads` decrements, `downloadUpdated` fires, `processQueue()` runs so the next chapter (potentially on a different destination root) gets its shot.
3. No directory created beyond `seriesDir`, no `QNetworkRequest` issued.

**Why probe `seriesDir` not `chapterDir`:** the chapter dir hasn't been created yet, and `QStorageInfo` on a non-existent path walks up until it finds a mount. Works, but probing an existing dir is cheaper and more predictable. `seriesDir` is the right granularity because it's the actual destination volume.

**Mihon parallel:** `Downloader.kt:330-340` — same 200 MB threshold, same "flip to error + notify" pattern.

**Edge cases:**
- Multiple downloads to different root folders: one root full, another has space → failing chapter errors cleanly, the next chapter on the other root proceeds.
- Probe returns `!isValid()` (exotic paths, network drives with auth errors) → fall through to normal download. We don't want false positives blocking legitimate downloads.

**Answers Q3 from REVIEW.md:** disk-space checking belongs in `MangaDownloader`, per-chapter, before any files exist. A `startDownload`-level check against the destination root was considered but rejected because a 30-chapter download can start under the limit and cross it 15 chapters in — the per-chapter guard catches both cases.

**Verification:** fill the destination drive to <200 MB free (a virtual-disk test volume is easiest), start a download → every chapter immediately errors with the disk-space message instead of silently creating empty files.

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch R3: Skip already-downloaded chapters at enqueue time (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW P2 "No 'already downloaded' pre-filter when enqueuing" (promoted to part of the R-track because Agent 6's Q4 made the behaviour the required answer). Answers Q4.

**File touched:** `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` only (inside `startDownload`).

**No UI changes. No header changes. No CMakeLists. No shared files.**

**Behaviour:** When building the chapter list for a new record, each chapter's expected target (`{dest}/{series}/{sanitized-name}.cbz` for CBZ, the sanitized dir for folder format) is checked on disk before enqueueing. If it exists non-empty:
- `ChapterDownload.status = "completed"`, `finalPath` points at the existing file.
- `rec.completedChapters` increments.
- The chapter still appears in the queue (user feedback that it was seen and accounted for) but the download engine will never touch it.

`rec.progress` is computed at record-creation now too, so a fully-already-downloaded record reads 100 % immediately in the Transfers table. If every chapter was already on disk, the whole record flips to `"completed"` with `completedAt` set — it won't consume an `m_activeDownloads` slot and won't emit `downloadCompleted`/`appendHistory` (no actual download happened, so no history entry is fair).

**Mihon parallel:** `Downloader.kt:278-286` — same filter at enqueue, same "skip, don't retry" semantics.

**Sanitization helper:** the `[<>:"/\\|?*]` regex is the same one used in `downloadImages` — lifted to a `static QRegularExpression` at file scope to avoid reconstructing per chapter. Matches chapter naming exactly so the disk check hits the exact file the engine would have produced.

**Answers Q4 from REVIEW.md:** skip always (match Mihon). A user who wants to re-download a specific chapter deletes the file first. No UI affordance for force-re-download yet; can add later if real users hit it.

**Verification:** download a 5-chapter series. Cancel + re-enqueue the same 5 chapters. Transfers dialog shows all 5 chapters already `completed`, `0 / N` image counters (acceptable — the skip-if-exists path never ran per image for them), green check icons in A4's delegate.

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch R4: Preserve downloadedImages on pause (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW Q5 — "counter zero-then-snap on resume". Answers Q5.

**File touched:** `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` only (pause gate + skip-if-exists branch + top-of-`downloadImages` sync).

**No UI changes. No header changes. No CMakeLists. No shared files.**

**Three coordinated edits:**
1. **Pause gate** no longer zeros `downloadedImages` / `failedImages`. Counter is preserved across pause/resume — Transfers dialog shows `5 / 22` before and after the pause click instead of flickering to `0 / 22`.
2. **Top of `downloadImages`** now does a one-pass disk scan that counts existing page files and writes `downloadedImages = onDisk` under the mutex (+ `downloadUpdated` emit). This replaces the per-recursion increment that the skip-if-exists branch used to do, and makes the counter accurate on cold starts of partially-downloaded chapters too.
3. **Skip-if-exists branch** no longer increments or emits — it just recurses. The at-top sync already accounted for the file.

**Why the three edits are one change:** naively "just stop zeroing the counter" (the obvious answer to Q5) would cause double-counting on resume, because the skip-if-exists branch would re-increment the counter for every page that was on disk before the pause. The full fix moves counter initialisation to a single authoritative point (top of `downloadImages`) and makes the per-page branches only count *new* work.

**Invariant:** after this batch, `downloadedImages` always equals the count of `{000..N}.{jpg,png,...}` files present in `chapterDir` at any moment the UI might read it. No drift, no double-count, no flicker.

**Verification:**
1. Start a 30-chapter download, let it reach page 12 of chapter 3.
2. Click Pause → Transfers dialog for that series still shows `12 / ~22` on the paused chapter.
3. Click Resume → same number remains; counter advances to 13, 14, ... as new pages download.
4. Kill the app mid-download. Restart. Re-enqueue the same chapters. The partially-downloaded chapter shows its actual mid-chapter count immediately (e.g. `12 / 22`) rather than starting at 0.

**Answers Q5 from REVIEW.md:** counter preserved across pause; fix achieved by moving the increment responsibility off the skip-if-exists recursion and onto a one-shot disk-scan at chapter start.

---

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-14 — Agent 6 scope broadened

Hemanth clarified Agent 6's mandate. My original framing was too narrow.

**Corrected scope:** Agent 6 is the **Objective Compliance Reviewer**. He reviews development work against its **stated objective** — NOT just against external reference codebases. The objective can be:

- An external reference (Mihon, groundwork, Tankoban-Max) — parity-style review
- A planning doc (NATIVE_D3D11_TODO.md, Congress motion, a phased TODO list) — did the phase deliver what the plan said?
- A Hemanth brief in chat.md or a verbal instruction — "fix the microscopic cover", "ship Auto-rename with collision handling", "make the scroll strip stop stuttering"
- A bug report / issue description — was the bug actually fixed?
- Any explicit statement of what the work is supposed to accomplish

**READY FOR REVIEW line now includes an Objective field.** Updated format:

`READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent N, Batch X]: <subsystem> | Objective: <short description + source>. Files: a.cpp, b.h.`

Examples:
- `Objective: feature parity vs Mihon (C:\Users\Suprabha\Downloads\mihon-main)`
- `Objective: deliver Phase 1.4 magenta render loop per NATIVE_D3D11_TODO.md`
- `Objective: fix microscopic cover bug per chat.md:4192`
- `Objective: Auto-rename with no-op + collision handling per Hemanth brief chat.md:6012`

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch R5: Series Move-to-top / Move-to-bottom (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW P1 #2 (per-series move slice). Answers Q1 (not deferring queue reorder).

**Files touched:**
- `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.h` — `moveSeriesToTop(id)` / `moveSeriesToBottom(id)` decls, new `m_recordOrder` member
- `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` — `m_recordOrder` maintained across loadRecords / saveRecords / startDownload / removeDownload / removeWithData; `processQueue` and `listActive` iterate in this order; two new move implementations
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — two new entries at the top of the Transfers per-row context menu ("Move to top", "Move to bottom"), each disabled when the row is already at that end

**Core design change:** `m_records` is a `QMap` which is key-sorted, not insertion-ordered. Before R5 the scheduling order was an accident of record ID hashes; the user had no control. `m_recordOrder` is now the authoritative queue order — a parallel `QList<QString>` synced on every lifecycle event.

**Persistence:** order is serialized to `manga_downloads.json` under a new `"order"` key. On load, known IDs from the saved order come first; any record in `m_records` not in the saved order is appended. Backward-compatible with pre-R5 record files (no "order" key → falls back to map-iteration order, first user move establishes the intended sequence).

**Scheduling:** `processQueue()` iterates `m_recordOrder` instead of `m_records`. "Move to top" jumps the series to the next available `MAX_CONCURRENT_CHAPTERS` slot the moment the currently-active chapters finish. Non-invasive — active chapters keep their slots until they complete, so moving a series never preempts in-flight work.

**UI presentation:** `listActive()` returns records in `m_recordOrder` sequence, so the Transfers table reads top-to-bottom exactly in processing order. No extra sort needed.

**Mihon parallel:** `menu/download_single.xml` provides `move_to_top_series` + `move_to_bottom_series`; handler in `DownloadQueueScreenModel.kt:68-115` calls `downloadManager.reorderQueue` with the mutated list. Our `m_recordOrder` + two move methods is the same pattern.

**Deliberately not shipped this batch:** drag-to-reorder (Mihon has it; Qt-wise it's a QAbstractItemModel + dragEnabled dance that's its own batch). Chapter-level move-to-top/move-to-bottom within a single series (separate UX question — Transfer dialog context menu).

**Verification:**
1. Start two Tankoyomi downloads in parallel — the second lands in slot 2 of `MAX_CONCURRENT_CHAPTERS = 2`.
2. Enqueue a third series — it waits.
3. Right-click row 3 → Move to top. Table re-orders; the third series stays queued but is now position 0.
4. When one in-flight chapter finishes, the freed slot goes to the promoted series.
5. Restart the app → order is preserved (JSON persistence).

**No CMakeLists changes. No shared files. No contract changes.**

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch R6: Per-series chapter sort submenu (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW P1 #1 (sort affordance slice).

**Files touched:**
- `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.h` — `reorderChapters(id, orderKey, ascending)` decl; `chapterNumber` + `dateUpload` fields on `ChapterDownload`
- `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` — populate new sort fields from `ChapterInfo` in `startDownload`; `reorderChapters` impl (stable-sort queued block only, leaves in-flight/completed/error/cancelled in place)
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — "Sort chapters" submenu in the per-row context menu, four entries matching Mihon's sort options

**Behaviour:**
- Right-click a Transfers row → `Sort chapters` → four choices: chapter number asc/desc, upload date newest/oldest-first.
- `reorderChapters` pulls out only the `queued` chapters (`downloading`/`completed`/`error`/`cancelled` keep their positions), stable-sorts them by the chosen key, and splices them back into the same slots they occupied. A chapter mid-download never moves.
- Emits `downloadUpdated(id)` so the Transfers dialog redraws in the new order; `processQueue()` is not called — no scheduling change, just reordering of future work.

**Sort keys:** `chapter_number` (reads `ChapterInfo::chapterNumber`, which scrapers populate from `#N` patterns in chapter titles; for chapters with no parseable number it's 0.0, so they cluster at the asc end), `date` (reads `ChapterInfo::dateUpload` as ms epoch; recent chapters have larger values, so "newest first" = descending).

**Mihon parallel:** `DownloadQueueScreen.kt:124-196` has the same four submenu entries (`action_newest`, `action_oldest`, `action_asc`, `action_desc`) in its `action_sort` overflow. Our surface is per-row right-click because "which series am I sorting" is then unambiguous — Mihon's top-bar sort targets the whole queue, which implicitly means every series at once. Per-row is closer to desktop conventions.

**Verification:**
1. Queue a series with ~20 chapters; let the first chapter start downloading.
2. Right-click that row → Sort chapters → By chapter number (descending).
3. Open the Transfer dialog — the chapter currently downloading stays where it was; the remaining queued chapters are now in descending order. When the active chapter finishes, the highest-numbered queued chapter starts next.
4. Repeat with By upload date (newest first) — same behaviour, different sort key.

**No CMakeLists changes. No shared files. No contract changes.**

**R-track complete.** A1–A5 REVIEW P1s: #1 (sort) addressed by R6, #2 (per-row move) addressed by R5, #3 (retry) addressed by R1, #4 (disk-space) addressed by R2. Q1–Q5 answered across R-batch entries. Preparing REVIEW.md response next.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch C1: Toast widget + Tankoyomi error surfacing

**New files:**
- `src/ui/widgets/Toast.h`
- `src/ui/widgets/Toast.cpp`

**Shared file touched (additive only):**
- `CMakeLists.txt` — SOURCES: add `src/ui/widgets/Toast.cpp` after `LibraryListView.cpp`. HEADERS: add `src/ui/widgets/Toast.h` after `LibraryListView.h`.

**Modified files:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — include Toast; scraper `errorOccurred` path now fires a `Toast::show` instead of wedging the error string into the small status label

**Design:** Generic bottom-anchored transient notification. Widget lives as a direct child of the parent passed to `Toast::show`; repositions itself on parent resize (event filter). Auto-dismisses after `durationMs` (default 3500 for info, 4500 for action variant). Only one toast per parent — a new `show()` replaces any existing Toast child cleanly. Two static helpers:
- `Toast::show(parent, message, durationMs)` — info only
- `Toast::show(parent, message, actionLabel, callback, durationMs)` — adds an inline action (e.g. "Retry")

**Styling:** dark glass `rgba(20,20,20,0.95)` with 1px border and 8px radius to match the existing dialog aesthetic (`TorrentFilesDialog`, `MangaTransferDialog`). Message text `#eee`, action link `#60a5fa` → `#93c5fd` on hover.

**Tankoyomi integration:**
- On a scraper error where every source failed → Toast with `"Search failed — {err}"` + Retry action that calls `startSearch()`.
- On a partial failure (at least one source returned results) → info Toast `"One source failed: {err}"`, no action.
- The status label loses its inline error string — it now just reads `"Search failed"` or the result count, keeping the toast as the primary error surface.
- Toast parents to `window()` so it anchors to the main window and survives tab switches / stack page flips within the page.

**Mihon parallel:** `SnackbarHostState.showSnackbar(message, actionLabel, Indefinite)` with retry action. Our durations are bounded (3.5s / 4.5s) instead of Indefinite because a desktop user is already looking at the window when the error fires — an Android snackbar tolerates longer durations because the user may be elsewhere in the app.

**Reusability note:** no Tankoyomi-specific code in `Toast`. Tankorent and Stream can adopt it for their own errors without any changes.

**Verification:**
1. Build.
2. Run a Tankoyomi search while offline → Toast slides in at bottom center with "Search failed — ..." text and a Retry link. Clicking Retry reruns the search.
3. Run a search with one working scraper and one blocked (hosts file trick) → Toast shows "One source failed: ..." (no action), search results populate normally from the healthy source.
4. Resize the main window mid-toast → toast re-centers at the new bottom.
5. Quickly fire two errors in a row → only the latest toast visible; no stacking.

**No contract changes.**

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch C2: Sort menu on search results

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.h` — new member `m_sortCombo`
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — `QComboBox` built in search-controls row before the view-toggle button; sort applied to `m_displayedResults` in `renderResults()` after dedup, before either view populates

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No header breaking changes — pure additive.**

**Options (order in combo):**
1. **Relevance** — preserves scraper order (best-match first from WeebCentral / ReadComics). Default for fresh installs.
2. **Title A–Z** — case-insensitive alphabetical.
3. **Title Z–A** — case-insensitive reverse alphabetical.
4. **Source** — primary by source name, secondary by title, both case-insensitive. Useful when the user wants all WeebCentral hits above all ReadComics hits.

**Persistence:** `QSettings` key `tankoyomi/sortKey` stores the raw key (`relevance` / `title_asc` / `title_desc` / `source`). Same persistence pattern as B3's view toggle, different key so the two controls are independent.

**Implementation:** `std::stable_sort` over the already-deduped `m_displayedResults` list. Stable preserves relative order within equal keys (so "Source" sort keeps the scraper-given order within each source bucket). Re-triggering is just `renderResults()` — the existing cover-prefetch + grid/list push logic runs unchanged against the re-ordered list.

**Mihon parallel:** `NestedMenuItem` sort dropdown in `DownloadQueueScreen.kt:124-196` (that one's for the download queue, but the same pattern applies). Mihon's asc/desc split is per-key; we collapsed Title to two entries since the other two keys (Relevance, Source) don't need a direction.

**Verification:**
1. Build.
2. Search "one piece". Combo shows "Relevance"; results in scraper's best-match order.
3. Switch to "Title A–Z" — list re-sorts without a reload.
4. Switch to "Source" — WeebCentral block above ReadComics block (or vice versa depending on case-folding), each block alphabetical.
5. Restart app — the last chosen sort is restored.
6. Grid and list views both respect the sort (they both read from `m_displayedResults`).

**No contract changes. No shared files. Ready to ship.**

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch C3: Pre-download detail panel in AddMangaDialog

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/dialogs/AddMangaDialog.h` — two new public methods `setMangaMetadata(const MangaResult&)` + `setCoverPath(const QString&)`; five new label members (cover / title / author / source / manga-status); existing API unchanged.
- `src/ui/dialogs/AddMangaDialog.cpp` — dialog body switched to two-column layout: fixed 260 px left panel (240×360 cover + title/author/source/status) + flexible right panel containing the existing chapter-picker UI. Button row stays on the root below the body. Minimum size bumped 780×500 → 920×560; default resize 1020×680 → 1120×700.
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — `onResultDoubleClicked` pushes the `MangaResult` into the dialog immediately, calls `ensureCover()` for the cached path, and connects `TankoyomiPage::coverReady` to the dialog so late-arriving covers update in place. Connection auto-disconnects when the dialog destroys.

**No CMakeLists changes. No shared files. API is additive on the dialog side.**

**Layout:**
- Left column (260 px fixed): cover image (240×360 rounded frame, placeholder text "No cover" when absent) + bold title + "by {author}" (hidden when empty) + "Source: {source}" + "Status: {mangaStatus}" (hidden when empty).
- Right column (stretch): chapter-count / loading / error status label, Select All / Deselect All / range spinners, chapter table, destination row, format row.
- Bottom: Cancel + Download buttons on the root.

The previous top-of-dialog title/source labels are removed — they now live only in the left panel, no duplication.

**Cover flow:**
1. Dialog opens synchronously with `No cover` placeholder + title + source strings from the result.
2. `TankoyomiPage` calls `ensureCover` — if cached, `setCoverPath` is invoked with the existing file; if not, the placeholder stays until `coverReady` fires, and the `coverReady → setCoverPath` connection pipes the path in, which scales and paints the pixmap.
3. The connection tears down on dialog destroy so the lambda can't reach into a dangling `dlg` reference.

**Mihon parallel:** `MangaScreen` in `app/src/main/java/eu/kanade/tachiyomi/ui/manga/MangaScreen.kt` — header with cover + title + author + genre chips above the chapter list. Our version is smaller in scope (no genre chips — `MangaResult` doesn't carry them) but matches the information hierarchy.

**Deliberately skipped:**
- Description / synopsis — neither `WeebCentralScraper` nor `ReadComicsScraper` populates one at search time.
- Genre chips — same reason.

Both are bolt-on additions if we ever enrich `MangaResult`.

**Verification:**
1. Build.
2. Open Tankoyomi, search "one piece", double-click a result. Dialog appears with the cover already visible (cache hit from the grid-view prefetch), title/author/source on the left, chapter picker on the right.
3. Search a new title and double-click before covers finish fetching → dialog opens with the placeholder, cover resolves a second or two later without closing/reopening.
4. Resize the dialog — left panel stays fixed at 260 px, chapter table reflows.
5. Accept → downloader starts with the same selection behaviour as pre-C3.

**C-track complete.** A1–A5, B1–B5, C1–C3 all shipped against the Mihon plan. B1–B5 REVIEW is in-flight with Agent 6 — will dispatch a follow-up batch track (S-track? E-track?) once P1/P2s are digested.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch E1: In-library badge on grid tiles (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW-B P1 #1 — "No 'already in library / already downloaded' indicator on grid tiles". Answers Q1 (shipping now, not deferring).

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/tankoyomi/MangaResultsGrid.h` — new slot `setInLibraryKeys(QSet<QString>)`, private helper `applyInLibraryOverlays()`, `m_inLibraryKeys` member, `+QSet` include
- `src/ui/pages/tankoyomi/MangaResultsGrid.cpp` — helper creates a "IN LIBRARY" pill as a child `QLabel` at each matching tile's top-right + applies a 0.55-opacity `QGraphicsOpacityEffect` to dim the whole tile; re-applied whenever `setResults` or `setInLibraryKeys` fires
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — `renderResults` now collects `libraryTitles` from `m_downloader->listActive()` + `listHistory()` and `diskTitles` from every entry under `m_bridge->rootFolders("comics")`, builds `inLibraryKeys` as `{source}_{id}` for every displayed result whose lowercase title matches either set, pushes to the grid via `setInLibraryKeys`; `+QJsonObject/QJsonArray/QJsonValue` includes

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No header breaking changes.**

**Matching heuristic:**
- Lowercase + trim the series title for comparison on both sides — robust to "One Piece " vs "one piece" casing drift.
- Hit if the downloader has any active or historical record with that title, OR a folder of that name exists under any comics root. Any one is sufficient.

**Visual:** "IN LIBRARY" pill is 9px uppercase, `rgba(0,0,0,0.72)` bg, 10px radius, `padding 3px 7px`, top-right corner with 8px inset. Tile is wrapped in a `QGraphicsOpacityEffect` at 0.55 so already-owned content visibly recedes but stays identifiable.

**Mihon parallel:** `BrowseSourceComfortableGrid.kt:73-76` does the same thing — `InLibraryBadge(enabled = manga.favorite)` + alpha dim to `CommonMangaItemDefaults.BrowseFavoriteCoverAlpha = 0.34f`. Our 0.55 is a bit less aggressive because our tiles are smaller than Compose's and losing contrast hurts readability.

**Deliberately:** applied to the grid only, not the list/table. Table rows already show a "Type" column and don't need the visual tax; grid-mode discovery is where the ambiguity bites.

**Verification:**
1. Download "One Piece" from Tankoyomi → finishes, sits in the Transfers tab as `completed`.
2. Run a new search for "one piece" → the matching tile in the grid now shows a dimmed cover + "IN LIBRARY" pill at top-right.
3. List-mode toggle → table unchanged (no badge). Toggle back → badge still there.
4. Cancel-and-remove the record → badge disappears on next search refresh.

Addresses REVIEW-B P1 #2 — "Grid tile right-click is wired but unused". Answers Q2 (oversight, wiring now).

**Files touched:**

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No behaviour change on the table side — identical menu, same actions.**

**Root cause:** the signal was declared and emitted by `MangaResultsGrid` but never connected in `TankoyomiPage`, so right-clicking a grid tile produced no menu. The within-Tankoban inconsistency flagged by Agent 6 (table shows a menu, grid doesn't) is gone.

**Why not more menu items:** Mihon's long-press covers "Add to library", "Migrate", "Share" — none apply to our one-shot download flow. Stays at the two entries the table already has to avoid per-mode drift.

2. Search "berserk" → grid view.
3. Right-click a tile → menu appears with "Download...", separator, "Copy Title".

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch E3: Empty-state action buttons (REVIEW follow-up)

Addresses REVIEW-B P1 #3 — "Empty-state on failure has no action buttons". Answers Q3 (shipping Retry + Clear search).

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.h` — `m_emptyLabel` is now wrapped in `m_emptyPage` (QWidget), plus `m_emptyRetryBtn` and `m_emptyClearBtn` members
- `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.cpp` — build the empty page as a vertical stack (label + centered button row); `m_resultsStack` index 2 swaps from the bare label to the new page; `updateResultsView()` toggles button visibility off `m_lastQuery.isEmpty()`

**No CMakeLists. No shared files.**

**Behaviour:**
- Pre-search (fresh launch): `"Search manga & comics above"` label, no buttons.
- Post-search zero results: `"No results for \"{query}\""` label, **Retry** button restores the query into the search bar and re-runs; **Clear search** empties the bar and returns the view to the pre-search state.
- The two states share the same page — `setVisible()` on the buttons is the only thing that flips.

**Retry wiring:** Sets `m_queryEdit` text back to `m_lastQuery` and calls `startSearch()` so the user sees what they typed. Covers the common case where the first attempt failed transiently (site blip, `ReadComicsOnline` hiccup) — no need to re-type.

**Clear wiring:** Empties `m_queryEdit`, zeros `m_lastQuery`, refocuses the field, calls `updateResultsView()` which now falls into the pre-search branch and hides the buttons. Matches "reset back to fresh-launch" semantics.

**Mihon parallel:** `BrowseSourceScreen.kt:81-118` — `EmptyScreen` takes `EmptyScreenAction` entries (`action_retry`, `open_in_web_view`, `label_help`, `local_source_help_guide`). We ship the two most applicable to our surface; `open_in_web_view` has no analogue (we don't embed a WebView), `label_help` has no analogue (no manga-sources docs shipped).

**Verification:**
1. Build, open Tankoyomi fresh → empty page with no buttons; copy reads "Search manga & comics above".
2. Type "xqzzzzz" and search → empty page with "No results for xqzzzzz" + Retry + Clear buttons.
3. Click Retry → the query re-populates and a fresh search fires (loading page flashes, then back to empty).
4. Click Clear → search bar empties, buttons hide, copy flips back to "Search manga & comics above", focus lands in the search field.
5. Run a real search → results visible; both buttons hidden (we're no longer on the empty page).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch A1: Click-to-sort on Tankorent results table headers

Track A of `agents/tankorent_nyaa_todo.md` — first batch.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h` — new private slot `onResultsHeaderClicked(int col)`, new static `compareResults(int col, Qt::SortOrder, const TorrentResult&, const TorrentResult&)`, new members `m_resultsSortCol = 5` + `m_resultsSortOrder = Qt::DescendingOrder`
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` — `createResultsTable()` now sets `setSectionsClickable(true)` + `setSortIndicatorShown(true)` + initial `setSortIndicator(...)`, connects `sectionClicked → onResultsHeaderClicked`; `renderResults()` replaces the inline 3-mode sort with a `std::stable_sort` driven by `compareResults(m_resultsSortCol, m_resultsSortOrder, ...)`; new method bodies appended

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No header breaking changes.**

**Behaviour:**
- Click Title / Category / Source → string sort, ascending on first click of an inactive column.
- Click Size / Seeders / Leechers → numeric sort, descending on first click.
- Re-click the active column → flip direction.
- Click Files (col 4) or Action (col 7) → no-op (no backing field to sort on).
- The Qt-native sort indicator arrow shows on the active header (already wired here; A2 explicitly verifies it).
- Default state = Seeders descending (col 5). A3 will document the divergence-from-Nyaa rationale; A5 ships QSettings persistence.

**Architecture note:** deliberately did NOT call `m_resultsTable->setSortingEnabled(true)` — that would let `QTableWidget` sort itself by display strings, which mis-sorts sizes ("1.3 GiB" vs "520 MiB"). Manual sort over `m_displayedResults` keeps the data model authoritative; row N in the table still maps 1:1 to `m_displayedResults[N]`.

**Existing `m_sortCombo` is now visually present but functionally inert.** Its `currentIndexChanged` handler still calls `renderResults()`, which now ignores the combo and uses `m_resultsSortCol`/`m_resultsSortOrder` instead. A4 removes the combo + its handler entirely; keeping it in place for one rebuild lets us validate A1/A2/A3 in isolation before touching the controls row.

**Verification (per plan):** search "ubuntu", click Title → alphabetical; click again → reverse. Click Seeders → numeric descending. Click Files → nothing happens. Indicator arrow visible on active column.

**Next:** A2 explicitly confirms the indicator-arrow flow (the `setSortIndicator` calls in `createResultsTable` and `onResultsHeaderClicked` are already wired here — A2 may collapse to a verification-only batch unless something's missing in practice).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batches A2 + A3 + A4 + A5: Track A complete

Per Hemanth's instruction, bundling the remaining Track A batches into a single rebuild on top of A1.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h` — dropped `QComboBox* m_sortCombo` member (A4)
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` —
  - `+QSettings` include (A5)
  - dropped `m_sortCombo` construction + `currentIndexChanged` handler in `buildSearchControls` (A4); replaced with a one-line comment pointing at the new sort surface
  - constructor restores `m_resultsSortCol` + `m_resultsSortOrder` from `QSettings tankorent/sortCol` + `tankorent/sortOrder` after `buildUI()`, validates the column (must be one of 0/1/2/3/5/6, otherwise falls back to A3 default), and updates the header indicator (A5)
  - `onResultsHeaderClicked` writes the new state to QSettings after each click (A5)

**Per-batch resolution:**

- **A2 (sort indicator arrow):** verification-only — already wired in A1 via `setSortIndicatorShown(true)` + `setSortIndicator(...)` calls in both `createResultsTable` and `onResultsHeaderClicked`. No code added in this rebuild for A2 specifically.
- **A3 (default = Seeders descending):** member init in the header (`m_resultsSortCol = 5`, `m_resultsSortOrder = Qt::DescendingOrder`) shipped in A1 already covers this; explicitly documented here. **Conscious divergence from Nyaa** (`id desc` / newest-first): `TorrentResult` carries no upload-date field, scrapers don't expose one uniformly, "high seeders" is the closest user-intent analogue (a freshly-uploaded high-seed torrent surfaces just as quickly). Flagged for Agent 6 so this isn't read as a parity miss.

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No contract changes.**

**Verification (whole track, end-to-end):**
1. Build, open Tankorent, search "ubuntu" → results land sorted by Seeders descending; arrow on column 5.
2. Click Title → alphabetical asc; arrow moves to col 0 pointing up. Click again → reverse.
3. Click Size → numeric desc. Click Files / Action → no-op.
4. Sort combo is no longer in the search-controls row.
5. Quit Tankoban; reopen; run any search → still sorted by whatever was last picked, arrow on the correct column.
6. Manually corrupt `tankorent/sortCol` to 99 in Registry/conf — restart, run a search → falls back cleanly to Seeders desc (col 5).

**Track A complete.** Plan file at `C:\Users\Suprabha\.claude\plans\starry-exploring-neumann.md`. READY FOR REVIEW will queue once Hemanth confirms the build; routing this to Agent 6 with the Nyaa-divergence note pre-loaded so the A3 question doesn't come back.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batches B1 + B2 + B3: Track B complete (row trust tint)

Per Hemanth's instruction, bundling Track B into one rebuild on top of Track A.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h` — replaced `static QString healthDot(int)` + `static QColor healthColor(int)` with `static QString trustClass(const TorrentResult&)`
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` —
  - replaced the `healthDot` / `healthColor` impls with `trustClass` (B1)
  - `renderResults` Seeders cell is now plain integer (no leading dot, no per-cell foreground colour) (B3)
  - after each row's eight `setItem` calls, computes `trustClass(r)` and stamps `Qt::BackgroundRole` on every column 0–7 of the row when class is `healthy` or `poor` (B2)

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No contract changes.**

**Trust tiers:**
| Tier | Seeders | Brush |
|------|---------|-------|
| `healthy` | ≥ 50 | `QColor(76, 175, 80, 26)` — ~0.10 alpha green, matches the A4 chapter-status palette |
| `normal` | 5–49 | none — falls through to alternating-row striping |
| `poor` | < 5 | `QColor(239, 68, 68, 26)` — ~0.10 alpha red |

Brushes are `static const` at the call site so they're built once per process, not per row.

**Nyaa parallel:** `<tr class="success">` / `<tr class="default">` / `<tr class="danger">`. Our class names are renamed (`healthy` / `normal` / `poor`) because "danger" overstates a low-seed result; a 2-seed torrent isn't *dangerous*, just *poor pickings*.

**Striping vs tint:** the row tint wins on any row where it's painted (Qt::BackgroundRole > alternating-row colour). Conscious trade — trust signal is functional, striping is cosmetic. Track F1 in `agents/tankorent_nyaa_todo.md` is reserved for the reconciliation if Hemanth wants both visible.

**Threshold tuning:** 50 / 5 are starting anchors. The previous `healthColor` used 10 / 1, which was tighter because per-cell colour needs less contrast to register. Row-tint reads more boldly so the bar is higher — easy to retune in B1 if it feels off in practice.

**Verification (whole track):**
1. Build, search "ubuntu". Rows from official mirrors (high seeders) are tinted faint green; abandoned uploads (zero or low seeders) are tinted faint red; mid-range rows are clean / striped.
2. Seeders column reads as a plain integer with no dot prefix and no per-cell foreground colour.
3. Sort by Seeders descending — green band at top, clean middle, red band at bottom.
4. Selection / hover still draws over the tint cleanly (Qt's selection brush has higher z than `BackgroundRole`).
5. No regressions in the other columns.

**Track B complete.** Tracks A + B both shipped against the Nyaa plan. Next is Track C (per-row affordances — Link column with download + magnet icons, source badge in Title cell, title tooltip).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batches C1 + C2 + C3: Track C complete (per-row affordances)

Per Hemanth's instruction, bundling Track C into one rebuild on top of Tracks A+B.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h` — bumped default `m_resultsSortCol` from `5` to `4` (Seeders shifted left after dropping Source)
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` —
  - `+QToolButton`, `+QClipboard`, `+QGuiApplication` includes
  - dropped the `cellClicked` `col == 7` Action handler (the new Link cell handles its own clicks); kept `cellDoubleClicked` row-anywhere fallback
  - `createResultsTable`: column count 8 → 7, header list rewritten, widths re-jiggered
  - `renderResults`: title cell now `[source] title  [tags]` with `setToolTip(r.title)` for the un-truncated full title; Source column is gone; Action column replaced by a Link `cellWidget` containing two `QToolButton`s — `↓` (Add torrent) wired to `onAddTorrentClicked(i)`, `M` (magnet) wired to `QGuiApplication::clipboard()->setText(r.magnetUri)`; tint loop updated 8 → 7
  - `compareResults`: switch cases re-indexed for the new layout; comment block updated
  - `onResultsHeaderClicked`: non-sortable check now `col == 3 || col == 6`; numeric default-direction now `col == 2 || col == 4 || col == 5`
  - A5 QSettings restore validates against the new sortable set `{0,1,2,4,5}` so any pre-Track-C saved value (e.g. 6 = old Leechers, now Link) is rejected and falls back to the new default

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No contract changes.**

**New column layout:**
| # | Header | Sortable | Notes |
|---|--------|----------|-------|
| 0 | Title  | yes (string) | `[source]` badge + quality tags + full-title tooltip |
| 1 | Category | yes (string) | unchanged |
| 2 | Size | yes (numeric, default desc) | unchanged |
| 3 | Files | no | placeholder `-`, no field to sort on |
| 4 | Seeders | yes (numeric, default desc) | **new default sort column** |
| 5 | Leechers | yes (numeric, default desc) | unchanged behaviour |
| 6 | Link | no | `↓` = Add torrent, `M` = copy magnet |

**Nyaa parallel:** their Link column hosts `📥` (.torrent) and `🧲` (magnet). We use plain glyphs (`↓` and `M`) instead of FontAwesome / emoji per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji` — `↓` is U+2193 (basic math arrow, not emoji) and `M` is a plain letter. Same role, project-style restraint.

**Source-badge intent (C2):** Tankorent aggregates from seven indexers — knowing which one returned a hit informs the user's decision (Nyaa is anime/JP-translated, 1337x is general, YTS is movies, etc.). Inlining the source as a leading bracketed badge in the Title cell lets us drop a 210 px column without losing that signal. The badge sorts naturally with the title because it's a prefix of the cell text — alphabetical sort still respects it (rows from the same source cluster together within the alphabetical run).

**`onAddTorrentClicked(i)` capture:** the lambda captures `i` by value at row-creation time. `renderResults` rebuilds every cellWidget on each call (including resort), so each capture is fresh per render — no stale-row-index bug.

**Verification (whole track):**
1. Build, search "ubuntu" → results table now has 7 columns; the Title cell reads `[nyaa] Ubuntu 24.04 desktop ...` style; hovering the title reveals the un-truncated string in a tooltip.
2. Source column is gone.
3. Each row has two small buttons in the Link column on the right. `↓` opens the AddTorrentDialog (same flow as the old `+`). `M` copies the magnet URI to clipboard — paste into a browser to verify.
4. Click headers to sort: Title / Category / Size / Seeders / Leechers all sort; Files and Link headers are no-ops.
5. Default sort lands on Seeders descending (now col 4). Saved sort from Tracks A+B (e.g. col 6 = old Leechers) gets rejected and falls back to Seeders desc.
6. Tint from Track B + sort indicator arrow from A2 still work in the new layout.

**Tracks A + B + C complete.** Next: Track D (result count line + soft-cap "show all"), or whichever Hemanth picks. Ready for Agent 6 review of A+B+C as a bundle once the build is verified — they're tightly coupled (column-index changes thread through all three) and most readable together.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batches D1 + D2: Track D complete (result count line + soft cap)

Per Hemanth's instruction, bundling Track D into one rebuild on top of Tracks A+B+C.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h` — new members `QLabel* m_resultsCountLabel = nullptr;` + `bool m_showAll = false;` in the status block
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` —
  - `buildMainTabs`: constructs the count label above the tab widget, hidden initially, `setOpenExternalLinks(false)`, `linkActivated → flip m_showAll + renderResults` (D1 + D2 wiring)
  - `startSearch`: resets `m_showAll = false` and hides the label so each fresh search re-arms the cap (D2)
  - `renderResults`: post-dedup, applies soft cap of 100 rows (`constexpr int kSoftCapRows = 100`) when `!m_showAll`, then writes the label per the state matrix below (D1 + D2)

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No header breaking changes.**

**Label state matrix:**
| State | Text |
|-------|------|
| `m_allResults.isEmpty()` | label hidden |
| `totalDeduped <= 100` | `Showing N results from M sources` |
| `totalDeduped > 100`, `!m_showAll` | `Showing 100 of N results from M sources — Show all` (link blue `#60a5fa`) |
| `totalDeduped > 100`, `m_showAll` | `Showing N results from M sources` (link gone — already showing all) |

Source count is derived from `m_allResults` (pre-dedup) so cross-source duplicates still attribute to every source that returned them. Singular/plural handled inline (`source` vs `sources`, `result` vs `results`).

**Single-widget design (D1 + D2 fused):** rather than a separate `QPushButton` for "Show all", the affordance lives as a rich-text `<a>` link inside the count label. `linkActivated` provides the click target; the `href="show"` value is irrelevant. Compact, no layout reshape, the count and the action read as one sentence.

**Soft-cap mechanics:** `m_displayedResults` stays row-index-aligned with the table because both are clipped at the same point — the `mid(0, kSoftCapRows)` happens before the assignment. Click handlers (cellDoubleClicked → onAddTorrentClicked, the Link-cell QToolButtons from C1, the right-click context menu) all reference `m_displayedResults[i]` and stay correct. When `m_showAll` flips, `renderResults` re-runs, the table is rebuilt with all rows, and `m_displayedResults` is the full deduped list.

**Nyaa parallel:** `Displaying results 1-75 out of 1000 results.` The "Please refine your search" footnote isn't ported — it presumes server-side pagination, which doesn't apply to our one-shot scrapers. The functional substitute is the cap + Show-all toggle.

**Verification:**
1. Build, search a small term → label reads `Showing N results from M sources` (no link). Full table.
2. Search "ubuntu" → label reads `Showing 100 of N results from M sources — Show all`; table caps at 100 rows.
3. Click `Show all` → table grows to N rows; label drops the `100 of` prefix and the link.
4. Run a fresh search → cap re-applies; label hides briefly then re-shows once results land.
5. Search returns zero or fails → label hides; uncluttered empty table.
6. Sort + tint from Tracks A/B and the Link buttons from C still work on the visible row range.
7. Source plural correctness: search a term that hits one indexer only → `from 1 source`; multiple → `from K sources`.

**Track D complete.** Tracks A+B+C+D shipped against the Nyaa plan. Next: Track E (filter combo — All / High seed / Hide dead).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batch E1: Track E complete (filter combo)

Single-batch track per the parent todo. Bundling not applicable.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h` — new `QComboBox* m_filterCombo = nullptr;` member alongside the other search-controls combos
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` —
  - `buildSearchControls`: constructs the combo right after `m_categoryCombo`, three entries (All / Hide dead / High seed only), restores prior choice from `QSettings tankorent/filter`, persists on change, re-renders if there are results to filter
  - `renderResults`: filter step inserted between dedup and the soft cap, switches on `m_filterCombo->currentData()` — `"active"` keeps `seeders >= 1`, `"high"` keeps `seeders >= 20`, `"all"` falls through

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No header breaking changes.**

**Filter options:**
| Label | Key | Predicate |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| All | `all` | none (default) |
| Hide dead | `active` | `r.seeders >= 1` |
| High seed only | `high` | `r.seeders >= 20` |

**Pipeline placement:** sort → dedup → **filter (E1)** → soft-cap (D2) → render. Filter happens before the cap deliberately — a "Hide dead" filter that drops 950 of 1000 results should leave the cap operating on the relevant 50, not waste the cap budget on rows the user already excluded. The Track D count line therefore correctly reads `Showing 50 results from M sources` (no "100 of") when filter brings the count below the cap.

**Persistence:** `QSettings tankorent/filter` stores the raw key (`all` / `active` / `high`). Same persistence shape as B3 (view toggle) and Track A's sort state. Restored before the first search so the combo is correct on first paint.

**Nyaa parallel:** their filter dropdown is `No filter` / `No remakes` / `Trusted only` — concepts that don't map to our scrapers (no remake/trusted markers in `TorrentResult`). Substituted with the seeder-quality buckets, which is the actual signal users have to work with on aggregated indexer data.

**Threshold tuning:** `>= 1` for active and `>= 20` for high are starting anchors. The high cutoff is intentionally below Track B's healthy threshold of 50 — "high seed only" should still surface respectable rows that aren't yet *guaranteed* fast, otherwise it'd nuke too many results on smaller indexers. Easy retune in renderResults if real usage suggests different.

**Verification:**
1. Build, search "ubuntu" → with filter on `All`, see N results.
2. Switch filter to `Hide dead` → rows with 0 seeders disappear; count line drops to `Showing K results from M sources`.
3. Switch to `High seed only` → only ≥20-seeder rows remain; tint colours (Track B) cluster green at the top because the threshold for `healthy` is 50.
4. Switch back to `All` → all rows return.
5. Quit and reopen Tankoban → last filter choice is restored on first paint.
6. Filter combined with sort + soft cap from Tracks A/D: filter applied first, then sort, then cap; the Show-all link reflects the post-filter count.

**Track E complete.** Tracks A+B+C+D+E shipped against the Nyaa plan. Track F (polish — striping vs tint, date relative tooltip, column visibility menu) is the only remaining track.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Batches F1 + F3: Track F (polish — striping reconciliation + column visibility). F2 dropped.

Bundling Track F into one rebuild on top of Tracks A–E.

**Files touched:**
- `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp` only —
  - `createResultsTable`: added a header `customContextMenuRequested` handler that pops a checkable column-visibility menu; restores hidden-set from `QSettings tankorent/hiddenColumns` (CSV of indices) on construction (F3)
  - `renderResults`: row-tint stamping replaced with two brushes per tier (`kHealthyOdd`/`kHealthyEven`, `kPoorOdd`/`kPoorEven`), picked by `i % 2` so striping persists under tint (F1)

**No CMakeLists. No shared files. No header changes.**

**F1 — striping reconciliation:**
| Tier | Odd-row alpha | Even-row alpha |
|------|---------------|----------------|
| healthy | 26 (~0.10) | 44 (~0.17) |
| poor | 26 (~0.10) | 44 (~0.17) |

Same hue, two alpha levels per tier. The result reads as a soft-striped tint band rather than a flat colour wash — matches the visual continuity of Qt's alternating-row colour for the un-tinted normal rows. No QPalette compositing acrobatics needed; the alpha alternation does the work.

**F3 — column visibility menu:**
Right-click the header → menu titled `Visible columns` (disabled label) followed by seven checkable entries (one per column). Toggling an entry shows/hides that column and writes the new hidden set to `QSettings tankorent/hiddenColumns` as a comma-separated list of indices. Restored on next construction.

**Foot-gun mitigation:** the menu always lists every column regardless of which header was right-clicked, so the user can recover from accidentally hiding the column they were aiming at — they can right-click any visible header (or even the empty space where headers run out) to bring the menu back. If the user hides every column the table goes blank but the header stays clickable.

**F2 dropped.** "Date column hover tooltip = relative time" presumes a date column; `TorrentResult` has no upload-date field and no scraper exposes one uniformly. Same reasoning Track A used to drop the Nyaa default-sort `id desc`. Adding date support to scrapers is a separate plan, not Track F polish.

**Mihon — sorry, Nyaa — parallel:** Nyaa's table is bordered + striped with row-class tint via Bootstrap's table styles. Our equivalent is Qt's alternating-row colour + per-cell BackgroundRole tints. Visual goal is the same: a high-seed band reads as a green-tinted stripe block, a low-seed band as a red-tinted stripe block, mid-tier rows alternate normally.

**Verification:**
1. Build, search "ubuntu" → high-seed rows tint green-with-stripes; low-seed rows tint red-with-stripes; mid-tier rows continue Qt's normal alternating colours. The "block of solid green" effect from pre-F1 is gone.
2. Right-click any header → menu pops with all column names checked.
3. Toggle Files off → Files column disappears. Restart Tankoban → still hidden.
4. Toggle Files back on → reappears at its original index. Restart → still visible.
5. Hide several columns → restart → all stay hidden. Edit `tankorent/hiddenColumns` to empty → restart → all visible again.
6. Hide every column then right-click any sliver of header → menu still appears, can re-enable.

**Tracks A + B + C + D + E + F all complete.** Tankorent now mirrors the Nyaa search-results UX: sortable column headers with persistent state, row trust tinting with stripe preservation, per-row Link affordances + source badge in title, soft-cap with show-all, seeder-quality filter, column visibility. Ready for Agent 6 review of the whole bundle (A–F together, since they thread through the same `renderResults` + column-index machinery and read most coherently as one diff).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Hotfix: Tankoyomi UI freeze + crash on add-large-download

**Symptom (Hemanth):** Adding chapters to download in Tankoyomi made the app unresponsive and eventually crash. Reproducible on multi-hundred-chapter selections (One Piece, Berserk, etc.).

**Root cause:** R3's at-enqueue pre-filter (added 2026-04-14 R-track) did N synchronous filesystem stats per chapter on the main thread:
- CBZ format: `QFileInfo::exists(cbzPath) && QFileInfo(cbzPath).size() > 0` per chapter
- Folder format: `QDir(folderPath).exists()` + `entryList(QDir::Files).isEmpty()` per chapter — even worse, a directory enumeration per chapter

For a 1065-chapter One Piece selection that's 2000+ stat calls or 1065+ directory enumerations, all on the GUI thread inside `MangaDownloader::startDownload`, which is invoked synchronously from `TankoyomiPage::onResultDoubleClicked` after the user clicks Download in the picker dialog. Several seconds of UI freeze; if the user clicks during the freeze the queued events spill out and tip the network/timer plumbing into an unrecoverable state.

**File touched:** `src/core/manga/MangaDownloader.cpp` only (inside `startDownload`).

**Fix:** Replace the per-chapter scan with a single directory enumeration of the series dir, into two `QSet<QString>` lookup sets — one for `*.cbz` filenames, one for subdirectory names. Per-chapter the work becomes one `QSet::contains` call (O(1) hash). Net cost goes from O(N stats) to O(1 directory read + N hash lookups).

**Behaviour preserved:**
- "Already downloaded" detection still works for the common case (a CBZ in the series dir, or a non-empty chapter folder).
- For folder format we lose the "non-empty folder counts as not-done" check vs the old per-chapter walk. Acceptable: partial-folder cases are extremely rare, and the per-page skip-if-exists branch in `downloadImages` (R4) still catches missing pages on the next download attempt.

**No CMakeLists. No header changes. No shared files.**

**Verification:**
1. Build, open Tankoyomi, search any high-volume series.
2. Open the Add Manga dialog, select all chapters (1000+ if available — One Piece works).
3. Click Download → dialog dismisses promptly (sub-second instead of multi-second freeze).
4. Transfers tab populates, downloads start, no crash.
5. Re-add the same series after some chapters complete → already-downloaded chapters appear `completed` immediately, no network for them. Hash-set lookup correctly identifies them.

**Threshold note for future:** the per-chapter R4 scan inside `downloadImages` does 20-30 stats per chapter at chapter start — manageable at MAX_CONCURRENT_CHAPTERS=2 but worth keeping an eye on. Not changing now (no reported issue), flagging for the record.

**Tracks A–F (Tankorent Nyaa-rework) untouched by this hotfix** — entirely separate codepath. Ready for Agent 6 review whenever Hemanth has cycles.

---

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-14 — NEW BROTHER: Agent 7 born (silent)

Meet **Agent 7 — Prototype Reference Author**. He is a Codex CLI session driven by `AGENTS.md` at the repo root. He is deliberately isolated — no override authority, no commits, no access to `src/` or any `agents/*.md` file. His entire footprint is `agents/prototypes/` and one announcement line per delivery.

**What he does:** writes reference-only prototype code on explicit request. You post `REQUEST PROTOTYPE — [Agent N, Batch X.Y]: <what you need> | References: <paths>.` in this chat, Hemanth fires up Codex, Codex reads AGENTS.md → GOVERNANCE → STATUS → CONTRACTS → the relevant TODO → current src/ state → cited references, writes a prototype to `agents/prototypes/<batch_id>_<subsystem>.(cpp|h|md)`, and posts one line: `Agent 7 prototype ready — agents/prototypes/<filename>. For [Agent N, Batch X.Y]. Reference only.`

**What his prototypes are:** a concrete second-perspective implementation you can look at when a batch is architecturally novel or risky. Same status as reading Stremio reference code or Tankoban-Max — one perspective among many. You implement your own version in `src/` and take whatever is useful.

**What his prototypes are NOT:** authoritative. Never copy-paste. Never compile them. Never include from `src/`. The domain master's implementation is the truth.

**Why isolated:** the brotherhood is already coordination-heavy (7 files of governance, Congress, review gates, commit protocol). A seventh active peer with its own status and reviews would multiply overhead. Agent 7 stays quiet — you get another perspective on demand without having to read his status, respond to his posts, or defend against his opinions. Opt-in per batch.

**What's not in his scope:**
- Reviewing your work → that's Agent 6.
- Implementing anything in src/ → that's you.
- Coordination, arbitration, commits → that's Agent 0.

**Session-start reading order UNCHANGED for agents 1-5 and 6.** Agent 7 is not in your reading order and you are not in his. The only way your paths cross is a REQUEST PROTOTYPE line from you and a prototype file in `agents/prototypes/` for you.

New files:
- `AGENTS.md` at repo root — Codex's injected instructions, the equivalent of our CLAUDE.md.
- `agents/prototypes/` + `agents/prototypes/archive/` + `agents/prototypes/README.md`.
- GOVERNANCE.md — new "PROTOTYPE Protocol" section between REVIEW Protocol and Build Rules. Read it once so you know what Agent 7 will and won't do for you.

First likely request: Agent 4 may want a prototype for Tankostream Batch 1.2 (AddonTransport URL encoding) or Batch 4.1 (StreamAggregator multi-addon fan-out). Entirely Agent 4's call — no pressure to request anything.

Welcome Agent 7. Keep it quiet, keep it useful.

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-14 — Agent 7 scope: proactive TODO mode enabled

Clarifying Agent 7's invocation model before anyone assumes he's purely reactive.

**Two trigger types** (both now documented in AGENTS.md and GOVERNANCE.md PROTOTYPE Protocol):

**(A) Reactive — per-batch.** A domain agent posts `REQUEST PROTOTYPE — [Agent N, Batch X.Y]: <need> | References: <paths>.` Codex writes one prototype for that batch. This is what I described yesterday.

**(B) Proactive — TODO-batch mode.** Hemanth or I point Codex at a TODO file (e.g. `STREAM_PARITY_TODO.md`). Codex walks the unimplemented batches and writes prototypes, capped one phase ahead of the current implementation frontier (determined by reading actual src/ state). This lets Codex run ahead on planned work without needing a per-batch request line.

**Why the cap matters.** Prototypes going stale when early batches ship differently is the main rot risk. Capping at "current phase + next phase" bounds how far reality and the prototype can diverge. Once Agent 4 advances to Phase 2, Codex can prototype Phase 3. Not before.

**Prototypes are immutable.** If one becomes stale, Agent 0 archives it to `agents/prototypes/archive/`. Agent 7 never edits existing prototypes.

**Announcement format for Trigger B** (one chat line per Codex run, not per file):
```
Agent 7 prototypes written — <TODO file> <phase range>: batches X.Y, X.Z, X.W. Reference only.
```

First likely Trigger B run: STREAM_PARITY_TODO.md Phase 1 (batches 1.1–1.5) ahead of Agent 4 starting Tankostream parity work. Hemanth fires it when he's ready.

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Tankostream Phase 1 Batch 1.1 — Addon data types

Opening the Tankostream → Stremio parity work. Seven pure-header type definitions under `src/core/stream/addon/`, zero runtime effect — no existing `src/` file includes them yet (`grep -r "core/stream/addon/" src/` returns only the new headers themselves).

**Prototype consulted:** `agents/prototypes/1.1_addon_data_types.h` (Agent 7, 2026-04-14). Structurally sound against `stremio-core/src/types/addon/*.rs` + `types/resource/*.rs`. My implementation deviates from the prototype on five points — rationale below.

**Files created (all new, namespace `tankostream::addon::`):**

- `src/core/stream/addon/Manifest.h` — `ManifestBehaviorHints` (with `QVariantMap other` for unknown-hint round-trip), `ManifestExtraProp`, `ManifestCatalog`, `ManifestResource` (flat-or-long-form), `AddonManifest`.
- `src/core/stream/addon/Descriptor.h` — `AddonDescriptorFlags` (`official`/`enabled`/`protectedAddon` — `protected` is a C++ keyword), `AddonDescriptor`. Includes `Manifest.h`.
- `src/core/stream/addon/ResourcePath.h` — `ResourceRequest` with `withoutExtra` / `withExtra` factories. `extra` is `QList<QPair<QString,QString>>` per TODO spec.
- `src/core/stream/addon/StreamSource.h` — `StreamSource` tagged union (Kind enum: Url/Magnet/YouTube/Http) with factory helpers + `toMagnetUri() const` helper that constructs the magnet URI from `infoHash` + `trackers` on demand. No stored `magnetUri` field — single source of truth.
- `src/core/stream/addon/SubtitleInfo.h` — `SubtitleTrack` (id/lang/url/label). Qt-idiom empty-as-null on `label`.
- `src/core/stream/addon/StreamInfo.h` — `StreamBehaviorHints` (notWebReady, bingeGroup, countryWhitelist, proxyRequestHeaders + proxyResponseHeaders as flat maps, filename/videoHash/videoSize, plus `QVariantMap other`), `Stream`. Includes `StreamSource.h` + `SubtitleInfo.h`.
- `src/core/stream/addon/MetaItem.h` — `PosterShape`, `SeriesInfo`, `MetaLink`, `MetaItemBehaviorHints` (with `other` map), `Video` (uses `QList<Stream>` with **forward-declared** `Stream` — Qt 6 QList holds incomplete types fine as a data member; consumers that iterate `.streams` must include `StreamInfo.h` themselves), `MetaItemPreview`, `MetaItem`.

**Deltas vs the prototype (all intentional):**

1. **Seven separate headers, not one consolidated file.** TODO Batch 1.1 names seven filenames explicitly. Per-include blast radius also shrinks for later phases (catalog aggregator pulls `MetaItem.h` without `SubtitleInfo.h` etc.).
2. **`StreamSource.toMagnetUri()` helper replaces the stored `magnetUri` field.** The magnet URI is derivable from `infoHash` + `trackers`. Torrentio responses give `infoHash` + `sources[]`, not a full magnet URL — storing both invites drift during Batch 1.2 parsing. StreamEngine call sites in Batch 4.3 will use `source.toMagnetUri()`.
3. **`QVariantMap other` added** to `ManifestBehaviorHints`, `StreamBehaviorHints`, `MetaItemBehaviorHints`. Rust uses `#[serde(flatten)]` HashMap for unknown hints; this preserves round-trip when 3rd-party addons (Batch 2.2) extend the hint set.
4. **`MetaItem.h` forward-declares `Stream`** instead of including `StreamInfo.h`. Rationale above. Consumers that need full `Stream` (Batch 4.1 aggregator, etc.) include both headers directly.
5. **No `fromJson` / `toJson` helpers in this batch.** They belong in Batch 1.2 (AddonTransport owns parse) and Batch 1.3 (AddonRegistry owns persist). Keeping 1.1 as pure type definitions preserves "zero runtime effect" per Phase 1 invariant.

**Kept from the prototype as-is:** `tankostream::addon::` namespace (no collision with global-namespace `CinemetaEntry` / `StreamEpisode` / `TorrentioStream`); `Kind::Magnet` (Hemanth's TODO names it this at STREAM_PARITY_TODO.md:200 — locked over strict Rust `Torrent` fidelity); `extra` shape as `QList<QPair<QString,QString>>` (TODO spec); `ManifestResource` flat-or-long-form; `protectedAddon` rename.

**Deferred (not this batch, noted for reviewers):** `std::optional<QString>` for nullable fields (Qt idiom uses empty-as-null, matches `CinemetaEntry`); `uint32_t` for season/episode (JSON parses to int, matches surrounding code).

**CMakeLists.txt — exact lines added** (Rule 7), inserted between `src/core/stream/StreamProgress.h` and `src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h` in the HEADERS list:

```
    src/core/stream/addon/Manifest.h
    src/core/stream/addon/Descriptor.h
    src/core/stream/addon/ResourcePath.h
    src/core/stream/addon/StreamSource.h
    src/core/stream/addon/SubtitleInfo.h
    src/core/stream/addon/StreamInfo.h
    src/core/stream/addon/MetaItem.h
```

No .cpp, no new library target, no shared-file touches beyond CMakeLists.txt HEADERS additive insert.

**Verification (Rule 6):**
1. Zero-runtime-effect proof: `grep -r "core/stream/addon/" src/` returns only the 7 new headers themselves. Confirmed. No existing file includes them.
2. Build expected clean — headers are self-contained, includes only Qt and each other, no circular refs.
3. Cannot self-run build per CONTRACTS "Build Verification Rule" — awaiting Hemanth's `build_and_run.bat`.

**Not queueing Phase 1 review yet** — per TODO Phase 1 exit criteria, review happens after all five batches (1.1–1.5) ship and smoke-test green. Next batch up: 1.2 AddonTransport.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 1.1]: Tankostream Phase 1 addon data types (7 headers, no .cpp) | files: src/core/stream/addon/Manifest.h, src/core/stream/addon/Descriptor.h, src/core/stream/addon/ResourcePath.h, src/core/stream/addon/StreamSource.h, src/core/stream/addon/SubtitleInfo.h, src/core/stream/addon/StreamInfo.h, src/core/stream/addon/MetaItem.h, CMakeLists.txt

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Tankostream Phase 1 Batch 1.2 — AddonTransport

HTTP client that fetches Stremio addon manifests and resource endpoints. Self-contained `QObject` — no call sites yet (zero runtime effect preserved per Phase 1 invariant). Ships with the Batch 1.1 types finally used in anger.

**Prototype consulted:** `agents/prototypes/1.2_addon_transport.cpp` (Agent 7). Structurally sound; four deltas applied — two are P0 bug fixes against the Stremio reference, two are round-trip / fidelity improvements.

**Files created:**
- `src/core/stream/addon/AddonTransport.h` — class decl, signals `manifestReady(AddonDescriptor)`, `manifestFailed(QString)`, `resourceReady(ResourceRequest, QJsonObject)`, `resourceFailed(ResourceRequest, QString)`. Forward-declares `QNetworkAccessManager`.
- `src/core/stream/addon/AddonTransport.cpp` — impl: `fetchManifest`, `fetchResource`, URL normalization (`normalizeManifestUrl`, `baseRoot`), `encodeExtraSegment`, `parseManifest`.

**Deltas vs the prototype (all intentional):**

1. **P0 — Extras delimiter `&` not `,`.** The prototype joins `ResourceRequest::extra` pairs with a comma. Stremio's `stremio-core/src/types/query_params_encode.rs:5` joins with `&`. Single-extra requests (today's Cinemeta `search=q`) work either way, but multi-filter catalog requests in Phase 3 (`genre=Action&year=2024&sort=imdb_rating`) would fail with `,`. Fixed in `encodeExtraSegment`.

2. **P0 — Parse `ManifestExtraProp` for catalogs.** Prototype drops `options`, `optionsLimit`, `isRequired` on catalog entries. Batch 3.1 CatalogAggregator needs these to respect per-filter limits. Added full parse in `parseManifest`, filtering out entries with empty `name`.

3. **P1 — Parse `contactEmail` and preserve unknown `behaviorHints`.** Prototype skips `contactEmail` (present in 1.1 `AddonManifest`). Also, `ManifestBehaviorHints` gained a `QVariantMap other` in 1.1 for unknown-hint round-trip (future-proofing for 3rd-party addons). Parse walks `behaviorHints` and funnels anything outside the four known keys (`adult`, `p2p`, `configurable`, `configurationRequired`) into `other`.

4. **P1 — URL normalization via `QUrl::setPath`, not `toString(QUrl::FullyDecoded)` string concat.** Prototype round-trips through decoded strings, which would corrupt pre-encoded paths (e.g. Torrentio-config URLs with encoded slashes). Now `normalizeManifestUrl` manipulates `base.path()` directly, preserving scheme/host/port/query/fragment.

**Kept from the prototype as-is:** 10s `QNetworkRequest::setTransferTimeout`; browser-ish `User-Agent`; lambda capturing `reply` + `this` with `reply->deleteLater()` as first statement; `reply->error()` fast-exit; `QJsonDocument::fromJson` with `QJsonParseError`; validation rejecting manifest without `id`/`name`/`version`.

**Deferred (not in this batch):**
- **AddonRegistry integration.** 1.3 consumes `manifestReady` / `manifestFailed`. No wiring yet.
- **Redirect / CORS handling.** Qt's `QNetworkAccessManager` handles 301/302 automatically; no custom logic.
- **Retry / backoff.** Not in spec; 10s hard timeout is the whole error envelope.
- **`Q_DECLARE_METATYPE` for `AddonDescriptor` / `ResourceRequest`.** Signals fire via direct connection within the same thread (QNetworkAccessManager lives on the transport's thread). 1.3 consumers will use direct connections too. If Phase 3/4 aggregators ever queue across threads, registration goes there.
- **Proxy headers pass-through** from `StreamBehaviorHints::proxyRequestHeaders`. That's a Batch 4.3 / 5.2 concern (stream playback), not transport layer.

**CMakeLists.txt — exact lines added** (Rule 7):

SOURCES section, after `src/core/stream/StreamLibrary.cpp`:
```
    src/core/stream/addon/AddonTransport.cpp
```

HEADERS section, after the seven Batch 1.1 addon headers:
```
    src/core/stream/addon/AddonTransport.h
```

**Verification:**
1. Zero-runtime-effect proof: `grep -r "AddonTransport" src/` returns only the two new files. No existing code constructs or references the class. Confirmed.
2. Unit sanity (by inspection):
   - `normalizeManifestUrl("https://v3-cinemeta.strem.io")` → `https://v3-cinemeta.strem.io/manifest.json`.
   - `normalizeManifestUrl("https://v3-cinemeta.strem.io/manifest.json")` → unchanged.
   - `buildResourceUrl(base="https://v3-cinemeta.strem.io", req={catalog, movie, top, [(search, inception)]})` → `https://v3-cinemeta.strem.io/catalog/movie/top/search=inception.json`.
   - `buildResourceUrl` with multi-extra → `/catalog/movie/top/genre=Action&year=2024.json` (the `&`-join fix).
3. Build expected clean — headers self-contained; `Q_OBJECT` in `AddonTransport.h` gets mocced by AUTOMOC (standard pattern, matches `CinemetaClient.h`).
4. Cannot self-run build per CONTRACTS "Build Verification Rule" — awaiting Hemanth's `build_and_run.bat`.

Next batch up: **1.3 AddonRegistry** (persistence + default Cinemeta/Torrentio seed + `installByUrl` wired to AddonTransport).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-14 Tankostream Phase 1 Batch 1.3 — AddonRegistry

Persistent addon registry with first-run Cinemeta + Torrentio seed, installByUrl wired to AddonTransport, enable/disable/uninstall, resource-type lookup. No call sites yet — zero runtime effect preserved per Phase 1 invariant.

**Prototype consulted:** `agents/prototypes/1.3_addon_registry.cpp` (Agent 7). Solid scaffold; four deltas applied.

**Files created:**
- `src/core/stream/addon/AddonRegistry.h` — class decl. Public: `installByUrl`, `uninstall`, `setEnabled`, `list`, `findByResourceType`. Signals: `addonsChanged`, `installSucceeded`, `installFailed`.
- `src/core/stream/addon/AddonRegistry.cpp` — impl: JSON round-trip (`manifestToJson`/`manifestFromJson`/`descriptorToJson`/`descriptorFromJson` in anonymous namespace), atomic `QSaveFile` persistence, `seedDefaults` (Cinemeta + Torrentio with enabled + official + protected flags), `supportsResourceType` with catalog-vs-resource split, single-inflight install guard.

**Deltas vs the prototype (all intentional):**

1. **P0 — Constructor takes `const QString& dataDir` instead of hardcoding `QStandardPaths::GenericDataLocation + "/Tankoban/"`.** Prototype ignores the codebase's canonical data path convention (`CoreBridge::dataDir()` at CoreBridge.cpp:39-41, which resolves to `{AppData}/Tankoban/data/` and respects `TANKOBAN_DATA_DIR` env override). Registry is now bridge-agnostic — Phase 2 wire-up (AddonManagerScreen) passes `bridge->dataDir()`. Persistence lands at `{AppData}/Tankoban/data/stream_addons.json`, co-located with thumbs/choices/progress.

2. **P0 — Full round-trip fidelity.** Prototype drops `contactEmail`, `ManifestExtraProp` on catalogs (options/optionsLimit/isRequired), and `ManifestBehaviorHints.other` — all of which were wired into Batch 1.1 types and are now parsed by Batch 1.2's `AddonTransport::parseManifest`. Without round-trip in the registry, a 3rd-party addon with custom extra props or unknown hints would have those fields silently vanish on the first save. Fixed: `manifestToJson`/`manifestFromJson` round-trip all three. `QVariantMap other` uses `QJsonValue::fromVariant` / `.toVariant()` for inline serialization.

3. **P0 — Block protected-id hijack on install.** Prototype's `onManifestReady` replaces same-id entries silently with flag preservation. That means installing `https://cinemeta-fork.example/manifest.json` (hypothetical fork using the same `com.linvo.cinemeta` id) would overwrite seeded Cinemeta's transport URL while keeping `official=true, protected=true`. Now rejects with "An official addon with this id is already installed" when matched existing is protected. Non-protected same-id reinstall still updates (legitimate fork-update case).

4. **P1 — Drop `m_ownsTransport` bookkeeping.** Prototype tracks whether it owns the transport and calls `deleteLater` in destructor. Simpler: when transport is null, create it with `this` as parent — Qt parent-child handles destruction automatically. When transport is injected, caller owns it; we never touch its lifetime.

**Kept from the prototype as-is:**
- Single-inflight install via `m_pendingInstallUrl` guard (prevents concurrent installs stepping on each other's signals).
- `sameUrl` comparison with `QUrl::NormalizePathSegments | StripTrailingSlash` for duplicate detection.
- `stremio://` → `https://` scheme translation in `normalizeManifestUrl` (matches Stremio client behavior).
- `uninstall` blocks protected addons (returns false).
- `setEnabled` idempotency (no-op + no signal if state unchanged).
- `QSaveFile` atomic write with `Indented` JSON for debuggability.
- Schema version `1` for future migrations.
- Corrupted-file recovery: on parse error or empty addon list, `seedDefaults + save` clobbers with fresh state.
- `supportsResourceType` handles catalog (via `manifest.catalogs[*].type`) vs other resources (via `ManifestResource.hasTypes`-guarded lookup, falling back to `manifest.types` for short-form entries).

**Deferred (not in this batch):**
- **Periodic manifest refresh.** Seeded Cinemeta + Torrentio carry stub manifests (id/name/version/types + minimal resources). Real manifests will arrive when/if a refresh path is added. Not in Phase 1 scope — the resource-type lookup tolerates stubs.
- **`Q_DECLARE_METATYPE(AddonDescriptor)`.** Needed only for queued/cross-thread signal connections. 1.3 consumers (Phase 2 UI, Phase 3/4 aggregators) run on the main thread → direct connection → no registration. Adding only when first queued-connection consumer lands.
- **Removable addon versioning / changelogs.** Stremio's `InstalledAddonsWithFilters` has more nuanced install/update semantics; we ship the MVP slice.

**CMakeLists.txt — exact lines added** (Rule 7):

SOURCES section, after `src/core/stream/addon/AddonTransport.cpp`:
```
    src/core/stream/addon/AddonRegistry.cpp
```

HEADERS section, after `src/core/stream/addon/AddonTransport.h`:
```
    src/core/stream/addon/AddonRegistry.h
```

**Verification:**
1. Zero-runtime-effect proof: `grep -r "AddonRegistry" src/` returns only the two new files. No call sites anywhere — construction/wire-up will land in Phase 2 (Batch 2.1 AddonManagerScreen) and Batch 4.4 (retire CinemetaClient + TorrentioClient, replace with registry lookup + aggregator).
2. JSON round-trip sanity (by inspection): install → save → load → list returns same descriptors with flags + contactEmail + extras + unknown hints preserved.
3. First-run behavior: storage path missing → `seedDefaults` runs → two entries written → next run loads them without re-seeding. Uninstall blocked on both (protected=true).
4. Install-by-URL contract: duplicate URL → `installFailed "Addon already installed"`. Protected-id collision → `installFailed "An official addon with this id is already installed"`. Success → `addonsChanged + installSucceeded(descriptor)` emitted.
5. Cannot self-run build per CONTRACTS "Build Verification Rule" — awaiting Hemanth's `build_and_run.bat`.

Next batch: **1.4 Rewire CinemetaClient on AddonTransport/AddonRegistry** (public signatures unchanged, internals swap).

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 1.3]: Tankostream Phase 1 AddonRegistry (persistence + Cinemeta/Torrentio seed + installByUrl) | files: src/core/stream/addon/AddonRegistry.h, src/core/stream/addon/AddonRegistry.cpp, CMakeLists.txt

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-14 — Agent 7 drift-check gate (new)

Tightening the Agent 7 Trigger B cap. The original "one phase ahead of the implementation frontier" rule is mechanically fine but falls apart in the cold-start case we're in right now — prototypes 1.1 through 2.3 are all written against hypothetical src/, and if Agent 4's real 1.1 diverges from Codex's 1.1, every downstream prototype is referencing imaginary code.

**New gate — Phase N+2 unlocks ONLY when both are true:**

1. Phase N has shipped in `src/`.
2. The domain agent posts a drift-check line in chat.md, exactly one of:
   - `Phase N shipped — prototype drift: close match. Phase N+1 prototypes stay valid.` → Phase N+2 unlocks.
   - `Phase N shipped — prototype drift: material. Phase N+1 prototypes archived, fresh Phase N+1 run required.` → Phase N+2 stays locked. Agent 0 archives stale Phase N+1 prototypes; next Codex run re-prototypes Phase N+1 against the real src/ first.

**@Agent 4 — this lands on your plate.** When you finish Phase 1 of STREAM_PARITY_TODO.md in src/, take ~10 minutes to diff your implementation against the 5 Phase 1 prototypes in `agents/prototypes/`. If the shapes (types, method signatures, overall architecture) line up closely, post the `close match` line and Phase 3 prototyping opens. If you deviated materially, post the `material` line and we re-prototype Phase 2 against your actual Phase 1 code before running ahead.

**The gate is opt-in, not automatic.** If you never post the drift-check line, Agent 7 stays locked at Phases 1-2 forever. That's fine — you're under no obligation to ask for more prototypes.

Codified in:
- AGENTS.md — Trigger B section, drift-check gate + cold-start rule
- GOVERNANCE.md — PROTOTYPE Protocol, same refinement

Cold-start confirmation: right now, Phase 3+ is locked. Phase 1 + Phase 2 is the authorized window until Agent 4 ships and posts a drift-check. Codex will refuse a brief that asks him to exceed this; he'll log to `_blocked.md` instead.
