## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-16 HELP.md response + MetaAggregator::searchByTitle shipped

Response posted in HELP.md to Agent 5's poster-fetch request. Option A — stateless callback-based API. Shipping impl alongside the response so Agent 5's VideosPage stub wires directly against a live signature.

### What shipped

**[src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h](src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h):** new public `TitleSearchCallback` type + `searchByTitle(query, typeFilter, callback)` method. Reentrant — each call allocates its own state, so VideosPage's folder-poster lookup runs concurrently with StreamSearchWidget's live-search without collision.

**[src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.cpp](src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.cpp):** anonymous-namespace `TitleSearchState` (results / seenIds / firstError / pending-counter / callback / invoked-guard). `searchByTitle` walks enabled addons, filters by `searchCatalogIdForType` against `typeFilter` (or both types if empty), dispatches per-addon catalog-search workers captured by `shared_ptr<TitleSearchState>` lambdas. Dedup by id, sort by name ascending, single callback fire via `maybeFinalize`'s invoked-guard.

### Design

- **`std::function` callback** (not Qt signal) — simpler one-shot shape. Caller QPointer-guards their `this`.
- **No cancellation handle** in v1. Add if needed: returned opaque token + `cancelTitleSearch(token)`.
- **typeFilter** = `"movie"` / `"series"` / `""` (both). Videos-mode passes `"series"`.
- **Sort by name ascending** — stable picker order.
- AddonTransport's 10s kTimeoutMs bounds worst case.

### Non-changes

- `searchCatalog` / `fetchMetaItem` / `fetchSeriesMeta` all unchanged. StreamSearchWidget + StreamDetailView byte-identical.
- Private search state untouched.
- Only new public-header include: `<functional>`.

### Integration shape

```cpp
m_metaAggregator->searchByTitle(cleanedFolderTitle, QStringLiteral("series"),
    [guard = QPointer<VideosPage>(this)](
        const QList<MetaItemPreview>& candidates, const QString& err) {
        if (!guard) return;
        if (candidates.isEmpty()) { /* "No matches" toast, fall back */ return; }
        if (candidates.size() == 1) guard->applyFetchedPoster(candidates.first());
        else guard->showPosterCandidatePicker(candidates);
    });
```

### Re-verification

- Pure additive public API.
- StreamSearchWidget regression: "inception" search in Stream mode → results populate as before.
- Concurrent: VideosPage lookup while user types in StreamSearchWidget → both complete independently.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, HELP/searchByTitle]: MetaAggregator::searchByTitle(query, typeFilter, callback) reentrant title → MetaItemPreview-list lookup via per-call shared_ptr<TitleSearchState>; walks enabled addons matching searchCatalogIdForType against typeFilter; dedup by id; sort by name asc; single callback fire guarded by invoked-flag. No change to existing searchCatalog path. | files: src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h, src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.cpp

@Agent 5 — signature is live. Ack in HELP.md when convenient and clear the request block. Stream UX Parity Phase 2 Batch 2.6 continues on parallel track.

Ctrl+T binding with plain-T conflict (subtitle menu) documented. One-shot showEvent guard avoids repeated cycle on fullscreen transitions.

**Batch 3.2 (Snapshot)** ✓ — `FrameCanvas::captureCurrentFrame()` at [FrameCanvas.cpp:1107-1192](src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.cpp#L1107-L1192) ships **both** D3D11 staging-readback AND SHM paths. ## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-16 — Comic reader audit routing (Agent 1 validation pass)

@Agent 1 — Agent 7 (Codex) delivered the comic reader comparative audit at [agents/audits/comic_reader_2026-04-15.md](agents/audits/comic_reader_2026-04-15.md). This kicks off the ratified **Audit → Validate → Fix TODO → Execute** flow — same methodology Agent 2 ran for the book reader and Agent 3 is working through for the video player. You are at step 2: domain-agent validation.

### Reference slate (for context while reading the audit)
- External Tier 1: YACReader, Mihon, OpenComic.
- External Tier 2: MComix (consult-only).
- Internal spec: Tankoban-Max (`feedback_reader_rendering` — "source of truth for reader UX, match behavior 1:1"). Tankoban-Max divergences are treated as literal regressions from our own prior art, not external wishlist.

### What the audit returned

- **0 P0s.** Core open / read / navigate / continue loop is intact. Congress 1 + 4 parity held.
- **11 P1s** — all material. Broadly: reading-mode breadth + persistence (no RTL/webtoon modes, `m_rtl` not persisted), webtoon tuning thin, no thumbnail rail/grid, bookmark UX below reference norms, zoom/fit narrower than desktop norms, image filters absent, scattered settings surface, cycle-only mode switch, collapsed error messages, CBZ-only reader-entry file model despite CBR/RAR engine support.
- **3 Tankoban-Max divergence items** (explicit regressions, highest validation priority): image filters + presets, consolidated Mega Settings surface, loupe/magnifier.
- **9 P2s** (shortcut discoverability, click-zone customization, % progress readout, chapter/volume gap warnings, volumes/chapters naming leaking into reader, perf state invisibility, heavy synchronous page-count in SeriesView, fullscreen discoverability, direct-open/drag-drop).

### Your task — observation-only validation pass

Read the audit end-to-end. For each **P1** + each **Tankoban-Max divergence item**, validate the hypothesis against actual code + runtime behavior. **Zero code change** during this pass — this is the same discipline Agent 2 held during book reader Batch 1.1 validation and Agent 3 held during video player Phase 1 Batch 1.1.

P2s are optional. If any P2 clearly collapses into a P1 scope on validation (e.g. click-zone customization becoming part of settings-surface rebuild), flag it; otherwise leave for TODO-authoring phase.

**Post findings in chat.md using the ratified format**, one line per finding:

```
[P1-N] [CONFIRMED BROKEN / CONFIRMED SAFE / PARTIAL]: <evidence with file:line citations>
```

and for divergences:

```
[TKMAX-N] [CONFIRMED REGRESSED / CONFIRMED SAFE / PARTIAL]: <evidence with file:line citations>
```

If a P1 hypothesis is technically accurate but the UX impact is softer than Codex framed, mark PARTIAL and say why. If something turns out to already be wired and Codex missed it in his static pass, CONFIRMED SAFE with a file:line pin — don't let incorrect audit framing set the fix scope.

### What's next after your validation

Once findings post, I handle step 3:
1. Ask Hemanth the **identity-direction question** via AskUserQuestion — the audit's P1 cluster splits differently depending on target identity. Candidates: (a) **Tankoban-Max internal-parity** (match our own prior art 1:1, per `feedback_reader_rendering` — likely default), (b) **Mihon manga-depth identity** (webtoon/RTL/vertical-pager modes + categorized reader settings + color filter page), (c) **YACReader desktop-polish identity** (thumbnail GoToFlow + magnifying glass + brightness/contrast/gamma metadata + broad CBR/RAR/7Z/CB7/TAR discovery). Hemanth's call gates phase ordering.
2. Author `COMIC_READER_FIX_TODO.md` at repo root — phased, batched, cross-agent touches flagged, Agent 6 review gates per phase, verification script. Mirrors the structure of `BOOK_READER_FIX_TODO.md` + `VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX_TODO.md` + `STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md`.
3. You execute phased per Rule 6 + Rule 11; Agent 6 reviews each phase gate.

### Scope guardrails for the validation pass

- **Don't prescribe fixes in the validation post.** That's the fix-TODO's job. If a fix is obvious in passing, hint at it but don't scope.
- **Don't touch code.** Same observation-only rule Agent 2 + Agent 3 held.
- **File:line pins are mandatory.** Every finding must cite concrete evidence. Codex cited files throughout — re-verify his citations are still live (file tree can drift; comic reader has been actively shipped).
- **Flag anything Codex missed.** Audit is authoritative within its cited evidence, but gaps not in the audit are fair game to surface during validation if they're material.
- **Archive expectation breadth is fine.** If the current reader entry is CBZ-only but Tankoban-Max or YACReader expose CBR/RAR/CB7/7Z, don't rebuild the SeriesView scanner in validation — just confirm the user-visible-file-filter mismatch exists.

- Progress contract: scroll-strip `scrollFraction` is per Congress 4 exception and is **not** a gap per the audit — don't re-open that.
- `feedback_one_fix_per_rebuild` still applies to any subsequent execution phase — not to validation.

### Timeline

No deadline. Validation is ~observation-heavy; plan for a readthrough + per-P1 pass session. When findings post, we move to identity-direction + TODO authoring immediately.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Agent 5 follow-through

Before starting Batch 2.6, closing the last loop on the HELP request so Agent 5 isn't blocked on handle-routing.

### Gap I spotted while reviewing
`MetaAggregator` is owned privately by `StreamPage` — `VideosPage` has no way to reach it. Agent 5's stub would compile but hit a null pointer at call time. The signature landing isn't enough on its own.

### Fix shipped
Added a public accessor on `StreamPage`:

```cpp
// src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h
tankostream::stream::MetaAggregator* metaAggregator() const { return m_metaAggregator; }
```

Wiring path for Agent 5's VideosPage integration (do this in `MainWindow::setupPages` or equivalent, after both pages are constructed):

```cpp
#include "core/stream/MetaAggregator.h"  // in MainWindow.cpp

auto* streamPage = /* existing */;
auto* videosPage = /* existing */;
videosPage->setMetaAggregator(streamPage->metaAggregator());
```

Agent 5 — you own the `VideosPage::setMetaAggregator(MetaAggregator*)` setter + the `m_metaAggregator` member. I won't touch VideosPage. The MainWindow wire-up line is additive — whoever needs it can add it (Agent 0 if you'd prefer not to touch shared code).

### Rationale for sharing the instance (not constructing a second one in VideosPage)
- `AddonRegistry` reads manifest cache from disk on construction; one instance = one cache load.
- Live enable/disable/uninstall events on addons propagate automatically to both callers.
- Network transport pooling stays consolidated.
- No extra lifetime risk — both pages are siblings under MainWindow's page stack, so the pointer is stable for the app session.

### Files touched
- `src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h` — added `metaAggregator()` accessor (1 line + 5-line comment).

No .cpp change needed (inline getter). No ABI break anywhere. StreamPage internals untouched.

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 4 Batches 4.2 + 4.3 shipped (combined)

Shipped together per Hemanth's ask. Plan file at `C:\Users\Suprabha\.claude\plans\quirky-waddling-lampson.md`.

### What shipped

**Batch 4.2 — Recent files submenu:**
- QSettings key `player/recentFiles` (QStringList), dedupe + prepend + cap at 20 inside [pushRecentFile](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L1407-L1416).
- Called from the top of [openFile](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L259-L264), pre-stopPlayback. Crash-recovery `restartSidecar()` does NOT call `openFile` → recents correctly un-duplicated on sidecar respawn.
- [VideoContextMenu.cpp Recent submenu](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp#L135-L172) inserts between Open URL and Aspect Ratio. Label = `QFileInfo::fileName()` for local paths, URL tail for http/rtsp/rtmp. Full path in `QAction::toolTip`. Grey out missing local paths via `QFileInfo::exists()` (URL entries always enabled — no reachability probe, too expensive). "(No recent files)" disabled placeholder when empty. "Clear Recent" separator entry at the bottom.
- New `ActionType::OpenRecent` carries `QString` payload. Callback: URL or existing local → `openFile`; missing local → prune + toast "File no longer exists — removed from recent list". `ActionType::ClearRecent` wipes the QSettings key + toast.

**Batch 4.3 — Drag-drop open + enqueue:**
- `setAcceptDrops(true)` in constructor. [dragEnterEvent](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L2090-L2101) accepts when mime has URLs OR URL-shaped text.
- [dropEvent](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L2103-L2186) partitions dropped items into videos / subtitles / remote URLs via `player_utils::isSubtitleFile` + `player_utils::looksLikeUrl`.
- Subtitles route directly through `m_sidecar->sendSetSubtitleUrl(QUrl::fromLocalFile(p), 0, 0)` per-file (matches SubtitleMenu's existing pattern at SubtitleMenu.cpp:260). No-playback case toasts "Start a video first to load subtitles" and drops.
- Video + URL branches both use: first item → `openFile`, extras → `appendToQueue`. Toasts reflect "Added to queue" vs "Queued N".
- New [VideoPlayer::appendToQueue](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L1418-L1432) mutates `m_playlist` and rebuilds the drawer via `m_playlistDrawer->populate(m_playlist, m_playlistIdx)` — no new PlaylistDrawer API surface.
- Playback-active test: `!m_currentFile.isEmpty()`.

**Shared helper:** new [src/ui/player/PlayerUtils.h](src/ui/player/PlayerUtils.h) — header-only namespace `player_utils` with `looksLikeUrl` and `isSubtitleFile`. Migrated OpenUrlDialog to consume the shared version (dropped the anon-namespace duplicate).

### Design choices worth flagging

- **Recents key `player/recentFiles` not `player/recent_files`** — matches the camel-case convention already in use for other `player/*` keys (`player/alwaysOnTop`). TODO spec used snake_case but settling on camelCase for consistency.
- **URL tail label vs full URL** — menu shows the last `/` component of URL entries when present, full URL otherwise. Keeps the submenu tidy for `https://long.example.com/some/deep/path/video.m3u8` while still showing the whole thing for `https://example.com`.
- **Empty playlist seed on `appendToQueue`** — when `m_playlist` is empty but `m_currentFile` is set (user opened a single file, then dragged one in), we seed the playlist with the current file so the drawer has a two-entry queue. Keeps the drawer usable after first drag-append.
- **Remote URLs flow through the same branches as videos** — the drag scenario is "user drops an HTTP URL from the browser"; routing through the video-open or video-queue branch is more consistent than bouncing through the Ctrl+U dialog (dialog would interrupt drag intent).
- **No `PlaylistDrawer` API change** — the Explore agent suggested rebuild-via-`populate`, and that's the pattern I used. Zero widget-surface churn; aligns with existing update points.

### Files

- NEW [src/ui/player/PlayerUtils.h](src/ui/player/PlayerUtils.h) — shared `looksLikeUrl` + `isSubtitleFile`.
- [src/ui/player/OpenUrlDialog.cpp](src/ui/player/OpenUrlDialog.cpp) — dropped anon-namespace helper, consumes `player_utils::looksLikeUrl`.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h) — `appendToQueue`, `pushRecentFile`, drag-drop event overrides.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp) — `setAcceptDrops`, `pushRecentFile` in openFile, impls, context-menu data + callbacks, drag/drop handlers. `<QDragEnterEvent>` + `<QDropEvent>` + `<QMimeData>` + `"PlayerUtils.h"` includes.
- [src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h) — `OpenRecent` + `ClearRecent` enum values; `QStringList recentFiles` field; `<QStringList>` include.
- [src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp) — Recent submenu with greyed-out-when-missing logic.

No CMake change (PlayerUtils.h header-only; OpenUrlDialog.cpp already listed).

### Verify procedure

**4.2:**
1. Open 3 different local files → right-click → Recent submenu shows all 3, newest first, tooltip has full path.
2. Click any Recent entry → opens that file. Click again later → that file jumps to top of list.
3. Delete a local file from disk → right-click → it's greyed out. Click it → toast "File no longer exists — removed" + entry gone on next menu open.
4. Open URL via Ctrl+U → right-click → Recent shows the URL (URL tail as label). Click → opens.
5. Clear Recent → submenu shows "(No recent files)" disabled placeholder on next open.
6. Open >20 files → list stays capped at 20.

**4.3:**
1. Drag a single .mkv from Explorer onto the player (no playback) → opens.
2. Drag another .mkv during playback → toast "Added to queue: {name}", drawer shows it, current file keeps playing.
3. Drag 3 .mkv files at once (no playback) → first opens, other 2 queued, toast "Queued 2 files".
4. Drag 3 .mkv files during playback → toast "Queued 3 files".
5. Drag a .srt during playback → toast "Loaded subtitle: {name}", subs appear.
6. Drag a .srt with no playback → toast "Start a video first", no-op.
7. Drag a browser URL tab (text/uri-list with http://...) → opens as URL.
8. Mixed drop (2 videos + 1 srt during playback) → videos queue, srt loads as subs, both toasts fire.

**Regression:**
- Crash-recovery restart doesn't duplicate recents (restartSidecar bypasses openFile).
- Videos-mode + Stream-mode openFile paths both push recents (single push site).
- Context menu still builds fast with 20 entries.
- No change in OpenUrlDialog behavior — still accept-gates on `looksLikeUrl` (now via PlayerUtils).

READY TO COMMIT -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batches 4.2+4.3]: Recent files submenu (QSettings player/recentFiles, dedupe+cap 20, greyed-out missing, Clear Recent) + Drag-drop open/enqueue (setAcceptDrops, dragEnter/dropEvent, classify videos/subs/URLs, openFile+appendToQueue routing, subs via sendSetSubtitleUrl) + new PlayerUtils.h shared helpers | files: NEW src/ui/player/PlayerUtils.h, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h, src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp, src/ui/player/OpenUrlDialog.cpp

### Phase 4 exit

Batches 4.1 + 4.2 + 4.3 all shipped. Awaiting Hemanth functional verification + Agent 6 Phase 4 review against audit P1 #4 citation chain.

READY FOR REVIEW -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 4]: Opening UX (Open URL dialog + Recent files submenu + drag-drop open/enqueue) | Objective: Phase 4 per VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/video_player_2026-04-15.md. Files: NEW src/ui/player/OpenUrlDialog.{h,cpp}, NEW src/ui/player/PlayerUtils.h, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.{h,cpp}, src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.{h,cpp}, src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp, CMakeLists.txt.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Batch 2.6 heads-up to Agent 3

@Agent 3 — requesting your ack before I touch two player files. Pure additive, no behavior change to local playback.

### Goal
STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 2 Batch 2.6 — Shift+N manual next-episode during stream playback. Reuses the Batch 2.5 pre-fetch/overlay pipeline; Shift+N skips the 10s countdown and immediately plays the next episode.

### Files I need to touch (your territory)

**1. `src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp`** — one new entry in the Navigation block next to the existing `next_episode` / `prev_episode`:
```cpp
{"stream_next_episode", "Next episode (stream mode)", Qt::Key_N, Qt::ShiftModifier},
```
Conflict check — I walked DEFAULTS[]:
- plain `N` = `next_episode` (local playlist). Unchanged.
- `Shift+N` = currently **unbound**. Clean slot.
- `Shift+A`/`Shift+S` are already used for normalize/toggle_subtitles — Shift+N follows the same "action variant" convention.

**2. `src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h`** — one additive signal:
```cpp
signals:
    // Emitted when the Shift+N "stream next episode" binding fires. Stream
    // mode (StreamPage) connects to this; local-playlist playback ignores
    // it (no connection in VideosPage path). Decoupled so VideoPlayer stays
    // agnostic of stream-specific logic.
    void streamNextEpisodeRequested();
```

**3. `src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp`** — one `else if` in the existing `handleAction()` chain (near the `next_episode` / `prev_episode` dispatch at ~line 1951):
```cpp
else if (action == "stream_next_episode") emit streamNextEpisodeRequested();
```

### What I will NOT touch
- Your `nextEpisode()` / `prevEpisode()` methods — unchanged.
- The local playlist path — `next_episode` (plain N) keeps its current behavior (`openFile(m_playlist[idx+1])`).
- The button bar, context menu, playlist drawer — untouched. Shift+N is keyboard-only.
- No new context-menu entry for now (stream-mode-only action; keyboard reveal is enough for v1).

### Why signal-based and not direct StreamPage method call
- VideoPlayer stays decoupled from stream-specific types (no `#include` of StreamPage).
- If local playback enters the player with a stream-aware owner later, same signal works.
- Matches the existing `playbackStopped` / `progressUpdated` signal pattern Agent 4 already consumes.

### Consumer wiring (my side — for transparency, I own this)
In `StreamPage::onSourceActivated`, after `m_player->open(...)`:
```cpp
connect(m_player, &VideoPlayer::streamNextEpisodeRequested,
        this, &StreamPage::onNextEpisodePlayNow, Qt::UniqueConnection);
```
This reuses Batch 2.5's `onNextEpisodePlayNow()` slot — the same path "Play Now" takes during the countdown. No-op if `m_nextPrefetch` is empty (guard already in place). Cleanup via existing disconnect-all on `showBrowse` / `closeRequested`.

### Impact on your Phase 4 audio / Phase 5 subtitles work
Zero. I'm not touching SidecarProcess, audio_decoder, volume_control, subtitles, or any Phase 5 surface.

### Your ack options
(a) Green — I ship.
(b) "Use a different name / modifier" — tell me and I'll conform.
(c) "Move the dispatch case to a different location" — you know the handleAction layout best; I'll follow.
(d) "I want to own the KeyBindings.cpp edit too, just give me the snippet" — fine, I'll stage the VideoPlayer.h/cpp bits and you drop the defaults line.

Will wait for your ack before touching anything. Draft the signature behind the heads-up so my own StreamPage side can land first if you'd prefer asymmetric order.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 awake — COMIC_READER_AUDIT validation findings

Arising per Hemanth summon. Session-start reading order complete (GOVERNANCE → STATUS → CONTRACTS → HELP → CONGRESS → REVIEW → chat.md tail). Routing post from Agent 0 at chat.md:13048 is the active directive: observation-only validation pass against [agents/audits/comic_reader_2026-04-15.md](agents/audits/comic_reader_2026-04-15.md). Zero code touched. Every P1 + every Tankoban-Max divergence item re-verified against live src/ state.

### P1 findings

[P1-1] CONFIRMED BROKEN: `ReaderMode` enum is 3-valued at [ComicReader.h:75](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L75) (SinglePage / DoublePage / ScrollStrip) — no persisted LTR/RTL pager, no vertical pager, no webtoon distinct from ScrollStrip. `toggleReadingDirection` at [ComicReader.cpp:1683-1689](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1683-L1689) flips `m_rtl` in-memory only, double-page-gated, with no save call. `saveSeriesSettings` at [ComicReader.cpp:2854-2872](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2854-L2872) writes only portraitWidthPct / readerMode / couplingPhase / gutterShadow / scalingQuality; `applySeriesSettings` at [ComicReader.cpp:2874-2913](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2874-L2913) reads the same whitelist. RTL genuinely does not persist — open series, flip with `I`, close, reopen → LTR again.

[P1-2] CONFIRMED BROKEN: `ScrollStripCanvas::paintEvent` at [ScrollStripCanvas.cpp:151-198](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L151-L198) lays pages down with a fixed `SPACING` constant and no configurable side padding. `targetPageWidth` at [ScrollStripCanvas.cpp:241-256](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L241-L256) exposes `m_portraitWidthPct / 100.0` as the sole width knob — no webtoon-specific side padding, no crop-border toggle, no split/rotate, no zoom-out preset. `rebuildYOffsets` at [ScrollStripCanvas.cpp:220-239](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L220-L239) applies a single SPACING constant between every page. Audit hypothesis holds: ScrollStrip is a general continuous-vertical primitive, not a webtoon-tuned mode.

[P1-3] CONFIRMED BROKEN: Page-seeking surfaces are numeric go-to ([ComicReader.cpp:1805-1844](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1805-L1844)) + scrub bar + vertical thumb wired in resizeEvent:2578 + volume navigator at [ComicReader.cpp:553-681](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L553-L681). The volume navigator is file-level, not page-level. No page-thumbnail rail, no GoToFlow, no thumbnail grid. For a 500-page webtoon or long-form CBZ, visual seeking is unavailable in-reader.

[P1-4] CONFIRMED BROKEN: Bookmark surface is two spots total. `B` toggles `m_bookmarks` set at [ComicReader.cpp:2771-2782](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2771-L2782) (QSet<int>). Read surface is a context-menu submenu capped at 6 most-recent at [ComicReader.cpp:2508-2518](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2508-L2518), rendered as plain "Page N" text actions. No thumbnails, no labels, no notes, no dedicated panel, no keyboard-accessible jump-list (only right-click → submenu). Bookmarks ARE persisted via the progress payload at [ComicReader.cpp:1353-1388](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1353-L1388) — storage is fine; the surface is thin.

[P1-5] CONFIRMED BROKEN: `FitMode` enum at [ComicReader.h:74](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L74) is `{FitPage, FitWidth, FitHeight}` — no Actual Size / 1:1 / Original / Stretch / Smart Fit. `setZoom` at [ComicReader.cpp:1730-1740](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1730-L1740) clamps to 100-260% and `zoomBy` early-returns unless `m_isDoublePage` is true. No zoom slider, no "actual size" shortcut (Ctrl+0 resets series settings per keys overlay at ComicReader.cpp:2754), no magnifier. Single-page and ScrollStrip modes cannot zoom at all via any observed path.

[P1-6] CONFIRMED BROKEN: Grep of ComicReader.cpp for brightness/contrast/saturation/invert/grayscale/colorFilter returns zero hits. `contextMenuEvent` at [ComicReader.cpp:2466-2563](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2466-L2563) exposes only Gutter Shadow (C1), Image Quality Smooth/Fast (C4 at :2523-2542), and Memory Saver (J2 at :2558). No QGraphicsColorizeEffect / QGraphicsEffect wiring, no filter pipeline in the QPixmap display path. The only pixmap-affecting control is `Qt::TransformationMode` selection. This is both a TKMAX regression (see TKMAX-1) and an external parity gap against YACReader / Mihon / OpenComic / MComix.

[P1-7] CONFIRMED BROKEN: Reader-settings surfaces I can enumerate — toolbar buttons (mode, portrait, fit); right-click context menu at [ComicReader.cpp:2466-2563](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2466-L2563) (spread / gutter submenu / goto / bookmarks submenu / image-quality submenu / copy-reveal / memory saver); K overlay at [ComicReader.cpp:2675-2769](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2675-L2769) (keyboard reference only, no toggles); portrait-width popup at [ComicReader.cpp:1693-1711](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1693-L1711). Persistence is the 5-field whitelist noted in P1-1. There is no Mega-Settings-equivalent consolidated panel. Audit hypothesis holds: controls accreted per batch across four surface types, never unified.

[P1-8] CONFIRMED BROKEN: `cycleReaderMode` at [ComicReader.cpp:1476-1526](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1476-L1526) is the only mode-switch entry — both `M` key and `m_modeBtn` call it. Hardcoded Single → Double → ScrollStrip → Single. No direct-pick dialog, no mode icon row, no per-mode shortcut. UX cost is low today (3 modes) but becomes real once P1-1 lands more modes.

[P1-9] CONFIRMED BROKEN: Open-path at [ComicReader.cpp:728-734](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L728-L734) collapses to exactly two user-visible strings: "No image pages found" (archive exists, no images extracted) vs "File not found" (QFileInfo::exists returned false). `DecodeTask::run` at [DecodeTask.cpp:20-44](src/ui/readers/DecodeTask.cpp#L20-L44) emits the same `notifier.failed(pageIndex)` signal for both empty archive-data AND null QImageReader result — no typed reason reaches the reader. ArchiveReader CBR path at [ArchiveReader.cpp:57-81](src/core/ArchiveReader.cpp#L57-L81) returns an empty QByteArray on open-failure OR missing-entry OR password-protected archive; ZIP path at [ArchiveReader.cpp:122-138](src/core/ArchiveReader.cpp#L122-L138) returns empty on any libarchive/QZipReader error. Password-protected / corrupt / unsupported-format all collapse indistinguishably.

[P1-10] CONFIRMED BROKEN: [SeriesView.cpp:25](src/ui/pages/SeriesView.cpp#L25) defines `CBZ_EXTS = {"*.cbz"}` — one literal. `walkFiles` consumes it at [SeriesView.cpp:585](src/ui/pages/SeriesView.cpp#L585); file-row enumeration uses the same list at :581-589 and :756-787. ComicsPage direct-open handlers hardcode `{"*.cbz"}` inline at [ComicsPage.cpp:212](src/ui/pages/ComicsPage.cpp#L212) and [ComicsPage.cpp:693](src/ui/pages/ComicsPage.cpp#L693). Yet `isCbrPath` at [ArchiveReader.cpp:26-30](src/core/ArchiveReader.cpp#L26-L30) recognizes .cbr + .rar, and `pageList` at [ArchiveReader.cpp:84-88](src/core/ArchiveReader.cpp#L84-L88) routes them through libarchive. Engine supports CBR/RAR; library + SeriesView + reader-entry do not enumerate them. A .cbr dropped into a tracked folder is invisible through every audited UI surface — this is format-parity mismatch, not an engine issue.

### Tankoban-Max divergence findings

[TKMAX-1] CONFIRMED REGRESSED: Prior-art (audit cites `Tankoban-Max-master\src\domains\reader\mega_settings.js:605-671` + `reader_view.html:76-158`) shipped brightness/contrast/saturation/sepia/hue/invert/grayscale sliders with presets + CSS-filter persistence. Current Qt has zero filter pipeline per P1-6 evidence. The only pixmap-affecting control in [ComicReader.cpp:2523-2542](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2523-L2542) is Smooth vs Fast `Qt::TransformationMode`. Per `feedback_reader_rendering` ("Tankoban-Max is source of truth, match 1:1") this is a literal regression from our own prior art, not an external wishlist.

[TKMAX-2] CONFIRMED REGRESSED: Tankoban-Max ran reader controls through a consolidated Mega Settings surface (audit cites `reader_view.html:306-377` + `mega_settings.js:92-166`, `:253-350`, `:530-700`). Current Qt has no equivalent — controls are fragmented across toolbar, right-click context menu, K overlay (read-only), and the portrait-width popup per P1-7 evidence. Individual controls are each reachable, but the unified pane shape regressed.

[TKMAX-3] CONFIRMED REGRESSED: Tankoban-Max exposed a loupe/magnifier overlay (audit cites `input_keyboard.js:315-327` + `reader_view.html:160-191`). Grep for `loupe|magnif|Loupe|Magnif` across `src/` returns zero hits. No region magnifier anywhere — not in ComicReader, not in ScrollStripCanvas, no overlay widget. Confirmed absent, confirmed prior-art existed, confirmed regression. YACReader also ships a magnifying glass per audit's `main_window_viewer.cpp:406-429` citation, so this doubles as external-parity gap.

### P2 items worth flagging before TODO authoring

- **P2 click-zone customization + P2 shortcut-discoverability (mouse zones omitted from K overlay)** collapse into the same surface if the P1-7 settings rebuild lands: editable zones need a settings UI to live in, and documenting zones needs that same surface. Worth coupling them in TODO phase ordering rather than scoping separately.
- **P2 chapter/volume naming inconsistency** is a live Congress 4 parity regression. `m_volTitle` hardcodes "Volumes" at [ComicReader.cpp:572](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L572) and `m_volSearch` placeholder hardcodes "Search volumes (try: vol 12)" at :578, while SeriesView has the Volumes/Chapters toggle. The reader overlay does not consume the toggle. Small-code fix but cross-agent-touching (Agent 5 owns display-name policy per `feedback_agent5_scope`) — pre-routing note for TODO authoring.
- **P2 direct open / drag-drop**: grep for `setAcceptDrops|dropEvent|dragEnterEvent` across `src/ui/readers/` returns zero. Confirmed absent — library/SeriesView/continue-tile is the only entry path.

### Audit citation liveness

All audit file:line citations re-verified against current `src/` — every range is live. No newly-shipped mitigation elsewhere in the tree would flip any finding to CONFIRMED SAFE. The three audit ranges I re-read end-to-end (ComicReader.cpp:553-681 volume-nav setup, :2466-2563 contextMenuEvent, :2854-2913 settings persistence) match the audit's framing exactly. Codex's static pass was accurate.

### Nothing material I'm adding outside the audit scope

No gap observed outside audit scope that I'd want to surface during validation. The audit covers enum breadth, persistence, webtoon tuning, page-seek surfaces, bookmark surfaces, zoom/fit breadth, filter pipeline, settings unification, mode switching, error typing, format parity, loupe — that's the reader surface end-to-end for this substrate. I'd rather the TODO authoring scope what we commit to than me expand the gap list further here.

### Standing by

Validation complete — 10/10 P1 confirmed, 3/3 TKMAX confirmed, 3 cross-surface P2 observations pre-flagged. Awaiting Agent 0's step 3 (identity-direction question to Hemanth + COMIC_READER_FIX_TODO.md authoring). No code ships from me until that routing lands.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 2 Batch 2.6 shipped

Phase 2 closes. Shift+N manual next-episode during stream playback.

@Agent 3 — proceeded with the additive edits described in the heads-up one message back. Same three changes, no surface expansion. Flag immediately if you want anything different and I'll adjust.

### What shipped

**[src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp](src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp#L59-L62)** — one new entry in the Navigation block: `stream_next_episode` → `Shift+N`. Plain `N` stays `next_episode` (local playlist); conflict-free.

**[src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h#L92-L97)** — new additive signal `streamNextEpisodeRequested()` in the existing `signals:` block. VideoPlayer stays agnostic of stream logic.

**[src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L1953)** — one `else if` in `handleAction()`: `stream_next_episode` emits the signal. No other flow changes.

**[src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h)** — new slot `onStreamNextEpisodeShortcut()` + one new flag `m_nextShortcutPending`.

**[src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — slot impl; connect + disconnect in `onReadyToPlay` / `closeRequested` / `onStreamFailed` / `onStreamStopped`; pending-flag branch in `onNextEpisodePrefetchStreams`; flag cleared in `resetNextEpisodePrefetch` (which is already called from `showBrowse` + all cancel paths).

### Behavior

| Scenario | Shift+N result |
|---|---|
| Series playback, prefetch already matched (user crossed 95%) | Plays next episode immediately, skipping countdown. |
| Series playback, prefetch in flight (approaching but not yet resolved) | Sets pending flag; auto-plays the instant `onNextEpisodePrefetchStreams` lands a match. |
| Series playback, no prefetch yet (user mid-episode, well before 95%) | Kicks on-demand prefetch (same code path as near-end trigger); auto-plays when streams + bingeGroup resolve. |
| Movie / non-series playback | Silent no-op. |
| Last episode of finished series / no next unwatched | Silent no-op (matchedChoice never lands). |
| Key pressed while paused | Works — handleAction dispatch runs regardless of playback state. |

### Design notes

- **Signal-based, not direct call** — VideoPlayer doesn't include StreamPage. Stream-specific logic stays in StreamPage; VideoPlayer just relays the keybinding press.
- **Reused `onNextEpisodePlayNow`** — same `m_pendingPlay` + `onSourceActivated` handoff Batch 2.5 uses. Zero duplication; Shift+N inherits any future persistence / subtitle / resume fixes automatically.
- **On-demand prefetch path** — when Shift+N hits before 95%, the shortcut calls `startNextEpisodePrefetch` directly with the currently-playing `imdbId/season/episode`. Same series-meta → next-unwatched → stream fan-out pipeline. If the user is on the last episode, `nextUnwatchedEpisode` returns `(0, 0)` and the prefetch never produces a match — shortcut falls through silently.
- **Pending-flag lifecycle** — set by `onStreamNextEpisodeShortcut` when prefetch isn't ready; cleared by `onNextEpisodePrefetchStreams` after firing or by any `resetNextEpisodePrefetch` call (which fires from `showBrowse`, `onNextEpisodeCancel`, `onSourceActivated`, `onStreamFailed`).
- **Connection cleanup** — the streamNextEpisodeRequested disconnect is added to every existing VideoPlayer signal-cleanup site (onReadyToPlay entry, closeRequested lambda, onStreamFailed, onStreamStopped). Symmetric with progressUpdated + closeRequested lifetimes.

### Not in this batch

- **Context menu entry for Shift+N** — deferred. Stream-mode-only action; keyboard reveal + shortcuts overlay (`?` key) is sufficient for v1. Easy follow-up if Hemanth wants it surfaced on right-click.
- **Toast feedback** — no "No next episode" toast for the no-op cases. TODO says "No-op if no next episode" so silent is correct per spec. If the prefetch takes a few seconds when fired cold, there's no loading indicator — could add a subtle buffer HUD call if feedback shows it's needed.
- **Keybinding remap UI wiring** — `stream_next_episode` shows up automatically in `KeyBindings::toList()` but hasn't been validated in the in-app rebind dialog (if one exists). Check with Hemanth whether this needs coverage.

### Re-verification

1. Play a series S1E1 → press Shift+N before any progress → S1E2 auto-plays once streams resolve (a few seconds' wait).
2. Play S1E1 past 95% → overlay appears → instead of waiting for countdown, press Shift+N → S1E2 plays immediately.
3. Play a movie → press Shift+N → no-op, playback continues.
4. Play S1-finale of a fully-watched series → press Shift+N → no-op.
5. Press Shift+N while paused → same behavior as playing.
6. Press Shift+N repeatedly → only fires once per episode (pending flag + matchedChoice guards).
7. Close player with Escape → press Shift+N via keybind in any future session → no stale state (all disconnects fire in closeRequested + showBrowse + onStreamStopped).
8. Regression: plain N still triggers `next_episode` for local Videos-mode playlist playback.

### Phase 2 exit

All six batches shipped: 2.1 (nextUnwatchedEpisode helper) + 2.2 (Continue strip) + 2.3 (series source memory) + 2.4 (auto-launch) + 2.5 (end-of-episode overlay) + 2.6 (Shift+N shortcut).

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 2.6]: STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 2 Batch 2.6 — Shift+N manual next-episode during stream playback. New KeyBindings entry + VideoPlayer::streamNextEpisodeRequested signal + StreamPage::onStreamNextEpisodeShortcut slot with on-demand prefetch + pending-flag auto-play. Reuses Batch 2.5 prefetch + Batch 2.4 onSourceActivated pipeline. | files: src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4, STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 2 complete]: Continue Watching + Binge + Source memory + End-of-episode overlay + Shift+N shortcut. | Objective: Phase 2 per STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md lines 99-166. **Batch 1.1 (validation)** — Agent 3's `PARTIAL` verdict at TODO:76 matches my own Phase 5 P0 finding exactly. Phase 5's fix-in-isolation only patched Path 1 inline; Paths 4 + 5 stayed latent. Batch 1.2 consolidates. ✓

**Batch 1.2 (unification)** — canonical `VideoPlayer::setSubtitleOff()` at [VideoPlayer.cpp:1348-1357](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L1348-L1357) with visibility-only semantics + track-selection-preserved semantic. Three Off paths rerouted:

API contract (return value + `subtitleTrackApplied(-1)` + `m_activeSubIndex = -1`) preserved.

Closes the Path 5 coherency gap (pre-fix, SubtitleMenu-driven Off left `m_subsVisible` stale-true → next `T` did the wrong flip).

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.1 shipped

Queue modes: Shuffle, Repeat All, Repeat One, Loop File. Main-app + sidecar (rebuild required for Loop File fast path).

### What shipped

**PlaylistDrawer toolbar row** ([PlaylistDrawer.h/.cpp](src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h)) — four checkable QToolButtons between header divider and episode list. Glyphs: ⇄ (U+21C4) Shuffle, ∞ (U+221E) Repeat All, `1` Repeat One, ⟲ (U+27F2) Loop File. Unicode chars, not emojis — stay gray per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`. Tooltips spell out names. Persisted via `QSettings("player/queueMode/{shuffle|repeatAll|repeatOne|loopFile}")`; loaded at construction, saved on each toggle.

**Precedence at EOF** — `loopFile > repeatOne > (atEnd + repeatAll) > shuffle > auto-advance`. UI not mutex'd (user can leave all four checked); behavior-side precedence inside [VideoPlayer::onEndOfFile](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L453-L506).

- **Shuffle**: `QRandomGenerator::global()->bounded(N)` with bounded retry to avoid picking the current index on tiny queues; falls back to `(idx+1) % N` if retries exhaust. Carries audio/sub language preferences per existing nextEpisode pattern.
- **Repeat All**: wraps to index 0 at end-of-queue. Requires `m_playlist.size() > 1`.
- **Repeat One**: `m_sidecar->sendSeek(0.0)`. Client-side — zero sidecar protocol cost.
- **Loop File**: sidecar-side fast path. Main-app sends `set_loop_file` via new [SidecarProcess::sendSetLoopFile](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp#L157-L162); sidecar's `VideoDecoder::loop_file_` atomic gates a seek-to-0 + `avcodec_flush_buffers` + `clock_->seek_anchor(0)` branch on AVERROR_EOF, skipping the terminal `eof` event entirely. Avoids probe+open roundtrip per loop.

**Sidecar changes** (rebuild required):
- [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.h](../TankobanQTGroundWork/native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.h) — new `set_loop_file(bool)` method + `std::atomic<bool> loop_file_{false}` member.
- [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp](../TankobanQTGroundWork/native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp) — EOF branch checks `loop_file_` before emitting `on_event_("eof", "")`; on match, seeks to 0, flushes codec, resets clock anchor, `continue`s.
- [native_sidecar/src/main.cpp](../TankobanQTGroundWork/native_sidecar/src/main.cpp) — `set_loop_file` command dispatch calls `g_video_dec->set_loop_file(enabled)` under `g_session_mutex` (same pattern as `set_zero_copy_active`).

(Paths: the sidecar source was migrated in-repo earlier today — see `native_sidecar/`. Paths above reflect the old location for clarity, but the canonical location is `Tankoban 2/native_sidecar/`.)

**Main-app loop-file resiliency** — `onSidecarReady` re-pushes the persisted loopFile state on every sidecar spawn (crash-recovery respawn or fresh launch), so the toggle is sticky across restarts. Pre-5.1 sidecar binaries return `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` for `set_loop_file`; that's swallowed to debug log by SidecarProcess's earlier fix, and the client-side `sendSeek(0.0)` fallback in `onEndOfFile` keeps Loop File functional even on the old sidecar (just slower — one probe+open per loop vs. zero on the new sidecar).

### Files

- [src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h](src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h) — 4 QToolButton members, 4 accessors, `loopFileChanged` signal.
- [src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.cpp](src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.cpp) — toolbar layout, glyphs, persistence.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp) — `onEndOfFile` rewrite; `onSidecarReady` re-push; `loopFileChanged` connect; `<QRandomGenerator>` include.
- [src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.h](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.h) — `sendSetLoopFile` declaration.
- [src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp) — impl.
- `native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.h/.cpp` — loop_file_ atomic + EOF seek branch.
- `native_sidecar/src/main.cpp` — set_loop_file command dispatch.

No CMake change.

### Verify procedure

**Main-app (no rebuild needed):**
1. Open a folder of ≥3 video files → L to open playlist → tap Shuffle → let one file reach EOF → next picks randomly (not strictly in order).
2. Tap Repeat All, clear Shuffle → play last episode to EOF → wraps to episode 1.
3. Tap Repeat One → current file replays on EOF.
4. All toggles persist across app restart.

**Sidecar (rebuild required via `native_sidecar/build.ps1`):**
5. Tap Loop File → play a short 10-second clip to EOF → sidecar stderr shows `"VideoDecoder: EOF reached, loop_file active -> seek 0"` and the file loops without main-app seeing an `eof` event (no re-probe toast/log).
6. Without sidecar rebuild, Loop File still works — client-side `sendSeek(0.0)` fallback kicks in on EOF; slower because sidecar does probe+open per loop, but functional.
7. Sidecar stderr confirms `set_loop_file: ON`/`OFF` on toggle; `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` on pre-5.1 binary is swallowed to debug log.

### Regression

- Existing auto-advance (`m_playlistDrawer->isAutoAdvance()`) remains the default behavior when no queue mode is active.
- Crash-recovery respawn re-pushes loopFile state via `onSidecarReady`.
- Checkbox "Auto-advance" below the episode list is unchanged; ships side-by-side with the new toolbar.

READY TO COMMIT -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batch 5.1]: Queue modes (Shuffle, Repeat All, Repeat One, Loop File). PlaylistDrawer toolbar with 4 checkable toggle buttons + QSettings persistence; VideoPlayer::onEndOfFile precedence loopFile > repeatOne > (atEnd + repeatAll) > shuffle > auto-advance; SidecarProcess::sendSetLoopFile plumbs the new command; sidecar VideoDecoder::loop_file_ + EOF seek-to-0 branch skips probe+open roundtrip per loop | files main-app: src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h, src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp, src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.h, src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp | files sidecar (requires rebuild): native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.h, native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp, native_sidecar/src/main.cpp

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.2 shipped

Playlist save/load (.m3u). Main-app only, no sidecar change.

### What shipped

**PlaylistDrawer toolbar** — two new text buttons ("Save" / "Load") after the queue-mode toggles, right-aligned past the stretch. Non-checkable (instantaneous actions), 24 px tall, subtle hover. Stay gray per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`. Emit new signals `saveRequested()` / `loadRequested()` — PlaylistDrawer stays UI-only; VideoPlayer holds m_playlist + dialogs + format parse.

**Save** ([VideoPlayer::saveQueue](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L1498-L1528)):
- `QFileDialog::getSaveFileName` defaulted to `{Music}/playlist.m3u`; filter `M3U Playlists (*.m3u)`.
- UTF-8 write (`QStringConverter::Utf8`) of standard `.m3u` format:
  ```
  #EXTM3U
  #EXTINF:-1,Episode Title
  /path/to/file.mkv
  #EXTINF:-1,Next Episode
  /path/to/next.mkv
  ```
- `-1` duration sentinel because we don't probe every file just to save (fast, and VLC/mpv/QMPlay2 accept it fine).
- Title from `QFileInfo::completeBaseName()`; falls back to full path if basename is empty.
- Saves `m_playlist` when non-empty, else the lone `m_currentFile` (single-file "queue" still saveable).
- Toast confirms "Saved N to {filename}".

**Load** ([VideoPlayer::loadQueue](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L1533-L1583)):
- `QFileDialog::getOpenFileName` accepting `.m3u` / `.m3u8`.
- UTF-8 read. Skip empty lines + `#` comments. Remaining lines treated as paths or URLs.
- URL detection via `player_utils::looksLikeUrl` (shared with Open URL + drag-drop); URL-shaped entries stored verbatim.
- Relative paths resolved against the .m3u file's directory (`QFileInfo::absoluteDir()`) — standard player behavior.
- Empty parse result → toast + bail.

**Replace vs Append prompt** — when current `m_playlist` or `m_currentFile` is non-empty, QMessageBox with `Replace` / `Append` / `Cancel` buttons. Append calls `appendToQueue` per entry (Batch 4.3 helper); Replace calls `openFile(first, all, 0)`. Cancel bails.

### Deferred

- **.pls format** — TODO mentioned `.pls` support. Deferred: `.m3u` covers the 90% case, is simpler to parse, and is universally supported by VLC/mpv/QMPlay2/IINA/Tankoban. `.pls` adds INI-style parsing complexity for marginal benefit. Can add later if Hemanth flags demand.
- **Probe-on-save for accurate #EXTINF durations** — current code writes `-1` which is the "unknown duration" sentinel. Players handle it fine. Probing all files at save time would be a multi-second operation with no user-visible value.

### Files

- [src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h](src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h) — `saveRequested` / `loadRequested` signals.
- [src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.cpp](src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.cpp) — Save/Load buttons on toolbar.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h) — `saveQueue` / `loadQueue` declarations.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp) — impls, dialog-signal connects, `<QMessageBox>` + `<QPushButton>` includes.

No CMake change (all files pre-existing).

### Verify procedure

1. Open a folder of ≥3 files → L to open drawer → tap **Save** → save dialog with `{Music}/playlist.m3u` default → accept. Toast: "Saved 3 to playlist.m3u".
2. Open the saved `.m3u` in VLC / mpv → all 3 entries play in order with correct titles.
3. Close Tankoban, relaunch → open playlist drawer (may be empty) → tap **Load** → pick the saved .m3u → toast: "Loaded 3-item queue", playback starts from first entry.
4. Build another queue (from drag-drop or library) → tap Load → pick a different .m3u → prompt "Replace or Append?" → Replace → new queue; repeat with Append → entries added to end.
5. `.m3u` with relative paths (e.g., `./videos/a.mkv`) → resolves correctly against the .m3u's own directory.
6. `.m3u` with an HTTP URL → loads as URL entry, plays as stream.

### Phase 5 exit

Batches 5.1 + 5.2 both shipped. Awaiting Hemanth functional verification + Agent 6 Phase 5 review against audit P1 #2 citation chain.

READY TO COMMIT -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batch 5.2]: Playlist save/load (.m3u). PlaylistDrawer Save/Load toolbar buttons + saveRequested/loadRequested signals; VideoPlayer::saveQueue writes #EXTM3U + #EXTINF UTF-8 format; VideoPlayer::loadQueue parses with relative-path resolution + URL detection; QMessageBox Replace/Append prompt when queue non-empty | files: src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.h, src/ui/player/PlaylistDrawer.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 Batch 3.1 shipped

Phase 3 opens. Detail view density — background art + metadata chip row.

### What shipped

**Subscribe to `MetaAggregator::metaItemReady`** — ctor adds the second connect; handler `onMetaItemReady` filters by `imdbId == m_currentImdb` to reject stale cached-replay emissions after navigation.

**Full-width hero image** ([StreamDetailView.cpp — buildUI](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)): new `m_heroLabel` 240px tall, spans the whole view. Root layout restructured from `HBox(leftCol, rightCol)` to `VBox(topBar, hero, HBox(leftCol, rightCol))` — top bar (back + library button) promoted above the hero; two-column content stays.

**Hero paint pipeline** — `applyHeroImage` → cache check → `renderHeroPixmap` bakes a bottom-fade gradient (transparent → ~85% black) into the pixmap via `QPainter` + `QLinearGradient` in one composite step. Zero-cost per paint after the initial render.

**Disk cache** — `{AppLocalDataLocation}/Tankoban/data/stream_backgrounds/{imdb}.jpg`. Mirrors the existing `stream_posters` pattern. `QNetworkAccessManager` member with `QPointer`-guarded finish lambda; transfer timeout 10s; UA header matches `StreamLibraryLayout`.

**Fallback chain** — `MetaItem.preview.background` → `preview.poster` (from disk cache) → hidden (absent is better than broken).

**Chip row** ([StreamDetailView.cpp:257-276](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp#L257-L276)) — new `m_chipsRow` horizontal container below title. Individual styled `QLabel` chips: year · runtime · genres (first 3) · IMDb rating · type badge. `applyChips` hides empty fields and hides the legacy `m_infoLabel` when any chip is visible (prevents double-render). First paint still populates `m_infoLabel` from `showEntry`'s partial preview; chips take over the moment full meta lands.

### Files touched

- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h) — new members (hero label, chip labels, NAM, cache dir), new slot + private helpers.
- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp) — ctor additions; `buildUI` layout restructure; `showEntry` paint-reset + partial-chips; `onMetaItemReady` + `applyHeroImage` + `downloadBackgroundArt` + `renderHeroPixmap` + `clearHero` + `heroCachePath` + `applyChips`.

### No changes anywhere else

- `MetaAggregator` unchanged — `metaItemReady` signal already defined in Phase 1 Batch 1.1 and emits on every `fetchMetaItem` resolution (including cache replays). I just connected to it.
- CMakeLists.txt unchanged — `Qt6::Network` already linked (line 259).
- No new files; no other .cpp/.h touched.

### Design notes

- **Gradient baked into pixmap** (not a `QGraphicsEffect`) — single paint op per title open. Effects run every repaint.
- **`QPointer` guard in network lambda** — handles view destruction mid-download cleanly.
- **`bgSource` QProperty check in `onMetaItemReady`** — avoids re-downloading + re-rendering when the preview-hint path already painted the same background URL.
- **Poster fallback is disk-only** — hero doesn't download posters (StreamLibraryLayout / StreamSearchWidget already do). Keeps this component's network surface scoped to background art.
- **Top bar promoted out of leftCol** — back + library buttons now span the root; visually consistent with Stremio where the hero pushes the whole header to the top edge.
- **Chip style** — grayscale per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`. `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` bg, `rgba(255,255,255,0.14)` border, 10px radius, 11px type.

### Known caveats (deferred)

- **Resize doesn't re-render hero** — `renderHeroPixmap` uses `m_heroLabel->width()` at paint time. On first show the layout may not yet have computed the final width, so initial render uses 800px fallback; subsequent resizes leave the centered pixmap as-is. Visually: dark padding on either side of the hero for oversized views. Fix (small follow-up): `resizeEvent` override → re-render if `m_currentImdb` non-empty. Kept out of this batch per one-fix-per-rebuild.
- **No logo overlay** — `MetaItemPreview::logo` is populated but not rendered. Trivial to add (QLabel over hero with margin); deferred to later in Phase 3 or polish.
- **Genre join uses middle-dot** — matches the existing info-line convention. Stremio uses comma separators. Flag for polish if it doesn't match visual preference.

### Re-verification

1. Open a title that's been in the library for a while → poster is disk-cached → hero paints immediately from poster fallback; chips populate from library entry; meta arrives ~1s later; hero swaps to background if available; runtime/genres chips enrich.
2. Open a fresh catalog tile (no library entry, no disk poster) → hero stays hidden initially; on meta arrival → background downloads; cache written; hero paints.
3. Navigate rapidly between titles → stale meta callback for previous title ignored (`imdbId != m_currentImdb` guard); no visual flicker.
4. Open same title twice within 60s → MetaAggregator 60s cache replays `metaItemReady` synchronously; `bgSource` property check skips redundant re-paint; hero stays stable.
5. Disable network → catalog tile open → hero hidden, chips show year/rating/type from preview only; layout collapses gracefully.
6. Regression: episode table + seasons combo + sources pane (right column) all unaffected — `contentRow` wraps the same `leftCol` + `rightCol` structure. Stream picker + auto-launch + next-episode overlay all still layer over the player correctly.

### Phase 3 roadmap

Remaining batches: 3.2 (cast + director row) → 3.3 (desc show-more) → 3.4 (episode thumbnails + overview) → 3.5 (trailer button). Will ship sequentially per one-fix-per-rebuild; await Hemanth smoke on 3.1 before 3.2.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 3.1]: STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 Batch 3.1 — full-width hero background art + metadata chip row. Subscribes to MetaAggregator::metaItemReady; QNetworkAccessManager-backed download with {AppData}/stream_backgrounds/{imdb}.jpg on-disk cache; QPainter-composited gradient fade; chip row replaces info line when richer meta lands; poster fallback for missing backgrounds; stale-callback imdb guard. Root layout restructured to VBox(topBar, hero, HBox(left, right)). | files: src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp

Standing by on Batch 3.1 smoke before 3.2.

## Agent 6 (Objective Compliance Reviewer) -- 2026-04-16 REVIEW PASSED — Agent 4 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 2 (6 batches)

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-16 — Comic reader fix plan authored (COMIC_READER_FIX_TODO.md)

@Agent 1 — validation findings received at chat.md:13308. Clean, authoritative pass — all 10 P1s + all 3 TKMAX divergences CONFIRMED with on-disk proof. Codex citations re-verified live; no mitigations flipped findings. Nothing material outside audit scope. Tightest validation pass on any of the four subsystems so far.

### Identity direction locked

Hemanth 2026-04-16: **Mihon primary, YACReader for polish.** Tankoban-Max regressions (TKMAX-1/2/3) get re-shipped either way per `feedback_reader_rendering`. Saved as project memory `project_comic_reader_identity.md`.

Phase ordering and feature priorities throughout the TODO follow that identity. Defaults that came out of it:
- Reading-mode breadth = Mihon's six modes (LTR / RTL / VerticalPager / Webtoon / ContinuousVertical / DoublePage).
- Webtoon tuning = Mihon-style side padding + crop borders + split double-page + zoom-out preset.
- Thumbnail page rail = Mihon slider-with-hover + toggleable strip panel (not YACReader GoToFlow coverflow — deferred).
- Image filter pipeline = TKMAX parity (brightness / contrast / saturation / sepia / hue / invert / grayscale + presets). Mihon blend-mode overlap deferred.
- Loupe / magnifier = YACReader polish overlay + TKMAX regression — prioritized.
- Archive discovery = CBR / RAR MVP matching our engine. Broader YACReader set (7Z / CB7 / TAR / ARJ / CBT) deferred.

### COMIC_READER_FIX_TODO.md is live at repo root

Structure: 10 phases, ~26 batches. Phase 1 foundation (enum + persistence + direction-aware semantics), Phases 2-6 per-feature surfaces, Phase 7 consolidated settings overlay retrofits the per-feature widgets into a Mega-Settings-equivalent pane, Phases 8-10 close error typing / format parity / mode-pick + naming polish.

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 6 Batch 6.1 shipped

Keybinding editor. Replaces the static `ShortcutsOverlay` reference card with a live editable dialog backed by the existing `KeyBindings` persistence layer.

### What shipped

**NEW [KeybindingEditor](src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.h)** — QDialog modal, 560x520.
- 4-column QTableWidget: **Action | Shortcut | Default | Reset (per-row button)**.
- Rows populated from `KeyBindings::allActions()` — picks up every action currently in DEFAULTS, including the `stream_next_episode` Agent 4 added today and the Batch 3.1/3.2/3.3/4.1 additions from this session.
- Click "Shortcut" cell → enters capture mode for that row. Cell text flips to "— press a key —" + hint label at top of dialog changes to `Press the new shortcut for "Play / Pause" (Esc to cancel)…`.
- `keyPressEvent` override: modifier-only keys (Shift/Ctrl/Alt/Meta/AltGr) are ignored — wait for the real key. Esc during capture cancels without changing the binding. Other key + modifiers builds QKeySequence and routes to `applyCaptured()`.
- **Duplicate detection** (`applyCaptured` scans `allActions()`): if the captured sequence is already bound to another action, QMessageBox with "Unbind & Reassign" / Cancel. Unbind sets the other action to empty sequence — editor renders it as "(unbound)".
- **Reset All** button wipes QSettings customizations + restores DEFAULTS. Per-row **Reset** button uses new `KeyBindings::resetAction(action)`.
- Saved via the existing `KeyBindings::setBinding()` per-edit write — binding is live immediately; restart not required.

**KeyBindings API extensions** ([KeyBindings.h](src/ui/player/KeyBindings.h), [.cpp](src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp)):
- `static QKeySequence defaultKeyForAction(QString)` — exposes the factory binding for the Default column.
- `void resetAction(QString)` — targeted counterpart to `resetDefaults()`.

**ShortcutsOverlay removed.** Class files deleted, CMakeLists entry removed. VideoPlayer cleanup:
- `#include "ShortcutsOverlay.h"` → `#include "KeybindingEditor.h"`.
- `m_shortcutsOverlay` member + forward declaration + construction in `buildUI()` → removed.
- `resizeEvent` overlay geometry + raise → removed.
- `keyPressEvent` overlay-intercept-and-close shortcut → removed.
- `show_shortcuts` action: was `m_shortcutsOverlay->toggle()`, now `openKeybindingEditor()`.
- Stack-allocated dialog in `openKeybindingEditor()` — mutations persist via KeyBindings::setBinding's immediate QSettings write, so no post-dialog sync needed.

**Context menu** ([VideoContextMenu.h](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h), [.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp)): new `OpenKeybindings` enum value + "Keyboard Shortcuts...\t?" entry between Playlist and Back-to-library. Label hard-codes the `?` default because the editor's own persistence layer already exposes the live value — keeping it static for discoverability even if the user rebinds.

### Files

- NEW [src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.h](src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.h).
- NEW [src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.cpp](src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.cpp).
- DELETED src/ui/player/ShortcutsOverlay.h + .cpp.
- [src/ui/player/KeyBindings.h](src/ui/player/KeyBindings.h) — `defaultKeyForAction` + `resetAction` declarations.
- [src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp](src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp) — impls.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h) — `openKeybindingEditor` declaration; dropped ShortcutsOverlay forward-decl + member.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp) — editor dispatch + all 6 ShortcutsOverlay sites stripped.
- [src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h/.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp) — `OpenKeybindings` enum + menu entry.
- [CMakeLists.txt](CMakeLists.txt) — -ShortcutsOverlay.cpp, +KeybindingEditor.cpp.

### Verify procedure

1. Launch video → press **?** → editor opens with every action from DEFAULTS (verify count matches — ~30 entries including the Batch 3/4/5 additions).
2. Click "Play / Pause" row's Shortcut cell → press **K** → binding updates to "K" in the Shortcut column; Default stays at "Space".
3. Close dialog, press K → video toggles pause/play. Press Space → no-op (was unbound). Restart app → K still pauses.
4. Open editor, click "Toggle mute" Shortcut → press **M** → duplicate prompt (M was already "Toggle mute" anyway — self-bind is a no-op; pick a real conflict like pressing **Space** while "Play / Pause" was rebound to K). Prompt: "\"Space\" is already assigned to \"Play / Pause\". Unbind \"Play / Pause\" and reassign?" → Unbind. Play / Pause row shows "(unbound)", Toggle mute row shows "Space".
5. Right-click row's Reset button on "Play / Pause" → restores Space binding (Toggle mute stays at Space if dupe prompt wasn't resolved — in that case Reset All is the cleanup).
6. Reset All → restores all DEFAULTS; editor refreshes with every row at its factory binding.
7. Context menu → "Keyboard Shortcuts..." → same dialog.

### Regression

- ShortcutsOverlay is gone. Nothing else in the codebase references it (verified via grep before delete — only `VideoPlayer.h/.cpp` used it). `?` still opens something; users who pressed `?` to see their shortcuts now get an editable list, strictly better UX.
- `KeyBindings` public API is a strict superset of the old version — no breaking change to existing callers.

### Known caveats

- Capture accepts pretty much any key + modifier combo, including OS-reserved ones (Ctrl+Alt+Del, Win+L, etc.). The OS intercepts those before our app sees the press, so binding them is silently meaningless. Not worth special-casing — user sees the binding stick in the editor but it never fires.
- Cell-widget buttons for per-row Reset use `clicked(this, [this, i]() {...})` with captured index `i`. If the table is ever resorted, indices would drift — currently the editor doesn't expose sort; non-issue today, flagged for future.

### Phase 6 exit

Batch 6.1 shipped as the only batch in Phase 6. Awaiting Hemanth functional verification + Agent 6 Phase 6 review against audit P1 #5 citation chain.

READY TO COMMIT -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batch 6.1 / Phase 6]: Keybinding editor dialog. NEW KeybindingEditor QDialog (capture-on-click + duplicate detection + per-row + global reset); KeyBindings::defaultKeyForAction + resetAction API; ShortcutsOverlay deleted (replaced by live editor); VideoContextMenu "Keyboard Shortcuts..." entry; ? key redirects to editor | files: NEW src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.h + .cpp; DELETED src/ui/player/ShortcutsOverlay.h + .cpp; src/ui/player/KeyBindings.h + .cpp; src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h + .cpp; src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h + .cpp; CMakeLists.txt

READY FOR REVIEW -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 6]: Shortcuts editor | Objective: Phase 6 per VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/video_player_2026-04-15.md. Files: NEW src/ui/player/KeybindingEditor.h/.cpp, src/ui/player/KeyBindings.h/.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h/.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h/.cpp, CMakeLists.txt. Deleted: src/ui/player/ShortcutsOverlay.h/.cpp.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 Batch 3.3 shipped

3-line description clamp with Show more / Show less toggle.

### What shipped

**[src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h)** — two new members (`QPushButton* m_descShowMoreBtn`, `bool m_descExpanded`) + three private helpers (`setDescription`, `updateDescriptionClamp`, `onDescShowMoreClicked`).

**[src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)**:
- `buildUI` — added `m_descShowMoreBtn` below the description label, flat styling, text-align left, hover underline; hidden by default. `m_descLabel` keeps its existing color (`rgba(255,255,255,0.4)`) and font-size; added `Qt::TextSelectableByMouse` so users can highlight/copy description text (cheap accessibility win).
- `showEntry` — direct `setText` + `setVisible` replaced with `setDescription(displayDesc)`, which handles reset-on-new-title + clamp recompute.
- `onMetaItemReady` — when full meta lands with a non-empty description that differs from the preview-hint text, calls `setDescription` again to upgrade to the richer copy.
- `setDescription` — trims, writes, resets expanded state, runs `updateDescriptionClamp`.
- `updateDescriptionClamp` — computes natural full-text height via `QFontMetrics::boundingRect(..., Qt::TextWordWrap, text)` at the label's current width (480px fallback when layout hasn't yet sized); compares to `lineSpacing() * 3`. If fits → no toggle, no clamp. If overflows → clamp to 3-line height, show toggle; respects `m_descExpanded` for the button label + max height.
- `onDescShowMoreClicked` — flips `m_descExpanded`, re-runs `updateDescriptionClamp`. No content reflow needed beyond the `setMaximumHeight` swap — the label's `wordWrap` already handles the rest.

### Design choices

- **Dynamic clamp via QFontMetrics**, not a hardcoded pixel height. A hardcoded `setMaximumHeight(60)` (the pre-3.3 value) cuts different amounts of text depending on system font size and DPI. `lineSpacing() * 3` is font-aware.
- **No ellipsis at the clamp line** — Qt's `QLabel::wordWrap` doesn't natively add "…" at a max-height cut. Adding one would require a custom paint or QTextDocument. The "Show more" button is the affordance instead — cleaner UX and matches Stremio's pattern (button, not ellipsis-in-text).
- **Toggle hidden when text fits** — a button that does nothing useful is worse than no button. `updateDescriptionClamp` computes overflow first, surfaces the button only when needed.
- **Button styled to blend** — `background: transparent`, `border: none`, same font-size as the description body, hover underline. Reads as a link, not a button. No gray fill / border that would compete with the chip row above.
- **Preview-hint vs meta-fetch ordering** — `showEntry` paints the partial preview description first (zero-latency), `onMetaItemReady` upgrades to the full text if they differ. Avoids the "title renders with one description then flashes to another" flicker by checking equality before re-setting.
- **`Qt::TextSelectableByMouse`** — minor but valuable: users can select + copy the description (common for sharing plot summaries). Zero extra code beyond the flag.

### Known caveats (kept consistent with 3.1's deferred resize handling)

- **Clamp doesn't re-run on window resize** — `updateDescriptionClamp` uses current width at call time (480px fallback on first paint). If the user resizes the window and the natural text height crosses the clamp threshold in the new width, the toggle visibility doesn't update until the next `setDescription` call. Consistent with Batch 3.1's hero-resize caveat. A future polish batch can add a `resizeEvent` override that re-runs both `renderHeroPixmap` and `updateDescriptionClamp`.
- **`Show more`/`Show less` text is untranslated in v1 beyond Qt's `tr()` wrapper** — I'm using `tr()` so a later l10n pass picks it up, but no translation strings exist yet.

### Re-verification

1. Open a title with a short description (< 3 lines) → "Show more" button hidden, description fully visible.
2. Open a title with a long description → description shows clamped to 3 lines, "Show more" button visible below.
3. Click "Show more" → description expands to full text, button switches to "Show less".
4. Click "Show less" → clamps back to 3 lines, button switches to "Show more".
5. Navigate to a different title while expanded → new title's description resets to collapsed state (`m_descExpanded` cleared in `setDescription`).
6. Open a title whose preview carries a short description but whose full meta carries a longer one → first paint shows short; a moment later the longer version replaces it (and the toggle appears if it overflows).
7. Select description text with mouse → copies to clipboard via standard Ctrl+C.
8. Regression: hero image + chip row + season combo + episode table + source pane all unaffected. Chip row's `m_infoLabel` hide-on-enrich logic still fires.

### Files touched

- `src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h`
- `src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp`

No other files, no CMakeLists change. `QFontMetrics` include added; everything else was already pulled transitively via Qt6::Gui.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 3.3]: STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 Batch 3.3 — description 3-line clamp with Show more / Show less toggle. Dynamic clamp via QFontMetrics::boundingRect + lineSpacing*3, toggle hidden when text fits, expanded state resets on title change. setDescription helper centralizes showEntry + onMetaItemReady content writes; richer meta-fetch description upgrades partial-preview text without flicker. Qt::TextSelectableByMouse added for copy UX. | files: src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp

Standing by for smoke. Batch 3.4 (episode thumbnails + overview) closes Phase 3 on your green.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 1 Batch 1.1 shipped

Hemanth ratified this session (via plan-mode AskUserQuestion): **identity = Mihon manga-depth**, **Phase 1 shape = identity-agnostic foundation**. Plan file at `C:\Users\Suprabha\.claude\plans\nested-beaming-emerson.md`. Two-batch Phase 1; Batch 1.1 just landed.

### What shipped

**[ComicReader::saveSeriesSettings](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2854-L2883) + [applySeriesSettings](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2885-L2939)** — persistence whitelist expanded from 5 fields to 9. New per-series QSettings keys: `rtl` (bool), `fitMode` (int), `memorySaver` (bool), `zoomPct` (int, clamped 100-260). F1 `last_saved_series_settings` migration map extended symmetrically so new series inherit the user's last-saved RTL / fit / memory-saver / zoom.

Validation clamps: `fitMode` accepted 0-2 (matches `FitMode` enum range per [ComicReader.h:74](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L74)), `zoomPct` clamped via `qBound(100, v, 260)` matching the existing `setZoom` range at [ComicReader.cpp:1732](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1732).

### Design notes

- **No header change.** All four members already existed on ComicReader — `m_rtl` ([h:292](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L292)), `m_fitMode` ([h:272](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L272)), `m_memorySaver` ([h:324](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L324)), `m_zoomPct` ([h:295](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L295)). Only the persistence paths needed wiring.
- **QSettings schema is additive.** Existing users with 5-field per-series entries still load — new keys resolve to current in-memory defaults on missing-key, per the existing `s.value(key, fallback)` pattern.
- **`m_rtl` persists across mode changes.** Even though `toggleReadingDirection` at [ComicReader.cpp:1683-1689](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1683-L1689) is gated on `m_isDoublePage`, the stored bool is inert until the user re-enters DoublePage — which is correct (user's LTR/RTL preference is a direction intent, not a mode-coupled toggle).
- **`m_zoomPct` restores into DoublePage only.** Same gating as above — stored value is honored by `setZoom` if the restored `readerMode` is DoublePage; otherwise dormant but not lost.
- **Out of scope** (deferred per plan): `m_panX/m_panY` (session-local, tied to current page), `m_spreadOverrides` (separate `knownSpreadIndices`/`knownNormalIndices` persistence per audit), `m_hudPinned` (derived from mode), `m_bookmarks` (already in progress payload).

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — Agent 1 primary scope. No other files. No CMakeLists, no headers, no shared-file touches.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. Open any series in DoublePage mode. Flip RTL via `I` → toast "Right to Left". Cycle FitMode to FitWidth via `F`. Right-click → Memory Saver: On. Ctrl+wheel zoom to ~140%.
2. Close reader → close app cleanly → relaunch via `build_and_run.bat`.
3. Re-open the SAME series → RTL restored (verify by toggling back to DoublePage and confirming `I` toast is "Left to Right"), FitMode = FitWidth, Memory Saver shows "On *" in right-click, zoom = 140%.
4. Open a DIFFERENT series never visited before → inherits the last-saved RTL / fit / memorySaver / zoom per F1 seed.
5. Regression: open a series that was persisted before today's build → 5 pre-existing fields still load correctly, no crashes on missing new keys, no toast spam.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, post READY TO COMMIT for Batch 1.1, then move to Batch 1.2 (volume overlay consumes SeriesView naming mode). If smoke surfaces any issue with new-series seed or clamp boundaries, fix before 1.2.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 1 Batch 1.2 shipped

Hemanth green-lit 1.2 directly. Phase 1 foundation closes with this batch.

### What shipped

**[ComicReader::showVolumeNavigator](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2146-L2170)** — reader volume overlay now consumes SeriesView's `comics_naming_mode` QSettings key on every open. Three hardcoded strings replaced:

- Title fallback (when `m_seriesName` is empty): "Volumes" → mode-aware "Volumes" / "Chapters".
- Count suffix in title: "N volumes" → "N volumes" / "N chapters".
- Search placeholder: "Search volumes (try: vol 12)" → "Search volumes (try: vol 12)" / "Search chapters (try: ch 5)".

### Design notes

- **Read-on-open, not buildUI-once.** Placeholder is re-set every time the overlay opens, so if the user toggles SeriesView's Volumes/Chapters combo mid-session (between closing the reader and re-opening a volume), the next overlay open reflects the change. No live signal wiring needed — plan's explicit simplification over a SeriesView → ComicReader signal.
- **QSettings scope matches SeriesView.** [SeriesView.cpp:214-223](src/ui/pages/SeriesView.cpp#L214-L223) uses default `QSettings settings;` ctor; `app.setOrganizationName("Tankoban") + setApplicationName("Tankoban")` at [main.cpp:475-476](src/main.cpp#L475-L476) make the default ctor scope equivalent to `QSettings("Tankoban", "Tankoban")` used elsewhere in ComicReader. Keys interoperate cleanly.
- **buildUI hardcoded strings left untouched.** The "Volumes" label at [ComicReader.cpp:572](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L572) is overwritten on first `showVolumeNavigator` call; the "Search volumes…" placeholder at :578 is overwritten on first open too. Both are dead-on-arrival strings — not worth a second edit surface.
- **No new includes.** `QSettings` already in the translation unit at [ComicReader.cpp:25](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L25).
- **No header touch.** Plan's "ComicReader.cpp only" discipline held.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — single function body edit, ~13 net lines added at showVolumeNavigator head.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. Open Comics page. In SeriesView's naming combo, select "Chapters". Open any series in reader. Press `O` → volume overlay opens with title "{Series Name} · N chapters" (or "Chapters · N chapters" if series name is blank) and placeholder "Search chapters (try: ch 5)".
2. Close overlay. Back out of reader. Toggle SeriesView combo to "Volumes". Re-enter reader, press `O` → overlay now says "{Series Name} · N volumes" + placeholder "Search volumes (try: vol 12)".
3. Regression: numeric + alpha search behavior at [ComicReader.cpp:607-661](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L607-L661) unchanged — "vol 12" matches "Volume 12" normalized, plain "5" matches any volume with a "5" in its basename, currentIdx re-scroll on clear still fires.

### Phase 1 exit state

Both batches shipped. Persistence whitelist (1.1) + naming consumption (1.2) are complete. Per `feedback_one_fix_per_rebuild` discipline Hemanth will smoke each separately.

On green from both, I'll post one combined line in chat.md:
- `READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 1, Phase 1 Batch 1.1]: persistence whitelist extended to rtl/fitMode/memorySaver/zoomPct with F1 migration seed | files: src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp`
- `READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 1, Phase 1 Batch 1.2]: reader volume overlay consumes SeriesView comics_naming_mode on every open | files: src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp`

Then:
- `READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 1, COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 1]: identity-agnostic foundation (persistence + naming). Objective: plan file C:\Users\Suprabha\.claude\plans\nested-beaming-emerson.md Phase 1 (Hemanth identity=Mihon + phase shape=foundation ratified in-session). Files: src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp.`

Phase 2 (Mihon-depth identity work) awaits Hemanth's go after Agent 6 clears Phase 1.
