## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 Batch 3.4 shipped

Episode rows: thumbnails + 2-line overview. Phase 3 closes.

### What shipped

**Struct extension** — `src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h` — `StreamEpisode` gains `QString overview` + `QUrl thumbnail`. Both empty when the addon's `MetaItem.videos[]` omits them.

**Parser** — `parseSeriesEpisodes` reads `overview` + `thumbnail` from each video object. Other fields unchanged.

**Episode-table layout** — `buildUI`:
- 4 columns to 5 columns: `[# | Thumb 64x36 | Title+Overview | Progress | Status]`
- Default row height 64px via `verticalHeader()->setDefaultSectionSize(64)`
- Thumb column 76px fixed (64 image + 12 padding); title column stretches; numeric cols tightened (36 / 80 / 60)
- Sorting disabled (see rationale below)

**Row build** — `populateEpisodeTable`:
- Column 0: episode # (QTableWidgetItem, carries UserRole data for Calendar-preselect + onEpisodeActivated — contract preserved).
- Column 1: QLabel cell widget inside a 6px-padded QHBoxLayout, 64x36px with subtle bg + border + 4px radius. Disk-cache hit paints synchronously; cache miss kicks `fetchEpisodeThumbnail` async.
- Column 2: QWidget cell with stacked QVBoxLayout: title (12px, 500 weight) + optional overview (10px italic, 45% white) clamped to 2 lines via `QFontMetrics::lineSpacing() x 2`. Overview omitted when empty — layout stretches the title's space.
- Column 3: progress % (unchanged text logic).
- Column 4: status check (unchanged).

**Thumbnail cache** — new dir `{AppLocalDataLocation}/Tankoban/data/stream_episode_thumbnails/{imdb}_{season}_{episode}.jpg`. Created in ctor via `QDir().mkpath`. Matches the `stream_posters` / `stream_backgrounds` pattern.

**Async download** — `fetchEpisodeThumbnail` reuses `m_nam` (the hero-pipeline NAM). Dual QPointer guards: view (selfGuard) + target label (labelGuard) — either destroyed mid-flight then no-op. Stale-title guard (`imdbId == m_currentImdb`) — a completion after navigation still writes the cache but won't paint into a recycled cell. Transfer timeout 10s; standard UA header.

**Paint** — `applyEpisodeThumbnail` uses `Qt::KeepAspectRatioByExpanding` + center-crop to 64x36 so landscape thumbnails don't distort and portraits crop to the middle band.

### Sorting decision

Disabled on the episode table. Cell widgets in cols 1/2 don't carry sortable QTableWidgetItem data, so a header-click sort would produce undefined row order for the visible (widget-backed) columns. Pre-3.4 behavior let users sort by title / progress / status — but the pound column's lexical sort was already broken for numeric episode lists (10 before 2) and episodes are intrinsically 1..N-ordered by parseSeriesEpisodes. Net: lost sort-by-title (low-value for episode lists), gained a consistent render order.

### Design choices

- QLabel cell widgets, not custom delegates. QStyledItemDelegate with custom paint is more performant at scale, but for per-season episode counts (6-24 rows typical) the widget path is simpler and cheaper to reason about. Re-evaluate if we ever render a full series in one table.
- 2-line overview clamp via QFontMetrics, no ellipsis — matches Batch 3.3's description approach. Qt QLabel doesn't natively add the ellipsis char at a max-height clip; adding one would require QTextDocument + custom paint. Row interaction is click-to-play, not expand, so silent clip is the right call.
- Thumbnail reused across the NAM — no second QNetworkAccessManager. Parallel in-flight requests to different hosts multiplex cleanly; Qt's NAM handles connection pooling.
- Calendar preselect path unchanged via `item(r, 0)->data(Qt::UserRole)` — col 0 still carries episode number.
- Placeholder styling for missing thumb is visible as a dark rectangle when no pixmap lands, so rows render consistently even for cache-miss-pre-download or addon-omitted thumbnails. Better than layout jump on late arrival.

### Known caveats (deferred)

- Thumbnails don't repaint on window resize — consistent with Batches 3.1 + 3.3 deferrals. They're fixed 64x36 so width doesn't matter.
- No LRU eviction on the thumbnail cache — `{imdb}_{s}_{e}.jpg` files accumulate forever. At ~3KB/thumb x 24 eps x ~50 series = ~3.5MB worst case, well under any reasonable bound. Add eviction if disk telemetry shows concern.
- Header Thumb column is unlabeled (empty string) — intentional to match the minimal look; a label in a 76px column would truncate awkwardly.
- Sort-by-title lost — documented above; flag for follow-up if you want it back via hidden QTableWidgetItem data + cellWidget coexistence.

### Re-verification

1. Open a series with episode thumbnails in its meta then first paint shows placeholder rectangles; rapid population as each HTTP thumbnail download completes and `applyEpisodeThumbnail` lands. Cache written.
2. Re-open the same series then thumbnails paint synchronously from disk cache (no network).
3. Open a series whose addon omits thumbnails then placeholder rectangles remain; overview renders if present; no broken-image icons.
4. Navigate rapidly between series then in-flight download completes for the first series; stale-title guard blocks paint into the second series's cell widgets. Cache write still happens for later retrieval.
5. Regression: Calendar-preselect then open detail via a calendar row then correct episode auto-scrolled-to + selected; col 0 UserRole data path intact.
6. Regression: `onEpisodeActivated` single-click then `playRequested` emitted with correct season/episode; source pane flips to loading.
7. Regression: progress % + status check update after watching an episode (cols 3 + 4 unchanged).
8. Layout: row height 64px accommodates thumbnail (36px) + padding + stacked text (~24px for 2 lines overview + 1 title at 12px).

### Files touched

- src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h (+5 lines — struct fields)
- src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.cpp (+4 lines — parser)
- src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h (+3 helpers, +1 cache-dir member)
- src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp (ctor cache-dir init, buildUI table restructure, populateEpisodeTable rewrite, 3 helper impls)

No new files, no CMakeLists change. QNetworkAccessManager + Qt6::Network already pulled.

### Phase 3 exit

Active batches all shipped: 3.1 (hero + chips) + 3.3 (description show-more) + 3.4 (episode thumbnails + overview). 3.2 + 3.5 deferred to STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md. Phase 3 closed.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 3.4]: STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 Batch 3.4 — episode rows with thumbnail + overview. StreamEpisode gains overview + thumbnail fields; parseSeriesEpisodes populates; 5-column table layout with left-side 64x36 thumb, stacked title + italic-gray 2-line overview clamp, 64px row height, sorting disabled. On-disk cache at {AppData}/stream_episode_thumbnails/{imdb}_{s}_{e}.jpg; dual-QPointer-guarded async download via shared NAM; imdb stale-callback guard. | files: src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h, src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.cpp, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4, STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 3 complete]: Detail view density — hero + chips + description show-more + episode thumbnails/overview. Batches 3.2 + 3.5 explicitly deferred per Hemanth; see STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md. | Objective: Phase 3 per STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md Batches 3.1, 3.3, 3.4. | Files: src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.h, src/core/stream/MetaAggregator.cpp, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp.

Standing by for smoke + Agent 6 scoped review (3.1/3.3/3.4 only). Phase 4 (search polish) next on the TODO once Hemanth greenlights.

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 7 Batch 7.1 shipped

Stats badge. Stretch batch — closes the TODO. Full IINA-identity plan complete.

### What shipped

**NEW [StatsBadge](src/ui/player/StatsBadge.h)** — lean widget. Single `QLabel` in a translucent-dark pill (`rgba(16,16,16,230)` bg + 1 px border + 10 px radius — consistent with ToastHud / CenterFlash palette, no accent colors per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`). Monospace text. `setStats(codec, w, h, fps, drops)` renders `{codec} · {W}×{H} · {fps} fps · {drops} drops`. Fallback `"—"` for unknown fps (fps == 0.0) and drops (quint64(-1) sentinel).

**Sidecar-side fps propagation** (rebuild required):
- [demuxer.h](../TankobanQTGroundWork/native_sidecar/src/demuxer.h) — `ProbeResult::fps` (double, default 0.0).
- [demuxer.cpp](../TankobanQTGroundWork/native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp) — reads `AVStream::avg_frame_rate`, falls back to `r_frame_rate` if avg is unset (some live-stream demuxers don't populate avg); `av_q2d` to double; zero denominator → stays 0.0.
- [main.cpp](../TankobanQTGroundWork/native_sidecar/src/main.cpp) `first_frame` event payload: `p["fps"] = probe->fps` alongside the existing `codec/width/height/ptsSec`.

**FrameCanvas accessor** — new `quint64 framesSkipped() const` exposes `m_framesSkippedTotal` (the Batch 1.2 sustained-lag Present-skip counter). Used as the drops field. Implicitly resets on D3D device-lost recovery (tearDownD3D path), which matches UX expectation — "drops since current display session."

**VideoPlayer wiring**:
- Constructor reads `QSettings player/showStats` (default false) into `m_showStats`. Constructor builds `m_statsBadge = new StatsBadge(this)`, hides it. `m_statsTicker` (1 Hz QTimer) polls drops from FrameCanvas while badge visible.
- `onFirstFrame` parses `codec/width/height/fps` from the sidecar payload, stashes into `m_stats{Codec,Width,Height,Fps}`. If `m_showStats`, shows + populates badge + starts ticker.
- `toggleStatsBadge()` flips `m_showStats`, persists, shows/hides badge, starts/stops ticker, toasts state.
- `resizeEvent` positions badge top-right below the toast strip: `width - sh.width() - 12, y=52`.
- `show_shortcuts` / `toggle_stats` / `show stats` dispatch chain: key `I` default, context-menu checkable entry "Show Stats\tI", case `ToggleStats` in the callback switch.

### Files

- NEW [src/ui/player/StatsBadge.h](src/ui/player/StatsBadge.h) + [.cpp](src/ui/player/StatsBadge.cpp).
- [src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.h](src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.h) — `framesSkipped()` accessor.
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h) — forward-decl, `toggleStatsBadge` declaration, state members (`m_statsBadge`, `m_showStats`, `m_stats{Codec,Width,Height,Fps}`, `m_statsTicker`).
- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp) — construction, constructor-time settings load, onFirstFrame parse, toggle impl, resize positioning, key dispatch, context-menu callback + data population.
- [src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h) — `ToggleStats` enum + `showStats` field.
- [src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.cpp) — menu entry.
- [src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp](src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp) — `toggle_stats` default (I).
- [CMakeLists.txt](CMakeLists.txt) — +StatsBadge.cpp.
- Sidecar (requires rebuild via `native_sidecar/build.ps1`): `native_sidecar/src/demuxer.h`, `native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp`, `native_sidecar/src/main.cpp`.

### Verify procedure

1. Play a local video file → press **I** → badge appears top-right, reads e.g. `h264 · 1920×1080 · 23.976 fps · 0 drops`.
2. Let playback continue. Drops counter updates when sustained-lag hits (same condition as `m_framesSkippedTotal` increments in FrameCanvas).
3. Press I again → badge hides. Press I → reappears.
4. Right-click → Show Stats checkable entry mirrors state.
5. Restart app — persisted `showStats` toggle reapplies on first firstFrame.
6. Pre-sidecar-rebuild: badge shows `h264 · 1920×1080 · — fps · 0 drops` because `fps` is missing from the payload. After sidecar rebuild (via `native_sidecar/build.ps1`): fps populates correctly.
7. Different codecs: test on HEVC / AV1 / VP9 source — codec label reflects the actual AVCodecDescriptor name.

### Regression

- ToastHud still sits at `y=12` top-right (280 wide); StatsBadge sits at `y=52` (below). No overlap even when both visible.
- FrameCanvas' device-lost recovery zeroes `m_framesSkippedTotal` via tearDownD3D — badge drops count "resets" after a DXGI reset. Intended UX; documented in FrameCanvas.h comment.

### Phase 7 exit

Batch 7.1 shipped. All of VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX_TODO.md's phases (1-7) complete.

READY TO COMMIT -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batch 7.1 / Phase 7]: Compact stats badge. NEW StatsBadge widget (translucent pill, monospace single-line); VideoPlayer parses codec/w/h/fps from firstFrame + polls drops from FrameCanvas on 1 Hz ticker + QSettings persistence; I key + context menu checkable entry; sidecar probe emits fps in first_frame payload | files main-app: NEW src/ui/player/StatsBadge.h + .cpp; src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.h; src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.h + .cpp; src/ui/player/VideoContextMenu.h + .cpp; src/ui/player/KeyBindings.cpp; CMakeLists.txt | files sidecar (requires rebuild): native_sidecar/src/demuxer.h, native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp, native_sidecar/src/main.cpp

READY FOR REVIEW -- [Agent 3, VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 7]: Stats badge (stretch) | Objective: Phase 7 per VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/video_player_2026-04-15.md. ## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 4 Batch 4.1 shipped

Phase 4 opens. Live search with 300ms debounce + subtle spinner.

### What shipped

**StreamPage.h** — new slots (`onSearchTextChanged`, `onSearchDebounceFired`, `setSearchBusy`); new members (`QTimer* m_searchDebounce`, `QWidget* m_searchBusy`).

**StreamPage.cpp — ctor** — two new connects off MetaAggregator's `catalogResults` + `catalogError` signals drive the spinner off-state. Lambdas capture `this`; StreamPage owns m_metaAggregator so lifetimes are coupled.

**StreamPage.cpp — buildSearchBar**:
- QLineEdit gains `setClearButtonEnabled(true)` so users can one-click clear the field (restores browse via the new textChanged handler).
- New `QProgressBar` (busy mode, range 0..0) 16x16 between input + Search button. Object name `StreamSearchBusy`; transparent background, 50% white chunk. Built-in Qt indeterminate animation.
- New QTimer `m_searchDebounce` single-shot 300ms; textChanged restarts it, timeout fires `onSearchDebounceFired`.

**StreamPage.cpp — handlers**:
- `onSearchTextChanged(text)` — empty text stops debounce + hides spinner + calls showBrowse if search is currently visible. <2 chars stops debounce (no fire, no revert). >=2 chars restarts the 300ms window.
- `onSearchDebounceFired` — reads current field text (defensive re-check for <2 chars in case user deleted between events and fire), hides browse, sets busy, calls `m_searchWidget->search(query)`.
- `setSearchBusy(bool)` — toggles `m_searchBusy` visibility.
- `onSearchSubmit` updated — stops the debounce + sets busy before the search call (Enter / button path now also surfaces the spinner consistently).
- `showBrowse` — defensive `setSearchBusy(false)` covers the "navigate away mid-search" case.

**StreamSearchWidget.cpp — search()** — removed the full-page "Searching..." status label (TODO explicitly called this out as the wrong UX). Status label stays for error + "no results" messages; hidden during loading. Search-bar spinner is the sole loading affordance now.

### Flow

1. User types "B" → textChanged("B") → len=1 → debounce stopped, no search, browse stays.
2. User types "Br" → textChanged("Br") → len=2 → debounce started (300ms).
3. User types "Brea" within 300ms → each textChanged restarts the timer. Final pause wins.
4. 300ms after last keystroke → `onSearchDebounceFired` → spinner shows → `m_searchWidget->search("Brea")` → `MetaAggregator::searchCatalog("Brea")`.
5. `catalogResults` arrives → spinner hides, results populate via StreamSearchWidget's existing handler.
6. User clears field → textChanged("") → spinner hides, search widget hides, home board restored.

### Design choices

- **QProgressBar busy mode over custom spinner** — Qt's built-in indeterminate animation handles the painting; zero new code, zero new assets. Minimal grayscale stylesheet keeps it consistent with `feedback_no_color_no_emoji` (white chunk at 50% opacity; transparent background).
- **Spinner lives in search bar (StreamPage), not StreamSearchWidget** — the TODO file list says StreamSearchWidget but the TODO prose says "in the search bar". The search bar visual is StreamPage's `m_searchBarFrame`. Went with the spirit ("subtle, in the search bar, not full-page") over the file-list mention. StreamSearchWidget still touched: removed the old "Searching..." label text which was the full-page state the TODO argued against.
- **Debounce via single-shot QTimer restart** — idiomatic Qt pattern, no extra state. `stop()` on every textChanged before `start()` is implicit in QTimer semantics (restart() is `stop()` + `start()`), but `start()` alone on a single-shot also restarts. Explicit `stop()` only in the empty / <2 char branches where we want to cancel without restarting.
- **<2 chars no-ops** — matches Stremio's minimum query length. Prevents thrashing the addon network on a 1-char query.
- **Empty field restores browse only if search was visible** — prevents a cold-boot "showBrowse on startup" from racing with the normal startup path. `m_searchWidget->isVisible()` is the gate.
- **onSearchSubmit still fires synchronously** — Enter / Search button path is instant (doesn't wait for debounce). Users who want immediate fire bypass the 300ms wait. Debounce only applies to live typing.
- **setClearButtonEnabled** — a nice affordance users expect from any search field. Standard Qt API, zero custom code.

### Known caveats (deferred)

- **Spinner chunk color isn't perfectly centered visually** — QProgressBar in busy mode animates a moving chunk across its width; at 16x16 the chunk is a small bar sliding left-to-right. Looks slightly less polished than a true spinner (rotating dot / arc). If Hemanth wants a true spinner, it's a QSvgWidget + QTimer rotation or a QMovie with a GIF — bigger lift, can add in 4.2/4.3 polish.
- **No minimum display time** — spinner can flash in/out on sub-100ms responses. Standard UX would ignore display <200ms. Defer unless it actually flickers in practice.
- **Spinner state during StreamSearchWidget's own downloadPoster phase** — spinner hides when catalogResults fires, but poster downloads continue in the background. Results are visible, so "loading" is arguably done; skipping spinner for posters is correct.

### Re-verification

1. Type "Brea" in search bar slowly → spinner appears ~300ms after last keystroke; results populate; spinner hides.
2. Type rapidly then stop → only one search fires (on the final pause).
3. Type "B" → no search runs (below 2-char threshold); spinner stays hidden.
4. Press Enter with partial input → instant fire (bypasses debounce).
5. Click the QLineEdit's × clear button → spinner hides, browse layer restored.
6. Delete chars back to empty → same as clear button path.
7. Start a search, hit Esc / Back before results arrive → showBrowse fires, spinner hides; when catalogResults lands, UI stays on browse (StreamSearchWidget handler repopulates its own strip silently, user won't see).
8. Regression: Enter / Search button click paths still work exactly as before; StreamSearchWidget result rendering unchanged; poster download + In-Library badge refresh unchanged.

### Files touched

- src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h (new slot decls, 2 members)
- src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp (include QProgressBar; ctor catalog signal connects; buildSearchBar spinner + debounce construction; 3 new handlers; onSearchSubmit + showBrowse tweaks)
- src/ui/pages/stream/StreamSearchWidget.cpp (search() — "Searching..." full-page label replaced with hide; no header change)

No CMakeLists change. No new files.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Batch 4.1]: STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 4 Batch 4.1 — live search with 300ms debounce + subtle search-bar spinner. QTimer single-shot restarted on textChanged; <2 chars no-op, empty restores browse, >=2 chars fires searchCatalog after 300ms idle. QProgressBar busy-mode 16x16 spinner between input and Search button; shown on search fire, hidden on catalogResults / catalogError / showBrowse. Full-page "Searching..." label removed from StreamSearchWidget per TODO. setClearButtonEnabled on the QLineEdit. | files: src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamSearchWidget.cpp

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.1 shipped

Phase 2 opens. Hemanth refined the Mihon-depth identity mid-session: no new reading modes (Single / Double / ScrollStrip already cover everything), so identity value shifts from mode breadth to capability breadth + UX polish. Batch 2.1 ships the color filter pipeline with **brightness only** — biggest Mihon signature (`ColorFilterPage` is iconic), simultaneously closes TKMAX-1 (Tankoban-Max image filters regression) and audit P1-6 (external parity gap). Plan file at `C:\Users\Suprabha\.claude\plans\nested-beaming-emerson.md` (overwritten for Phase 2).

### What shipped

**Anonymous-namespace `applyBrightness(QPixmap, int delta)` helper** at [ComicReader.cpp:42-65](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L42-L65) and mirrored at [ScrollStripCanvas.cpp:9-32](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L9-L32) — QImage::Format_ARGB32 pixel loop, delta mapped ±100 → ±255, R/G/B clamped, alpha preserved, DPR preserved. Delta=0 early-return (zero-cost "filter off" path). Duplicated in both translation units for isolation; Batch 2.2 will promote to a shared helper when contrast/saturation join.

**Paged-mode integration** at [ComicReader::displayCurrentPage](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1094-L1283) — applied in all 4 branches:
- Single-page: after final scaling before `setPixmap`.
- Double-page spread: to full canvas after gutter composite.
- Double-page cover/single: same.
- Double-page normal pair: same, after gutter shadow overlay.

Cache key extended with `m_displayCacheBrightness` (new field at [ComicReader.h:313](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L313)) so state-unchanged early-return still hits when filter is stable, but re-renders on filter change.

**Incidental bug fix:** single-page mode previously `return`-ed out of displayCurrentPage without populating the display cache, so the cache early-return never fired for single-page. Fixed — single-page now caches state + pixmap like the double-page branches. Net perf improvement for single-page re-entry.

**Scroll-strip integration** — new [ScrollStripCanvas::setFilterBrightness(int)](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L90-L100) stores value + wipes `m_scaledCache` on change. Filter applied inside `onPageDecoded` at [ScrollStripCanvas.cpp:69](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L69) immediately after `scaledToWidth`. Scale-then-filter order keeps math on display-size pixels. `buildScrollStrip` at [ComicReader.cpp:1934](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1934) now pushes `m_filterBrightness` into the canvas on creation, so reloaded series come up correctly.

**Context menu** at [ComicReader.cpp:2553-2572](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2553-L2572) — new `Image Filters → Brightness` submenu with presets `-50 / -25 / Off / +25 / +50`, active preset marked with trailing ` *` (mirrors Gutter Shadow pattern at :2483-2500). On click: set `m_filterBrightness`, invalidate paged cache, push to strip via `setFilterBrightness`, call `refreshVisibleStripPages` if in scroll-strip mode (re-feeds decoded pages through the new filter — image-quality toggle doesn't do this today and relies on user scroll to trigger; filter path does it explicitly for snappier visual feedback), `displayCurrentPage()` + `saveSeriesSettings()`.

**Persistence** — extends Batch 1.1's whitelist:
- `saveSeriesSettings`: new `s.setValue(k + "/filter_brightness", ...)` + F1 seed map entry.
- `applySeriesSettings`: symmetric reads in both F1 migration block and main block, clamped `qBound(-100, v, 100)`.
- `resetSeriesSettings`: Ctrl+0 zeros brightness + pushes 0 to strip.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — 2 new members.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — `<QImage>` include, helper, BRIGHTNESS_PRESETS const, 4-branch display integration, context-menu submenu, persistence triple-extension, buildScrollStrip push, resetSeriesSettings zero.
- [src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h) — setter decl + member.
- [src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp) — `<QImage>` include, helper, filter in onPageDecoded, setter impl.

No CMakeLists, no shared files, no cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **Pixel loop performance.** ~3.5M pixels on a 1500×2300 full-res page × 3 channels well under 50ms on modern hardware. Scaled-pixmap size is smaller in the display path (page width ≈ viewport × portraitWidthPct × DPR), so actual workload is 1/2 to 1/4 of worst case. No SIMD/threading in 2.1 — optimize only if smoke surfaces lag.
- **Filter-off early return is free.** 99% of users don't touch filters; the `delta == 0` check returns the input QPixmap unchanged (Qt refcounts pixmap data).
- **Scroll-strip eviction unaffected.** `evictScaledOutsideZone` operates on slot indices, not pixmap content.
- **Helper duplication intentional.** Batch 2.2 consolidates into shared `ImageFilters.h` when contrast/saturation/grayscale/invert join. Per-TU duplication keeps Batch 2.1 tight.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. **Single-page +50:** Right-click → Image Filters → Brightness → +50 → page brightens. Next/prev page stays brightened. Off → restored.
2. **Single-page -50:** Page darkens. Off → restored.
3. **Double-page +25:** Both pages brightened uniformly (no seam at gutter). Spread pages brightened uniformly. Off → restored.
4. **Scroll-strip +50:** All visible pages brightened. Scroll down — new pages come up brightened. Scroll back up — re-render brightened. Off → all restored.
5. **Active marker:** Each preset, on next right-click, shows " *" after currently-active value.
6. **Persistence:** Set +25, close reader/app, relaunch, re-open same series → brightness = +25 restored.
7. **F1 new-series seed:** Open a series never visited before → inherits last-saved +25.
8. **Ctrl+0 reset:** Zeros brightness along with other series settings.
9. **Regression:** Gutter shadow, image quality Smooth/Fast, memory saver, portrait width, RTL (I), fit cycle (F), mode cycle (M), Ctrl+wheel zoom — all unchanged. Batch 1.1 persistence fields still restore.
10. **Performance:** Scroll-strip at +50 on a long CBZ feels as responsive as unfiltered. Page-turn has no perceptible added lag.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, READY TO COMMIT line posts per Rule 11. Phase 2 continues with Batch 2.2 (contrast + saturation + grayscale + invert, extends same submenu tree + consolidates helper into shared header).

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.1 shipped

Phase 3 opens on Hemanth's call to skip ahead past Batch 2.2 / 2.3 for now (color filter polish deferred). Phase 3 adds the webtoon-style tuning knobs the audit flagged as P1-2 — these are real new capabilities on top of existing ScrollStrip, not UX rearrangement. Batch 3.1 lands the side-padding knob.

### What shipped

**Side padding on ScrollStrip — configurable comfort bars each side of the page column.** Users reading long webtoon-style CBZs often want visual width reduced for eye comfort without touching the per-series portraitWidthPct (which also affects paged modes). Side padding is ScrollStrip-specific and orthogonal to portrait width.

**New state on ScrollStripCanvas** at [ScrollStripCanvas.h:18](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h#L18) + [:75](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h#L75) — `setSidePadding(int px)` setter + `m_sidePadding` member. Setter clamps to 0..400px (beyond that pages collapse), early-returns on no-change, calls `invalidateScaledCache()` on change (page widths move, scaled cache is for the wrong width, Y offsets must rebuild).

**`targetPageWidth` update** at [ScrollStripCanvas.cpp:290-310](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L290-L310) — computes `usableWidth = viewport - 2 * m_sidePadding` before applying the existing `portraitWidthPct` or spread-fills-full-width fraction. Padding applies to both portraits AND spreads (so a full-bleed spread at 160px padding still leaves two 160px bars, not edge-to-edge). No-upscale guard against `origW` unchanged. Auto-centering in paintEvent via `drawX = (width() - logW) / 2` handles the centering — padding just reduces the rendered width and the existing center math slots it into the middle.

**ComicReader state** at [ComicReader.h:330](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h#L330) — `int m_stripSidePadding = 0`. `buildScrollStrip` at [ComicReader.cpp:1947](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L1947) pushes the value into the canvas at creation, mirroring the filter/quality pushes.

**Context menu** at [ComicReader.cpp:2566-2588](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2566-L2588) — new ScrollStrip-gated `Side Padding` submenu (only appears when `m_isScrollStrip`), parallel to the existing DoublePage-gated Gutter Shadow block. Presets: `Off / Small (40px) / Medium (80px) / Large (120px) / X-Large (160px)`, active preset marked with trailing ` *`. On click: update `m_stripSidePadding`, push to canvas (setter internally wipes scaled cache), call `refreshVisibleStripPages` to re-feed decoded pages through the new page-width pipeline, `saveSeriesSettings`.

**Persistence** — extends the whitelist Batch 1.1/2.1 already grew:
- `saveSeriesSettings`: new `s.setValue(k + "/strip_side_padding", ...)` + F1 seed map entry.
- `applySeriesSettings`: symmetric reads in both F1 migration block and main block, clamped `qBound(0, v, 400)`.
- `resetSeriesSettings`: Ctrl+0 zeros side padding + pushes 0 to strip canvas.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h) — setter decl + member.
- [src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp) — setter impl + targetPageWidth usableWidth math.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — one new member.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — SIDE_PADDING_PRESETS const, buildScrollStrip push, ScrollStrip-gated context menu block, persistence triple-extension.

No CMakeLists, no shared files, no cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **Orthogonal to `portraitWidthPct`.** The existing portrait-width preset already shrinks portrait pages (not spreads) as a general "how wide are portraits" knob across all modes. Side padding narrows the viewport itself and affects spreads too. Example math at 1200px viewport, portraitWidthPct=80, sidePadding=80: usable = 1200 - 160 = 1040; portrait page = 1040 × 0.8 = 832; spread = 1040. A user who wants wide-webtoon pages with comfort bars can set portraitWidthPct=100 and rely on side padding alone.
- **Gated on ScrollStrip only.** Menu entry appears only in scroll mode — avoids menu clutter for users who don't scroll-read. Internal state persists regardless of active mode (persists per-series like other settings).
- **Why pixels, not percent.** Absolute pixel bars stay consistent across viewport resizes (window resize doesn't suddenly make the bars grow/shrink). Mihon uses a percent-of-viewport model but most webtoon readers go pixel-based because it's what eyes actually experience. If smoke reveals Hemanth wants percent, trivial to swap.
- **No-upscale still honored.** The target width shrinks with padding applied, which never exceeds original dimensions — so padding can only ever shrink display, never force upscale.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. Open any CBZ in ScrollStrip mode. Right-click → notice new "Side Padding" submenu appears.
2. Select Medium (80px) → all visible pages shrink by 160px total width, black bars appear each side, pages re-center. Scroll — new pages come up with the same padding.
3. Switch to Large (120px) → pages narrow further, bars wider.
4. Switch to Off → pages expand back to the portraitWidthPct-scaled width.
5. Combine with portraitWidthPct: set padding to 80px + portrait width to 70% → double-shrinking applies cleanly (narrower pages with comfort bars).
6. Switch to SinglePage → "Side Padding" submenu disappears (gated on ScrollStrip). Switch back to ScrollStrip → menu returns, showing the previously-set preset with ` *` marker.
7. Persistence: set padding to Large, close app, relaunch, re-open same series in ScrollStrip → Large restored (right-click confirms " *" on Large).
8. F1 new-series seed: open a never-visited series → inherits last-saved padding.
9. Ctrl+0 reset: zeros padding + all other series settings.
10. Regression: Brightness filter (2.1), image quality, portrait width, mode cycle (M), ScrollStrip smooth-scroll — all unchanged.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, READY TO COMMIT line posts per Rule 11. Phase 3 continues with Batch 3.2 (crop borders toggle) or Batch 3.3 (split-on-wide) on your direction, or back to Phase 2 unfinished work.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.2 shipped

Auto-crop uniform borders on decoded pages. Solves the "scanner left a 30px white margin all around" problem that wastes vertical space in scroll mode and shrinks the readable area in paged modes.

### What shipped

**`autoCropBorders(QPixmap)` helper** at [ComicReader.cpp:67-128](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L67-L128) (anonymous namespace). Algorithm:
- Convert to `QImage::Format_ARGB32` for fast scanLine access.
- Sample corner pixels as the per-edge "border colour" reference (top-left for top/left edges; bottom-right for bottom/right).
- Scan inward row-by-row (top + bottom) and col-by-col (left + right). A row/col counts as "border" when ≥97% of its pixels match the reference within RGB tolerance ±18.
- Stop at the first non-border row/col on each side.
- Hard cap: never crop more than 15% inset per side — guards against over-crop on uniformly-light/dark pages or all-uniform splash pages.
- If no inset detected on any side: return original pixmap (zero-cost fallback).
- Else: `src.copy(cropRect)`, preserve DPR.

Detection cost is bounded — typically scans 30-150 rows/cols before finding non-border content, so ~5-15ms on a 2000×3000 page. Runs once per decoded page (not per paint).

**Decode-time integration** at [ComicReader::onPageDecoded](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L972-L1019) — applied BEFORE caching, so:
- `m_cache.insert` stores the cropped pixmap.
- `m_pageMeta[i].width / .height` reflect post-crop dims.
- Spread detection (`width / height > SPREAD_RATIO`) computes against cropped aspect ratio (correct — a portrait page that gets its white side-bars stripped becomes more portrait-y).
- Strip canvas receives cropped pixmap + cropped dims via the existing `m_stripCanvas->onPageDecoded(pageIndex, finalPx, finalW, finalH)` call — strip layout auto-recalculates page heights with no further code changes.
- Paged mode's `m_currentPixmap = finalPx` substitution keeps `displayCurrentPage`'s scaling math consistent.

**Single intercept point** — both paged and strip paths converge through `ComicReader::onPageDecoded`, so the helper is called exactly once per decoded page. ScrollStripCanvas needs no crop knowledge.

**Context menu** at [ComicReader.cpp:2725-2748](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2725-L2748) — new mode-agnostic `Crop Borders: On / Off` toggle entry, placed right after the Image Filters submenu. ` *` marker on active state. On click:
- Flip `m_cropBorders`.
- Wipe everything: `m_cache.clear()`, null `m_currentPixmap` + `m_secondPixmap`, `m_displayCachePage = -1`, reset `m_pageMeta[i].decoded = false` across the board, `m_stripCanvas->invalidateScaledCache()`.
- Re-request decode for visible pages: `refreshVisibleStripPages` in scroll mode; `requestDecode(m_currentPage)` + pair re-decode in paged modes.
- `saveSeriesSettings` + toast confirmation.

The full re-decode is necessary because crop happens at decode time and `m_pageMeta` widths/heights are baked from the cropped pixmaps. Toggle is a "rebuild the page set" operation, not a cheap display-time toggle. User-perceived: ~100ms blank-then-redraw on the current page, which is acceptable for a one-off toggle.

**Persistence** — extends the whitelist:
- `saveSeriesSettings`: `s.setValue(k + "/crop_borders", m_cropBorders)` + F1 seed map entry.
- `applySeriesSettings`: symmetric reads in both F1 migration and main blocks.
- `resetSeriesSettings`: Ctrl+0 zeros it back to false.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — one new bool member.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — `autoCropBorders` helper, decode-time intercept (5 line substitution: `pixmap`/`w`/`h` → `finalPx`/`finalW`/`finalH`), context-menu toggle entry, persistence triple-extension.

No changes to ScrollStripCanvas (intentional — it's data-contract-clean since onPageDecoded already takes width/height parameters that reflect whatever pixmap is passed in). No CMakeLists, no shared files, no cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **Decode-time crop, not display-time.** Display-time crop would require running detection on every paint AND complicate strip-canvas page-height layout (which uses origW/origH for slot heights — cropped-on-display would mis-size slots). Decode-time crop sidesteps both: detection runs once, `m_pageMeta` reflects what the user sees, all downstream layout math is automatic.
- **Conservative tolerance.** ±18 RGB tolerance + 97% match threshold + 15% max inset are conservative defaults that should never crop content. Aggressive enough to catch typical scanner white/black bars; safe enough to leave artistic borders intact.
- **No detection on small pages.** Pages < 20×20 short-circuit to original — protects against degenerate inputs.
- **Re-decode on toggle is intentional.** Could be optimized by caching uncropped + cropped pixmaps in parallel, but doubles memory cost. Toggle is rare; re-decode from disk is fast (~100ms per page on a hot OS cache).
- **Helper duplication NOT mirrored to ScrollStripCanvas** unlike applyBrightness — because the crop operation lives upstream of where the strip canvas sees pixmaps. Strip canvas stays oblivious.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. **Visual on a CBZ with borders:** Open a series whose pages have visible white/black borders (most scanlated CBZ qualifies). Right-click → Crop Borders: Off → click → entry shows "Crop Borders: On *" + toast. Current page redraws with borders trimmed; visible area is larger.
2. **Toggle off:** Right-click → Crop Borders: On * → click → entry now shows "Crop Borders: Off" + toast. Page redraws with borders restored.
3. **Scroll mode:** Switch to ScrollStrip. Toggle on. All visible pages redraw cropped — page heights tighten, total scroll length shortens. Scroll up/down — new pages come in cropped.
4. **Double-page mode:** Switch to DoublePage. Toggle on. Both pages cropped, gutter math still aligns properly. Spread pages cropped uniformly.
5. **No-op safety:** Open a page that genuinely has no uniform borders (e.g. full-bleed art to edge). Toggle on → page unchanged (helper returns original).
6. **Persistence:** Set Crop Borders: On, close reader/app, relaunch, re-open same series → state restored, pages come up cropped from first decode.
7. **F1 new-series seed:** Open a never-visited series → inherits last-saved crop state.
8. **Ctrl+0 reset:** Crop borders zeros to off along with other series settings.
9. **Stack with Brightness (Batch 2.1):** Crop on + brightness +25 → cropped pages also brightened. Both filters compose cleanly.
10. **Stack with Side Padding (Batch 3.1):** ScrollStrip + crop on + side padding Medium → cropped pages with strip-side bars. Composes.
11. **Regression:** All Batch 1.1/1.2/2.1/3.1 features unchanged. Spread detection still works (toggle doesn't break two-page pairing). Page navigation, scrubbing, bookmarks, volume nav — all untouched.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, READY TO COMMIT line posts per Rule 11. Phase 3 has one more batch left (3.3 split-on-wide) before phase exit, or you can redirect to other work.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.3 shipped

Phase 3 closes with split-on-wide. ScrollStrip now offers to break wide pages (publisher-printed two-page spreads scanned as one image) into two stacked half-width portraits within the same page slot. Page index stays one-per-file — navigation, bookmarks, progress, scrub bar all keep their existing semantics. Only the visual rendering splits.

### What shipped

**Per-slot right-half storage.** [ScrollStripCanvas.h PageSlot](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h#L57-L67) extended with `QPixmap rightHalf` member. When split is active and a page is wide, `m_scaledCache[i]` holds the LEFT half scaled to portrait fraction width; `m_slots[i].rightHalf` holds the RIGHT half scaled to the same width. paintEvent stacks them vertically with a 16px gap.

**Split-aware width math** at [ScrollStripCanvas::targetPageWidth](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L301-L321) — when `m_splitOnWide && slot.isSpread`, the spread fraction (1.0) is replaced by the portrait fraction (`portraitWidthPct/100`). No-upscale guard now compares against `origW/2` for split pages instead of `origW`. Both halves render at the user's chosen portrait width — same comfort as a normal portrait page.

**Decode-time split branch** at [ScrollStripCanvas::onPageDecoded](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L91-L121) — if splitting, the `fullRes` pixmap is cut horizontally via two `QPixmap::copy(rect)` calls, each half scaled independently with `scaledToWidth(physW)`, brightness applied to each, left stored in the main scaled cache + right stored on the slot. Otherwise: existing single-pixmap path with `slot.rightHalf = QPixmap()` to clear any stale half from a previous mode.

**Layout math** at [ScrollStripCanvas::rebuildYOffsets](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L289-L312) — when splitting, slot height = `2 × halfHeight + SPLIT_GAP` where halfHeight = `pw × (2 × origH / origW)` (each half has aspect ratio twice the original). Ensures the strip layout reserves correct vertical space for stacked halves before decode arrives.

**Render** at [ScrollStripCanvas::paintEvent](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L189-L228) — for split slots, after drawing the cached left half, computes `rightDrawY = leftDrawY + leftHeight + 16px` and draws the right half horizontally centered (independently of left in case widths differ slightly due to integer rounding).

**Cache lifecycle** — `invalidateScaledCache`, `setFilterBrightness`, `evictScaledOutsideZone` all wipe `slot.rightHalf` alongside the main scaled cache so left/right always live and die together. No orphaned right halves can render against fresh left pixmaps.

**Setter** [ScrollStripCanvas::setSplitOnWide](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp#L162-L170) — early-return on no-change, `invalidateScaledCache()` on change to force re-decode through the new branch.

**Context menu** — new ScrollStrip-gated `Split Wide Pages: On / Off` toggle placed inside the existing strip-only block right after the Side Padding submenu. ` *` marker on active state. On click: flip member, push to canvas, `refreshVisibleStripPages` to re-feed cached decoded pages through the new split branch, `saveSeriesSettings`, toast.

**Persistence** extends the whitelist: `split_on_wide` saved/applied/reset, F1 migration seed updated. ResetSeriesSettings (Ctrl+0) clears canvas state too.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.h) — setter decl, `m_splitOnWide` member, `PageSlot::rightHalf`.
- [src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp](src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.cpp) — setter impl, split branches in `onPageDecoded` + `targetPageWidth` + `rebuildYOffsets` + `paintEvent`, `rightHalf` lifecycle in invalidate + brightness setter + evict.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — one new bool member.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — push to canvas at buildScrollStrip + resetSeriesSettings, ScrollStrip-gated context menu toggle, persistence triple-extension.

No CMakeLists, no shared files, no cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **Page index stays one-per-file.** A wide page rendered as two stacked halves still counts as ONE page in `m_pageNames` / `pageAtCenter` / `pageTopY` / scrub bar / bookmarks / progress payload. Navigation "next page" advances to the next file, regardless of split. Page label HUD shows "47 / 100" for a split page, not "47-left / 47-right". Matches Mihon convention; keeps progress contract intact.
- **LTR-only for now.** Left half always above right half. RTL manga readers expecting right-half-first can be a small follow-up batch (plumb `m_rtl` from ComicReader to ScrollStripCanvas, swap the draw order in paintEvent).
- **Stacks cleanly with crop borders (3.2).** Crop runs at decode time in ComicReader BEFORE the pixmap reaches ScrollStripCanvas. So if a wide page has white borders, crop trims them first; the cropped pixmap (still wide) then gets split at the strip level. Both effects compose naturally.
- **Stacks with side padding (3.1).** `targetPageWidth` subtracts side padding before applying portrait fraction → split halves respect both knobs.
- **Stacks with brightness (2.1).** Both halves get brightness applied independently in onPageDecoded.
- **Mode-gated UX.** Toggle only appears in ScrollStrip — no menu clutter for SinglePage/DoublePage users. Internal state persists per-series regardless of active mode.
- **DoublePage and SinglePage unaffected.** Split doesn't apply there. SinglePage shows wide pages as wide (user can zoom or switch mode); DoublePage already pairs/spreads correctly. This batch is webtoon-comfort-specific.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. **Find a series with wide pages.** Most manga CBZs include occasional spreads (publisher-printed two-page art). Open in ScrollStrip mode.
2. **Toggle on:** Right-click → Split Wide Pages: Off → click → entry shows "Split Wide Pages: On *" + toast. Wide pages now render as two stacked half-width portraits with 16px gap between halves. Portrait pages unchanged.
3. **Toggle off:** Click again → wide pages collapse back to full-viewport-width single image.
4. **Page navigation across split:** Scroll to a split page. Press → / Space / PgDn for next page → advances to next FILE, not next half. Page label says "Page X / N" using the file index throughout. Bookmark at split page (B) → bookmarks at file index, not half index.
5. **Stack with Side Padding:** Set Side Padding = Medium (80px) + Split = On → both halves render at portrait-fraction-of-(viewport-160px). Bars + split compose.
6. **Stack with Crop Borders:** Open a series with wide pages that have white borders → enable Crop Borders → enable Split → cropped wide pages split into two cropped halves. No double-application bug.
7. **Stack with Brightness:** Set brightness +25 → split halves brighten uniformly (filter applied to each independently in decode).
8. **Mode switch:** Switch to SinglePage / DoublePage → "Split Wide Pages" submenu disappears (gated). Switch back → menu returns with previous state preserved.
9. **Persistence:** Set split on, close reader/app, relaunch → state restored, wide pages come up split.
10. **F1 new-series seed:** Open never-visited series → inherits last-saved split state.
11. **Ctrl+0 reset:** Clears split alongside other series settings.
12. **Regression:** Portrait pages totally unchanged. Spread auto-detection (slot.isSpread = w/h > 1.08) still works. Decoding, prefetch, scroll-strip smoothness, page cache eviction — all untouched.

### Phase 3 exit

All three batches shipped: side padding (3.1) + crop borders (3.2) + split-on-wide (3.3). Phase 3 closes the audit's P1-2 (webtoon-specific tuning) without inventing new reading modes per Hemanth's "Single/Double/Scroll covers everything" call. The existing ScrollStrip mode now has the Mihon-equivalent webtoon knobs.

On smoke green for all three batches, I'll post READY FOR REVIEW to Agent 6:
- Objective: COMIC_READER_FIX_TODO Phase 3 (audit P1-2) per plan file `nested-beaming-emerson.md`.
- Files: src/ui/readers/ComicReader.{h,cpp}, src/ui/readers/ScrollStripCanvas.{h,cpp}.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke on Batch 3.3 + Phase 3 exit decision. Next directions Hemanth has marked-up potential: Phase 4 (format discovery — cross-agent w/ Agent 5), back to Phase 2.2 (filter completion), or skip to other phases.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY paused → STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX opened

STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 4 paused at the end of Batch 4.1 (live search). Two user-reported playback bugs escalated to P0 after Agent 7 audit:

- Bug 1: 15-20% of the video buffers before playback starts.
- Bug 2: Mid-playback jumps from e.g. 0:05 to 5:16.

### Audit-driven plan

Agent 7 audit at [agents/audits/tankostream_playback_2026-04-15.md](agents/audits/tankostream_playback_2026-04-15.md) confirmed two P0 gaps vs Stremio / Peerflix / torrent-stream / Webtor:

- **P0 #1 — Sparse file exposed to ffmpeg.** StreamEngine returned a direct file path after a 2 MB contiguous gate; the sparse on-disk file has zero-filled holes for un-downloaded pieces; ffmpeg resyncs forward to the next keyframe, producing the visible jumps.
- **P0 #2 — Soft `sequential_download` without `set_piece_deadline()`.** libtorrent's own docs call `sequential_download` sub-optimal for streaming. All reference apps layer deadline-based piece retargeting on top.

### Architectural call: Option A (HTTP hardening)

Validated via targeted Explore runs — Option B (custom ffmpeg AVIOContext in sidecar) would require ~50 + 200 + 100 LOC plus a new sync-IPC piece-availability channel; current JSON-over-stdin/stdout is wrong-shaped for sync per-read queries. Option A routes torrent playback through the existing `StreamHttpServer`, hardens `waitForPieces` ignored-return, and adds `set_piece_deadline()` primitives on TorrentEngine. Reuses Peerflix model and inherits the sidecar's existing HTTP retry/stall logic at [video_decoder.cpp:807,834,850](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L807).

### New track

NEW [STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md](STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md) — 3 phases, 9 batches:

- **Phase 1 — Torrent-aware read boundary** (closes Bug 2): 1.1 route through HTTP server · 1.2 fix waitForPieces return · 1.3 per-connection deadline + graceful shutdown.
- **Phase 2 — Piece-priority streaming model** (closes Bug 1 bulk): 2.1 TorrentEngine deadline primitives · 2.2 head deadline on stream start · 2.3 sliding-window retargeting from progressUpdated · 2.4 seek/resume target pre-gate.
- **Phase 3 — Startup gate + UX alignment** (P1): 3.1 align/remove MIN_HEADER_BYTES · 3.2 buffering % shows gate progress not whole-file · 3.3 session-settings tune.

Agent 6 review triggers at each phase exit. STREAM_UX_PARITY 4.2+ resumes after this TODO closes.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 1 Batch 1.1 shipped

Routes magnet playback through the existing local HTTP server instead of handing ffmpeg a direct path to the still-downloading file.

### What shipped

[src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp:218-243](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp#L218-L243) — removed the direct-file-return branch that manually assembled `rec.savePath + "/" + filename`. Replaced with `result.url = buildStreamUrl(rec.infoHash, rec.selectedFileIndex)` — which was the documented contract at [StreamEngine.h:21,54](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h#L21) and already in the codebase as a helper at [StreamEngine.cpp:446-452](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp#L446). Closes the P2 header/impl disagreement Agent 7 flagged.

`flushCache` call retained — HTTP serving goes through libtorrent's own file layer, but the `have_piece() → on-disk` contract is consumed elsewhere and keeping the flush preserves parity.

### Downstream behavior changes

- Consumer at [StreamPage::onReadyToPlay](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1162) already names the variable `httpUrl` — no caller changes needed.
- Sidecar's existing HTTP retry / stall / reconnect / 30s timeout logic at [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp:166-180,834,850](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L166) now activates on torrent playback — previously only non-torrent HTTP streams inherited it.
- On slow-piece conditions, the HTTP server's piece-wait loop now gates reads (though Batch 1.2 closes the "ignored-return" bug where timeouts fall through to sparse reads).
- HTTP server was already started at engine init and `onMetadataReady` already calls `m_httpServer->registerFile(infoHash, fileIdx, filePath, size)` — no new registration code needed.

### Why this is the right first batch

Per Peerflix / torrent-stream / Webtor reference comparison in the audit, all three use an HTTP layer between the decoder and the incomplete file. This one change removes the sparse-file exposure that causes the jump symptom; further hardening (Batch 1.2) and piece-priority work (Phase 2) build on top.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — one function-tail rewrite, ~25 lines removed, ~5 lines added, plus a ~15-line rationale comment.

No header change, no CMake change, no other file touched.

### Re-verification

1. Start any magnet-backed stream. Inspect the URL passed through `onReadyToPlay` — should be `http://127.0.0.1:{port}/stream/{hash}/{fileIndex}`, not a local file path.
2. Play for 10 continuous minutes on a mid-seed torrent (5-10 seeders). Expected: no visible forward jumps. Playback may stall on slow pieces (this batch routes through the HTTP server's 15s wait with ignored-return — Batch 1.2 fixes that), but stalls should surface as the sidecar's existing buffering overlay, not silent garbled frames.
3. Regression: complete local videos (non-torrent Videos mode) unaffected — StreamEngine isn't on that path.
4. Regression: continue-watching, source-picker, next-episode overlay, auto-launch — all unchanged, they only consume the URL string.

### Known remaining issues (fixed in subsequent batches)

- HTTP server still falls through to sparse reads on piece timeout (Batch 1.2).
- No per-connection idle timeout; in-flight sockets on shutdown dangle (Batch 1.3).
- No `set_piece_deadline()` — pieces still arrive rarest-first-ish under load (Phase 2).
- Buffering % still reports whole-file progress, not gate progress (Batch 3.2).
- Startup 2 MB gate smaller than sidecar probe size (Batch 3.1).

None of these prevent 1.1 from being independently testable and mergeable — this is the load-bearing architectural flip; the follow-ups strengthen it.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 1.1]: Route magnet playback through the existing local HTTP server (buildStreamUrl) instead of handing ffmpeg a direct sparse-file path. Closes audit P0 #1 sparse-file exposure root cause; sidecar HTTP retry/stall machinery now activates on torrent path as a side benefit; closes P2 header/impl disagreement about magnet URL shape. | files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp

Standing by on Hemanth smoke. Batch 1.2 (fix waitForPieces ignored-return) ships on green.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 1 Batch 1.2 shipped

Fixes the `waitForPieces` ignored-return bug — the co-cause of Bug 2 jumps alongside the direct-file-return from Batch 1.1.

### The bug

[StreamHttpServer.cpp:203-218](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L203) pre-fix:
```cpp
if (engine)
    waitForPieces(engine, entry.infoHash, entry.fileIndex, offset, toRead);
QByteArray chunk = file.read(toRead);      // reads even if wait timed out
if (chunk.isEmpty()) break;
if (socket.write(chunk) < 0) break;
```

The boolean return from `waitForPieces` was discarded. On a 15s piece timeout the loop fell through to `file.read`, which returned sparse-zero bytes from the un-downloaded region. The decoder interpreted the hole as corrupt stream and resynced forward to the next keyframe.

Combined with Batch 1.1 (which now actually routes torrent playback through this code path), leaving this bug in would have simply moved the sparse-read symptom from the pre-HTTP direct-file path to the HTTP path. 1.2 closes it properly.

### What shipped

**[StreamHttpServer.cpp — serving loop](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp)** — `waitForPieces` return captured. On timeout: `qWarning` the starvation details and `break` the loop without reading. The response headers already set `Connection: close` so the client cleanly tears down; the sidecar's av_read_frame sees `AVERROR(EIO)`, which routes into the existing HTTP retry/stall-buffering path at [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp:834-849](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L834). User sees a buffering indicator and playback resumes when pieces catch up — no silent garbled frames.

**[StreamHttpServer.cpp — error responses](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp)** — `sendErr` lambda and the 416 path both gain `Content-Type: text/plain` and explicit `Connection: close`. Some ffmpeg builds warn / retry unexpectedly on type-less 4xx/5xx bodies; making the responses conformant avoids that noise and lets clients tear down cleanly.

**Logging** — five `qWarning` sites added around the serve loop (piece timeout, empty read, socket.write failure, waitForBytesWritten failure). The server was completely silent on failure pre-1.2, making production debugging impossible.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) — `<QDebug>` include; sendErr + 416 Content-Type + Connection additions; serving-loop return check + 5 qWarning sites.

No header change. No other file touched.

### Why qWarning, not qCritical / qDebug

`qWarning` is the right severity: piece-starvation is not a fatal app error (decoder retry fixes it), but it's not normal either — signals a slow seed or network problem. `qCritical` would spam logs on every mildly-slow torrent; `qDebug` would be invisible in production builds.

### Response-body consistency

The response header commits a `Content-Length: {length}` value before the loop starts, so a mid-stream break leaves the client with fewer bytes than promised. This is acceptable because:
1. The response already advertises `Connection: close` — HTTP semantics allow premature close as end-of-stream signal for `Connection: close` responses.
2. ffmpeg with a connection-closed mid-read issues a new range request at the current offset — exactly the retry path the sidecar's [video_decoder.cpp:834](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L834) loop already implements.
3. The alternative — chunked-transfer-encoding with a trailer-based error — adds a lot of code for marginal benefit; none of the reference apps (Peerflix, torrent-stream) bother either.

### Re-verification

1. **Happy path (well-seeded magnet):** play continuously for 5 minutes. Expected: no qWarning from StreamHttpServer in logs; no jumps; buffer counter progresses monotonically.
2. **Slow-piece path (throttle network, or use a fresh 1-seed magnet):** playback hits a stall. Expected: `qWarning: StreamHttpServer: piece wait timed out for …` appears in the log; the player shows the sidecar's buffering overlay (not silent forward jump); playback resumes within 30s when pieces arrive.
3. **Regression: error paths.** Hit the 404 path (invalid infoHash in URL) — response includes `Content-Type: text/plain` + `Connection: close`. Hit the 416 path (request past end-of-file via curl `Range: bytes=9999999999-`) — same.
4. **Regression: HEAD requests still work, range requests still support single range and suffix range (`bytes=X-` / `bytes=-Y`).**
5. **Regression: socket-write-failure during a normal close** (user hits Back during playback) — logs `qWarning: socket.write failed — client gone` and tears down cleanly, no hang.

### Known remaining issues (fixed in subsequent batches)

- In-flight connection threads on shutdown are not force-closed (Batch 1.3).
- No per-connection idle timeout (Batch 1.3).
- `set_piece_deadline()` still absent, so pieces still land rarest-first under load (Phase 2).

Batches 1.3 closes Phase 1 — per-connection deadline + graceful shutdown. Phase 2 opens with the TorrentEngine piece-deadline primitives.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 1.2]: Fix StreamHttpServer's waitForPieces ignored-return. On 15s piece timeout now break the serve loop (instead of falling through to a sparse file.read), letting the socket close and the sidecar's HTTP retry path at video_decoder.cpp:834 kick in. qWarning logging added on piece-timeout, empty-read, and socket-write failures (server was silent pre-1.2). Error responses (sendErr + 416) gain Content-Type: text/plain + explicit Connection: close for ffmpeg compat. | files: src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 1.3 (per-connection idle timeout + graceful shutdown) closes Phase 1 on your green.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 1 Batch 1.3 shipped

Phase 1 closes. Per-connection idle watchdog + graceful shutdown drain + RAII socket teardown.

### What shipped

**[StreamHttpServer.h](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.h)** — new `std::atomic<bool> m_shuttingDown` + `std::atomic<int> m_activeConnections`. Three public accessors (`isShuttingDown()`, `connectionStarted()`, `connectionEnded()`) so the static `handleConnection` function can coordinate with the server object. `<atomic>` include.

**[StreamHttpServer.cpp — `ConnectionGuard` RAII helper](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp)** — anonymous-namespace struct increments `m_activeConnections` on ctor; dtor does a best-effort `disconnectFromHost()` + `waitForDisconnected(2000)` and then decrements. Guarantees proper TCP FIN + counter drain regardless of which branch returns — early `setSocketDescriptor` failure, `waitForReadyRead` timeout, header-parse error, 416, serve-loop break, whatever.

**Serve loop** — three per-iteration guards added before any piece-wait / file-read / socket-write:
1. **Shutdown check:** `if (server->isShuttingDown()) break;` — set by `stop()`. Logs `qWarning` on exit.
2. **Idle-progress watchdog:** `QElapsedTimer` reset on every successful chunk write; if 30s elapses without progress (not bytes-on-wire, but piece-availability + write-completion cycle), break. Caps pathological hangs where a slow torrent + dead client leaves a thread wedged for minutes.
3. **Client-disconnect fast-path:** `if (socket.state() != ConnectedState) break;` — catches "user closed the player mid-stream" without entering another 15s `waitForPieces` first.

**Final `socket.close()` removed** — ConnectionGuard's destructor handles it with explicit FIN.

**[StreamHttpServer::stop()](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp)** — now drains in-flight connections:
1. Set `m_shuttingDown = true` (flag propagates to worker threads' next iteration).
2. Busy-wait up to 2s for `m_activeConnections` to reach 0, `QThread::msleep(25)` between polls.
3. `qWarning` if drain times out — teardown proceeds regardless; the surviving threads will hit ConnectionGuard's disconnect as their scope exits.
4. Then tear down `m_server` and clear registry (unchanged).
5. **Deliberately NOT resetting `m_shuttingDown`** — a thread stuck inside `waitForPieces` (15s blocking sleep) could unblock after drain timeout, read a reset flag, and keep serving on a torn-down server. Flag is reset only in `start()`.

**[StreamHttpServer::start()](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp)** — clears `m_shuttingDown` at the top so stop/start cycles (e.g. engine restart during tests) behave predictably.

### Why RAII for the connection counter

Without the guard, any early-return path in `handleConnection` (there are 6+) would need a manual `connectionEnded()` call. Easy to forget, easy to mis-order with `socket.close()`. RAII runs at scope exit in all cases including exceptions.

### Why `waitForDisconnected(2000)` and not longer

FIN ack should take < 100ms on localhost. 2000ms is generous; longer would undermine the 2s `stop()` drain timeout (if 10 connections all wait 5s to disconnect, drain fails 50s before the scope-level wait finishes). Two seconds matches the drain budget and is long enough for any realistic TCP teardown on 127.0.0.1.

### Why the 30s idle watchdog

The failure mode it closes: a torrent with only 1 seeder drops offline mid-stream. `waitForPieces` keeps returning false (its own 15s timeout → break → but next range request re-enters the loop from the sidecar retry). Without an overall idle budget, a thread can re-enter `waitForPieces` forever on each retry. 30s of no progress means the seeder is truly gone and we should release the socket entirely so the sidecar sees a clean connection reset instead of continued piece-timeout cycles.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.h](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.h) — atomic members + 3 accessors + `<atomic>` include
- [src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) — `<QElapsedTimer>` include, `ConnectionGuard` RAII, 3 per-iteration guards, reset idle timer on progress, `stop()` drain, `start()` flag reset

No other files. No CMake change.

### Re-verification

1. **Happy path:** stream plays to completion. Expected: no qWarning, clean termination, `m_activeConnections == 0` post-session.
2. **Player-close mid-stream:** start playback, click Back within 5s of play start. Expected: file handle released within 1s (Batch 1.3's goal); re-clicking the same source immediately succeeds without "file locked" error. TCP FIN visible in Wireshark/netstat if checking.
3. **Graceful shutdown:** close the entire app mid-stream. Expected: `StreamEngine::stop` path (which calls `StreamHttpServer::stop`) drains within 2s; if drain times out the `qWarning: drain timeout` appears in logs but shutdown completes.
4. **Dead-seed path:** play a torrent whose seeders all go offline. Expected: sidecar buffering overlay within 15s (waitForPieces break per Batch 1.2); after 30s of no progress, `qWarning: idle timeout` fires and connection tears down; sidecar then either retries fresh or surfaces `onStreamFailed`.
5. **Regression:** normal playback throughput unchanged (the per-iteration checks are <1μs each; 4 atomic loads + 1 timer compare per 256 KB chunk).
6. **Regression:** 404 / 416 paths still work; Content-Type / Connection headers from Batch 1.2 unchanged.

### Phase 1 exit

Batches 1.1 + 1.2 + 1.3 all shipped. Phase 1 closes the torrent-aware read boundary per audit P0 #1 (sparse-file exposure). Subjective on Hemanth's smoke:
- Bug 2 (random jumps) should no longer reproduce under normal conditions.
- Bug 1 (15-20% pre-play buffer) is partially improved by re-enabling HTTP piece gating but the core fix is Phase 2 (set_piece_deadline).

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 1]: Torrent-aware read boundary. Magnet playback now routes through local HTTP server with hardened piece-wait + per-connection idle watchdog + graceful shutdown drain. Closes audit P0 #1 (sparse-file exposure) + P1 #2 (HTTP server safety). | Objective: STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md Phase 1 Batches 1.1-1.3. Files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp, src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.h, src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 1.3]: Per-connection idle timeout + graceful shutdown drain. std::atomic m_shuttingDown + m_activeConnections on StreamHttpServer; ConnectionGuard RAII increments/decrements the counter and runs disconnectFromHost + waitForDisconnected for a clean TCP FIN on scope exit; serve loop gains 3 per-iteration guards (shutdown check, 30s idle watchdog, client-disconnect fast-path); stop() drains active connections up to 2s before tearing down QTcpServer + registry; start() resets the shutdown flag. | files: src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.h, src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Phase 2 opens with Batch 2.1 (TorrentEngine deadline primitives) on your green.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.1 shipped

Phase 2 opens. Four piece-deadline primitives on TorrentEngine. No streaming-behavior change — primitives only. Callers wire in later batches.

### Why this batch exists as a standalone

libtorrent's own streaming docs at https://www.libtorrent.org/streaming.html call `sequential_download` "sub-optimal for streaming" and point at `set_piece_deadline()` as the streaming primitive. Our code never used deadlines. Adding them in one batch (no callers yet) keeps the change isolated and testable — no streaming path changes behavior from this batch alone. Batches 2.2–2.4 + 3.2 consume.

### What shipped

**[TorrentEngine.h](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h)** — 4 new public methods + `<QList>` / `<QPair>` includes. Documented inline with the rationale for each and notes on when each caller wires in.

**[TorrentEngine.cpp — HAS_LIBTORRENT block](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp)** — 4 impls, all using the existing `m_mutex` lock pattern + `m_records.find` + `handle.is_valid()` + `torrent_file()` validity gate.

**[TorrentEngine.cpp — !HAS_LIBTORRENT block](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp)** — matching stubs so the non-libtorrent build still links.

### The four primitives

1. **`setPieceDeadlines(infoHash, QList<QPair<int piece, int msFromNow>>)`** — wraps `handle.set_piece_deadline(piece, ms)`. Lower ms = more urgent. libtorrent queues pieces-with-deadlines against peers expected to deliver soonest (unlike `sequential_download`, which can request early pieces from slow peers). Invalid piece indices silently skipped; no-op on invalid infoHash or missing metadata.

2. **`clearPieceDeadlines(infoHash)`** — wraps `handle.clear_piece_deadlines()`. Used on seek / resume (rebuild fresh deadlines around new window) and on playback stop (stop pre-fetching).

3. **`pieceRangeForFileOffset(infoHash, fileIndex, offset, length) → QPair<int,int>`** — translates file-byte range to [first, last] piece index range via `ti->map_file()`. Same shape as the existing `haveContiguousBytes` mapping at [TorrentEngine.cpp:753](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L753), extracted as a reusable helper. Returns `{-1,-1}` on invalid input. Clamps `length` to the file's actual end.

4. **`contiguousBytesFromOffset(infoHash, fileIndex, offset) → qint64`** — counts have_piece()-true bytes forward from `offset`. First-piece handling accounts for non-piece-aligned offsets via `startReq.start` (the in-piece byte offset). Subsequent pieces contribute their full piece_length, clamped to `fileSize - offset` so multi-file-torrent cross-file tails don't overcount. Used by Batch 3.2 (buffering % source) and Batch 2.4 (seek pre-gate).

### Locking + lifetime

All four functions acquire the existing `m_mutex` (same pattern as every other TorrentEngine method). Each checks `handle.is_valid()` AND `torrent_file()` not-null — the latter is important because metadata-not-ready-yet returns `nullptr` from `torrent_file()`. Callers should tolerate empty results in the pre-metadata window (return `{-1,-1}` for range lookup, 0 for byte count, no-op for deadlines).

### Not wired in this batch

- No call sites from StreamEngine or StreamPage. Those land in 2.2 (head deadline), 2.3 (sliding window), 2.4 (seek pre-gate), 3.2 (buffering %).
- `sequential_download` flag still set at [StreamEngine.cpp:147](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp#L147) — kept deliberately per plan; deadlines supersede but the flag is a cheap tiebreaker for unflagged pieces.
- No new signals or events — these are pure query/command primitives; any state reporting comes through existing alert channels that libtorrent already emits.

### Files

- [src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h) — `<QList>` + `<QPair>` includes; 4 method decls with inline docs
- [src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp) — 4 impls in HAS_LIBTORRENT block; 4 stubs in the non-libtorrent fallback

No CMake change. No header-contract change for any consumer.

### Re-verification (primitive-only scope)

1. **Build:** compiles cleanly in HAS_LIBTORRENT and non-HAS_LIBTORRENT modes (verified by symbol/stub parity, not a physical build here).
2. **No regression:** existing TorrentEngine paths (addMagnet, setFilePriorities, haveContiguousBytes, flushCache) unchanged. Any Tankorent / Tankoyomi / seeding behavior unaffected.
3. **Smoke (when wired):** Batches 2.2-2.4 + 3.2 will exercise the primitives; this batch alone is invisible at runtime.

### libtorrent API assumptions

- `set_piece_deadline(piece_index_t, int)` — present since libtorrent 1.0; our libtorrent 2.0.11 has it (confirmed via audit reference to https://libtorrent.org/reference-Torrent_Handle.html).
- `clear_piece_deadlines()` — same availability.
- `ti->map_file(fi, offset, length)` returns `peer_request{piece, start, length}` — already used elsewhere in `haveContiguousBytes`, so the signature is proven.
- `ti->num_pieces()`, `ti->piece_length()` — standard libtorrent accessors, no surprises.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 2.1]: TorrentEngine piece-deadline primitives — setPieceDeadlines(list of piece+ms pairs) wraps handle.set_piece_deadline; clearPieceDeadlines wraps handle.clear_piece_deadlines; pieceRangeForFileOffset maps byte-range to [first,last] piece index via ti->map_file; contiguousBytesFromOffset walks have_piece forward from an offset accounting for non-piece-aligned start + file-end clamp. Existing m_mutex + handle.is_valid + torrent_file() validity gate pattern. Stubs in non-libtorrent build. No call sites yet — Batches 2.2-2.4 + 3.2 wire in. | files: src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h, src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp

Standing by on smoke (no user-visible change; compile + no-regression is the test). Batch 2.2 (head deadline on stream start) on your green.
