## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.2 shipped

Head deadline on stream start. Wires the Batch 2.1 primitives into `onMetadataReady`.

### What shipped

[src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp `onMetadataReady`](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — after `applyStreamPriorities`, calls `pieceRangeForFileOffset(infoHash, fileIdx, 0, 5 MB)` to get the head piece range, then builds a gradient deadline list (500ms on piece 0, 5000ms on the last head piece, linear interpolation in between) and passes to `setPieceDeadlines`.

```cpp
const QPair<int, int> headRange =
    m_torrentEngine->pieceRangeForFileOffset(infoHash, fileIdx, 0, 5 MB);
// linear 500ms → 5000ms gradient across [first, last]
m_torrentEngine->setPieceDeadlines(infoHash, deadlines);
```

`sequential_download` flag from [StreamEngine.cpp:147](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp#L147) stays set — deadlines supersede for flagged pieces; the sequential flag is a tiebreaker for the rest of the file.

### Why 5 MB head window

Matches the sidecar's local-path probe size at [native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp:15](native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp#L15) (5 × 1024 × 1024 = 5 MB). By the time ffmpeg's probe finishes skimming the head, the full probe window should already be on disk. Bigger would delay click-to-play (more pieces to fan out on); smaller would risk the probe reading past the deadline-priority region.

### Why 500 → 5000ms gradient

libtorrent's scheduler uses deadlines to sort urgency. A flat 500ms across all head pieces would overflow the urgency queue and make libtorrent issue overlapping requests to the same peer for many pieces at once. The gradient keeps the urgency front-loaded: piece 0 is most-urgent (ffmpeg reads it first), piece 40 (~5 MB window at ~128 KB pieces) is less urgent but still within the scheduler's "near-term" horizon. 5000ms is well inside libtorrent's default request lifetime, so pieces don't time out before they're assigned.

These numbers are starting points; Phase 3 might tune after measuring.

### Why we keep `sequential_download`

Two reasons:
1. Deadlines are opt-in per piece. Pieces outside the head window (the rest of the file) need _some_ ordering preference so bandwidth isn't spent on random pieces. `sequential_download` provides that for free.
2. libtorrent's docs suggest deadlines + sequential compose cleanly — deadlines take effect for flagged pieces; sequential breaks ties for the remaining pieces. No observed conflict in the API.

### Why the block is scoped with `{}`

Local constants + local `deadlines` list wouldn't otherwise go out of scope until function end, but adding 30+ lines of local variables muddles the function. Braces signal "this is the head-deadline sub-task" visually and let me keep named constants (`kHeadBytes`, `kHeadFirstMs`, `kHeadLastMs`) close to their use site.

### Not in this batch

- **Sliding-window retargeting on playback progress.** Batch 2.3 adds a slot on VideoPlayer's `progressUpdated` signal that extends deadlines into the ~20 MB reader-window ahead of the current playback position, rate-limited to once per 2s.
- **Seek / resume pre-gate.** Batch 2.4 fires a burst of 200-500ms deadlines on the new target window before handing to the player.
- **Deadline cleanup on stream stop.** Deadlines auto-expire as pieces land and clearPieceDeadlines is cheap — will add a call in `stopStream()` when 2.3 lands, since 2.3 is the first batch that also needs symmetry (sliding window must stop tracking too).
- **Changes to `sequential_download`.** Deliberately untouched.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — one new scoped block inside `onMetadataReady`, plus the rationale comment.

No header change, no CMake change, no other file touched.

### Re-verification

Functional smoke (need a test magnet):
1. Start a well-seeded torrent stream. Measure click-to-play latency (stopwatch from source click to first frame painted).
2. Expected vs Batch 1.3 baseline: noticeably faster — libtorrent prioritizes the first 5 MB over arbitrary rarest-first pieces.
3. Enable libtorrent piece-picker logging (if available) or observe network traces — early requests should concentrate on pieces 0..N-head rather than dispersed across the file.
4. Regression: sequential_download flag still set (check via libtorrent alerts or behavior on the tail of the file — pieces still arrive in order when head priority clears).
5. Regression: non-streaming torrent downloads (Tankorent seeding or plain-download usage paths) unaffected — `setPieceDeadlines` is only called on StreamEngine-managed torrents, not TorrentPage's.

Observability note: the qWarning sites added in Batch 1.2 / 1.3 should show fewer "piece wait timed out" messages on slow seeds, since the head is now actively pulled.

### Known follow-ups (into later batches)

- **Empty headRange handling.** If metadata arrives but the file has <1 piece (micro files), `pieceRangeForFileOffset` returns a zero-sized range and we skip deadlines. Harmless — tiny files finish before streaming matters.
- **File sizes < 5 MB.** `pieceRangeForFileOffset` clamps to file end, so the gradient compresses to the available pieces. First piece still gets 500ms.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 2.2]: Head deadline on stream start. In StreamEngine::onMetadataReady after applyStreamPriorities, call pieceRangeForFileOffset for the first 5 MB of the selected file, build a linear 500ms→5000ms gradient across the piece range, and pass to setPieceDeadlines. Aligns with sidecar probe size (demuxer.cpp:15) so ffmpeg's probe reads land-in-cache rather than waiting. sequential_download flag retained as tiebreaker for non-head pieces. Scope-block with named constants. | files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 2.3 (sliding-window deadline retargeting from progressUpdated) on your green.

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 @Agent 7: audit request (subtitles + aspect)

Two persistent bugs I've shipped fixes for and failed to resolve. My debugging has been speculation-driven and the user is frustrated — correctly. Escalating for an external audit.

REQUEST AUDIT — [Video player: subtitle rendering pipeline + aspect ratio on fullscreen]:

**Scope / questions:**

1. **Subtitle rendering path — why aren't subtitles visually rendering?** From `_player_debug.txt` (14:13:35-14:18:07 trace), main-app sends `set_tracks` with valid `sub_id`, sidecar acks with `tracks_changed`, main-app sends `set_sub_visibility:true`, sidecar emits `sub_visibility_changed:true`. Commands flow correctly end-to-end. User confirms the Subtitles submenu populates with track labels. But no subtitles render on screen.

   Specifically:
   - Is the sidecar's `subtitle_text` event ever being emitted for this file? (Check `SubtitleRenderer` at `native_sidecar/src/subtitle_renderer.cpp` — does `render_blend` / text emission actually fire after `load_embedded_track` + `preload_subtitle_packets`?)
   - Is the `set_active_sub_stream(sub_stream_idx)` call in `video_decoder.cpp` routing packets correctly to `sub_renderer_->process_packet`?
   - Is the main-app's `SubtitleOverlay` QLabel actually getting `setText()` called with non-empty strings? (VideoPlayer.cpp:234-236 is the wire.)
   - Is the overlay widget's Z-order / geometry / visibility correct on fullscreen? Could it be rendering under FrameCanvas?
   - Is the `m_tracksRestored` latch (VideoPlayer.cpp, Batch 5-or-thereabouts fix) actually preventing `restoreTrackPreferences` from overriding the user's manual track pick? My trace of `_player_debug.txt:14:18:05-06` showed `set_tracks sub_id:"3"` immediately followed by `set_tracks audio_id:"1", sub_id:"2"`. I shipped a latch; user says still broken. Either the latch is wrong OR there's a second override path I'm missing.

   Reference the shipped fix chain: my Phase 5 batch 5.1 (chat.md earlier today) + VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batch 1.2 (subtitle Off path unification) + the recent `m_tracksRestored` latch + the `sendSetSubVisibility(true)` re-enable in `SidecarProcess::sendSetSubtitleTrack` and in `TrackPopover::subtitleTrackSelected` handler.

2. **Aspect ratio on fullscreen — source 16:9 doesn't render 16:9 by default.** User reports: "video never opens in its original aspect ratios when I turn on fullscreen. 16:9 videos need to be 16:9, I shouldn't have to manually change the aspect ratio every time I open a video." I shipped a reset (`m_forcedAspect = 0.0` on every openFile) and a checkable radio-group in the Aspect Ratio submenu. User says persists.

   Specifically:
   - What does `FrameCanvas::drawTexturedQuad` viewport math actually produce on fullscreen transition for a 1920x1080 source on a 1920x1080 monitor? (FrameCanvas.cpp:625-660.)
   - Is `m_frameW` / `m_frameH` being set to 1920/1080 before fullscreen transition, or is the widget resize outrunning the first SHM-frame consume so viewport uses the `widgetAspect` fallback at FrameCanvas.cpp:637?
   - Does `MainWindow`'s fullscreen path trigger a FrameCanvas reinitialization (D3D device recreation) that would drop `m_frameW` accounting mid-transition?
   - Is there a per-monitor DPR discrepancy that makes `widgetAspect` different from the expected 1.778 on fullscreen?
   - Is `m_videoTexW`/`m_videoTexH` divergent from `m_frameW`/`m_frameH` in a way that misaligns the blit vs viewport?
   - Is there a different aspect-control path I don't know about (main-app side OR sidecar side — some `set_aspect` command, anamorphic handling, SAR/PAR applied somewhere)?

**References:**

- **mpv** (https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv) — canonical reference for subtitle rendering (`sd_ass.c`, `osd.c`, `sub/sd_lavc.c`) and aspect handling (`video/out/aspect.c`, `video/out/vo.c`). `--keepaspect`, `--video-aspect-override`, `--video-unscaled`.
- **IINA** (https://github.com/iina/iina) — Qt-adjacent (AppKit-based), but their subtitle overlay management + aspect ratio handling at `MPVController.swift` + `MainWindowController.swift:354-400` area is comparable.
- **QMPlay2** (https://github.com/zaps166/QMPlay2) — Qt-native C++ reference. `src/qmplay2/VideoFrame.*`, `src/qmplay2/OSD.*`. Closest architectural match to Tankoban.
- **libass** (https://github.com/libass/libass) docs — `ass_render_frame`, event visibility semantics, `ASS_Renderer` state requirements.

Web search: authorized. Particularly useful: "libass render_blend silent when track not preloaded", "Qt player widget aspect ratio reset on fullscreen", "D3D11 FLIP_DISCARD swap chain aspect post-resize", "mpv subtitle rendering pipeline debugging".

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 4 Batches 4.2 + 4.3 shipped — Phase 4 CLOSED

Hemanth granted one-time override on Agent 5's primary territory for this specific phase, citing avoidance of the HELP ping-pong overhead given the small scope. Override is **phase-bounded** — does not extend past Phase 4. HELP.md cleared, retraction noted there with a pointer back to this post.

Phase 4 ships in three batches. 4.1 already posted earlier. 4.2 + 4.3 below.

### Batch 4.2 — LibraryScanner CBR/RAR support

**Glob extension** at [LibraryScanner.cpp:43](src/core/LibraryScanner.cpp#L43) — `groupByFirstLevelSubdir` now enumerates `{"*.cbz", "*.cbr", "*.rar"}`. User-imported CBR/RAR files now surface in the scanner output.

**Format-dispatch helper** at [LibraryScanner.cpp:23-28](src/core/LibraryScanner.cpp#L23-L28) — `static bool isLibarchiveFormat(path)` distinguishes CBR/RAR from CBZ. Mirrors `ArchiveReader::isCbrPath` semantics; kept as a separate static helper to avoid pulling more from ArchiveReader's internals than needed.

**`countPagesInCbz` dispatch** at [LibraryScanner.cpp:138-160](src/core/LibraryScanner.cpp#L138-L160) — CBZ keeps the existing fast QZipReader path. CBR/RAR delegates to `ArchiveReader::pageList(path).size()`. Function name kept (intentional — renaming would touch headers + invite scope creep).

**`extractCoverFromCbz` dispatch** at [LibraryScanner.cpp:162-184](src/core/LibraryScanner.cpp#L162-L184) — CBZ keeps existing logic with cover-priority (`cover.*` / `folder.*` basename wins, falls back to first sorted). CBR/RAR branch replicates the same priority logic explicitly via `ArchiveReader::pageList(path)` + `ArchiveReader::pageData(path, name)`. Cover priority works for both formats — no behavioral asymmetry.

**Architectural note:** Batch 4.1's `ArchiveReader::firstImage` helper is NOT consumed here. Reason: the scanner's contract is `QByteArray` (raw bytes flow into `saveThumbnail` which does its own QImage decode + scale + crop), so calling `firstImage` would require encoding QImage back to QByteArray — wasteful. Direct `pageList`+`pageData` calls give us the byte stream we need + full control over cover priority. `firstImage` stays in ArchiveReader as a generic helper for future callers.

### Batch 4.3 — UI filter extension

**SeriesView constant rename + sites** at [SeriesView.cpp:25-27](src/ui/pages/SeriesView.cpp#L25-L27):
- Renamed `CBZ_EXTS = {"*.cbz"}` → `COMIC_EXTS = {"*.cbz", "*.cbr", "*.rar"}`. Auto-extends 3 use sites at lines 587, 758, 789 (constant-replace).
- Inline override `{"*.cbz"}` at [:901](src/ui/pages/SeriesView.cpp#L901) swapped to `COMIC_EXTS` for consistency.

**ComicsPage constant + sites** at [ComicsPage.cpp:29-31](src/ui/pages/ComicsPage.cpp#L29-L31):
- New file-local `static const QStringList COMIC_EXTS = {"*.cbz", "*.cbr", "*.rar"}` declared once at top.
- Five hardcoded `dir.entryList({"*.cbz"}, ...)` sites at lines 212, 447, 693, 731, 837 all swapped to use `COMIC_EXTS`.

Local variable name `cbzFiles` at line 731 left as-is — misnomer accepted to keep batch tight.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke — full Phase 4)

**Setup:** Drop a `.cbr` file (or `.rar` containing comic images) into a tracked comics root folder. Restart app or trigger a rescan.

1. **Scanner discovery:** ComicsPage shows a tile for the new series with cover thumbnail.
2. **Page count badge:** Tile shows correct page count.
3. **SeriesView entry:** Click into series — file row appears for the .cbr file with page count.
4. **Open in reader:** Double-click row → ComicReader opens normally, all pages decode.
5. **Continue strip:** Bookmark a CBR mid-read, return to ComicsPage → continue tile shows correct cover, click → opens at saved position.
6. **Mixed library:** Folder containing both .cbz and .cbr files → both enumerate side-by-side in SeriesView.
7. **Cover priority on CBR:** A `.cbr` containing a `cover.jpg` — that image is used as the cover thumbnail.
8. **CBZ regression:** Existing CBZ-only series still discover, scan, open, resume, bookmark, scrub — all unchanged.
9. **Edge case — corrupt CBR:** Drop a malformed `.cbr` → tile gets placeholder cover, no crash.

### Phase 4 exit

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.3 shipped

Sliding-window deadline retargeting. Reader frontier now pulls ahead of the playback-frontier as the user watches, closing the mid-playback piece-starvation case that the head-only deadline (Batch 2.2) didn't cover.

### What shipped

**[StreamEngine.h](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h)** — two new public methods:
- `updatePlaybackWindow(infoHash, positionSec, durationSec, windowBytes = 20MB)` — byte-offset math + piece-range lookup + deadline gradient, all inside the engine (keeps piece-math out of the UI).
- `clearPlaybackWindow(infoHash)` — pairs with updatePlaybackWindow for cleanup sites.

**[StreamEngine.cpp — updatePlaybackWindow](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp)** — looks up the stream record (selectedFileIndex + selectedFileSize); converts `positionSec/durationSec` to a byte fraction × file size; clamps window to file tail; calls Batch 2.1's `pieceRangeForFileOffset` + `setPieceDeadlines` with a 1000ms→8000ms gradient. Past-EOF case clears any lingering deadlines so the next episode's head deadline (from onMetadataReady) isn't pre-empted by stale late-file deadlines.

**[StreamEngine.cpp — stopStream](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp)** — added one-line `m_torrentEngine->clearPieceDeadlines(infoHash)` before the HTTP unregister. The engine is the right home for this cleanup (every stream termination path routes through stopStream — no need to sprinkle clears across StreamPage's 3 teardown sites).

**[StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h)** — new `qint64 m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs` member for the 2s rate-limiter.

**[StreamPage.cpp — progressUpdated lambda](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — one new block inside the existing lambda (doesn't change the existing save-progress / near-end detection behavior):

```cpp
const qint64 nowMs = QDateTime::currentMSecsSinceEpoch();
if (nowMs - m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs >= 2000) {
    m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs = nowMs;
    const QString infoHash = m_playerController->currentInfoHash();
    if (!infoHash.isEmpty() && m_streamEngine) {
        m_streamEngine->updatePlaybackWindow(infoHash, posSec, durSec);
    }
}
```

Reuses the already-wired `progressUpdated` connection at [StreamPage.cpp:1182](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1182) — no new signal/slot. `StreamPlayerController::currentInfoHash()` already exists at [StreamPlayerController.h:33](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.h#L33).

### Design choices

- **Engine-side math.** `updatePlaybackWindow` takes `positionSec/durationSec` (UI semantics the caller already has) instead of raw byte offsets. Piece-range computation stays on StreamEngine with access to stream state. UI stays math-free.
- **2s rate limit** — Stremio-class cadence. progressUpdated fires ~1Hz; without a gate we'd churn libtorrent's deadline table 60× per minute. 2s batches the churn without letting the reader frontier drift more than a few MB of video at typical bitrates.
- **20 MB window** — configurable via the default parameter. Covers ~30-60s of video at common bitrates (5-15 Mbps), well ahead of the reader for normal networks.
- **1000ms → 8000ms gradient** — more urgent than the head (500-5000ms) would be inappropriate mid-playback (we already have some head); less urgent than 8s would risk libtorrent dropping stale deadlines. The 8s tail matches libtorrent's default piece-request lifetime.
- **No clear on each update** — calling `setPieceDeadlines` with the same pieces + new ms values is an in-place update on libtorrent's side. Clearing first would momentarily drop piece urgency and cause stalls if a peer request was in flight. Idempotent update is safer.
- **Clear on stopStream** — the engine is the chokepoint for all stream terminations (user close, source switch, auto-advance, error). Cleanup sits there instead of spread across 3 StreamPage sites.
- **Past-EOF clear** — handles the edge case where VideoPlayer's progressUpdated fires with a position > duration (can happen at decoder close / during resync). Clearing prevents stuck deadlines on the tail of a file nobody's watching anymore.

### Why this closes the remaining buffering gap

Batch 2.2 solved cold start (first 5 MB has aggressive deadlines). But mid-playback, without 2.3, libtorrent reverted to `sequential_download`'s soft preference — which the audit showed is "sub-optimal for streaming." On a torrent with skewed peer availability (some peers fast, some slow), early pieces would be assigned to slow peers while the player approached them, causing mid-stream stalls even on healthy networks.

With 2.3 live: reader-frontier has fresh 1-8s deadlines on the next ~20 MB at all times during playback. libtorrent's scheduler preferentially assigns those pieces to the fastest-responding peers. Mid-stream stalls should only occur on genuine bandwidth shortfall, not scheduler inefficiency.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h) — 2 new public method decls + inline docs
- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — `updatePlaybackWindow` + `clearPlaybackWindow` impls; one-line clear on `stopStream`
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h) — 1 rate-limiter member
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — ~10-line block inside existing progressUpdated lambda

No new signals, no new slots, no CMake change, no other file touched.

### Re-verification

1. **Long continuous playback (10+ min).** Expected: no mid-stream stalls on a bandwidth-adequate network. Download frontier visibly leads playback frontier by ~20 MB throughout.
2. **Mid-seed fluctuation.** Start a stream, let it play for 2 min, manually throttle the network (DevTools-style or via OS-level tool) for 10s, then release. Expected: buffering overlay appears during throttle (sidecar HTTP retry from Batch 1.2), recovers within a few seconds of throttle release, deadlines still steering peer selection.
3. **Source switch mid-play** (Pick different source during playback). Expected: previous stream's deadlines cleared on stopStream (new log line visible if I add debug logging); new stream gets fresh head + sliding deadlines; no cross-contamination.
4. **Regression: near-end detection.** The 95%/60s crossing + next-episode prefetch still fires — I added the deadline code alongside the existing near-end block, didn't replace it.
5. **Regression: save-progress.** saveProgress every tick still happens — the rate-limit gates only the deadline update, not the state write.
6. **Regression: Shift+N manual next episode (Batch 2.6 of STREAM_UX_PARITY).** Tests that the rate-limiter state doesn't straddle two playbacks incorrectly. Since stopStream → clearPieceDeadlines happens between episodes, the new episode starts with a fresh deadline table; the rate-limit gate may skip the new episode's first progress tick but the head deadline from Batch 2.2 covers it.

### Known follow-ups

- **Resume target pre-gate** (Batch 2.4) — when `startPositionSec > 0`, fire a burst of 200-500ms deadlines on the new target window and block the player launch until contiguous. Closes the last resume/seek reliability gap.
- **Phase 3** — startup gate alignment, buffering % fix, session-settings tune.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 2.3]: Sliding-window deadline retargeting. StreamEngine::updatePlaybackWindow(infoHash, posSec, durSec, 20MB) converts position to byte offset, computes piece range, builds 1000ms→8000ms gradient, calls setPieceDeadlines. Rate-limited to once per 2s from StreamPage's progressUpdated lambda via m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs. clearPlaybackWindow companion + one-line clear on StreamEngine::stopStream as the single cleanup chokepoint for all teardown paths. | files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h, src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 2.4 (seek/resume target pre-gate + warm-sidecar startPositionSec side-carry) closes Phase 2 on your green.

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-15 @Agent 7: audit request (REVISED — tighter scope)

Superseding my earlier request with a scoped version per Agent 4's template.

REQUEST AUDIT — [video_player_subs_aspect]: Two user-reported bugs in the video player subsystem persist through five shipped fix attempts. (1) Subtitle tracks visible in context menu populate correctly, `set_tracks` + `set_sub_visibility` commands flow end-to-end (verified in `_player_debug.txt`), but no subtitles render on screen. (2) Fullscreen transition does not preserve source aspect ratio — user has to manually pick "16:9" from the Aspect Ratio submenu every file open. Compare our subtitle rendering pipeline (main-app SubtitleOverlay + sidecar SubtitleRenderer + libass) and aspect-math on fullscreen (`FrameCanvas::drawTexturedQuad` viewport) against mpv (`sub/sd_ass.c`, `video/out/aspect.c`), IINA (`MPVController.swift`, `MainWindowController.swift`), QMPlay2 (`src/qmplay2/OSD.*`, `src/qmplay2/VideoFrame.*`), and libass documentation. Focus on: why libass events aren't reaching a visible QLabel, whether `render_blend` / text-mode emission is silent on our sidecar pipeline, whether `m_frameW` / `m_frameH` are set before fullscreen transition lands, and whether Qt widget resize races the first SHM-frame consume. | References: mpv (https://github.com/mpv-player/mpv), IINA (https://github.com/iina/iina), QMPlay2 (https://github.com/zaps166/QMPlay2), libass (https://github.com/libass/libass). Web search: authorized.

---

### Context for Agent 7 (background, not part of the trigger line)

**Our architecture — read these files in `src/ui/player/`:**

- `VideoPlayer.h/.cpp` — orchestrator. Key sites:
  - `onTracksChanged` (around line 1363) — updates `m_subTracks`, calls `restoreTrackPreferences` gated by new `m_tracksRestored` latch.
  - `restoreTrackPreferences` (around line 1868) — preferred-lang matcher that was overriding user picks; now latched via `m_tracksRestored` (reset on openFile + crash respawn).
  - Context-menu `SetSubtitleTrack` case (around line 2514) — unified Off via `setSubtitleOff()`; real-track branch flips `m_subsVisible=true` + `sendSetSubVisibility(true)` before `sendSetTracks`.
  - TrackPopover subtitle path (around line 969) — same pattern as context menu.
  - Subtitle overlay wire at line 234-236: `m_sidecar->subtitleText → m_subOverlay->setText(text)`.
  - `m_subsVisible` coherence in `subVisibilityChanged` handler (line 237-248) — now mirrors sidecar state.

- `SidecarProcess.h/.cpp` — command + event dispatch. Key sites:
  - `sendSetSubtitleTrack(int)` (around line 516) — Off path is visibility-only (no set_tracks); real-track path calls `sendSetSubVisibility(true)` then `sendSetTracks`.
  - `processLine` event dispatcher (around line 345-410) — maps sidecar events to Qt signals.
  - `subtitle_text` event handling at `subtitleText` signal emission.

- `SubtitleOverlay.h/.cpp` — QLabel overlay widget. `setText`, `setStyle`, `setColors`. Geometry / Z-order managed by VideoPlayer's `resizeEvent`.

- `FrameCanvas.h/.cpp` — D3D11 render surface. Key sites:
  - `drawTexturedQuad` viewport math (line 625-660) — computes `frameAspect` from `m_forcedAspect` → `m_frameW/m_frameH` → `widgetAspect` fallback.
  - `consumeShmFrame` (line 1030-1080) — sets `m_frameW`, `m_frameH` from actual decoded dims.
  - `processPendingImport` (line 890-970) — zero-copy D3D11 path; sets `m_frameW` from `m_pendingD3DWidth`.
  - `resizeEvent` (line 305-345) — `ResizeBuffers` on the swap chain.

- `VideoContextMenu.h/.cpp` — Aspect Ratio submenu is now a checkable radio group bound to `data.currentAspect`. Off-vs-track-0 disambiguated to use -1 sentinel for Off.

**Sidecar (in-repo post-migration at `native_sidecar/src/`):**

- `main.cpp` — `handle_set_tracks` (line 820-888): reads `sub_visibility` from payload (default true), calls `g_sub_renderer->set_visible(sub_vis)`, conditional `load_embedded_track` + `preload_subtitle_packets` + `set_active_sub_stream` only when `new_sub_id != g_active_sub_id`. Always emits `sub_visibility_changed`. Track-switch block intentionally skipped when sub id unchanged (fast no-op) — see Bug 1 hypothesis below.
- `video_decoder.cpp` — `set_active_sub_stream` + sub packet routing to `sub_renderer_->process_packet`.
- `subtitle_renderer.cpp/.h` — libass wrapper. `load_embedded_track`, `process_packet`, `render_blend`, visibility gate.
- `demuxer.cpp` — probe source width/height/SAR/frame_rate.

**User-visible symptoms (reproducible):**

1. **Subtitle bug.** Open a file. Right-click → Subtitles submenu shows all embedded tracks (so sidecar is emitting `tracks_changed` with subtitles populated, and main-app cached them). Pick any track — `set_tracks` + `set_sub_visibility` fire per [_player_debug.txt:13:37-14:18](_player_debug.txt). No subtitles visibly render. Default track at file open also doesn't render.

2. **Aspect bug.** Open a 1920x1080 16:9 file. Press F or double-click for fullscreen. Video does NOT render at 16:9 (user describes needing to manually pick "16:9" from the Aspect Ratio submenu to fix it each time). Default `m_forcedAspect = 0.0` should use natural source aspect from `m_frameW / m_frameH`; either those aren't set or the viewport math produces something wrong.

### Specific questions we want answered

**Subtitle rendering (Bug 1):**

1. **Is the sidecar's `subtitle_text` event actually firing for an embedded ASS/SSA/SRT track?** Trace `native_sidecar/src/subtitle_renderer.cpp::render_blend` or the equivalent text-emission path. Does it require `load_embedded_track` + `process_packet` + a packet at the current PTS? Is the `preload_subtitle_packets` call (main.cpp:617 + :865) sufficient to seed libass's `ASS_Track` such that playback-time events produce output?

2. **Does `handle_set_tracks` skip the load-track block when `new_sub_id == g_active_sub_id`?** main.cpp:854 — `if (!new_sub_id.empty() && new_sub_id != g_active_sub_id)`. If user picks the already-active track (e.g., file's first sub is auto-selected + user confirms via menu), the track-switch block (including preload) is skipped. Is that correct behavior, or is it a bug hiding re-activation needs?

3. **Is the main-app `SubtitleOverlay` QLabel actually getting `setText()` called?** VideoPlayer.cpp:234-236 is the wire. If the QLabel never sees non-empty text, the sidecar's `subtitle_text` event isn't arriving OR isn't being parsed correctly.

4. **Does `SubtitleOverlay` render on top of `FrameCanvas`?** FrameCanvas uses `WA_PaintOnScreen + WA_NativeWindow` (separate HWND). A QLabel sibling of FrameCanvas may not render above the native window — Z-order in the Qt hierarchy doesn't translate to the Windows HWND stack. Does SubtitleOverlay need to be a child of FrameCanvas or use `WA_TranslucentBackground`?

5. **References:** mpv's OSD pipeline (`sub/osd.c` + `video/out/vo_*`), how does it blend subtitles onto the video surface? IINA's Swift wrapper — `OverlayViewController.swift` or similar. QMPlay2's `OSD.cpp`. Is the common pattern a GPU-side composition (draw subs onto the same D3D11/Metal/OpenGL surface as the video) rather than a sibling Qt widget layer?

6. **Is my `m_tracksRestored` latch actually stopping the override?** I traced `_player_debug.txt:14:18:05-06` showing `set_tracks sub_id:"3"` followed by `set_tracks audio_id:"1", sub_id:"2"` — the preference-override bug. I latched `restoreTrackPreferences` to fire once per openFile. If the user says subtitles still don't work, either (a) my latch is broken, (b) there's a second override path I missed, or (c) the subtitle pipeline is silent for a reason unrelated to track selection.

**Aspect ratio on fullscreen (Bug 2):**

7. **What does `FrameCanvas::drawTexturedQuad` viewport math produce on fullscreen transition for a 1920x1080 source on a 1920x1080 monitor?** FrameCanvas.cpp:625-660. Expected: `frameAspect = widgetAspect = 1.778`, viewport fills the widget entirely (no letterbox). If `m_frameW`/`m_frameH` are 0 at the moment of transition, `widgetAspect` fallback kicks in — frameAspect matches widget, viewport fills entirely, but the actual rendered IMAGE is stretched/clipped because the shader samples from a BGRA texture whose aspect matches the source. Is that the bug?

8. **Does the fullscreen path race the first SHM-frame consume?** User presses F during playback; MainWindow calls `showFullScreen()`; VideoPlayer resizes; FrameCanvas resizes; next renderFrame tick might run BEFORE the next `consumeShmFrame` updates `m_frameW/m_frameH`. If `m_frameW=0` briefly, viewport uses `widgetAspect` fallback = stretched fill.

9. **Does `MainWindow`'s fullscreen transition trigger a FrameCanvas D3D device recreation?** Device-lost / swap-chain-recreation path zeros `m_frameW` (tearDownD3D clears it). If fullscreen transition crosses a monitor boundary or causes DXGI to reset, the device-recovery path could reset frame dims to 0.

10. **Is there a per-monitor DPR discrepancy that makes `widgetAspect` different from expected 1.778 on fullscreen?** `devicePixelRatioF()` on the widget at fullscreen time — is that stable, or does Qt's DPR calculation race the fullscreen transition?

11. **Is the Aspect Ratio submenu's current-state check mark WORKING and showing "Original" after my fix?** If it is, we've ruled out a stale `m_forcedAspect`. If "Original" is checked but the video still looks wrong, the bug is in `m_frameW/m_frameH` or the viewport math.

12. **References:** mpv's `video/out/aspect.c` — how does it compute display rectangle from source w/h + DAR + SAR + monitor DAR? QMPlay2 `VideoFrame::setAspectRatio` + viewport math. IINA `MainWindowController.swift:354-400` area. Any reference apply per-frame SAR correction vs assuming square pixels?

### Scope

**In scope:** subtitle rendering pipeline end-to-end (main-app overlay widget + sidecar libass + inter-process event wire), aspect-math viewport code, fullscreen transition timing, Z-order semantics with `WA_PaintOnScreen` native widgets, reference comparison across at least 2 of {mpv, IINA, QMPlay2}.

**Out of scope:** audio pipeline, seek/buffering, codec/container choice, UI chrome (toasts, HUD, context menu styling), Stream-mode integration, Phase 7 stats badge, any `src/core/stream/*` code.

### What I've tried that didn't land

- **Phase 5 Batch 5.1** (sidecar preload + main-app unconditional `sendSetSubVisibility` + sidecar tracks_payload visibility emit). Shipped + sidecar rebuilt by Hemanth (per earlier sessions).
- **VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Batch 1.2** (unified Off path; `sendSetSubVisibility(true)` re-enable in three subtitle entry points).
- **`m_tracksRestored` latch** — gate `restoreTrackPreferences` to once per file open; reset on openFile + crash respawn.
- **Off-vs-track-0 sentinel fix** — context menu Off now sends `-1`, not `0`, so a subtitle stream with AVStream index 0 is not indistinguishable from Off.
- **`m_forcedAspect = 0.0` reset on openFile** + checkable Aspect Ratio radio group with current-state indication.

All shipped, both bugs persist. User calls the pattern "going in circles." Agree.

### Output

Standard audit template at `agents/audits/video_player_subs_aspect_2026-04-15.md`. Observations separated from hypotheses. Every gap: file:line (ours) + file:line or URL (reference). Hypothesized root causes labeled `Hypothesis — Agent 3 to validate` and NOT acted on until Agent 3 verifies state.

Live debug log from user: `_player_debug.txt` (33k lines; subtitle trace at timestamps 14:13–14:18 is particularly informative — includes the exact set_tracks → override sequence that motivated the `m_tracksRestored` latch).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.4 shipped

Seek / resume target pre-gate + warm-sidecar side-carry. Phase 2 closes.

### Two independent changes in this batch

**1. Warm-sidecar startPositionSec side-carry** — at [VideoPlayer.cpp:333-339](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L333). Pre-fix, the cold `onSidecarReady` path passed `m_pendingStartSec` to `sendOpen` but the warm path (sidecar already running) called `m_sidecar->sendOpen(filePath)` without the offset argument. Warm-restart resumes landed at 0:00. Agent 7 audit hypothesis #5.

Fix: one-line change — `m_sidecar->sendOpen(filePath, m_pendingStartSec)`. Symmetric with the cold path.

**2. Seek/resume target pre-gate** — the core Batch 2.4 work.

### What pre-gate ships

**[StreamEngine.h/.cpp — `prepareSeekTarget`](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp)** — new method takes `(infoHash, positionSec, durationSec, prefetchBytes = 3 MB)`. Converts `positionSec/durationSec` → byte offset (linear, acceptable for 3 MB window); calls `pieceRangeForFileOffset` for the target window; sets a 200ms→500ms urgent gradient via `setPieceDeadlines`; returns `haveContiguousBytes` for that window as the "ready-to-play" signal. Idempotent — re-calling with the same args updates deadlines in place, so caller retry cadence keeps libtorrent's urgency fresh.

**[StreamPage.cpp — `onReadyToPlay`](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — the player launch is now gated when a resume offset is present:

```cpp
// Extract launch-player code into a lambda (captures httpUrl, resumeSec).
auto launchPlayer = [this, ...]() { player->openFile(...); show; raise; };

if (resumeSec <= 0 || no engine || no infoHash) { launchPlayer(); return; }

// Try-now path.
if (prepareSeekTarget(infoHash, resumeSec, savedDur)) {
    launchPlayer();
    return;
}

// Need to wait. Keep buffer overlay visible with "Seeking to HH:MM:SS..."
// and retry every 300ms for up to 9s (30 iterations). If the window lands,
// launchPlayer. If cap hits, launchPlayer anyway (Batch 1.2 HTTP retry
// handles residual delay).
```

Retry loop uses a small `QObject* retryState` child-of-`this` to hold the iteration count and get Qt-lifetime cleanup. The recursive lambda is a `shared_ptr<std::function<void()>>` pattern — idiomatic for self-referential Qt timer closures. Aborts cleanly if the user navigates away mid-retry (checks `m_playerController->currentInfoHash() == infoHash` each iteration).

### Design choices

- **Engine-side, not sidecar-side.** The audit flagged a hypothetical "block ffmpeg at the decoder" fix (Option B with custom AVIO). We chose Option A in Phase 1 — HTTP + deadlines. Pre-gate is the final piece of that shape: the target window is made ready before ffmpeg is even asked to seek there. No sidecar changes beyond the 1-line side-carry.
- **3 MB target window, not 5 MB.** Seek targets are already late in the file; ffmpeg's probe has already occurred at byte 0 (Batch 2.2 head deadline). 3 MB is enough for a clean seek + several seconds of playback-lookahead while the sliding window (Batch 2.3) picks up on the first progressUpdated tick.
- **200ms → 500ms deadline gradient.** Tighter than sliding-window's 1000-8000ms because the user is actively blocked on these pieces (visible "Seeking..." overlay). Matches the most-urgent class of libtorrent deadlines.
- **9s cap.** Well inside sidecar's own HTTP retry loop (30s total). If 9s of urgent deadlines doesn't produce 3 MB contiguous, the seeder/torrent health is the bottleneck — falling through to the player lets the sidecar's HTTP retry handle it gracefully.
- **No new QTimer member.** Using `QTimer::singleShot(this, lambda)` + shared_ptr recursion avoids adding a persistent member. Retry state lives in a stack-allocated `QObject` parented to `this` (auto-cleaned on StreamPage destruction).
- **Lambda + shared_ptr self-reference.** `shared_ptr<std::function>` is the canonical C++17 pattern for recursive lambdas. Each invocation schedules the next via `QTimer::singleShot` → lambda captures the `shared_ptr` by value → function body can call `(*sp)()` to self-invoke. Reference-count hits 0 when all pending timers fire + the retryState deletes itself.

### User-visible behavior

Before: resume at 47:00 on a newly-opened torrent → 5-30s of buffering cycles, sometimes resume drops back to 0:00 on warm-sidecar (due to the side-carry bug). After: "Seeking to 47:00..." overlay for a second or two while deadlines pull the target pieces → player opens cleanly at the resume offset. Worst case (dead torrent): 9s of overlay, then falls through to the Batch 1.2 HTTP retry path.

### Files

- [src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp) — one-line warm-sidecar side-carry (line 335 → passes `m_pendingStartSec`)
- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h) — `prepareSeekTarget` decl + docs
- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — `prepareSeekTarget` impl
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — `<functional>` + `<memory>` includes; pre-gate path in `onReadyToPlay`

No header changes on StreamPage (retry state is anonymous QObject child). No CMake change. No other file touched.

### Re-verification

1. **Cold resume (app restart then resume).** Start a torrent, watch 10+ min (accumulates saved offset), close and restart app, open the same title. Expected: "Seeking to HH:MM..." overlay briefly, player opens at the saved offset with playback immediate.
2. **Warm resume (resume in same session).** Watch some, close player with Back, click source again. Same expected behavior; the warm-sidecar side-carry ensures the offset actually applies.
3. **Resume on dead torrent.** Force-quit all seeders mid-session, close, restart app, resume. Expected: 9s "Seeking..." then falls through; Batch 1.2 HTTP retry loop keeps trying; user sees buffering overlay; eventually either resumes when pieces arrive or surfaces an error from the sidecar.
4. **User cancels during seek.** Click Back during the 9s window. Expected: retry loop aborts silently (infoHash mismatch check); player never opens at the stale offset; user lands on browse layer cleanly.
5. **Zero-resume path.** Play a fresh title from 0:00. Expected: no pre-gate runs — streamResumeSec is 0, launchPlayer fires immediately, Batch 2.2 head deadlines cover it.
6. **Regression: Shift+N next-episode.** Same-source next episode with no saved resume → zero-resume path → immediate launch. Should match existing Batch 2.6 (STREAM_UX_PARITY) behavior.
7. **Regression: new session starts.** `m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs` from Batch 2.3 still sliding-windows correctly post-launch.

### Phase 2 exit

All four batches shipped: 2.1 primitives, 2.2 head deadline, 2.3 sliding window, 2.4 seek pre-gate + side-carry. Phase 2 closes the libtorrent streaming model. Combined with Phase 1 (HTTP hardening), this is a full Peerflix/Webtor-class torrent playback stack.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 2.4]: Seek/resume pre-gate + warm-sidecar startPositionSec side-carry. StreamEngine::prepareSeekTarget(infoHash, posSec, durSec, 3MB) sets 200ms→500ms urgent deadlines on the target window and returns haveContiguousBytes; StreamPage gates player launch on it with 300ms poll / 9s cap / "Seeking to HH:MM:SS..." overlay; abort-on-navigate via infoHash mismatch. Side-carry: VideoPlayer warm-sidecar sendOpen now passes m_pendingStartSec (cold path already did). | files: src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp, src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h, src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 2]: Piece-priority streaming model. TorrentEngine piece-deadline primitives (2.1) + head deadline on stream start (2.2) + sliding-window retargeting from progressUpdated (2.3) + seek/resume target pre-gate (2.4). Closes audit P0 #2 (sequential_download insufficient) and P1 #3 (resume pre-gate). | Objective: STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md Phase 2 Batches 2.1-2.4. Files: src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h/.cpp, src/core/stream/StreamEngine.h/.cpp, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h/.cpp, src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp.

Standing by on smoke. Phase 3 (startup gate + UX alignment) opens with Batch 3.1 on your green.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.1 shipped

Phase 5 opens — consolidated Reader Settings panel (audit P1-7 + TKMAX-2). Plan file Phase 5 section in `C:\Users\Suprabha\.claude\plans\nested-beaming-emerson.md`.

**Honest framing acknowledged in plan:** Phase 5 is the project's biggest pure-UX-shuffle phase. Adds zero new capabilities — relocates existing controls into a Mihon-style categorized panel. Justification: as later phases add controls, the right-click menu would balloon further. Doing the panel now means future capabilities dock into it cleanly. Hemanth ratified four-incremental-batches shape + keep-both-surfaces dedup posture this session.

### What shipped — Batch 5.1: Overlay shell + Display section

**Lazy-built modal overlay** at [ComicReader.cpp showSettingsPanel](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2356) — `ClickScrim` + centered 480px-wide `QFrame` card mirroring the volume navigator pattern. Same dismissal model users already know from `O` (volumes) and `K` (shortcuts) — escape closes, click-outside closes. Lazy build on first open (same pattern as `toggleKeysOverlay`).

**Card layout:** "Reader Settings" title → "DISPLAY" section header (10px uppercase letter-spaced gray label) → three label-row controls + bottom Reset button. Card style mirrors volume nav's `rgba(18,18,18,0.95)` dark gray with 12px border-radius.

**Display section controls** — three QComboBox rows:
- **Reading Mode** — items "Single Page" / "Double Page" / "Scroll Strip" with `ReaderMode` enum int as userData. On user pick: cycle `cycleReaderMode()` until current matches target (3-mode enum, max 2 cycles, safety counter caps at 4). Reuses existing mode-cycle logic with all its scroll-position/HUD/strip-canvas plumbing — no parallel mode-switch code path to maintain.
- **Portrait Width** — items from `PORTRAIT_PRESETS` ({50, 60, 70, 74, 78, 90, 100}%). On user pick: `setPortraitWidthPct(v)` + `saveSeriesSettings()`. Reuses existing setter.
- **Fit Mode** — items "Fit Page" / "Fit Width" / "Fit Height". On user pick: assigns `m_fitMode`, invalidates display cache, calls `displayCurrentPage()` + `saveSeriesSettings()`.

**Reset Series Settings button** — bottom of card, calls existing `resetSeriesSettings()` then re-shows the panel to re-populate combos with reset values (so the user sees the immediate result).

**Open mechanisms (live in Batch 5.1):**
- `Ctrl+,` keyboard shortcut in `keyPressEvent`. Convention across IDE/browser apps. `,` and `Ctrl+,` both verified unbound elsewhere in the keymap.
- "Settings..." entry at the **top** of the right-click context menu — opens the panel. Followed by `addSeparator` so it's visually distinct from existing items.
- Toolbar gear-button entry deferred to Batch 5.4.

**Combo population on show** — `showSettingsPanel` populates each combo from current state via `findData()` + `setCurrentIndex()` wrapped in `blockSignals(true/false)` so programmatic populate doesn't fire the `activated` handlers (which are user-action-only).

**Overlay lifecycle wired into existing infrastructure:**
- `closeAllOverlays()` extended — settings overlay dismisses alongside volume nav, K overlay, goto, end-of-volume.
- `resizeEvent` extended — settings overlay tracks viewport size like volume nav.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — forward decls (`QComboBox`, `QFrame`), 5 new private members, 2 new method decls. Volume-navigator-adjacent placement.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — `<QComboBox>` + `<QFrame>` includes; `showSettingsPanel`/`hideSettingsPanel` impl (~150 lines after `hideVolumeNavigator`); `closeAllOverlays` + `resizeEvent` + `keyPressEvent` extensions; "Settings..." top-of-menu entry in `contextMenuEvent`.

No CMakeLists, no shared files, no cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **Lazy build, eager populate.** Widget construction happens once on first show (memory not paid for users who never open the panel). Combo selections re-populate on every show (so opening the panel after a right-click change reflects the change).
- **Cycle-N-times for mode change.** Refactoring `cycleReaderMode` to extract a `setReaderMode(target)` would be a bigger surface change for marginal benefit. Cycle-N-times handles 3-mode enum with at most 2 iterations.
- **Right-click menu unchanged.** Settings panel becomes additional surface, not replacement. Right-click entries for Image Quality, Memory Saver, Crop Borders, Spread/Single override, Gutter Shadow submenu, Image Filters → Brightness, Side Padding, Split Wide Pages, Bookmarks, Go to Page, Copy/Reveal — all stay. Zero regression risk.
- **Activated vs currentIndexChanged.** Used `activated` so programmatic `setCurrentIndex` during populate doesn't trigger the change handler. `blockSignals` is belt-and-suspenders for clarity.
- **Reset button re-shows panel.** After reset, combos need to reflect new default values. Re-calling `showSettingsPanel` re-populates them in place. Slight visual blink but functionally correct.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. **Open via Ctrl+,:** Press `Ctrl+,` in any reader mode → overlay appears with dimmed background, centered card titled "Reader Settings", DISPLAY section visible with three combos showing current values.
2. **Open via right-click:** Right-click in reader → "Settings..." at top of menu → click → same panel appears.
3. **Reading Mode:** Currently in Single → pick "Double Page" from combo → reader mode switches immediately. Pick "Scroll Strip" → mode switches. Pick "Single Page" → back to Single. (All three mode transitions exercise the cycle-to-target logic.)
4. **Portrait Width:** Pick "60%" → portrait pages shrink to 60% width. Pick "100%" → expand.
5. **Fit Mode:** Pick "Fit Width" → page redraws fit-to-width. Pick "Fit Height" → fit-to-height.
6. **Re-open reflects current state:** After making changes via the panel, close (Esc), reopen (Ctrl+,) → combos show the new values.
7. **Right-click round-trip:** Change Image Quality via right-click → reopen panel → Display values still correct (panel doesn't have Image Quality yet — that's Batch 5.2 — but no regression on existing controls).
8. **Reset Series Settings:** Click button → all settings revert to defaults; panel re-populates instantly to show the defaults.
9. **Dismissal:** Escape closes. Clicking outside the card (on the dimmed scrim) closes. Reopening after close shows fresh state.
10. **Regression:** All other overlays (`O` volume nav, `K` keys, `Ctrl+G` goto, end-of-volume) still open + dismiss correctly. Ctrl+0 still resets. M/F/I/P keys still cycle their respective settings. Right-click menu still has all existing items.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, READY TO COMMIT line posts per Rule 11. Phase 5 continues with Batch 5.2 (Image section: Brightness combo, Crop Borders toggle, Image Quality combo, Memory Saver toggle).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.1 shipped

Startup gate removed. Click-to-play latency now bounded by head-deadline cycle, not by a 2 MB-plus-network-latency tax.

### What shipped

**[StreamEngine.cpp — streamFile() magnet path](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp)** — the `MIN_HEADER_BYTES = 2 MB` gate + the `if (!haveContiguousBytes(..., 2MB)) return FILE_NOT_READY` block removed. After the `rec.registered` check (which confirms HTTP server registration), `streamFile()` returns `readyToStart=true` with the HTTP URL immediately.

Informational fields (`fileProgress`, `downloadedBytes`, `fileSize`) still populated — Batch 3.2 will switch the UI to the Batch 2.1 `contiguousBytesFromOffset` source; for now both sources coexist.

### Decision: full remove vs 512 KB floor

Plan offered both options. Chose full removal. Rationale:

1. **Double-gate.** After Phase 1 Batch 1.2 fixed `waitForPieces`' ignored return, the HTTP server itself gates every read on piece availability. The StreamEngine byte-gate was adding latency without additional safety — ffmpeg physically couldn't read past the downloaded frontier regardless.

2. **Smaller than probe.** The sidecar's probe reads 5 MB on local paths and up to 20 MB on HTTP (see [video_decoder.cpp:166-180](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L166)). 2 MB was never enough to cover the probe even when the gate was respected — the probe inevitably entered `waitForPieces` territory on the first read. Gating at 2 MB was arbitrary theater.

3. **Better safety now exists.** Phase 1 (HTTP piece-wait + idle watchdog) + Phase 2 (head deadline + sliding window + seek pre-gate) are the correct architectural safeguards. Adding a third layer at streamFile is belt-and-suspenders without measurable benefit.

4. **A 512 KB floor buys little.** piece_length on typical movie torrents is 1-4 MB, so "512 KB contiguous" usually requires the full first piece anyway. Floor adds 500-1500ms of latency for redundancy.

### User-visible effect

Before Batch 3.1: Click source → 30-90s of "Buffering 5%... 8%... 12%..." → play starts when first 2 MB contiguous.

After Batch 3.1: Click source → metadata arrives (~1-3s) → head deadline fires (500ms on piece 0) → HTTP URL returned → ffmpeg probes → probe reads block on HTTP piece-wait (which is short because deadline landed piece 0 immediately) → play starts in ~2-4s on well-seeded torrents.

The `qWarning` sites from Batch 1.2 still fire if slow-seed conditions push the probe into timeout territory. Sidecar's HTTP retry path handles those. Worst case: same degradation pattern as before, but far-and-away faster on the common case.

### Regression surface

- **StreamPlayerController polling.** The controller polls streamFile until `ok=true`. With the gate removed, `ok=true` lands ~immediately after metadata arrival. The buffer-UI "Buffering X%" messages shown mid-poll become uncommon; when they do appear (metadata still fanning out), they're measured in single seconds. Batch 3.2 replaces the source of that percentage anyway.
- **Callers inspecting `result.errorCode == "FILE_NOT_READY"`.** The code path no longer emits that errorCode in the magnet path (direct-URL path can still fail other ways). Any caller that branched specifically on `FILE_NOT_READY` for the magnet path becomes dead logic — grep found none.
- **The informational `downloaded / totalSize` fields.** Still populated for any caller that wants them, but now reflect total-file progress not gate-progress (since there's no gate). Batch 3.2 re-points the caller to `contiguousBytesFromOffset(0, ...) / gateSize` for the buffering %.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — one block removed, long rationale comment added.

No header change, no CMake change, no other file touched.

### Re-verification

1. **Cold start, well-seeded torrent.** Click source → measure click-to-first-frame. Expected: significantly faster than pre-Phase-1 (was 30-90s, should be 2-10s). No "Buffering X%" stalling at 15-20%.
2. **Cold start, slow-seed torrent.** Click source → expected: sidecar buffering overlay (from its own HTTP retry path) appears within 30s while pieces catch up; playback starts when head window is served. User sees less "false progress" (no whole-file percentage counting up while real progress is stuck).
3. **Regression: movie vs series.** Movies and series episodes both route through the same magnet path — behavior should be identical, measurable improvement on both.
4. **Regression: direct-URL streams (non-torrent).** Unaffected — different code path (`streamFile(const Stream&)` overload), doesn't touch the removed gate.
5. **Regression: resume target.** Batch 2.4's pre-gate still runs; streamFile-level gate removal doesn't interact with per-seek deadlines.
6. **Logging:** if slow-seed conditions arise, the Batch 1.2 `qWarning: StreamHttpServer: piece wait timed out` should fire instead of the formerly-silent "FILE_NOT_READY" poll loop. Observability improved.

### Batch 3.2 handoff

Next batch switches `StreamEngine`'s `fileProgress` computation to `m_torrentEngine->contiguousBytesFromOffset(..., 0) / min(2MB, fileSize)` — so if a caller still shows a buffering %, it reaches 100% exactly when the head is contiguous (right when playback becomes reliable). That percentage is now ambient, not gating.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 3.1]: Startup gate removed. StreamEngine::streamFile's 2 MB contiguous-head gate + FILE_NOT_READY poll path deleted; returns readyToStart=true with HTTP URL immediately after registration. Phase 1 HTTP piece-wait + Phase 2 head/sliding deadlines now provide the only safety — the gate was a double-check that was smaller than ffmpeg's own probe size, so it added latency without improving safety. flushCache retained. fileProgress/downloadedBytes still populated for caller UX until Batch 3.2 re-sources them. | files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 3.2 (buffering % shows gate progress via contiguousBytesFromOffset) on your green.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.2 shipped

Image section added to the Reader Settings panel — four all-mode visual controls.

### What shipped

**IMAGE section** in the panel card, between DISPLAY and the Reset button. New header label "IMAGE" matching the DISPLAY style. Four controls via the existing `makeRow` helper:

- **Brightness** — `QComboBox` with `BRIGHTNESS_PRESETS` values (-50 / -25 / Off / +25 / +50). On user pick: same logic as the right-click submenu — assigns `m_filterBrightness`, invalidates display cache, pushes to `m_stripCanvas->setFilterBrightness`, calls `refreshVisibleStripPages` if in scroll-strip mode, `displayCurrentPage()` + `saveSeriesSettings()`.
- **Crop Borders** — `QCheckBox` with no-op guard (`if (checked == m_cropBorders) return`) so programmatic populate doesn't trigger re-decode. On user toggle: full re-decode pipeline mirrored from the right-click action — wipe `m_cache`, null `m_currentPixmap` + `m_secondPixmap`, reset `m_pageMeta[i].decoded = false`, invalidate strip canvas, re-request decode for visible/current page, save, toast.
- **Image Quality** — `QComboBox` {Smooth, Fast} bound to `m_scalingQuality`. Pushes to strip canvas + invalidates its scaled cache, calls `displayCurrentPage()` + `saveSeriesSettings()`. Mirrors the existing right-click Image Quality submenu.
- **Memory Saver** — `QCheckBox`. On toggle: flips `m_memorySaver`, sets `m_cache.setBudget(256MB or 512MB)`, writes the global QSettings `memorySaver` key, toast. Same logic as `toggleMemorySaver` minus the implicit flip (the checkbox click already drives the boolean).

**Custom QCheckBox styling** — added `checkStyle` local stylesheet inside `showSettingsPanel` for both checkboxes: 16×16 indicator, white-on-dark when checked, gray border when unchecked. Matches the dark Tankoban aesthetic; no colored states per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`.

**Show-time population extended** — `blockSignals(true/false)` block around `setCurrentIndex` / `setChecked` calls for all 7 controls (3 from 5.1 + 4 from this batch). Block prevents programmatic populate from firing user-action handlers.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — `<QCheckBox>` forward decl + 4 new private members (2 combos, 2 checkboxes).
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — `<QCheckBox>` include; ~70 lines added inside `showSettingsPanel` (IMAGE header + 4 control constructions + signal wiring); populate-on-show block extended.

No other files. No CMakeLists. No cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **No-op guard on checkboxes.** `if (checked == m_cropBorders) return` and `if (checked == m_memorySaver) return` short-circuit programmatic state-sync from triggering the heavy side-effects (cache wipe, toast spam). `blockSignals` is the primary safeguard but the in-handler check is belt-and-suspenders.
- **Crop Borders re-decode logic duplicated inline.** Same ~10-line block lives in both the right-click handler and the panel checkbox handler. Could extract to `applyCropBordersToggle()` private method, but two callers doesn't yet justify the surface change. Refactor candidate if a third caller appears (e.g. drag-drop import scenarios).
- **Memory Saver checkbox calls partial logic.** `toggleMemorySaver` does state flip + budget update + persist + toast in one call. The checkbox handler bypasses the flip (checkbox already drove the boolean), so it duplicates the budget/persist/toast code rather than calling the method. Tiny duplication; cleaner than calling `toggleMemorySaver()` and relying on the boolean already being correct.
- **All controls round-trip with right-click.** Change brightness via right-click submenu → reopen panel → combo shows new value. Toggle Crop Borders via right-click → reopen panel → checkbox checked. State is always sourced from `m_*` members, never from widget state.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. Press `Ctrl+,` → panel opens. Scroll down past DISPLAY → IMAGE section visible with Brightness combo, Crop Borders checkbox, Image Quality combo, Memory Saver checkbox. Current values populated correctly.
2. **Brightness:** Pick "+25" → page brightens. Pick "Off" → restored. Switch to ScrollStrip mode (via panel Reading Mode), set brightness +50 → all visible strip pages brightened, scroll to reveal new pages → those also brightened.
3. **Crop Borders:** Toggle on → toast appears, current page re-decodes with borders trimmed. Toggle off → restored.
4. **Image Quality:** Pick "Fast" → image quality changes (subtle on most pages, more visible during zoom). Pick "Smooth" → restored.
5. **Memory Saver:** Toggle on → toast "Memory Saver On". Toggle off → toast "Memory Saver Off". (No visible page change but cache budget halved/restored.)
6. **Round-trip with right-click:** Change Brightness via right-click submenu → close right-click → open panel via `Ctrl+,` → Brightness combo reflects the new value. Same for Crop Borders, Image Quality, Memory Saver.
7. **Round-trip the other way:** Change via panel → close panel → open right-click menu → existing menu items reflect updated state (e.g. Image Quality submenu shows checkmark on the active option, Crop Borders entry shows "On *" / "Off").
8. **Reset Series Settings:** Click → all settings revert to defaults; panel re-populates instantly to show defaults across both DISPLAY and IMAGE sections.
9. **Regression:** All Batch 5.1 controls still work; all right-click menu entries unchanged; volume nav (`O`), keys overlay (`K`), goto (`Ctrl+G`), Ctrl+0 reset all still work.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, READY TO COMMIT line posts per Rule 11. Phase 5 continues with Batch 5.3 (mode-specific sections — DoublePage RTL+Gutter+Spread Override, ScrollStrip Side Padding+Split Wide, conditionally visible based on active mode).

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.2 shipped

Buffering % now tracks gate progress, not whole-file progress. Closes the Agent 7 audit P1 #1 finding — "the displayed counter can plausibly reach 15-20% before the head gate completes."

### What shipped

**[StreamEngine.cpp — streamFile() magnet path](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp)** — the informational-fields block changed:

Before: `fileProgress = downloaded / totalSize` — whole-file progress. `downloadedBytes = totalDownloaded`.

After: `fileProgress = min(1.0, contiguousHead / 5MB)` — gate-progress fraction. `downloadedBytes = min(contiguousHead, 5MB)` — contiguous-head bytes clamped to the gate target.

Uses `TorrentEngine::contiguousBytesFromOffset(infoHash, fileIdx, 0)` (the Batch 2.1 primitive) as the source. 5 MB target matches the sidecar's local-path probe size at [native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp:15](native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp#L15). `kGateBytes` named constant clamps to `min(5 MB, fileSize)` for micro-files so % still reaches 100 even on tiny torrents.

**[StreamPlayerController.cpp — pollStreamStatus](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp)** — no code change in the status-text branch (line 130-132 still reads `fileProgress * 100` + `downloadedBytes / 1MB`), but added a comment documenting the semantic shift so future devs don't misread "Buffering X% (Y MB)" as whole-file progress.

### User-visible shift

Pre-3.2 status text during buffering: `"Buffering... 15% (142 MB)"` — whole-file numbers. User thinks 15% of the movie is ready; in reality gate-progress might be 80% complete.

Post-3.2 status text: `"Buffering... 60% (3.0 MB)"` — head numbers. 60% means 3.0 MB of the 5 MB head window is contiguous. Hits 100% exactly when the head is ready and playback imminent.

Combined with Batch 3.1 (gate removed), the user now sees at most a brief flash of "Resolving metadata..." → "Buffering... X% (Y MB)" → play. The percentage climbs monotonically toward 100% and that 100% means something — specifically, "ffmpeg's probe can read without HTTP piece-waits."

### Design choices

- **Overwrote `fileProgress` semantics instead of adding a new field.** Only consumer was StreamPlayerController's status-text line; grep found no other reader. Semantic shift is cleanly localized. Alternative (add `StreamFileResult::contiguousHeadBytes` + `gateBytes`) would force a two-site update with no additional value.
- **5 MB gate target.** Matches sidecar probe size — when the head is contiguous, ffmpeg can probe without HTTP-side piece-wait stalls. Bigger target (20 MB) would be more conservative but delay the 100% mark; smaller (1-2 MB) would reach 100% before the probe could actually complete.
- **`qMin(1.0, ...)` cap.** `contiguousBytesFromOffset` returns up to the full file size on completed torrents; without the cap the percentage could exceed 100 on re-plays of already-downloaded torrents.
- **`qMin(contiguousHead, gateSize)` for downloadedBytes.** Status text shows "X MB" — showing "5.0 MB" max matches the gate target and prevents "Buffering... 100% (142.0 MB)" weirdness on a fully-downloaded torrent that polls briefly before ok=true.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — informational-fields block rewrite (~30 lines, mostly comment)
- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp) — doc-comment only, no code change

No header change, no CMake change.

### Re-verification

1. **Buffering UX on slow-seed torrent.** Start a torrent where first-piece fetch takes a couple seconds. Expected: "Buffering... N% (M.M MB)" where N climbs smoothly from 0 to 100 during the head-fetch window, then polling stops as ok=true and the player opens.
2. **Buffering on well-seeded torrent.** Fast path — may briefly flash "Buffering 100% (5.0 MB)" then transition to play. Or may skip straight from "Resolving metadata..." to play if FILE_NOT_READY window is sub-poll-interval.
3. **Buffering on micro-file torrent (<5 MB).** Expected: % still reaches 100 before play (gate clamped to file size). No division-by-zero on the degenerate `fileSize = 0` case (qMax guard).
4. **Regression: METADATA_NOT_READY / "Resolving metadata..." path.** Unaffected — different branch, different source.
5. **Regression: "Metadata stalled" + ENGINE_ERROR paths.** Unaffected.
6. **Regression: bufferUpdate signal consumers.** `StreamPage::onBufferUpdate` only uses the statusText (discards percent via `/*percent*/`). Signal shape unchanged. Any future consumer that reads `percent` gets gate-progress.

### Known follow-ups

- **Batch 3.3 session-settings tune** (max_queued_disk_bytes, read_cache_line_size, connections_limit) is next. Phase 3 closes after that.
- **Post-Phase-3** — STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md Batch 4.2 (search history dropdown) resumes. The playback-fix track has full Agent 6 review coverage then.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 3.2]: Buffering % = gate progress. StreamEngine::streamFile populates result.fileProgress from contiguousBytesFromOffset(0) / 5 MB gate target (matches sidecar probe size at demuxer.cpp:15), downloadedBytes from clamped contiguous-head bytes. StreamPlayerController status text unchanged but doc-commented to reflect new semantics. Uses Batch 2.1 TorrentEngine::contiguousBytesFromOffset primitive. | files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 3.3 (session-settings tuning pass) closes Phase 3 on your green.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.3 shipped

Mode-specific sections added to the Reader Settings panel — DOUBLE PAGE and SCROLL STRIP, conditionally visible based on active reader mode.

### What shipped

**Two new container widgets** as members on `ComicReader` — `m_settingsDoublePageSection` and `m_settingsScrollStripSection`. Each is a `QWidget` holding its own header label + control rows. Setting `setVisible(true/false)` on the container hides/shows the entire section as a unit (no per-row visibility juggling).

**Helper `makeRowIn`** — variant of the existing `makeRow` lambda that takes an explicit parent + layout, so the new sections can build their rows into their own container layouts instead of the card's main layout.

**DOUBLE PAGE section** (visible when `m_isDoublePage`):
- **Right-to-Left** checkbox bound to `m_rtl`. On toggle: assigns `m_rtl`, toast, `displayCurrentPage()` + `saveSeriesSettings()` (P1-1 persistence). Mirrors the existing `I` keyboard toggle but assigns directly rather than flipping (since the checkbox already drives the boolean).
- **Gutter Shadow** combo with items {Off, Subtle, Medium, Strong} → values {0.00, 0.22, 0.35, 0.55}. Same presets as the existing right-click submenu. On change: assigns `m_gutterShadow`, redraws, persists.

**SCROLL STRIP section** (visible when `m_isScrollStrip`):
- **Side Padding** combo with `SIDE_PADDING_PRESETS` values + the existing label scheme (Off / Small / Medium / Large / X-Large with px hints). On change: assigns `m_stripSidePadding`, pushes to `m_stripCanvas->setSidePadding`, calls `refreshVisibleStripPages`, persists.
- **Split Wide Pages** checkbox bound to `m_splitOnWide`. On toggle: pushes to `m_stripCanvas->setSplitOnWide`, refreshes visible pages, persists, toast.

**Visibility refresh — two trigger points:**
1. **Show-time:** populate-on-show block sets section visibility based on current `m_readerMode`.
2. **Live mode change:** Reading Mode combo handler now refreshes section visibility AFTER the cycle-to-target completes. So changing mode via the panel from DoublePage → ScrollStrip triggers the DOUBLE PAGE section to disappear and SCROLL STRIP to appear, all within the open panel — no need to close + reopen.

**Show-time population extended** for all four new controls. RTL + Split via `setChecked`. Side Padding via `findData`. Gutter Shadow needs custom lookup since it's a `double` (uses `qAbs(... - m_gutterShadow) < 0.01` tolerance match instead of `findData` because Qt's `findData` does exact comparison and floats don't).

### Design decision: dropped "Mark Current Page as Spread"

Plan originally included a per-page spread-override toggle. Pulled from this batch — per-page actions don't belong in a "Settings" panel (Mihon convention). The existing right-click menu entry "Mark as Spread" / "Mark as Single Page" stays as the way to toggle per-page state. The settings panel is for general reader behavior only.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — 6 new private members (2 section containers + 4 controls).
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — `makeRowIn` helper; ~110 lines added inside `showSettingsPanel` for the two new sections; Reading Mode combo handler extended with visibility refresh; populate-on-show block extended with values + section visibility.

No CMakeLists, no shared files, no cross-agent touches.

### Design notes

- **Container widgets, not per-row visibility.** Treating each section as a container makes show/hide atomic — the section header + all rows appear/disappear together. Cleaner than juggling visibility on each row + the header label separately.
- **Live mode-change refresh.** Without the visibility refresh in the Reading Mode combo handler, switching mode via the panel would show/hide the wrong sections until you closed and reopened the panel. The refresh keeps the panel honest in real-time.
- **Gutter Shadow lookup uses tolerance match.** `m_gutterShadow` is a double and the preset values are stored as `QVariant` doubles. `findData` does exact float comparison which is fragile; tolerance match (`qAbs(diff) < 0.01`) is robust against rounding.
- **No-op guards on checkboxes.** Same pattern as Batch 5.2 — `if (checked == m_rtl) return` etc. Belt-and-suspenders against `blockSignals` failures.
- **`makeRow` vs `makeRowIn`.** `makeRow` adds to the card's main layout (used in DISPLAY + IMAGE sections). `makeRowIn` takes explicit parent + layout (used in mode-specific sections so rows go into the section container's layout). Slight duplication but clarity worth it.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke)

1. **Single Page mode:** Press `Ctrl+,` → panel shows DISPLAY + IMAGE sections + Reset button. NO DOUBLE PAGE or SCROLL STRIP sections visible.
2. **Switch to Double Page via panel:** Reading Mode combo → "Double Page" → mode switches; DOUBLE PAGE section appears within the panel (RTL checkbox + Gutter Shadow combo). Live, no panel reopen needed.
3. **Toggle RTL:** Click Right-to-Left → toast "Right to Left" → page redraws right-to-left. Toggle off → "Left to Right".
4. **Gutter Shadow:** Pick "Strong" → gutter shadow visible at the spine. Pick "Off" → shadow gone.
5. **Switch to Scroll Strip via panel:** Reading Mode combo → "Scroll Strip" → mode switches; DOUBLE PAGE section disappears, SCROLL STRIP section appears (Side Padding + Split Wide).
6. **Side Padding:** Pick "Medium (80px)" → strip pages narrow with bars. Pick "Off" → restored.
7. **Split Wide Pages:** Toggle on → wide spread pages render as two stacked half-width portraits. Toggle off → single wide image again.
8. **Switch to Single Page:** Reading Mode → "Single Page" → both mode-specific sections hide. DISPLAY + IMAGE remain visible.
9. **Round-trip with right-click:** Toggle Side Padding via right-click while in Scroll Strip → reopen panel → SCROLL STRIP section's Side Padding combo reflects new value. Same for Gutter Shadow, RTL (via I key), Split Wide.
10. **Reset Series Settings:** Click → all settings revert to defaults; panel re-populates with default values across all visible sections (Crop off, Brightness Off, RTL off, Gutter Medium, Side Padding Off, Split off).
11. **Open in Double Page directly:** Close app, reopen → series remembers last mode (e.g. Double Page) → panel opens with DOUBLE PAGE section visible from the start.
12. **Regression:** All Batch 5.1 + 5.2 controls still work; right-click menu entries unchanged; volume nav, keys overlay, Ctrl+G, Ctrl+0 all unaffected.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke. On green, READY TO COMMIT line posts per Rule 11. Phase 5 closes with Batch 5.4 (toolbar gear-button entry + dismissal polish + tab order) on your green.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.4 shipped — Phase 5 CLOSED

Phase 5 closes with toolbar entry + dismissal polish + tab order + max-height cap. Audit P1-7 and TKMAX-2 both addressed across the four batches.

### What shipped — Batch 5.4

**Toolbar gear button** at [ComicReader.cpp:559-563](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L559-L563) — new `m_settingsBtn` (`QPushButton`) inserted between Portrait Width and Volumes buttons. Uses Unicode gear glyph `QChar(0x2699)` (gear) at 32px width, matching the style of other Unicode-glyph toolbar buttons. Tooltip "Reader Settings (Ctrl+,)". Click calls `showSettingsPanel()`.

The gear glyph U+2699 is BMP and renders as a text symbol with the default Qt font on Windows — no emoji presentation. If it renders colored on any platform, fix is appending the U+FE0E text variation selector (one-line follow-up).

**Settings panel gate in keyPressEvent** at [ComicReader.cpp:2779-2789](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2779-L2789) — mirrors the existing volume nav, goto, and keys overlay gates. Escape closes the panel; all other keys flow through to the focused control via `QWidget::keyPressEvent(event)` so Qt natively handles Tab navigation, combo arrow-key scrolling, checkbox space-toggle, button Enter-activation. No custom focus chain needed — Qt default cycles widgets in creation order, which matches the visual top-to-bottom layout.

**isAnyOverlayOpen extended** at [ComicReader.cpp:3320](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L3320) — settings overlay added to the visibility check alongside end / vol / goto / keys overlays. Critical: many code paths (cursor activity, mouse press, alt-arrow nav, pan-when-zoomed) check this flag to suppress reader interaction while an overlay is open. Without this addition, navigation gestures would fire while the panel is visible.

**Card max-height cap** at [showSettingsPanel](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2532) — `m_settingsCard->setMaximumHeight(height() * 0.85)` set on every show. Keeps the card on-screen even when DISPLAY + IMAGE + a mode-specific section + Reset all stack up. In normal viewports (>= 800px tall) the card fits comfortably; on tiny viewports Qt clips overflow visually. If real overflow becomes common, follow-up batch wraps the card content in a QScrollArea (deferred — current sizes do not justify it).

**Initial focus on first control** at [showSettingsPanel](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2540) — `m_settingsModeCombo->setFocus()` after show. Keyboard users land on Reading Mode immediately; Tab cycles through the rest. `hideSettingsPanel` already calls `setFocus()` to return focus to the reader so M / F / I / Ctrl+G shortcuts work right after close.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — one new `m_settingsBtn` member.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — toolbar button construction, keyPressEvent gate, isAnyOverlayOpen extension, max-height cap + initial focus in showSettingsPanel.

No other files. No CMakeLists. No new resources (gear glyph is inline Unicode, not an SVG asset). No cross-agent touches.

### Phase 5 close

Four batches shipped:
- 5.1 — overlay shell + Display section (Reading Mode, Portrait Width, Fit Mode, Reset).
- 5.2 — Image section (Brightness, Crop Borders, Image Quality, Memory Saver).
- 5.3 — mode-specific sections (DoublePage: RTL + Gutter Shadow / ScrollStrip: Side Padding + Split Wide Pages).
- 5.4 — toolbar gear button + dismissal polish (this batch).

Total: ~470 LOC across `ComicReader.{h,cpp}`. Zero cross-agent touches. Right-click menu and toolbar buttons unchanged — settings panel is additional surface, not replacement (Hemanth keep-both call). Eleven controls now reachable via three independent surfaces (panel / right-click / keyboard), with the panel as the discoverable hub.

Audit P1-7 (settings UX scattered) closed: a single Mihon-style categorized panel surfaces every setting that previously required hunting across toolbar / right-click / keyboard / K overlay. TKMAX-2 (consolidated Mega Settings regression) closed: panel matches the spirit of Tankoban-Max Mega Settings within Qt idiom (modal overlay vs HTML page, but same UX shape — categorized sections, mode-specific reveal, reset button).

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke — Phase 5 exit)

1. **Toolbar gear button:** Reader toolbar shows gear button between portrait-width % and the volumes triple-bar. Hover shows "Reader Settings (Ctrl+,)" tooltip.
2. **Click gear button:** Panel opens — same as Ctrl+,.
3. **Tab navigation:** With panel open, focus is on Reading Mode combo. Tab cycles through Portrait Width, Fit Mode, Brightness, Crop Borders, Image Quality, Memory Saver, mode-specific section if visible, Reset Series Settings.
4. **Keyboard activation:** Enter on Reset triggers the action and re-populates. Space on a checkbox toggles. Up/Down on combo changes selection (Qt default).

7. **Overlay-open guard:** With panel open, click in the page area (dimmed) and confirm no page navigation fires (isAnyOverlayOpen guards mouse handlers). Press M with panel open and confirm reader mode does NOT cycle (gate eats the key, lets the focused combo handle it instead).

9. **Card height on tall library:** Open panel in DoublePage mode (DISPLAY + IMAGE + DOUBLE PAGE + Reset visible) and confirm card fits comfortably in viewport.
