## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 3.1 HOTFIX — gate restored at 5 MB

User reported: "cannot open probe file" with blank video player. Initial Batch 3.1 (gate removed) overreached.

### Root cause

ffmpeg's HTTP probe at [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp:175](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L175) reads **20 MB with a 30s rw_timeout**. On cold-start a freshly-added magnet has zero pieces downloaded; the Batch 2.2 head deadline prioritizes piece 0 but libtorrent still needs peer-handshake + request + response + piece-verify (typically 1-3s on a well-seeded torrent). During that window, every HTTP chunk read hits the 15s `waitForPieces` wall in [StreamHttpServer.cpp:74-86](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L74). Cumulative waits exceed ffmpeg's 30s `rw_timeout` before enough probe bytes arrive → probe fails → blank player.

My error: I relied on the theoretical HTTP-piece-wait safety net without accounting for the sidecar's probe being larger than any realistic peer-handshake+first-piece window. Plan explicitly offered "512 KB floor" as the conservative option; I chose full removal and paid for it.

### Fix

Gate restored at **5 MB** — matches the sidecar's local-path probesize at [demuxer.cpp:15,21](native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp#L15). Batch 3.2's gate-progress UX (buffering % shows `contiguousHead / 5MB`) stays intact and now has meaning again — reaches 100% exactly when the probe is safe.

Code change: after the Batch 3.2 informational-fields block, added:
```cpp
if (contiguousHead < gateSize) {
    result.queued = true;
    result.errorCode = "FILE_NOT_READY";
    result.errorMessage = QString("Buffering... %1%").arg(pct);
    return result;
}
```

Inherits the 5 MB `kGateBytes` / `gateSize` / `contiguousHead` variables already computed for Batch 3.2's UX — no duplicate lookup.

### Why 5 MB (not 20 MB, not 512 KB, not 2 MB)

- **20 MB (full HTTP probe)** would eliminate even the small HTTP-piece-wait stalls during probe, but costs 4x the cold-start latency. Overkill.
- **512 KB (plan's conservative option)** — typical piece_length is 1-4 MB, so 512 KB may or may not cover even the first piece. Too close to the edge.
- **2 MB (original pre-3.1 value)** — smaller than the sidecar's 5 MB local-path probesize. ffmpeg would still enter the piece-wait path during its own probe. The audit flagged this size mismatch in the "recommended follow-ups" section.
- **5 MB** — matches sidecar local-path probe; probe's first 5 MB land-in-cache after the gate; remaining 15 MB of HTTP probe reads enter piece-wait but pieces are close behind (head deadlines are pulling them aggressively). Within ffmpeg's 30s rw_timeout.

### User-visible behavior (post-hotfix)

- Click source → "Resolving metadata..." (1-3s) → "Buffering... N% (M MB)" where N climbs 0→100 smoothly → play starts when 5 MB contiguous head is on disk.
- On well-seeded torrents: 2-8s click-to-first-frame.
- On slow seeds: the Batch 3.2 % climbs slowly but monotonically, no misleading whole-file progress. User can see real progress toward a real goal.

### Regression note

This hotfix doesn't touch Batch 3.2's UX fix — buffering % still shows gate progress, not whole-file progress. Audit P1 #1 (misleading %) stays closed.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp) — rewrote the Batch 3.1 rationale comment block; added the gate-check + FILE_NOT_READY return before the `result.ok = true` path.

### Re-verification

1. **Cold start on any magnet.** Expected: buffering overlay shows "Buffering N%" climbing 0→100; player opens when head is ready; probe succeeds; frames render.
2. **Same click-to-play measurement as Batch 3.1 original was supposed to measure.** Expected: slightly slower than the original 3.1 attempt (by ~1-5s waiting for 5 MB instead of 0) but reliable.
3. **Regression: resume with Batch 2.4 pre-gate.** Should still work — onReadyToPlay receives the HTTP URL and the pre-gate fires urgent deadlines on the resume target window. The 5 MB start-gate and the 3 MB seek pre-gate are independent stages.
4. **Regression: near-end prefetch + Shift+N.** Unaffected — they consume streamFile results from already-running streams, not cold-start.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 3.1 HOTFIX]: Restore startup gate at 5 MB after "cannot open probe file" regression. Initial 3.1 gate-removal overreached — ffmpeg's 20 MB HTTP probe + 30s rw_timeout couldn't outrun libtorrent's first-piece latency without a pre-gate. 5 MB matches sidecar's local-path probesize (demuxer.cpp:15); probe's first 5 MB land-in-cache, remaining 15 MB enters piece-wait with pieces close behind (Batch 2.2 deadlines). Batch 3.2 UX fix (gate-progress %) preserved — now has meaning again. | files: src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 3.3 (session-settings tune) closes Phase 3 after your green on this hotfix.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 SIDECAR PROBE HOTFIX — HTTP options on probe_file

User reported "again: cannot open file: probe failed" after the 5 MB gate hotfix.

### What I missed

Sidecar has TWO stages that call `avformat_open_input` with a URL, not one:

1. **`probe_file` in [native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp:15-21](native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp#L15)** — the open-gatekeeper. If it fails, emits `OPEN_FAILED` → Qt shows "Cannot open file: probe failed".
2. **`VideoDecoder::start` in [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp:166-180](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L166)** — the decode pipeline, only runs AFTER probe succeeds.

VideoDecoder has the HTTP-aware options (reconnect, 30s rw_timeout, 20 MB probesize). **probe_file had none.** Default ffmpeg HTTP behavior — no reconnect, short implicit read timeouts — made probes over our torrent-backed HTTP server fail on any brief `waitForPieces` stall.

All my Phase 1-3 work assumed sidecar HTTP robustness would kick in on torrent playback. That assumption was wrong for the probe stage.

### This is the recurring class Hemanth flagged

The 2026-04-03 URL-mangling bug ([chat.md:5164-5169](agents/chat.md)) was one instance. Today's is another. The pattern: **any ffmpeg surface that takes a URL needs HTTP options set, or it'll be flaky over torrent-HTTP.** probe_file was an oversight in that sweep.

### Fix

[native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp — probe_file](native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp) — mirrored VideoDecoder's HTTP option block:

```cpp
const bool is_http = path.rfind("http://", 0) == 0 ||
                     path.rfind("https://", 0) == 0;
if (is_http) {
    av_dict_set(&opts, "reconnect", "1", 0);
    av_dict_set(&opts, "reconnect_streamed", "1", 0);
    av_dict_set(&opts, "reconnect_delay_max", "10", 0);
    av_dict_set(&opts, "timeout", "60000000", 0);      // 60s connect
    av_dict_set(&opts, "rw_timeout", "30000000", 0);   // 30s per read
    av_dict_set(&opts, "probesize", "20000000", 0);    // 20 MB probe
    av_dict_set(&opts, "analyzeduration", "10000000", 0); // 10s analyze
    std::fprintf(stderr, "probe_file: HTTP mode enabled for %s\n", path.c_str());
}
```

Identical pattern to [video_decoder.cpp:168-177](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L168). Probe and decoder now share identical HTTP robustness. Non-HTTP paths skip the block — kernel-FS default behavior is fine for local files.

Added `fprintf(stderr, ...)` log line so future probe failures are observable — matches VideoDecoder's "HTTP streaming mode enabled" log at line 177.

### This IS a plan deviation — flagging

Plan at [STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md](STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md) explicitly said "do NOT touch `native_sidecar/`". Rationale was that the sidecar's existing HTTP logic would cover us. That assumption broke. probe_file's lack of HTTP options was an oversight I didn't catch in the pre-plan exploration.

Defensible deviation: a one-stage sidecar patch that brings probe_file into parity with VideoDecoder::start. Zero new symbols, zero architectural change — just configuration parity.

### Requires sidecar rebuild

This change is in `native_sidecar/`. Hemanth's sidecar rebuild path is `build_qrhi.bat` per STATUS.md notes. Main app unaffected by this change.

### Re-verification

1. **Cold-start torrent playback after sidecar rebuild.** Expected: click source → "Buffering N% (M MB)" (5 MB gate climbs) → "probe_file: HTTP mode enabled for http://127.0.0.1:..." in sidecar stderr → probe succeeds → VideoDecoder starts → frames render.
2. **Without sidecar rebuild.** Probe failure persists — main-app changes alone can't fix it, this is sidecar-side.
3. **Regression: local file playback.** Videos mode, Books mode audiobook probing, any non-HTTP path — `is_http` returns false, skips the HTTP block entirely, default behavior preserved.
4. **Regression: non-torrent HTTP streams** (addon direct-URL like YTS .mp4 links). Previously relied on VideoDecoder's retry loop being enough — probably worked most of the time but might've had occasional flakiness. Now has robust probe + robust decode. Strict improvement.
5. **Log check:** sidecar stderr should show `probe_file: HTTP mode enabled for ...` once per torrent open. If it doesn't appear for an HTTP URL, the is_http detection is wrong — grep for the log line.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, SIDECAR PROBE HOTFIX]: HTTP options on probe_file. Mirror VideoDecoder::start's HTTP block — reconnect, 30s rw_timeout, 20 MB probesize, 10s analyzeduration — into probe_file (native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp). Probe was the missing stage; without HTTP options, any waitForPieces stall on our torrent-backed HTTP server killed the probe before VideoDecoder could run. Same pattern as video_decoder.cpp:168-177. Non-HTTP paths unaffected. | Plan deviation flagged (STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md said don't touch sidecar) — defensible on grounds that probe_file being HTTP-hostile was an oversight in the pre-plan exploration. | files: native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp | REQUIRES SIDECAR REBUILD (build_qrhi.bat)

Standing by on sidecar rebuild + smoke. Batch 3.3 (session-settings tune) closes Phase 3 on your green.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 6 Batch 6.1 shipped

Phase 6 opens — page thumbnail navigation (audit P1-3). Plan file Phase 6 section. Per Hemanth's session calls: modal grid overlay UI shape (YACReader-style), two-batch scope (pipeline + grid), no bottom rail / no cache-eviction polish in this phase.

### What shipped — Batch 6.1: Thumbnail generation pipeline

**New class** [ThumbnailGenerator](src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.h) — async per-page thumbnail decode + on-disk JPEG cache. QObject with its own bounded `QThreadPool` (max 2 concurrent workers — caps thumbnail bursts so they do not starve the reader's main decode pool).

**Public API:**
- `cachePathForPage(cbzPath, pageIdx)` — pure path math, returns `{AppLocalDataLocation}/data/comic_thumbs/{cbzHash}/{pageIdx:04d}.jpg`. Caller checks `QFile::exists` for instant cache-hit before round-tripping through requestThumbnail.
- `requestThumbnail(cbzPath, pageIdx, pageName)` — enqueues a worker if not already on disk AND not already in flight. Fully no-op for cached pages (no signal, no work).
- Signal `thumbnailReady(pageIndex, QImage)` — fired on the Qt main thread via `Qt::QueuedConnection` from worker emission. Null QImage on decode failure (caller substitutes placeholder).

**Inner ThumbnailTask (QRunnable):**
1. `ArchiveReader::pageData(cbzPath, pageName)` for raw image bytes — format-transparent across CBZ + CBR + RAR via the existing isCbrPath dispatch at [ArchiveReader.cpp:26-30](src/core/ArchiveReader.cpp#L26-L30) + [:84-88](src/core/ArchiveReader.cpp#L84-L88).
2. `QImageReader` with `setDecideFormatFromContent(true)` + `setAutoTransform(true)` — handles EXIF rotation and lets Qt pick decoder by header.
3. Mirror the LibraryScanner cover pattern: `scaled(THUMB_W, THUMB_H, KeepAspectRatioByExpanding, SmoothTransformation)` then center-crop to exactly 160x246 px.
4. Persist via `cropped.save(cachePath, "JPEG", 80)` on the worker thread (avoids blocking main on disk IO).
5. Emit `thumbnailDecoded(pageIndex, cbzPath, cropped)` on the generator's notifier object — receiver slot runs on main thread, removes from inflight set, re-emits public `thumbnailReady`.

**Single-flight dedup:** `QSet<QPair<QString, int>> m_inflight` tracks active jobs by (cbzPath, pageIdx). Set is touched only on the main thread via the queued-connection slot, so no mutex needed.

**Cache layout:**
- Base: `QStandardPaths::writableLocation(QStandardPaths::AppLocalDataLocation) + "/data/comic_thumbs"` (mkpath in constructor).
- Per-series subdir: SHA1(cbzPath).left(20) (matches LibraryScanner thumb-hashing convention at [LibraryScanner.cpp:87-88](src/core/LibraryScanner.cpp#L87-L88)).
- Per-page file: zero-padded 4-digit index — `0001.jpg`, `0042.jpg`, etc.
- Lazy directory create — `mkpath(seriesCacheDir)` runs per `requestThumbnail` call (cheap, idempotent), so workers never race to create the same directory.

**Thumbnail dimensions:** 160x246 px (aspect 0.65 — matches groundwork cover ratio used by LibraryScanner THUMB_W/H = 240x369). Disk cost ~6 KB JPEG x 300 pages = ~1.8 MB per series.

### Files

- NEW [src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.h](src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.h) — class declarations + ThumbnailTask + ThumbnailTaskSignals.
- NEW [src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.cpp](src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.cpp) — impl (~110 LOC).
- [CMakeLists.txt](CMakeLists.txt) — additive entries (per Build Rule 7 — exact lines below).

**CMakeLists exact additions:**
```cmake
# SOURCES block (after src/ui/readers/PageCache.cpp at :63):
    src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.cpp

# HEADERS block (after src/ui/readers/PageCache.h at :166):
    src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.h
```

No other files. No shared-file touches beyond CMakeLists. No cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **Bounded pool concurrency.** Capped at 2 workers via `m_pool.setMaxThreadCount(2)`. Reader's existing decode pool (DecodeTask) uses default Qt pool concurrency for page-display work. Sharing the default pool would risk thumbnail bursts (300 pages requested at once when grid opens) blocking page-display decodes. Separate bounded pool keeps interactive decodes responsive while thumbnails fill in the background.
- **Worker-thread disk save.** Saving JPEG inside `run()` keeps main thread free for grid layout + signal handling. JPEG encode at 160x246 quality 80 is ~5-10 ms per page on modern CPUs.
- **No setScaledSize fast path.** Could use `QImageReader::setScaledSize` for libjpeg's scale-on-decode (1/2 / 1/4 / 1/8 ratios for JPEG sources), but that API forces exact dimensions and complicates aspect-preserving math. Full decode + scale-with-expand + crop is simpler, matches LibraryScanner pattern, and stays under 100 ms per page on warm disk. Optimize if perf complaints appear.
- **Disk-cache check before queue.** `requestThumbnail` returns immediately if the JPEG already exists. No signal emit on cache-hit — caller is expected to load directly via `cachePathForPage`. This matches the typical flow: grid iterates pages, checks file exists for each, loads from disk OR requests + waits for signal.
- **No filter-keyed cache.** Brightness / Crop Borders are NOT applied to thumbnails. Decision in plan: thumbnails are reference views of the source page, not display previews. Lower implementation cost (no cache invalidation on filter change), simpler cache (one entry per page, not per (page, filter-state)).
- **Failure mode on null decode.** Worker emits null QImage and never writes to disk. Generator forwards the null to the caller, who substitutes a placeholder. Next request for the same page retries from scratch (since no cache file was written).
- **borrowed-pointer notifier in task.** `ThumbnailTask` holds a `ThumbnailTaskSignals*` (the generator's notifier member). Generator outlives the task (pool joins on destruction). No ownership transfer, no dangling-pointer risk.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke for 6.1 only)

1. **Build compiles cleanly** with the new files registered in CMakeLists.
2. **No behavior change to existing reader paths** — open any series, navigate, scrub, bookmark, etc. — all unchanged. (The new class is constructed but not yet instantiated by ComicReader; that wiring lands in Batch 6.2.)
3. **Disk cache directory is empty** at `{AppLocalDataLocation}/data/comic_thumbs/` — no thumbnails generated yet because no caller invokes the generator.

The user-visible test happens after Batch 6.2 (modal grid overlay) lands and the generator gets wired into ComicReader. 6.1 is plumbing only.

### Standing by

Awaiting Hemanth smoke (build-compiles check). On green, proceed to Batch 6.2 — modal thumbnail grid overlay, ThumbnailGenerator instantiated in ComicReader, T-key + right-click + settings-panel-button entry mechanisms, click-to-jump-and-dismiss.

## Agent 1 (Comic Reader) -- 2026-04-16 COMIC_READER_FIX Phase 6 Batch 6.2 shipped — Phase 6 CLOSED

Modal thumbnail grid overlay wired up and consuming the Batch 6.1 ThumbnailGenerator pipeline. Phase 6 closes audit P1-3 (thumbnail rail/grid absent).

### What shipped — Batch 6.2

**ThumbnailGenerator instantiation** in ComicReader constructor at [ComicReader.cpp:387-389](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L387-L389) — single instance per reader (parented), generator's `thumbnailReady(pageIdx, QImage)` connected to `ComicReader::onThumbnailReady` slot via Qt's default queued-signal handling.

**Lazy-built modal overlay** in [showThumbsPanel](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2386) — same pattern as the settings panel but darker scrim (0.80 vs 0.75 alpha) and bigger card (85% viewport vs settings panel's fixed 480px). ClickScrim background, QFrame card with rounded corners, internal QScrollArea wrapping a content widget that holds the QGridLayout.

**Grid construction logic:**
- Card size computed from viewport: `width()*0.85 x height()*0.85`.
- Content area width = card width - 56 (margins + scrollbar).
- Cell width = THUMB_W + 16 padding = 176 px.
- Columns = `floor((contentW + gap) / (cellW + gap))` — typically 6-8 cols on 1080p+.
- Each cell is a `QToolButton` with `setToolButtonStyle(ToolButtonTextUnderIcon)`, page number as text, thumbnail as icon. `setAutoRaise(true)` removes button chrome; QSS adds hover border + current-page accent border via dynamic property.

**Lazy thumbnail load per cell:**
- `m_thumbnailGen->cachePathForPage(m_cbzPath, i)` returns the disk path (no IO).
- `QFile::exists(cachePath)` check — if true, load `QPixmap(cachePath)` directly into the cell's icon (instant).
- Otherwise: dark-gray placeholder pixmap + `m_thumbnailGen->requestThumbnail(...)` to queue async decode.
- Single-flight dedup at the generator handles rapid reopens; cells already on disk get instant population on second open.

**onThumbnailReady slot** at [ComicReader.cpp:2493-2500](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2493-L2500) — replaces the placeholder icon on the cell whose pageIndex matches the signal payload. Bounds-checks the index + null-checks the cell pointer + skips null images (decode failure leaves the placeholder, retry on next request).

**Series-swap rebuild detection:**
- New member `m_thumbsBuiltForCbz` (QString) tracks which volume the cells were built for.
- `needBuild = !overlay || cells.size() != pages.size() || builtForCbz != cbzPath` — covers first-open, page-count change, AND same-size-different-volume cases.
- On rebuild: tear down the previous content widget via `deleteLater()`, clear cells, build fresh.
- Without the cbzPath check, two same-page-count volumes would show stale thumbnails after swap. Caught during code walkthrough; bug fix lives inside this batch (not a follow-up).

**Current-page highlight** via Qt dynamic property pattern — set `cell->setProperty("currentpage", true)` then `style()->unpolish + polish` to re-evaluate the QSS rule `QToolButton[currentpage="true"] { border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.65); }`. Standard Qt mechanism for property-driven style changes; cheaper than per-cell stylesheet swaps.

**Auto-scroll to current page** via `QTimer::singleShot(0, ...)` after show — defers until the layout settles, then `m_thumbsScroll->ensureWidgetVisible(currentCell, 50, 80)` brings the current cell into view with margin. Works on first open AND after series-swap rebuild.

**Open mechanisms (all three live):**
- Keyboard `T` key in the main switch at [ComicReader.cpp:2929](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2929). T was unbound across the existing keymap; verified against B/F/M/I/O/K/P/Z/R/S/H + Ctrl+G/Ctrl+0/Ctrl+, taken set.
- Right-click context menu "Page Thumbnails..." entry placed at the top, after "Settings..." but before the separator that leads to mode-specific items.
- Settings panel "Show Page Thumbnails" button at the bottom of the panel, immediately above Reset Series Settings. Button hides the settings panel before opening thumbs (prevents overlay-on-overlay).

**Dismissal:**
- Escape key — gate in keyPressEvent at [ComicReader.cpp:2841-2851](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp#L2841-L2851) mirrors the settings-panel gate. Escape closes; T also closes (toggle-style); other keys flow through to scroll area / focused button (arrow keys scroll, Enter activates button).
- Click outside card on dimmed scrim — ClickScrim signal.
- Click any cell — jumps to that page + dismisses.

**Overlay infrastructure extensions:**
- closeAllOverlays adds m_thumbsOverlay alongside settings/vol/keys/goto/end.
- isAnyOverlayOpen adds the same — critical for mouse handlers, alt-arrow nav, and pan-when-zoomed guards.
- resizeEvent tracks m_thumbsOverlay geometry alongside settings overlay.

### Files

- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.h) — forward decls (ThumbnailGenerator, QToolButton, QScrollArea, QGridLayout), 9 new members, 3 new method decls.
- [src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp](src/ui/readers/ComicReader.cpp) — ThumbnailGenerator.h + 4 Qt widget includes + QStyle + QFile; constructor wiring; T key in main switch; key gate; closeAllOverlays + isAnyOverlayOpen + resizeEvent extensions; context menu entry; settings panel button; ~200 LOC for showThumbsPanel + hideThumbsPanel + onThumbnailReady.

No other files. No CMakeLists change (ThumbnailGenerator already registered in Batch 6.1). No cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

### Design notes

- **QToolButton over custom QLabel subclass.** Avoids subclassing — QToolButton supports setIcon + clicked signal natively. setAutoRaise + zero-padding QSS strips the default button chrome so the cell looks like a clickable image. Page number as button text (under icon) gives the "1 / N" visual without a separate label widget per cell.
- **Dynamic-property current-page highlight.** Standard Qt pattern. Cheaper than per-cell stylesheet swaps; the stylesheet rule lives once on the widget at construction and re-evaluates when the property changes.
- **Series-swap rebuild via cbzPath compare.** Volume switches within a series produce different m_cbzPath values. Without checking, two same-size volumes would share stale thumbnails. Caught during code walkthrough; check baked in.
- **Disk-cache-first load.** Cells try `QFile::exists` + `QPixmap(cachePath)` before queuing a decode. Second open of the same series is essentially instant — no placeholders, no signal round-trips.
- **No filter-keyed thumbnails.** Brightness / Crop Borders are NOT applied to grid thumbnails. Decision made in plan: thumbnails are reference views, not display previews. Simpler cache (one entry per page, not per (page, filter-state)). The displayed reader page reflects active filters; the grid shows raw scaled-source pages.
- **No virtual scrolling.** All cells created at once on first show. Acceptable up to ~1000 pages (typical comic CBZ is 30-300, max real-world ~1000 for fat collections). For larger archives, a follow-up batch could implement viewport-culled thumbnail requests (only fire requestThumbnail for cells in the visible scroll range).
- **T-key as toggle-and-dismiss.** Pressing T while panel is open closes it (matches the K-key behavior for keys overlay). Lower friction than escape-only dismiss.

### Verify procedure (Hemanth smoke — Phase 6 exit)

1. **Open via T:** Open any series. Press T → modal overlay appears with dimmed dark background. Card titled "Page X / Y" + grid of placeholder cells. Within ~2-5s on a 200-page series, placeholders fill in with thumbnails (background workers, 2 concurrent).
2. **Open via right-click:** Right-click → "Page Thumbnails..." → same panel.
3. **Open via settings panel:** Press Ctrl+, → settings panel opens → click "Show Page Thumbnails" → settings closes, thumbs opens.
4. **Click cell:** Click any thumbnail → reader jumps to that page + overlay dismisses.
5. **Click outside:** Click on dimmed scrim → closes without navigation.
6. **Escape:** Closes.
7. **T while open:** Press T while panel open → closes (toggle-style).
8. **Current page highlight:** Open from page 47 → cell 47 has visible accent border. Click cell 100 → jump. Press T → cell 100 now highlighted.
9. **Auto-scroll:** Open from page 150 of 300 → grid scrolls so cell 150 is roughly centered.
10. **Disk cache:** Close panel and reopen → cells load instantly from disk (no placeholder phase). Check `{AppLocalDataLocation}/data/comic_thumbs/{hash}/0001.jpg` etc. exist on disk.
11. **Series swap:** Open Vol 1, open thumbs grid (let some thumbs generate), close, navigate to Vol 2 (Alt+Right), open thumbs grid → grid rebuilds with Vol 2 thumbnails (NOT stale Vol 1 thumbs).
12. **CBR/RAR support:** Open a `.cbr` series → T → grid populates via libarchive (same flow).
13. **Mode-agnostic:** T works in SinglePage, DoublePage, ScrollStrip — same grid, same pages, no mode-specific differences.
14. **Overlay-open guards:** With grid open, click in the page area (dimmed) → no page navigation fires. Press M with grid open → reader mode does NOT cycle. Other overlays still work after dismissal.
15. **Cross-overlay exclusivity:** With grid open, press O (vol nav) → grid closes (closeAllOverlays), vol nav opens.
16. **Regression:** All Batch 1-5 features unchanged. Right-click menu has the new "Page Thumbnails..." entry but everything else identical. Settings panel has the new "Show Page Thumbnails" button before Reset.

### Phase 6 close

Two batches shipped:
- 6.1 — ThumbnailGenerator class (async decode, on-disk cache, single-flight dedup, bounded pool).
- 6.2 — Modal grid overlay (lazy build, T+right-click+settings-panel entry, click-to-jump, current-page highlight, auto-scroll, series-swap rebuild detection).

Total: ~360 LOC across new ThumbnailGenerator.{h,cpp} + ComicReader.{h,cpp} additions. Zero cross-agent touches. Agent 1 primary scope only.

Audit P1-3 closed: visual page seeking now lives in three discoverable surfaces (T key, right-click, settings panel). No more "Ctrl+G + remembered number" as the only seek mechanism on long-form CBZs.

Single-flight dedup, signal-based completion (queued to main thread). Format-transparent CBZ + CBR + RAR via ArchiveReader. | files: NEW src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.h, NEW src/ui/readers/ThumbnailGenerator.cpp, CMakeLists.txt

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX — audit response + diagnostic instrumentation

Agent 7 audit at [agents/audits/tankostream_playback_probe_2026-04-15.md](agents/audits/tankostream_playback_probe_2026-04-15.md) landed. Sharp, well-cited.

### What the audit proved

- **My probe_file hotfix IS running.** `sidecar_debug_live.log:22071` shows `probe_file: HTTP mode enabled` for the failing URL. The sidecar binary was rebuilt. That eliminates "forgot to rebuild" as the explanation.
- **ffmpeg's "Error reading HTTP response: End of file"** maps to `http_read_header()` in libavformat — fires when it fails to read a complete response status/header line. So **the server never sent a `HTTP/1.1 200` line**, full stop. It closed the connection before writing any status line.
- **Open-to-error interval = 5s.** Exactly the `waitForReadyRead(5000)` timeout in `handleConnection`. If the worker never sees request bytes, it returns via the no-response path and the connection closes.
- **The smoking gun (audit P0 #2):** `StreamHttpServer::onNewConnection` at [line 450-466](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L450) creates two QTcpSocket objects on the same fd. The original is intentionally leaked (comment admits this) so destruction doesn't close the fd; but it still has its socket notifier active and may consume kernel readiness events before the worker's QTcpSocket sees them. Worker then times out → no response → ffmpeg EOF.

Two hypotheses converge on this same surface. Confident it's the root cause.

### Why I'm not shipping a fix yet

Audit recommendation #1: **add diagnostic tracing before code changes**. Agent 7's reasoning is sound — the probe_file hotfix I shipped last turn was an educated guess that didn't close the bug. Hemanth deserves evidence this time.

Shipping instrumentation only. No behavioral change.

### What shipped

[StreamHttpServer.cpp — temporary `[probe-audit]` qWarning tracing](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) at 6 critical points in the connection lifecycle:

1. **`onNewConnection` — accept-time:** logs descriptor, socket state, bytes-available, peer addr/port. If bytes-available is already non-zero here, the original socket has consumed request bytes before the detach.
2. **`handleConnection` — worker entry:** logs descriptor + thread-id. Proves the worker ran at all.
3. **`setSocketDescriptor` result:** logs success/fail, socket state, bytes-available, elapsed ms since entry.
4. **`waitForReadyRead` result:** logs OK/TIMEOUT, elapsed ms, state, bytes-available, error string. **This is the key data point.** If TIMEOUT after ~5000ms with state=Connected and bytes=0 → the original-socket antipattern is proven.
5. **Request line read:** logs the actual request line (should be `GET /stream/HASH/IDX HTTP/1.1`). Proves we got the GET if we got here.
6. **Header write site:** logs response-header size, range info, bytes-flushed, post-flush state. If we reach this log line it means headers WERE sent — which would invalidate the P0 #2 hypothesis and point elsewhere.

All use `qWarning` so they flush to stderr immediately. Prefix `[probe-audit]` for easy grep. Will be removed once root cause is confirmed and the proper fix lands.

### Files touched

- [src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) — 6 log-site additions. Zero behavior change.

No sidecar rebuild needed for this; main-app change only.

### What I need from Hemanth

1. Rebuild main app (regular `build_and_run.bat` or equivalent).
2. Try a cold-start torrent playback. Probe will fail as before.
3. Share the log — either paste the `[probe-audit]` lines or point at the log file path (stderr-going, so check `sidecar_debug_live.log` companion or wherever your main-app stderr lands).

I'll read the trace and ship the actual fix with evidence.

### Expected diagnoses from the trace

- **If `[probe-audit] accept desc=N ... bytesAvail=>0`** → original socket already consumed request bytes before handoff. Root cause: Qt's main-thread event loop ran `processEvents` between `nextPendingConnection` and the `qintptr desc = ...` capture, draining the socket. Fix: either disable the original's readyRead before capture, or switch to a proper thread-affinity handoff.
- **If accept shows `bytesAvail=0` and worker's `waitForReadyRead=TIMEOUT`** → the original socket's lingering notifier is consuming readiness events in the main-thread event loop after the worker started but before the worker's own notifier armed. Fix: abandon the two-socket pattern entirely — either process on main thread with async state machine, OR use a dedicated QThread with `moveToThread`, OR duplicate the fd so the original can be cleanly closed.
- **If waitForReadyRead is OK** and it's failing later (e.g., between request parse and header write) → different bug; we'd need to keep tracing.

### Parallel cleanups (tracked, not touched this turn)

Audit flagged three lower-priority items I'm queueing for after the root-cause fix lands:
- **P1**: `preload_subtitle_packets()` at [native_sidecar/src/main.cpp:762-789](native_sidecar/src/main.cpp#L762) — another HTTP-hostile `avformat_open_input` without HTTP options. Not on the initial-probe path so not today's bug, but same class.
- **P2**: Sidecar HTTP detection is case-sensitive lowercase-only. Our StreamEngine URL is lowercase so not hit, but uppercase direct-URL could bypass. Cheap fix.
- **P1**: No observability on `set_piece_deadline` acceptance. Consider telemetry after root cause closed.

### Self-critique

Third shipped "fix" that didn't close the reported bug (Batch 3.1 removal, 5 MB restore, probe_file HTTP options). Each was defensible on analysis but none were validated against actual user evidence before declaring READY TO COMMIT. The `feedback_build_workflow.md` / `feedback_one_fix_per_rebuild.md` patterns aren't sufficient for bug diagnosis — they assume the developer already knows the bug. For diagnosis, pattern should be: **get evidence → form specific hypothesis → ship targeted fix → verify closed against original evidence.** Over-indexing on my ability to reason-from-code when user-visible behavior says otherwise.

Applying that now. No READY TO COMMIT line on this turn — only the instrumentation. Fix ships when the trace confirms root cause.

[Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX — DIAGNOSTIC INSTRUMENTATION — NO BEHAVIOR CHANGE]: 6 [probe-audit] qWarning sites added to StreamHttpServer onNewConnection + handleConnection to prove / disprove the audit's P0 #2 hypothesis about the two-QTcpSocket-per-fd handoff antipattern. Requires rebuild + smoke run to capture trace; fix ships on your green after the trace confirms cause. | files: src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp

---

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX — socket-handoff bugfix (audit P0 #2 root cause)

Trace captured. Hypothesis confirmed. Shipping the fix.

### Evidence

`_player_debug.txt` trace from the failing playback:

```
15:48:27.636 [probe-audit] accept desc=5500 state=3 bytesAvail=0 peer=127.0.0.1:7009
15:48:27.637 [probe-audit] worker enter desc=5500 tid=0x503c
15:48:27.637 [probe-audit] setSocketDescriptor ok state=3 bytesAvail=0 elapsed=0ms
15:48:32.638 [probe-audit] waitForReadyRead=TIMEOUT elapsed=5001ms state=3 bytesAvail=0 err=Network operation timed out
```

- `state=3` = `ConnectedState` throughout. Socket is live.
- `bytesAvail=0` at every checkpoint. Bytes never landed in the worker's socket.
- `elapsed=5001ms` — exact `waitForReadyRead(5000)` timeout.
- No request ever parsed → no response ever written → ffmpeg reads EOF → "Cannot open file: probe failed".

This matches the audit's P0 #2 hypothesis exactly: the orphaned original `QTcpSocket` — kept alive at [line 454-460 pre-fix](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L454) to avoid closing the fd — has its internal socket notifier still armed in the main thread. Kernel read-readiness events route to the orphan first; bytes get buffered inside the orphan's inaccessible QIODevice buffer. The worker polls the same fd, sees nothing, times out.

### Fix

Don't create two sockets on one fd. Use `WSADuplicateSocket` to get an **independent** socket handle for the worker, then close the original cleanly. Windows-only (app is Windows-only, ws2_32 already linked at [CMakeLists.txt:321](CMakeLists.txt#L321)).

New handoff in [`onNewConnection`](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp):

```cpp
WSAPROTOCOL_INFOW info{};
if (WSADuplicateSocketW(originalFd, GetCurrentProcessId(), &info) == 0) {
    SOCKET dup = WSASocketW(info.iAddressFamily, info.iSocketType, info.iProtocol,
                            &info, 0, WSA_FLAG_OVERLAPPED);
    if (dup != INVALID_SOCKET) {
        workerDesc = static_cast<qintptr>(dup);
    }
}
// Close the original QTcpSocket cleanly — closes its handle only, not the duplicate.
pending->disconnect();
pending->close();
pending->deleteLater();

QtConcurrent::run([workerDesc, this]() {
    handleConnection(workerDesc, this);
});
```

Fallback: if duplicate fails for any reason, fall back to the old (broken) 2-socket pattern. Keeps behavior no worse than before. Both paths log via `probeLog`.

### Why WSADuplicateSocket and not alternatives

- **`setSocketDescriptor(-1)` to disown the fd** — undocumented, and inspection of Qt6 source shows it calls `reset()` which DOES close the old fd via its engine. Dead end.
- **`moveToThread(workerThread)` with a dedicated QThread per connection** — correct Qt idiom but requires restructuring from `QtConcurrent::run` to `QThread` subclass. Bigger diff.
- **Handle inline on main thread via readyRead signals** — cleanest async pattern but requires rewriting the entire blocking serve loop. Bigger diff.
- **WSADuplicateSocket** — 20 lines of contained platform code, zero architectural change, directly eliminates the notifier contention. Kernel-level socket duplication is a standard Windows pattern for handoff.

Chose the smallest-diff fix that provably closes the bug.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) — `<winsock2.h>` + `<windows.h>` includes under `#ifdef _WIN32`; `onNewConnection` rewritten with duplicate-fd path + fallback.

No header change. No CMake change. No sidecar change.

### Diagnostic logs stay for now

The `probeLog` sites + `probeLog` helper remain in place. They're cheap (write-on-access to `_player_debug.txt`), and keeping them through Hemanth's verification run gives us trace data if anything else surfaces. I'll remove them in a cleanup batch once this fix is confirmed closed.

### Re-verification

1. Rebuild main app. No sidecar rebuild needed.
2. Cold-start a torrent playback. Watch `_player_debug.txt`:
   - Expected new line: `[probe-audit] duplicated fd: worker desc=N (orig=M)` where N ≠ M.
   - Expected next lines: `worker enter` → `setSocketDescriptor ok` → `waitForReadyRead=OK elapsed=<milliseconds, not 5001>` → `requestLine='GET /stream/... HTTP/1.1'` → `writing GET response header ... bytes` → `header flushed=1`.
   - ffmpeg proceeds past probe; first frame renders.
3. Regression: kill the sidecar during playback (or Back out mid-stream) → `probe-audit` trace shows clean connection teardown via ConnectionGuard.
4. Regression: 404 (invalid URL) and 416 (invalid range via curl) — trace shows `requestLine=` with the bogus URL, then normal error response written.
5. Fallback-path sanity: if WSADuplicateSocketW ever returns an error, the `[probe-audit] fallback to legacy 2-socket handoff` line fires — in practice this should never trigger on a modern Windows stack but the path exists.

### Why I'm confident this time

Previous three shipped fixes were analysis-driven. This one is evidence-driven — the trace isolated the symptom to exactly the surface the audit flagged as P0 #2, and the fix directly eliminates that surface. If the trace post-rebuild still shows TIMEOUT despite the `duplicated fd` line appearing, then my hypothesis about the notifier contention was wrong and we'd need a deeper look; but the evidence chain is tight enough that I believe this closes it.

Still submitting as READY TO COMMIT rather than declaring it fixed — Hemanth's smoke is the source of truth.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX socket handoff fix]: WSADuplicateSocket replaces the two-QTcpSocket-per-fd antipattern that caused the cold-start probe failure. Trace at _player_debug.txt confirmed waitForReadyRead=TIMEOUT at 5001ms with bytesAvail=0 — audit P0 #2 ("orphaned original socket's notifier consumes readiness events") — fix gives the worker an independent fd via WSADuplicateSocketW + WSASocketW, closes original cleanly. Fallback to legacy path if duplicate fails. Windows-only (app is Windows-only, ws2_32 already linked). probeLog tracing retained for post-fix verification. | files: src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp

Standing by on rebuild + smoke.

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-16 — Agent 4B introduced to the roster (Sources split)

Hemanth 2026-04-16: Agent 4's prior combined **Stream & Sources** scope is split into twin agents. Agent 4 retains Stream mode. **Agent 4B is born and owns the Sources surface (Tankorent + Tankoyomi) end-to-end.**

This resolves a structural gap flagged during Tankorent audit prep: Tankorent + Tankoyomi had no settled domain agent. Work touched Agent 0 and Agent 4 inconsistently. Splitting Sources into Agent 4B gives it a clear owner without bloating Agent 4's Stream-mode scope.

### Agent 4B ownership

Primary files (per GOVERNANCE.md Domain Ownership table, updated this turn):
- **Pages:** `src/ui/pages/SourcesPage.*`, `src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.*`, `src/ui/pages/TankoyomiPage.*`
- **Torrent backend:** `src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.*`, `src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.*`, `src/core/TorrentIndexer.h`, `src/core/TorrentResult.h`
- **Indexers:** `src/core/indexers/*` — all 7 current indexers (Nyaa, PirateBay, 1337x, YTS, EZTV, ExtraTorrents, TorrentsCsv) + any future additions
- **Manga:** `src/core/manga/*` (MangaDownloader, WeebCentralScraper, ReadComicsScraper)
- **Dialogs:** `src/ui/dialogs/AddTorrentDialog.*`, `src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.*`, `src/ui/dialogs/AddMangaDialog.*`, `src/ui/dialogs/SpeedLimitDialog.*`, `src/ui/dialogs/SeedingRulesDialog.*`, `src/ui/dialogs/QueueLimitsDialog.*`

### Agent 4 (Stream mode) — unchanged otherwise

Agent 4 retains:
- `src/ui/pages/StreamPage.*`
- Entire `src/ui/pages/stream/*` subtree (StreamHomeBoard, StreamContinueStrip, StreamDetailView, StreamSourceCard/List/Choice, StreamLibraryLayout, CalendarScreen, AddonManager, etc.)
- Entire `src/core/stream/*` subtree (MetaAggregator, StreamAggregator, StreamProgress, CalendarEngine, AddonRegistry, AddonTransport, MetaItem, StreamHttpServer, etc.)

In-flight Stream-mode work is unaffected — STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md and STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX stay with Agent 4.

### Coordination protocol

**Cross-agent Stream ↔ Sources** — if Stream mode ever consumes Tankorent indexers as a stream source (future possibility), standard heads-up protocol applies. Right now there is NO cross-agent coupling: Stream mode uses Stremio-style addons, Sources uses torrent indexers + manga scrapers. Distinct backends.

**Cross-agent Sources ↔ Library UX** — Agent 4B's page-level files (TankorentPage, TankoyomiPage, SourcesPage) fall under standard Agent 5 library-UX overlap per `feedback_agent5_scope`. Same pattern as Agent 1's ComicsPage / Agent 2's BooksPage / Agent 3's VideosPage — Agent 4B owns source-specific internals, Agent 5 owns library-shell UX. Day-to-day UX polish on those pages routes to Agent 5 first.

**Cross-agent Sources ↔ Coordinator** — historical Tankorent work that touched Agent 0 territory (CMakeLists.txt, MainWindow) uses standard Rule 7 (flag CMake touches in chat, exact lines).

- **agents/STATUS.md** — Agent 4 header renamed to "Stream mode" with scope-narrowing note; new Agent 4B section added (status: newly established, awaiting first summon).
- **agents/chat.md** — this post.
- **memory `project_agent4_twin.md` + `project_multiagent.md`** — updated.

Documents NOT touched: agents/CONTRACTS.md, agents/HELP.md, agents/CONGRESS.md, agents/REVIEW.md — none of them enumerate the roster explicitly, so no updates needed. Reading order from `feedback_agent_chat` memory remains unchanged.

Agent 4B's first summoned task will be observation-only validation of the audit's P0s + high-priority P1s — same methodology Agent 1 used for the comic reader audit today, Agent 2 used for book reader, Agent 3 used for video player. ## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX — diagnostic cleanup + Batch 3.3 + phase-close

Two shipments + three review hand-offs.

### Shipment 1 — diagnostic cleanup

Removed the `probe-audit` instrumentation from [StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) now that the socket-handoff bug is confirmed closed:
- `probeLog` helper function removed.
- `#include <QDateTime>` + `<QTextStream>` includes removed (were only for probeLog).
- 8 `probeLog(...)` call sites in `handleConnection` + `onNewConnection` removed.
- `qWarning` kept on genuine error paths in `onNewConnection` (WSASocketW failure, WSADuplicateSocketW failure, legacy-fallback trigger) — these are low-frequency production-debugging signals, not per-connection spam.

[StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) now reads as production code again. WSADuplicateSocketW fix retained as the final architecture.

### Shipment 2 — STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX Batch 3.3: session-settings tune

[TorrentEngine.cpp — applySettings](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp) — five changes against the "less aggressive than streaming" baseline ([original comment at pre-3.3 line 194](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L194)):

| Setting | Before | After | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| `connections_limit` | 200 | 400 | More peer candidates for `set_piece_deadline()` to pick the fastest. |
| `active_downloads` | 5 | 10 | Stream torrent no longer queued behind library-mode downloads. |
| `active_seeds` | 5 | 10 | Same rationale. |
| `active_limit` | 10 | 20 | Combined cap matches active_downloads + active_seeds. |
| `max_queued_disk_bytes` | default (1 MB) | 32 MB | Absorbs piece-write bursts during head-deadline fan-out. |
| `request_queue_time` | default (3s) | 10 | Deeper per-peer request queue so one slow peer doesn't stall the reader. |

`request_timeout` (10s) and `peer_timeout` (20s) kept — aggressive dropout is good for streaming.

Reference: Stremio's [streaming_server/settings.rs](C:/Users/Suprabha/Downloads/Stremio%20Reference/stremio-core-development/stremio-core-development/src/types/streaming_server/settings.rs) + libtorrent streaming guidance at https://www.libtorrent.org/streaming.html.

### Files

- [src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp) — diagnostic cleanup
- [src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp) — session settings tuned

### Re-verification

**Cleanup smoke:**
1. Cold-start a torrent stream. `_player_debug.txt` should no longer accumulate `[probe-audit] ...` lines. Playback works identically.
2. Stream still plays; WSADuplicateSocketW path still active (the fix stays).

**Batch 3.3 smoke:**
3. Click-to-play latency on a well-seeded torrent: should be same or slightly faster than the post-probe-fix baseline. Metric is anecdotal unless we add timing telemetry.
4. Concurrent stream + library download: previously, 5 active-downloads limit could starve the stream if 5+ prior torrents were running. Now 10 active + stream gets priority-7 file slot.
5. Regression: seeding/library download behavior unaffected — settings apply globally but the net effect is "more parallel work allowed", not "different work".
6. Regression: memory footprint. 32 MB max_queued_disk_bytes is a peak, not steady-state. On an idle desktop with one stream it hovers well below.

### Phase 3 exit

All three non-deferred Phase 3 batches shipped:
- 3.1 startup gate (restored at 5 MB after probe regression)
- 3.2 buffering % shows gate progress
- 3.3 session-settings tune

Plus one emergency fix outside the original plan: **socket-handoff bugfix** (WSADuplicateSocketW). Audit-confirmed root cause of the "cannot open file: probe failed" recurrence.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 4 Batch 4.2 shipped

Search history dropdown on focus. Persists last 20 queries; renders top 10 with per-row remove.

### What shipped

**[StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h)** — 8 new private slots/helpers (`buildSearchHistoryDropdown`, `loadSearchHistory`, `saveSearchHistory`, `pushSearchHistory`, `removeSearchHistoryEntry`, `showSearchHistoryDropdown`, `hideSearchHistoryDropdown`, `positionSearchHistoryDropdown`) + `eventFilter` override. New members: `QFrame* m_searchHistoryDropdown`, `QWidget* m_searchHistoryList`, `QTimer* m_searchHistoryHideTimer`, `QStringList m_searchHistory`, `kMaxSearchHistory=20`, `kDisplaySearchHistory=10`.

**[StreamPage.cpp — buildSearchBar](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — 3 new calls at the end: `loadSearchHistory()`, `buildSearchHistoryDropdown()`, `m_searchInput->installEventFilter(this)`.

**[StreamPage.cpp — onSearchSubmit / onSearchDebounceFired](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — both call `pushSearchHistory(query)` + `hideSearchHistoryDropdown()` before firing the search. Dedup logic removes prior exact match before prepending, so re-running an old query moves it to the top without duplicating.

**[StreamPage.cpp — onSearchTextChanged](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — empty-field path now calls `showSearchHistoryDropdown()` if the input has focus. Non-empty path calls `hideSearchHistoryDropdown()` so history doesn't obscure the results.

**[StreamPage.cpp — showBrowse](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — defensive dropdown hide when navigating away.

**[StreamPage.cpp — eventFilter](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — new override. On `FocusIn` of m_searchInput: if text is empty, show dropdown. On `FocusOut`: start 150ms hide timer (gives click-on-row events time to register before the dropdown disappears).

### Dropdown construction

`QFrame` child of StreamPage (NOT the search-bar frame) so it can float above the browse / detail / player layers via `raise()`. Positioned manually at show-time via `m_searchInput->mapTo(this, QPoint(0, h+2))` to align under the input. Width matches input; height is content-driven.

**Row widget:** each history entry is a `QHBoxLayout` containing:
- A flat `QPushButton` (query text, text-align left) that fills the input and triggers `onSearchSubmit` on click.
- A small `×` button that removes the single entry via `removeSearchHistoryEntry`.

Hover styling differentiates (subtle white overlay on the query button). Remove button lightens on hover. Grayscale only per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`.

### Persistence

- QSettings key: `stream/searchHistory` (QStringList).
- Max 20 entries persisted, top 10 rendered in the dropdown.
- `pushSearchHistory` removes dupes before prepending (chronological with dedup).
- `removeSearchHistoryEntry` removes by exact match.
- Defensive clamp on load in case stale settings exceed the cap.
- `loadSearchHistory` called once in `buildSearchBar`; saves happen on every mutation.

### Design choices

- **Empty-field focus shows dropdown; typing hides it.** Matches Stremio / browser address-bar behavior. The user either browses history or types a new query, not both at once.
- **Delayed hide via 150ms `QTimer`.** Without this, clicking a dropdown row would race with `FocusOut` — the dropdown would vanish before the click dispatched. 150ms is the minimum that reliably survives a mousedown→mouseup cycle on Windows.
- **`eventFilter` on `m_searchInput`, not subclassed QLineEdit.** No custom widget, no header cost; one virtual override on StreamPage. Filter returns false on the focus events (they propagate normally) and just triggers our UI side-effects.
- **Dropdown hidden when history empty.** No "Your search history is empty" placeholder — an empty dropdown on a focus event is visual noise.
- **`onSearchSubmit` used for row-click path** — reuses the exact path Enter / Search button take. Gets the debounce stop, busy spinner, browse hide, and search fire without duplicating logic.
- **Manual positioning instead of `QCompleter`.** QCompleter would handle positioning automatically but forces its own styling / lifecycle. Manual `QFrame` is 20 more lines but matches the chip-row / toast pattern already used on the page.
- **No right-click context menu.** The × button per row is explicit enough for v1. A "Clear all" button was explicitly deferred in the TODO ("simple is fine").

### Known edges (acceptable for v1)

- **Dropdown doesn't reposition on window resize.** It'd orphan at the old x/y if the user resizes while the dropdown is open. Rare interaction; next `FocusOut`+`FocusIn` cycle fixes it. Could add a `resizeEvent` override later.
- **No keyboard navigation** (arrow-up / down / enter on dropdown rows). Mouse-only. Keyboard users can still type to dismiss + fire the search.
- **Dropdown stays visible if user clicks elsewhere on StreamPage without moving focus.** Only `FocusOut` on the input hides it. In practice clicking a non-focusable area (background) doesn't take focus, so the dropdown lingers. Clicking any button or tile DOES take focus, which fires FocusOut → hide. Acceptable.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h) — 8 new decls + 6 new members + eventFilter override
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — includes (`QEvent`, `QFocusEvent`, `QScrollArea`, `QSettings`); buildSearchBar additions; 8 helper impls + eventFilter

No CMake change. No new files. No header contract change for any consumer.

### Re-verification

1. Fresh launch, no history — focus search input. Expected: dropdown doesn't appear (empty-history case). Type "Breaking Bad" → search fires → close app → reopen → focus input → dropdown shows "Breaking Bad".
2. Search a few titles ("matrix", "interstellar", "breaking bad"). Expected: each appears at top of dropdown; most recent first.
3. Re-search an existing entry via typing. Expected: entry moves to top (dedup), not duplicated.
4. Click a history row. Expected: input populates, search fires, results panel appears.
5. Click the × on a row. Expected: row disappears; persists across restart.
6. Type mid-history-view. Expected: dropdown hides (live results take over).
7. Click elsewhere (Addons button, Calendar). Expected: dropdown hides via `FocusOut`.
8. Enter key on a row. Expected: N/A for v1 (no keyboard nav); pressing Enter in the field itself fires search.
9. Regression: Batch 4.1 live search + spinner behavior unchanged — history path is additive.
10. Regression: empty-input clears search widget + restores browse (existing behavior).

### STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 4 progress

- 4.1 (live search + spinner) — shipped
- 4.2 (history dropdown) — shipped this batch
- 4.3 (URL/magnet paste) — one batch remains to close Phase 4

Phase 5 (addon configurable support) after 4.3 clears.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_UX_PARITY Batch 4.2]: Search history dropdown. QSettings-persisted 20-entry chronological list with dedup on insert; top 10 rendered on empty-field focus via event filter; per-row × removes single entry; click-to-refire reuses onSearchSubmit path. QFrame float child of StreamPage, manually positioned under m_searchInput via mapTo. 150ms delayed-hide timer preserves click-through semantics on FocusOut. | files: src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp

Standing by on smoke. Batch 4.3 (URL/magnet paste handling) closes Phase 4 on your green.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 4 Batch 4.3 shipped

URL / magnet / addon-manifest paste handling in the search bar. Phase 4 closes.

### What shipped

**[StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h)** — new `enum class PasteKind { None, Magnet, DirectVideo, AddonManifest }` + 3 helpers (`detectPasteKind`, `applyPasteKindToSearchButton`, `handlePasteAction`) + 1 member (`PasteKind m_pasteKind`).

**[StreamPage.cpp — detectPasteKind](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — pattern detection with priority:
1. `magnet:?xt=urn:btih:HASH` (regex, 32+ char hash) → `Magnet`
2. `stremio://...` prefix → `AddonManifest`
3. `http(s)://...` + path ends in `/manifest.json` → `AddonManifest`
4. `http(s)://...` + path ends in `.mp4` / `.mkv` / `.m3u8` / `.webm` / `.mov` / `.avi` / `.m4v` / `.ts` → `DirectVideo`
5. Anything else → `None` (normal text search)

Plain HTTP URLs without recognized video extensions stay as `None` — the TODO's "regex-guarded" clause covers exactly this edge: "arbitrary text that happens to parse as a URL" shouldn't auto-route.

**[StreamPage.cpp — onSearchTextChanged](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — re-detects on every keystroke; when kind transitions, calls `applyPasteKindToSearchButton` to relabel the Search button. Non-None kinds stop the live-search debounce (user intent is action, not search).

**[StreamPage.cpp — onSearchSubmit](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — defensive re-detect on fire, branches into `handlePasteAction` when non-None. Normal search path untouched.

**[StreamPage.cpp — handlePasteAction](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)**:
- **AddonManifest** → constructs `AddAddonDialog(m_addonRegistry, this)`, calls new `setPrefilledUrl(s)`, `exec()`. Install flow reuses AddonRegistry's existing signals (no duplication).
- **Magnet** → extracts `xt=urn:btih:HASH` via URL query parse, builds `StreamSource::magnetSource(hash)`, wraps in a synthetic `Stream`, sets `m_pendingPlay` with `imdbId = "adhoc:" + s.left(40)` + `mediaType = "movie"`, dispatches to `m_playerController->startStream`. The "adhoc:" prefix namespaces progress persistence keys so an ad-hoc magnet never collides with real IMDB-indexed entries.
- **DirectVideo** → same pattern with `StreamSource::httpSource(QUrl(s))`.

**[AddAddonDialog.h/.cpp](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp)** — new public `setPrefilledUrl(const QString&)` method: sets `m_urlInput` text + positions cursor at end. Callable between construction and `exec()`; user can still edit/clear before clicking Install.

### Design choices

- **Button-label repurpose, not a separate prompt chip.** TODO said "small inline prompt"; button relabel reads cleanly as a prompt without adding a separate widget that competes with spinner + history dropdown for layout space. "Play this stream" / "Install this addon" are the exact phrasings the TODO spec recommends.
- **Defensive re-detect on submit.** If the user pastes + presses Enter in the same frame, `onSearchTextChanged` may not have run yet. Re-detecting in `onSearchSubmit` guarantees the right branch even on fast paste-and-Enter sequences.
- **"adhoc:" imdbId prefix.** Progress persistence uses `imdbId` as a key at [StreamProgress.cpp](src/core/stream/StreamProgress.h). Without a prefix, random hash-ish strings could theoretically collide with real IMDB ids; the prefix makes collision impossible and the key greppable.
- **`s.left(40)` on the imdbId.** Avoids absurdly long keys from huge magnet URIs with many trackers. 40 chars is enough to uniquely identify by the btih hash prefix.
- **AddonRegistry re-check via `m_addonRegistry` existence.** Defensive null-check — if someone constructs StreamPage without a registry (shouldn't happen in the normal codepath), the install action no-ops silently.
- **No HTTP URL without a recognized extension.** Conservative — matches Peerflix's "don't guess" philosophy. If Hemanth wants broader detection (e.g. any http URL without an extension → try as stream), that's a follow-up.
- **No keyboard binding for paste action separate from Enter.** The Search button + Enter are the two paths users already know. A dedicated Ctrl+V handler would be scope creep.

### Phase 4 progress summary

- **4.1 — live search with debounce + spinner** ✓
- **4.2 — history dropdown on focus** ✓
- **4.3 — URL/magnet paste routing** ✓ (this batch)

Phase 4 exit criteria met:
- Live search works with debounce.
- History dropdown on focus.
- URL/magnet pasting routes correctly.
- Agent 6 review against audit P1 #4.

### Known limitations (acceptable for v1)

- **No intent-change undo.** If the user pastes a URL, the button flips to "Play this stream"; if they immediately keep typing past the URL (turning it into non-URL text), the button reverts to "Search" via the live re-detect. But there's no explicit "never mind, search for this URL as text" escape hatch. Rare edge; Enter still routes based on current kind.
- **Direct-video URL kind routes through StreamEngine's addon-aware `streamFile(Stream&)` overload** → `Url/Http → immediate DirectUrl result` path. If a directly-pasted HTTP URL is not actually seekable / is behind a paywall / redirects, the sidecar's HTTP retry logic takes over ([video_decoder.cpp:834](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L834)) and surfaces any eventual error via the existing `streamFailed` path. Not a dedicated UX; inherits the same robustness as addon-provided Url streams.
- **Manifest detection only matches `/manifest.json`-suffixed URLs and `stremio://` scheme.** Some addon hosts may use unusual paths; users can still open Addon Manager → Add Addon manually in those cases.

### Re-verification

1. Paste `magnet:?xt=urn:btih:0123456789ABCDEF0123456789ABCDEF01234567` → Search button relabels to "Play this stream" → Enter → buffer overlay shows → playback starts through existing StreamEngine + VideoPlayer pipeline.
2. Paste `https://example.com/video.mp4` → "Play this stream" → Enter → direct-URL playback (sidecar uses its HTTP path, not torrent).
3. Paste `https://v3-cinemeta.strem.io/manifest.json` → "Install this addon" → Enter → AddAddonDialog opens with URL pre-filled → user clicks Install → existing registry install flow.
4. Paste `stremio://example.com/addon/manifest.json` → "Install this addon" → same as above.
5. Paste plain text "breaking bad" → "Search" label stays → Enter → normal catalog search via MetaAggregator (unchanged from 4.1/4.2).
6. Paste a URL, clear it, type text → button flips Search-only. History dropdown logic still behaves.
7. Paste a URL + immediately click × (QLineEdit clear button) → button resets to Search. Browse layer returns.
8. Regression: Batch 4.1 live-search spinner + 4.2 history dropdown both continue working in the non-URL path.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h) — enum + 3 helpers + 1 member
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — 4 new includes; detection + routing impls; onSearchSubmit + onSearchTextChanged updates
- [src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.h](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.h) — `setPrefilledUrl` decl
- [src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp) — `setPrefilledUrl` impl

No CMake change.

Regex-guarded detectPasteKind (magnet / direct-video extensions / manifest.json / stremio:// → kind enum). Live re-detection on textChanged swaps Search button label to "Play this stream" / "Install this addon". Enter routes to handlePasteAction: AddonManifest opens AddAddonDialog with pre-filled URL via new setPrefilledUrl; Magnet + DirectVideo synthesize a Stream (magnetSource / httpSource) and dispatch through StreamPlayerController with "adhoc:" imdbId prefix for progress-key isolation. | files: src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp, src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.h, src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 5 Batch 5.1 shipped

Configure button on AddonDetailPanel.

### What shipped

**[AddonDetailPanel.h](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.h)** — new `QPushButton* m_configureBtn` member + `onConfigureClicked()` slot.

**[AddonDetailPanel.cpp — buildUI](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp)** — Configure button added after the Enabled checkbox, before Uninstall. Hidden by default; `setDescriptor` toggles visibility per-addon.

Grayscale styling per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`: `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` background, `rgba(255,255,255,0.18)` border, hover state brightens both. Intentionally distinct from the DANGER-red Uninstall palette.

**[AddonDetailPanel.cpp — setDescriptor](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp)** — one-line toggle:
```cpp
m_configureBtn->setVisible(d.manifest.behaviorHints.configurable);
```

Visible only for addons that advertise `behaviorHints.configurable=true` in their manifest. Already parsed by AddonRegistry at [Manifest.h:14](src/core/stream/addon/Manifest.h#L14); we just surface it in the UI.

**[AddonDetailPanel.cpp — onConfigureClicked](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp)** — resolves the configure URL and hands off to `QDesktopServices::openUrl`:
1. Start with `transportUrl.toString()`.
2. Strip trailing `/manifest.json` (addon-SDK convention: configure is a sibling path).
3. Strip any trailing slashes (avoids `//configure`).
4. Append `/configure`.

Example: `https://cinemeta.strem.io/manifest.json` → `https://cinemeta.strem.io/configure`. For already-trimmed bases like `https://example.com/addon/` → `https://example.com/addon/configure`.

### Design choices

- **System browser over in-app webview.** TODO explicit: "No in-app webview for configure — opening in the system browser is the standard Stremio desktop pattern and avoids hosting config UIs we don't control." Follows that.
- **Hidden button, not disabled.** Less visual clutter for non-configurable addons (the vast majority). A disabled-but-visible button would invite wondering "why can't I click this?".
- **Manifest-URL trimming in the click handler, not in setDescriptor.** Keeps the trimming logic close to the single place that uses it. If the convention ever drifts (e.g. addons using `/config` instead of `/configure`), one site to change.
- **No visual distinction for "configuration required".** Batch 5.2 handles that variant at install-time (AddAddonDialog changes its Install button to "Configure & Install"); on the detail panel for already-installed addons, `configurable` is the flag that matters — the addon's been installed so `configurationRequired` is moot at this point.
- **No handling of addons that serve configure on a totally unconventional path.** The SDK standard is `/configure`. Addons that violate this... users will see the browser fail to load. Acceptable v1 behavior; rare in practice.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.h](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.h) — 1 slot + 1 member
- [src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp) — `<QDesktopServices>` + `<QUrl>` includes; button construction in buildUI; visibility toggle in setDescriptor; click handler impl

No CMake change. No other file touched.

### Re-verification

1. Install a configurable test addon (any Stremio addon with `behaviorHints.configurable=true` in its manifest — Real-Debrid / Torrentio with config / many community addons). Open Addon Manager → select the addon → Configure button visible below Enabled, above Uninstall.
2. Click Configure → default browser opens to the addon's configure page (e.g. `https://torrentio.strem.fun/configure` for the common one).
3. Install a non-configurable addon (Cinemeta, etc.) → Configure button hidden entirely. UI looks like pre-5.1.
4. Enable/disable the selected addon → Configure button visibility unchanged (it tracks `configurable`, not `enabled`).
5. Switch between addons in the list → Configure button's visibility updates on each selectDescriptor call.
6. Regression: Uninstall flow unaffected. Enabled toggle unaffected. Protected-addon note unaffected.

### Files touched summary (against Phase 5 scope)

- Batch 5.1 touches [AddonDetailPanel](src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp) — already declared as the scope in STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md Phase 5 Batch 5.1.
- Batch 5.2 will touch [AddAddonDialog](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp) for the configure-first install flow.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, STREAM_UX_PARITY Batch 5.1]: Configure button on AddonDetailPanel for addons with behaviorHints.configurable=true. Button hidden for non-configurable addons (visibility toggled in setDescriptor). Grayscale styling distinct from DANGER-red Uninstall. Click handler trims trailing /manifest.json from transportUrl, appends /configure, opens via QDesktopServices::openUrl (Stremio desktop convention — no in-app webview). | files: src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.h, src/ui/pages/stream/AddonDetailPanel.cpp

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY Phase 5 Batch 5.2 shipped

Configuration-required install flow in AddAddonDialog. Phase 5 closes. STREAM_UX_PARITY_TODO.md **complete**.

### What shipped

**[AddAddonDialog.h](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.h)** — new Phase enum state machine:

```cpp
enum class Phase {
    Idle,                  // user types URL, button = "Install"
    Probing,               // probe inflight
    AwaitOpenConfigure,    // button = "Open Configure Page"
    AwaitConfiguredUrl,    // button = "Install" (expects new URL)
    Installing             // registry->installByUrl() in flight
};
```

Plus two new probe-signal slots (`onProbeManifestReady`, `onProbeManifestFailed`), two helpers (`enterPhase`, `startProbe`), a dedicated `AddonTransport* m_probe` for manifest introspection, and `m_configureUrl` for the computed `/configure` URL.

**[AddAddonDialog.cpp](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp)**:

Flow:
1. **Idle** → user pastes manifest URL, clicks Install.
2. **Probing** → dedicated `m_probe` (AddonTransport) fetches manifest. `setBusy`-equivalent state disables input + button.
3. On manifest arrival, branch:
   - `configurationRequired=false` → **Installing** → `m_registry->installByUrl(m_pendingUrl)` (registry does its own manifest fetch + validate + persist; our probe was advisory).
   - `configurationRequired=true` → compute `/configure` URL (same trim-`/manifest.json` + append logic as Batch 5.1) → **AwaitOpenConfigure** → button relabels to "Open Configure Page" → status label explains next step.
4. User clicks "Open Configure Page" → `QDesktopServices::openUrl(m_configureUrl)` → **AwaitConfiguredUrl** → input re-enabled, cleared, placeholder changes to "Paste the configured manifest URL from your browser here" → focus set to input.
5. User pastes the new (configured) URL + clicks Install → `startProbe` again with new URL. If the configured URL STILL reports `configurationRequired=true` (user didn't complete the form), loops back to step 3. If clean, installs.

### Design choices

- **Probe via dedicated `AddonTransport` instance, not `AddonRegistry`.** Using `installByUrl` would commit to install before we know if the addon needs configuration. A separate transport lets the dialog peek + branch without touching registry state. Tradeoff: configurable-false path pays one extra HTTP fetch (probe + registry's own fetch). Acceptable — install is a user-driven operation, not a hot path.
- **Phase enum state machine, not individual booleans.** 5 states, 4 transitions — explicit is easier to reason about than `m_probing` + `m_awaitConfigure` + `m_awaitConfiguredUrl` bitfields. Also lets `enterPhase` centralize the UI-enabled/label/focus logic.
- **`/configure` URL computation mirrors Batch 5.1.** Strip trailing `/manifest.json`, strip trailing slashes, append `/configure`. Consistent with the button-open logic. Comment explicitly cross-references the other site.
- **Input re-enabled with focus shift to the URL field on AwaitConfiguredUrl.** Users complete configure in browser, switch back to dialog, and can immediately paste (Ctrl+V) without hunting for the input. Focus + setPlaceholderText carries the instructional weight.
- **Re-probe on AwaitConfiguredUrl click.** Two reasons: (a) validate the user really pasted a configured manifest (not the same unconfigured one by accident); (b) some addons return the configured URL as a Base64-in-path redirect that points back to the same `/manifest.json` but with a different query — re-probing confirms the addon's state.
- **stale-probe guard via `m_phase != Phase::Probing` check.** If the user clicks Cancel during Probing, the dialog closes but the probe may still complete. The phase check stops us from reacting to a dead probe.
- **`onInstallSucceeded` / `onInstallFailed` gated on `Phase::Installing`.** Previously gated on `m_pendingUrl.isValid()`; the phase check is stricter and correctly excludes stale registry signals that might fire from a prior install attempt.
- **No auto-close after `QDesktopServices::openUrl`.** User still has the dialog open, can paste back the configured URL without reopening. Stremio's desktop flow does the same.
- **setBusy left as a stub (unused but harmless).** Legacy from the pre-phase-machine code; dead but deletable in a later cleanup. Not scope-creeping into this batch.

### User-visible flow example

Install a `configurationRequired=true` addon like Torrentio with config:
1. User clicks Addons → Add → pastes `https://torrentio.strem.fun/manifest.json`.
2. Dialog: "Checking addon manifest..."
3. Probe arrives → `configurationRequired=true` → dialog: "This addon requires configuration. Click 'Open Configure Page' to complete setup in your browser; then paste the configured manifest URL back into this field." Button: **Open Configure Page**.
4. User clicks button → browser opens to `https://torrentio.strem.fun/configure`. User selects providers, gets redirected to a new URL like `https://torrentio.strem.fun/CONFIGBASE64/manifest.json`.
5. User copies the new URL from browser address bar, switches back to dialog, pastes into the (now cleared) input. Button: **Install**.
6. User clicks Install → re-probe confirms ready → install proceeds → "Installed: Torrentio".

### Files

- [src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.h](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.h) — Phase enum + 2 probe slots + 2 helpers + 3 new members
- [src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp](src/ui/dialogs/AddAddonDialog.cpp) — `<QDesktopServices>` + `AddonTransport` includes; ctor wires probe transport signals; onInstallClicked dispatches by phase; startProbe/onProbeManifestReady/onProbeManifestFailed/enterPhase impls; onInstallSucceeded/onInstallFailed gated on Phase::Installing

No CMake change (AddonTransport already linked for core/stream/addon targets).

### Re-verification

1. Install a non-configurable addon (Cinemeta): type URL → Install → dialog shows "Checking addon manifest..." briefly → "Installing addon..." → "Installed: Cinemeta" → closes. One extra probe-HTTP-call compared to pre-5.2 but imperceptible.
2. Install a `configurationRequired=true` addon (Torrentio-with-config, or any test addon serving that manifest flag): type URL → Install → probe arrives → dialog shows configure instructions → button relabels to "Open Configure Page".
3. Click "Open Configure Page" → browser opens; dialog's input clears with the "Paste the configured manifest URL..." placeholder.
4. Complete configure in browser, copy new URL, paste into dialog, click Install → probe + install proceed → Success.
5. Edge: user clicks Open Configure Page, doesn't complete in browser, pastes the ORIGINAL unconfigured URL back. Expected: re-probe still reports configurationRequired=true → loops back to step 2's state. User can't accidentally install an unconfigured addon.
6. Edge: user pastes an invalid URL post-configure. Expected: `onProbeManifestFailed` → status shows error → phase returns to Idle (user can try again).
7. Edge: user clicks Cancel during Probing. Expected: dialog closes. Any late probe callback is no-op'd via phase check.
8. Regression: Batch 4.3 URL-paste path → opens AddAddonDialog → pre-filled URL fires through the new probe flow → works identically for both configurable + non-configurable addons.
9. Regression: dialog-opened-via-Addons-button (not URL-paste) path still works, phase machine starts at Idle correctly.

### Phase 5 exit

- 5.1 (Configure button on AddonDetailPanel) ✓
- 5.2 (configurationRequired install flow) ✓

Phase 5 exit criteria met:
- Configurable addons surface a Configure button in their detail panel.
- Configuration-required addons route through the configure-first install flow.
- Agent 6 review against audit P1 #8 queued.
