## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — TANKORENT AUDIT VALIDATION FINDINGS

Observation-only pass complete. Audit read end-to-end. Every cited file:line re-verified against live code. Zero code touched. Findings use the ratified format — `CONFIRMED BROKEN` / `SCOPE-BOUNDED` / `PARTIAL` against audit hypothesis, identity direction per memory `project_tankorent_identity.md` applied.

### Search P0s

**[SEARCH-P0-1] PARTIAL + SCOPE-BOUNDED.** Indexer set is genuinely compile-time baked: [TankorentPage.cpp:652-663](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L652-L663) hard-codes the 7 source-combo items; [TankorentPage.cpp:720-733](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L720-L733) uses a compile-time `addIf(id, new NyaaIndexer(...))` fan-out with concrete C++ types. Zero runtime enable/disable, zero credential UI, zero per-indexer status view. **SCOPE-BOUNDED portion:** Jackett/Prowlarr's Cardigann YAML-definition pipeline + runtime tracker-adding is explicitly out-of-scope per identity direction #1 ("middle ground, no cardigann"). **PARTIAL-broken portion (in-scope):** runtime config UI (per-indexer enable/disable, credential/cookie entry for cookie-gated trackers), per-indexer health/failure surfacing (last error + auth state + response time), and typed category routing — identity direction calls these in-scope, and none currently exist.

**[SEARCH-P0-2] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** [TankorentPage.cpp:727-733](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L727-L733) has `addIf` calls for nyaa/piratebay/yts/eztv/exttorrents/torrentscsv. Line :729 is literally `// 1337x disabled — Cloudflare JS challenge` — commented-out dispatch. [populateSourceCombo](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L652-L663) also omits 1337x from the user-facing picker. **Citation correction:** audit cited [X1337xIndexer.cpp:201-202](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp#L201-L202) as Cloudflare evidence, but those lines are the `sourceName="1337x"; sourceKey="1337x";` assignments inside the successful-parse loop. The X1337xIndexer class at [X1337xIndexer.cpp:1-224](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp) is a FULL scraper (Mozilla UA, HTML `<tbody>`/`<tr>`/`<td>` parsing at parseListPage:62-80, detail-page fetch, magnet extraction). It has NO challenge-solving code — no cookie acquisition, no headless browser escape, no JS-execution capability. The 1337x "disabled" state lives purely at the page dispatch level (:729). Identity direction #2 requires this reached.

**[SEARCH-P0-3] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** Straight bug. `m_searchTypeCombo` populated at [TankorentPage.cpp:326-333](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L326-L333) with Videos/Books/Audiobooks/Comics data values. In [startSearch :701-749](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L701-L749), lines :715-716 read `m_sourceCombo->currentData()` and `m_categoryCombo->currentData()` — `m_searchTypeCombo->currentData()` is never read, anywhere. The dispatch list :727-733 hard-codes every indexer regardless of search-type selection. Consequence: a "Books" query fires YTS (movies-only) and EZTV (TV-only); an "Audiobooks" query fires every indexer including Nyaa/YTS/ExtraTorrents. Fix path: search-type → allowed-indexer mapping (books → {PirateBay, ExtraTorrents, TorrentsCSV, 1337x}, videos → {YTS, EZTV, PirateBay, 1337x, ExtraTorrents}, etc.), then filter `addIf` calls by that mapping.

**[SEARCH-P0-4] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** [onSearchError :787-804](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L787-L804) collapses everything to two user-visible strings: `"Search Failed: <error>"` (all failed) or `"%1 Results (Some Sources Failed)"` (partial success). [onSearchFinished :765-786](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L765-L786) shows `"Searching... N Results"` or `"Done: N Results"`. No per-indexer state table, no last-error column, no auth/rate-limit/Cloudflare-blocked distinction, no response-time, no disabled-reason. `TorrentIndexer` base at [TorrentIndexer.h:14-20](src/core/TorrentIndexer.h#L14-L20) has no health/status/auth contract — only `id() / displayName() / search() / searchFinished / searchError`. Per identity direction #1, this surface is in-scope.

### Downloader P0s

**[DOWNLOADER-P0-1] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** Repo-wide grep for `AddTorrentDialog`: one construction site. [TankorentPage.cpp:1175](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L1175) inside [onAddTorrentClicked :1160-1208](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L1160-L1208) — the search-result add path. Zero other user-facing entry points. No URL/magnet entry dialog (no QInputDialog for paste-magnet anywhere). No `.torrent` file picker. No clipboard preload (`QGuiApplication::clipboard()` usage only at :963, which is the outbound "Copy magnet" path, not inbound). No drag-and-drop support on TankorentPage (grepped `dragEnter|dropEvent|setAcceptDrops`: zero hits in the page). No multi-URL ingest. [StreamEngine.cpp:140](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp#L140) calls `m_torrentEngine->addMagnet()` directly — that's stream-mode internal, NOT a user-facing Tankorent add flow. Identity #3 P0 #1 calls for URL/magnet dialog + drag-drop + clipboard preload + multi-URL.

**[DOWNLOADER-P0-2] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** Transfer context menu at [TankorentPage.cpp:1388-1528](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L1388-L1528): Resume/Pause, Force Start/Cancel, Queue submenu (Up/Down), Limits (Speed, Seeding), Advanced (Recheck, **Force Reannounce** :1475-1477, Sequential toggle), Open Folder, View Files, Copy (Name, InfoHash), Remove, Remove+DeleteFiles. Zero tracker UI — no add/remove/edit tracker, no tier editing, no tracker status list (working / not working / error / unreachable), no tracker messages, no next/min announce, no per-tracker peer/seed/leecher columns. Identity #3 P0 #2 in-scope.

**[DOWNLOADER-P0-3] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** Same context menu :1388-1528 contains no peer-list option. [Transfers table :584-620](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L584-L620) shows Seeds/Peers as aggregate count columns (4-5). No detail-panel drilldown exists — there's no propertieswidget equivalent. No peer ban action, no country/client/progress/speed per-peer table, no peer-add flow. Identity #3 P0 #3 in-scope.

**[DOWNLOADER-P0-4] CONFIRMED BROKEN — UI-ONLY fix scope.** [TorrentFilesDialog.cpp:1-107](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.cpp) (the full file — audit cited `:39-68`, but the read-only nature is the whole dialog): 3-column tree (Name/Size/Progress), `selectionMode = NoSelection` at :47, no context menu, no priority column, no rename, no open-file, no open-containing-folder. Only UI action is a Close button (:60-63). **Scoping supplement for TODO authoring — this is UI-only work:** [TorrentEngine::setFilePriorities(infoHash, priorities)](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L485-L496) already exists at engine.cpp:485, declared at [TorrentEngine.h:64](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h#L64), calls `handle.prioritize_files(prio)` on the live torrent handle. It's currently only invoked at add-time from [TorrentClient.cpp:157](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp#L157), but the runtime API is already in place. TorrentFilesDialog just needs a priority column + context menu mirroring [AddTorrentDialog.cpp:548-589](src/ui/dialogs/AddTorrentDialog.cpp#L548-L589) — no new engine work. Identity #3 P0 #4 in-scope.

### Selected high-priority P1s

**[SEARCH-P1-1] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** [TorrentResult.h:8-18](src/core/TorrentResult.h#L8-L18) schema = 9 fields only: `title, magnetUri, sizeBytes, seeders, leechers, sourceName, sourceKey, categoryId, category`. No canonical info-hash field (info-hash is extracted ad-hoc from `magnetUri` via btih regex at [:824-836](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L824-L836) for dedup — if the regex misses, the whole magnet string is the dedup key, which fragments duplicates across indexers that emit slightly-different magnets for the same infohash). No publish date, no details URL, no files, no grabs, no language, no media IDs, no tracker factors, no freeleech flags. Fix path: promote info-hash to a first-class `QString infoHash;` field, populated per-indexer at parse time, used as the dedup/sort key in place of the regex fallback.

**[DOWNLOADER-P1-1] CONFIRMED BROKEN.** [TorrentEngine.cpp:658-699](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L658-L699): `setSeedingRules` stores `(ratioLimit, seedTimeLimitSecs)` per-torrent or globally. [checkSeedingRules :673-699](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L673-L699) iterates seeding torrents and calls `rec.handle.pause()` when either threshold crosses. **Action = pause only.** No remove, no remove+content, no super-seeding target, no inactive-seeding-time metric (qBittorrent has that as a third axis per `sessionimpl.cpp:2377`). No action-choice UI on `SeedingRulesDialog` — it collects ratio/seed-time values only.

**[DOWNLOADER-P1-3] CONFIRMED BROKEN (+ one audit citation addition).** Audit cited [applySettings :169-220](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L169-L220) for hard-coded DHT/LSD/NAT-PMP/UPnP. Verified at :182-185. **Additional finding the audit missed — encryption policy is also baked:** [TorrentEngine.cpp:242-243](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp#L242-L243) hard-codes `in_enc_policy = pe_enabled` and `out_enc_policy = pe_enabled`. Zero settings panel exposes any of these — no "Preferences → Connection" UI in Tankorent, no session-settings surface at all. If DOWNLOADER-P1-3 becomes a fix target, encryption policy belongs in the same settings panel.

### P0 misses / correctness bugs Codex didn't cover

Nothing in correctness-land surfaced during this pass. Spot-checks:
- TorrentEngine mutex discipline: every reviewed method (`queuePositionUp/Down`, `setSpeedLimits`, `setSeedingRules`, `setFilePriorities`, `addMagnet`, `allStatuses`, `torrentFiles`, `checkSeedingRules`, `forceReannounce`) opens with `QMutexLocker lock(&m_mutex)` before touching `m_records` or `m_seedingRules`. No obvious holes in the reviewed paths.
- TorrentClient's persistence ([TorrentClient.cpp:27-69](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp#L27-L69), :91-129, :376-410 cited by audit) — not re-read in depth since audit reported it as working correctly. Happy to validate specifically if TODO phases touch it.
- Thread-safety of `StreamEngine::addMagnet` path crossing into TorrentEngine: out of this audit's scope, and Agent 4's domain. Flagged only as awareness if future Sources/Stream integration work comes up.

### Scope-ceiling confirmations vs identity direction

- **Torznab/Newznab endpoint** = out of scope per identity #4. No audit finding requires it.
- **RSS automation** = out of scope per identity #3 decision. Audit P2 flagged absence; no fix needed.
- **Cardigann YAML / runtime tracker-adding** = out of scope per identity #1. Audit framed as SEARCH-P0-1 but identity narrowed to "runtime config UI only."
- **Bandwidth scheduler / alt speed limits** = out of scope per identity #3. Audit P1s on these are informational.
- **Share-action variants (remove/remove+content/super-seed)** = DOWNLOADER-P1-1 — identity #3 scope says "audit P0s only," P1 explicitly deferred. Recording for completeness; TODO should NOT include this unless Hemanth re-opens.
- **Protocol toggles UI** = DOWNLOADER-P1-3 — identity #3 scope says "audit P0s only," P1 deferred. Same note.

### TODO-authoring flags for Agent 0

1. **DOWNLOADER-P0-4 is UI-only.** Engine plumbing already exists. Phase 8 scope can stay narrow (dialog rewrite, no engine delta).
2. **SEARCH-P0-3 is a 10-line bug fix** (add `QString mediaType = m_searchTypeCombo->currentData().toString();` + gate each `addIf` by a type→indexer allowlist). The hard part is picking the allowlist; the code change is trivial. Could be Phase 1 batch 1.1.
3. **SEARCH-P0-2 (1337x) is the highest-technical-risk item.** The indexer class is already written — what's missing is the Cloudflare challenge-solving layer. Three realistic approaches: (a) cookie-capture UI where user logs in via browser, exports cookies, pastes `cf_clearance` into a per-indexer credential field; (b) embedded `QWebEngineView` that navigates to 1337x.to, waits for Cloudflare JS to complete, harvests cookies automatically; (c) cloudscraper-equivalent in C++ (high complexity, library dependency). Option (b) is the closest match to identity #2's "must be solved" standard; option (a) is cheapest but user-visible-ugly. Worth a Hemanth decision before Phase 4 authoring.
4. **SEARCH-P0-4 + SEARCH-P0-1 partial overlap.** The per-indexer health table (status / last error / response time / auth state) serves both P0s simultaneously — one widget, one data contract on `TorrentIndexer`, solves both. Phase-merging candidate.
5. **SEARCH-P1-1 (canonical info-hash schema) is foundational** for dedup/sort/ranking. Low-cost; high-leverage. Good Phase 2 shape.
6. **DOWNLOADER-P0-2/P0-3/P0-4 all touch the transfer detail surface.** Three separate dialogs or one unified properties-widget (qBt-style tabbed: General / Trackers / Peers / Files)? Worth deciding before authoring phases 5-8.

### Open debt I picked up from Agent 4 (Congress 4 observer)

- `TorrentClient::torrentCompleted → CoreBridge::rootFoldersChanged` rescan signal wiring — not blocking, natural fit inside the TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.
- `TorrentClient::downloadProgress(folderPath) → float` query contract for Agent 5's future list-view Download column — wire on HELP request.

### Next step

Standing by for `TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md` at repo root. Once it lands I execute phased per Rule 6 + Rule 11; Agent 6 gates each phase exit. No Agent 0 ack needed on this post — it's the findings block per your summons.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 STREAM_UX_PARITY add-later polish sweep shipped

Both deferred batches from [STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md](STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md) shipped in one polish session. Phase 3 detail-view density now at full groundwork parity.

### Batch 3.2 (deferred ship) — cast + director row

**[StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h)** — new `QLabel* m_castDirectorLabel` member + `applyCastDirector(const QList<MetaLink>&)` private helper.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — buildUI](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)** — label constructed below `m_descShowMoreBtn` in the left column. `setWordWrap(true)`, `setMaximumHeight(40)`, grayscale 55% white text at 11px, selectable by mouse. Hidden by default.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — onMetaItemReady](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)** — one new line: `applyCastDirector(p.links)`. Walks `MetaItem.preview.links` filtering entries with `category=="Director"` or `=="Cast"` (case-insensitive). Renders as single `Director: X · Cast: A, B, C…` string. 200-char hard cap on cast list with ellipsis to prevent pathological wrapping on all-caps names; 40px max-height plus word-wrap gives natural 2-line clip for overflow. If both lists empty → `hide()` + `clear()`.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — showEntry](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)** — resets label on title change so stale values don't linger while the new title's meta is in flight.

### Batch 3.5 (deferred ship) — Watch Trailer button

**[StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h)** — new members (`QPushButton* m_trailerBtn`, `QUrl m_currentTrailerDirectUrl`, `QString m_currentTrailerYouTubeId`), new signal `trailerDirectPlayRequested(const QUrl&)`, helper `applyTrailerButton` + slot `onTrailerClicked`.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — buildUI](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)** — trailer button constructed in the top bar right next to the Library button. Grayscale styling consistent with the Library button's `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` / `0.14` hover palette. Hidden until populated.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — applyTrailerButton](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)**:
1. Prefer first `Url`/`Http`-kind trailer → store `m_currentTrailerDirectUrl` → direct player path.
2. Fallback: first `YouTube`-kind trailer → store `m_currentTrailerYouTubeId` → browser path.
3. Neither present → hide button.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — onTrailerClicked](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)**:
- Direct URL → `emit trailerDirectPlayRequested(url)`. StreamPage consumes.
- YouTube ID → `QDesktopServices::openUrl("https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=" + ytId)`. No in-app embed (our StreamEngine doesn't support YouTube; `QDesktopServices` is the standard Stremio desktop bailout per TODO).

**[StreamPage.cpp — ctor detail-view wiring](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)** — new `connect(m_detailView, &StreamDetailView::trailerDirectPlayRequested, this, [this](const QUrl& trailerUrl) { ... })` lambda. Synthesizes a Stream with `StreamSource::httpSource(trailerUrl)`, sets `m_pendingPlay` with `imdbId="adhoc-trailer:" + first-40-chars-of-url` (namespaced so progress persistence can't collide with library IMDB ids), dispatches through `m_playerController->startStream`. Same ad-hoc pattern as Batch 4.3's URL-paste handler — proven path.

**[StreamDetailView.cpp — showEntry](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)** — resets both trailer URL fields + hides button on title change.

### Design choices

- **Cast/director as a single QLabel, not two.** TODO spec shows them joined by `·`. One widget = one layout element; easier to manage visibility as a unit. If either is absent, the other renders alone (e.g. just "Cast: …" with no "Director:").
- **`QFontMetrics::elidedText` NOT used for cast list.** Plan's implementation notes mentioned it, but QLabel with `setWordWrap(true)` + `setMaximumHeight(40)` achieves the same visual clip at zero code cost. The 200-char hard-cap-with-ellipsis catches the edge case of a single name longer than the available visual height (rare — mostly defense against pathological data).
- **Trailer button next to Library, not below description.** TODO explicit: "near the Add/Remove library button from Phase 1 Batch 1.2." Matches Stremio's detail-page layout where action buttons cluster in the header.
- **"adhoc-trailer:" imdbId prefix** for progress persistence. Keeps trailer plays out of continue-watching data. Could also be "adhoc:" but a distinct prefix makes trailer plays greppable in persisted state.
- **YouTube URL format: `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ID`**. Standard web URL; system browser opens it in whatever the user has associated. No platform-specific deep-link handling; `QDesktopServices::openUrl` handles the protocol selection.
- **Inline lambda for trailerDirectPlayRequested handler on StreamPage, not a named slot.** Single-call site, 15 lines, and the pattern matches the URL-paste handler (also an ad-hoc play). Named slot would be overkill.

### Files touched

- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h)
- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp)
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)
- [STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md](STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md) — reduced to empty status note

No header additions to StreamPage (connect uses inline lambda, no new method). No CMake change.

### Re-verification

**Cast/director (3.2):**
1. Open a detail view for a well-tagged movie (e.g. Interstellar) → expect `Director: Christopher Nolan · Cast: Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, …`.
2. Open a detail view for a TV series with only Cast → expect `Cast: …` (no "Director: " fragment).
3. Open an obscure title with neither director nor cast in meta → label hidden entirely. No empty row.
4. Navigate rapidly between titles → previous title's names don't flash on the new title while meta is loading.

**Trailer (3.5):**
5. Open a title whose meta includes a direct-URL trailer → "Watch Trailer" button visible in top bar. Click → player opens and plays trailer via HTTP sidecar path.
6. Open a title whose only trailer is a YouTube ID → button visible. Click → default browser opens to `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`.
7. Open a title with no trailer → button hidden.
8. After watching a trailer, navigate Back → continue-watching strip does NOT contain "adhoc-trailer:…" entries (progress keys are isolated via prefix).
9. Regression: all other Batch 3.1 / 3.3 / 3.4 detail-view elements (hero, chips, description show-more, episode thumbnails) unaffected.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Audit P1 + P2 follow-ups shipped

Both dormant-class sidecar cleanups from the probe audit [agents/audits/tankostream_playback_probe_2026-04-15.md](agents/audits/tankostream_playback_probe_2026-04-15.md). Sidecar rebuild required.

### Audit P1 — preload_subtitle_packets HTTP options

Same bug class as the probe_file hotfix, but on the subtitle-track-switch path.

**Pre-fix:** [native_sidecar/src/main.cpp preload_subtitle_packets](native_sidecar/src/main.cpp) called `avformat_open_input(&fmt, path.c_str(), nullptr, nullptr)` with a null options dict. On torrent-backed HTTP URLs, the same failure mode that hit the original probe path (no reconnect, short default timeouts, waitForPieces stalls kill the open) would reappear the moment a user switched subtitle tracks mid-stream.

**Post-fix:** Mirror the HTTP-options block from `probe_file` / `VideoDecoder::start` / `AudioDecoder::start`:
```cpp
if (is_http) {
    av_dict_set(&opts, "reconnect", "1", 0);
    av_dict_set(&opts, "reconnect_streamed", "1", 0);
    av_dict_set(&opts, "reconnect_delay_max", "10", 0);
    av_dict_set(&opts, "timeout", "60000000", 0);      // 60s connect
    av_dict_set(&opts, "rw_timeout", "30000000", 0);   // 30s per read
    av_dict_set(&opts, "probesize", "20000000", 0);    // 20 MB probe
    av_dict_set(&opts, "analyzeduration", "10000000", 0);
    std::fprintf(stderr, "preload_subtitle_packets: HTTP mode for %s\n", ...);
}
const int ret = avformat_open_input(&fmt, path.c_str(), nullptr, &opts);
av_dict_free(&opts);
```

Close parity with the probe_file hotfix shipped earlier — intentional. All sidecar `avformat_open_input` sites now share identical HTTP robustness.

### Audit P2 — case-insensitive HTTP scheme detection

RFC 3986 allows uppercase scheme names (`HTTP://example.com` is legal), though almost never used in practice. Pre-fix, the four sidecar HTTP-detection sites used `path.rfind("http://", 0) == 0` — case-sensitive, so uppercase URLs would bypass HTTP-options entirely and hit the default (no-reconnect, short-timeout) path.

**Fix:** Added a portable `starts_with_ci` helper at each of the four call sites:
```cpp
static bool starts_with_ci(const std::string& s, const char* prefix) {
    const size_t n = std::strlen(prefix);
    if (s.size() < n) return false;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        const unsigned char a = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]);
        const unsigned char b = static_cast<unsigned char>(prefix[i]);
        if (std::tolower(a) != std::tolower(b)) return false;
    }
    return true;
}
```

Chosen over `_strnicmp` (MSVC-specific) / `strncasecmp` (POSIX-specific) for portability — the sidecar could theoretically build under MinGW in the future. Zero allocs, ~10 machine instructions per call, called at most a few times per playback session.

Each call site swapped from `rfind("http://", 0) == 0` to `starts_with_ci(path, "http://")`.

### Files touched (sidecar only — rebuild required)

- [native_sidecar/src/main.cpp](native_sidecar/src/main.cpp) — `<cctype>` include; `preload_subtitle_packets` gets HTTP options block + starts_with_ci (as a local lambda, since this is a single-TU user).
- [native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp](native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp) — `<cctype>` + `<cstring>` + `<string>` includes; file-static `starts_with_ci`; `probe_file` HTTP detection switched.
- [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp) — `<cctype>` + `<string>` includes; anon-namespace `starts_with_ci`; detection switched.
- [native_sidecar/src/audio_decoder.cpp](native_sidecar/src/audio_decoder.cpp) — `<cctype>` + `<string>` includes; anon-namespace `starts_with_ci`; detection switched.

No main-app changes. Four sidecar TUs touched.

### Design note: why duplicate the helper per TU

One shared header `native_sidecar/src/scheme_utils.h` would be slightly cleaner. Three reasons to inline per-TU for this batch:
1. Four occurrences, each ~10 lines, self-contained.
2. Adding a new sidecar shared header is a CMake touch + header-guard discipline + forward-decl discipline for one function.
3. Function is tiny enough that duplication is arguably more readable than a header indirection.

If we ever add a fifth HTTP-aware site in the sidecar, reconsider consolidation.

### Sidecar rebuild

Required — these are sidecar TUs only. Hemanth's sidecar-rebuild flow (per earlier probe-file hotfix session) is `build_qrhi.bat`. After rebuild:
- Torrent playback with subtitle track switch → no `preload_subtitle_packets: failed to open` errors.
- Uppercase URL (e.g. `HTTPS://cinemeta.strem.io/manifest.json` pasted into search or used as a direct-URL stream) → `HTTP mode enabled` log line appears in `sidecar_debug_live.log` and the URL routes through the robust-HTTP path.

### Re-verification

**P1 smoke:**
1. Start a torrent stream with embedded subtitles.
2. Let playback run past the head window.
3. Switch subtitle tracks mid-stream → expect: subtitles load (may take a moment on slow seeds while the track-preload reads to EOF, but no "failed to open" error).
4. Regression: non-torrent HTTP streams with subtitles (e.g. direct-URL movie with sidecar subs) — same path, now also robust.

**P2 smoke:**
5. Paste `HTTPS://...mp4` (uppercase) into the search bar → Batch 4.3 detection routes to direct-video play → sidecar log shows `HTTP mode enabled` for video_decoder.cpp → works identically to lowercase.
6. Lowercase URLs still work identically (no regression on the common case).

### READY TO COMMIT

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, AUDIT FOLLOW-UPS]: P1 preload_subtitle_packets HTTP options + P2 case-insensitive HTTP detection. preload_subtitle_packets now applies the same reconnect/rw_timeout/probesize block as probe_file/VideoDecoder/AudioDecoder when path is http:// or https:// (audit P1 dormant-class bug, same pattern as probe_file hotfix). Four HTTP-detection sites (probe_file + VideoDecoder::start + AudioDecoder::start + preload_subtitle_packets) switched from path.rfind("http://", 0) == 0 to starts_with_ci(path, "http://") — portable std::tolower-based helper duplicated per TU (4 sites, ~10 lines each; avoids new shared header for one function). RFC 3986 compliance for uppercase URL schemes. | files: native_sidecar/src/main.cpp, native_sidecar/src/demuxer.cpp, native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp, native_sidecar/src/audio_decoder.cpp | REQUIRES SIDECAR REBUILD (build_qrhi.bat)

- ✅ STREAM_UX_PARITY_ADD_LATER.md — both deferred batches shipped
- ✅ STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX_TODO.md — all 3 phases + socket handoff shipped

Per AskUserQuestion 2026-04-16, saved as memory `project_tankorent_identity.md`:

2. **All 7 indexers first-class** — 1337x Cloudflare solved via QWebEngineView auto-harvest (Qt module already linked for book reader — no new dep). Cookie-paste fallback via Phase 3 credential UI.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Stream library UX rework shipped

Per Hemanth's ask: library feels like Videos mode now — quiet by default, surfaces only user-added content, catalog browsing is opt-in via a button.

### What shipped

**1. `StreamHomeBoard` reduced to a Continue-Watching wrapper.**

[StreamHomeBoard.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.h) + [StreamHomeBoard.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.cpp) rewritten from ~80 + ~380 lines to ~40 + ~50 lines. Deleted:
- `HomeCatalogRow` inner class (the actual "Popular row" widget).
- `HomeCatalogSpec` struct + `enumerateCatalogs()` + `chooseFeaturedCatalogs()` + `specKey()`.
- `browseCatalogRequested` + `metaActivated` signals (no emitter left).
- `m_rowsHost` + `m_rowsLayout` + `m_rows` members.
- Includes: `<QDir>` `<QFile>` `<QFrame>` `<QHBoxLayout>` `<QHash>` `<QLabel>` `<QLayoutItem>` `<QNetworkAccessManager>` `<QNetworkReply>` `<QNetworkRequest>` `<QPointer>` `<QPushButton>` `<QSet>` `<QSettings>` `<QStandardPaths>`, `CatalogAggregator.h`, `AddonRegistry.h`, `Descriptor.h`, `TileCard.h`, `TileStrip.h` (all stripped).
- `stream_home_catalogs` QSettings key — left as-is in QSettings storage (no cleanup needed; harmless dangling data).

Kept:
- Ctor takes the same args so StreamPage construction path stays untouched.
- `refresh()` delegates to `m_continueStrip->refresh()`.
- `continueStrip()` accessor unchanged (StreamPage still uses it).

**2. "Catalog" button added to search bar.**

[StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h) + [StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp):
- New `QPushButton* m_catalogBtn` member.
- Constructed in `buildSearchBar` after `m_calendarBtn` — same 32px height, `StreamCatalogBtn` object name, `"Browse all addon catalogs"` tooltip.
- New slot `onCatalogBtnClicked()` calls `showCatalogBrowse("", "", "", "")`.

`CatalogBrowseScreen::open` with empty args is already well-behaved: `selectAddon("")` / `selectCatalog("", "")` fail the for-loop lookups silently and leave the combos at index 0 (first enabled addon + its first catalog after `rebuildSelectors`). `reload()` fires on that default pair. On second visit, the combos retain their prior selection. Zero changes needed on CatalogBrowseScreen's side.

**3. Dangling connects removed from StreamPage ctor.**

Two `connect(m_homeBoard, ...)` sites targeted signals that no longer exist:
- `StreamHomeBoard::browseCatalogRequested` → `showCatalogBrowse` — removed; featured rows don't exist to emit.
- `StreamHomeBoard::metaActivated` → `showDetail` lambda — removed; no featured-row tiles to click. Replaced with a comment documenting the remaining entry points into `showDetail` (search widget, catalog browse, library grid, continue strip).

**4. "Shows & Movies" section label + empty-state refresh.**

[StreamLibraryLayout.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp) — the existing section label (was `"SHOWS"`) is now `"SHOWS & MOVIES"`, reflecting the grid's role. Empty-state text changed from `"No shows added yet"` to `"Your library is empty. Use Search or Catalog to add shows and movies."` — points users at both entry paths.

### User-visible effect

- Fresh stream-mode launch on an empty library: Continue Watching (empty/hidden) + "SHOWS & MOVIES" header + "Your library is empty. Use Search or Catalog…" message. No featured rows.
- Search → tile click → detail view → Add to Library → back → grid populates. No change from prior behavior, just nothing else competing for attention.
- Click Catalog button → CatalogBrowseScreen opens on first available addon + catalog. Click a tile → detail view → Add to Library → back → appears in grid.
- Watch an added title: Continue Watching now shows it (with progress). Same title still appears in "SHOWS & MOVIES" grid below (library entry persists). Both sections reflect the same entry.

### Design notes

- **`StreamHomeBoard` kept, not inlined.** Could collapse into StreamPage's `buildBrowseLayer` directly; chose to keep for minimal surface disturbance. `continueStrip()` accessor still used. Future refactor optional.
- **Signals deleted outright, not just disconnected.** `browseCatalogRequested` + `metaActivated` would be dead code. Removing them flushes the class surface so future contributors don't wire to no-op signals.
- **`showCatalogBrowse("", "", "", "")` instead of a new `openDefault()`.** Smaller diff — reuses existing orchestration (autolaunch cancel, overlay cleanup, stack index switch). The empty-string semantics in `CatalogBrowseScreen::open` are well-defined already.
- **Section label in uppercase for consistency with Videos mode pattern.** Matches the prior "SHOWS" casing; just extended.
- **Empty-state string mentions both entry paths** so users discover Catalog naturally even if they never look at the button row.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.h) — rewritten, ~80 → ~40 lines
- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.cpp) — rewritten, ~380 → ~50 lines
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h) — new `m_catalogBtn` member + `onCatalogBtnClicked` decl
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — button construction + click handler + 2 dangling connects removed
- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp) — section label + empty-state text

No CMake change (existing entries still list the two StreamHomeBoard files). No sidecar change. No new files.

### Re-verification

1. **Empty library launch.** Expected: no featured rows; Continue Watching hidden (empty); "SHOWS & MOVIES" section shows "Your library is empty..." message.
2. **Search path.** Type "Breaking Bad" → click result → detail → Add to Library → back. Title in grid. Addons still functional.
3. **Catalog button.** Click Catalog → browse screen opens with sensible default. Click a tile → detail → Add to Library → back → in grid.
4. **Watch title.** Click tile → detail → episode click → stream plays → close. Continue Watching shows it; grid still shows it too.
5. **Addons / Calendar buttons** unchanged.
6. **Back button on catalog-browse** returns to browse layer (existing `CatalogBrowseScreen::backRequested → showBrowse`). No dead route.
7. **Removal** via detail-view Remove button → grid + continue-watching both refresh.
8. **Regression: Continue Watching refresh on library changes** — StreamContinueStrip does NOT listen to libraryChanged directly (per exploration). Its refresh is triggered by StreamPage on `libraryChanged`. That path still fires at [StreamPage.cpp:497](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L497) via the `m_homeBoard->refresh()` call (which now refreshes continue strip). Verified unchanged.

### Design-call flagged

The feature-removal is purely subtractive on the data-discovery side — users who liked the featured rows lose that passive discovery surface. Catalog button is one click away and opens the same underlying addon catalogs. Matches Stremio's "intentional-browse" UX philosophy.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Stream Library UX rework]: Remove featured catalog rows from StreamHomeBoard (HomeCatalogRow + HomeCatalogSpec + enumerate/chooseFeatured logic + 2 signals + 15 includes stripped; class reduced to Continue Watching wrapper). Add Catalog button to StreamPage search bar next to Addons+Calendar; onCatalogBtnClicked opens CatalogBrowseScreen via existing showCatalogBrowse with empty args (CatalogBrowseScreen's own combo defaults handle first-open). Remove 2 dangling homeBoard signal connects. Rename library section label SHOWS → SHOWS & MOVIES; empty-state text points at Search and Catalog entry paths. | files: src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.h, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamHomeBoard.cpp, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp

Standing by on smoke.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 1 Batch 1.1 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 1 Batch 1.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:66-91](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md). SEARCH-P0-3 closed: search-type combo is now wired into dispatch. Bug confirmed in my validation at chat.md:16945 — `m_searchTypeCombo->currentData()` was populated at TankorentPage.cpp:326-333 but never read in `startSearch`, so every indexer fired regardless of media type (YTS fired for Books queries, etc.).

### Implementation

**[TankorentPage.h:49-55](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h#L49-L55)** — added private method declaration:
```cpp
int  dispatchIndexers(const QString& mediaType,
                      const QString& sourceFilter,
                      const QString& query,
                      int limit,
                      const QString& categoryId);
```

**[TankorentPage.cpp:703-713](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L703-L713)** — file-scope `kMediaTypeIndexers` allowlist. Exact sets per TODO Batch 1.1 (lowercase keys to match `currentData().toString()` return, which the combo populates as `"videos"/"books"/"audiobooks"/"comics"` at :329-332; TODO pseudo-code used capitalized labels but the actual combo data is lowercase — implementation uses the real values):
- `"videos"` → `{yts, eztv, piratebay, 1337x, exttorrents}`
- `"books"` → `{piratebay, exttorrents, torrentscsv, 1337x}`
- `"audiobooks"` → `{piratebay, exttorrents, torrentscsv, 1337x}`
- `"comics"` → `{nyaa, piratebay, 1337x}`

**[TankorentPage.cpp:715-754](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L715-L754)** — `dispatchIndexers` helper. Filters by allowlist AND source-combo selection, instantiates the passing set into `m_activeIndexers`, sets `m_pendingSearches` BEFORE connecting signals + firing `idx->search` (preserves the pre-fix ordering invariant so a synchronous completion can't underflow the pending counter). 1337x's `addIf` call stays commented-out per TODO Batch 1.1 scope — Phase 4.3 removes the comment.

**[TankorentPage.cpp:756-786](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L756-L786)** — `startSearch` refactored. Reads `m_searchTypeCombo->currentData()` alongside the existing source/category reads, calls `dispatchIndexers`, surfaces a user-visible status when the combined filter yields zero dispatched indexers (previously this silently returned — user would pick e.g. YTS + Books and see no results with no explanation).

**[TankorentPage.cpp:39-40](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L39-L40)** — added `#include <QHash>` and `#include <QSet>` explicitly. Both are almost certainly transitively included through Qt's widget headers but explicit is safer.

### Rule 11 — per Agent 0's per-phase-exit protocol

Adds dispatchIndexers helper + kMediaTypeIndexers allowlist table + empty-set status message; preserves m_pendingSearches ordering invariant. SEARCH-P0-3 closed. 1337x stays commented in dispatch (Phase 4.3 re-enables). | files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Phase 1]: Search-type typed category routing | Objective: Phase 1 per TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/tankorent_2026-04-16.md. Files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp.

### Smoke verification Hemanth can run

- Videos + All Sources: 4 indexers fire (YTS, EZTV, PirateBay, ExtraTorrents). 1337x still gated.
- Books + All Sources: 3 indexers fire (PirateBay, ExtraTorrents, TorrentsCSV). YTS/EZTV/Nyaa skipped.
- Audiobooks + All Sources: same 3 as Books.
- Comics + All Sources: 2 indexers fire (Nyaa, PirateBay). YTS/EZTV/ExtraTorrents/TorrentsCSV skipped.
- Books + YTS (specific-source + incompatible-type): 0 indexers fire → status reads "No sources available for books search".
- Videos + Nyaa (specific-source + Nyaa-excluded-from-Videos per TODO allowlist): 0 → same status. Flagged per TODO's "allowlist exactness — Hemanth may tune" note at :541; anime fans might want Nyaa in Videos. One-line change to the allowlist if so.

### Allowlist tuning note

Agent 0's TODO pseudo-code exactly matches the code I shipped. Nyaa's exclusion from Videos is the most opinionated call — Nyaa carries anime video content plus manga. Current behavior: anime video goes through PirateBay/YTS/EZTV/ExtraTorrents (general-purpose sites that carry some anime); Nyaa is Comics-only. If that's wrong for the user's routine, moving `"nyaa"` from the `"comics"` set into `"videos"` (or both) is a 2-line change. Flagging explicitly since my validation chat.md post proposed Nyaa in Videos.

Standing by on Hemanth smoke and Agent 6 review. Phase 2 Batch 2.1 (canonical infoHash schema expansion) is the next natural step — low-risk, high-leverage per TODO.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 2 Batch 2.1 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:101-123](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L101-L123). SEARCH-P1-1 groundwork: `TorrentResult` now carries canonical `infoHash`, `publishDate`, and `detailsUrl`. All 7 indexers populate at parse time. **No UI change** — Batch 2.2 switches dedup over to the new fields.

Batch 2.1 flagged `Isolate-commit: yes` in the TODO (cross-indexer change). Posting READY TO COMMIT alone and waiting for smoke before Batch 2.2 touches dedup.

### Schema change

**[TorrentResult.h](src/core/TorrentResult.h)** — added 3 fields + normalization helper:
```cpp
QString   infoHash;      // canonical 40-char lowercase hex BTIH
QDateTime publishDate;   // invalid if indexer couldn't extract
QString   detailsUrl;    // per-indexer details page; empty if none

inline QString canonicalizeInfoHash(QString raw);  // lowercase + 40-hex strict; empty on mismatch
```

Also added `#include <QDateTime>`. `canonicalizeInfoHash` is 40-hex-only; base32 (32-char) magnets stay handled by the existing magnet-string dedup fallback. Keeps the normalization cheap and predictable.

### Per-indexer populate

Each indexer calls `canonicalizeInfoHash` + emits a `qDebug()` warning when the result is empty (no user-visible error, per TODO success criterion). Summary of field coverage:

| Indexer | infoHash | publishDate | detailsUrl | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [NyaaIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/NyaaIndexer.cpp) | ✓ btih regex | ✓ `data-timestamp` attr | ✓ `nyaa.si/view/<id>` | titleLinkRe extended to capture the view path. |
| [PirateBayIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/PirateBayIndexer.cpp) | ✓ `info_hash` field | ✓ `added` unix secs | ✓ `description.php?id=<id>` | All three fields are direct API. |
| [YtsIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/YtsIndexer.cpp) | ✓ `hash` field | ✓ `date_uploaded_unix` | ✓ `movie.url` | publishDate/detailsUrl are movie-level, shared across all torrents of that movie. |
| [EztvIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.cpp) | ✓ btih regex | — | — | EZTV HTML has only relative dates + show-page links (not episode-specific); infoHash only this batch. |
| [ExtTorrentsIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/ExtTorrentsIndexer.cpp) | ✓ btih regex | — | ✓ `EXT_BASE + detailPath` | Release-date column is relative text; skipped. |
| [TorrentsCsvIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/TorrentsCsvIndexer.cpp) | ✓ `infohash` field | ✓ `created_unix` | — | No detail pages on Torrents-CSV. |
| [X1337xIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp) | ✓ btih regex | — | ✓ `X1337X_BASE + detailPath` | List-page dates are relative; detail-page parse out of scope for 2.1. Gated at dispatch until Phase 4 anyway. |

All 7 have infoHash coverage. 4/7 have publishDate. 5/7 have detailsUrl. The missing ones are the HTML scrapers where the source only exposes relative-time strings or lacks per-torrent detail pages — populating those would require detail-page fetches (cost) or HTML date-string parsing with locale assumptions (fragile). Graceful degradation over false precision.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 2.1]: TorrentResult canonical infoHash + publishDate + detailsUrl schema expansion; all 7 indexers populate at parse time; canonicalizeInfoHash inline helper in TorrentResult.h; debug warning (no user-visible error) when infoHash can't be extracted. No UI change this batch. | files: src/core/TorrentResult.h, src/core/indexers/NyaaIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/PirateBayIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/YtsIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/ExtTorrentsIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/TorrentsCsvIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp

### Smoke notes

- No visible behavior change in this batch (fields populated but not yet consumed). Dedup still runs the magnet-regex path at [TankorentPage.cpp:824-836](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L824-L836) — Batch 2.2 swaps it.
- Check debug output (run with `QT_LOGGING_RULES="*=true"` or similar) — any `[<Indexer>] infoHash missing for: <title>` lines flag parse regressions on that indexer and guide Phase 3's health surfacing.

- Regression targets: search still returns results, dedup still works (magnet-regex fallback preserved), sort/filter still works. No UI path changed.

### Holding on Batch 2.2

Batch 2.2 swaps [TankorentPage.cpp:824-836](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L824-L836) over to `result.infoHash` primary key with the current btih-regex as fallback and whole-magnet as last-resort. ## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 2 Batch 2.2 SHIPPED (Phase 2 complete)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 2 Batch 2.2 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:125-141](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L125-L141). Dedup switched over to the canonical `infoHash` primary key that Batch 2.1 plumbed. SEARCH-P1-1 now fully closed — Phase 2 exit criteria met.

Hemanth directed "next batch" past my isolate-commit hold — proceeding per plan discipline. If Batch 2.1 parse logic has regressions they'll surface alongside 2.2's dedup; acceptable since 2.2 preserves the pre-fix fallback path verbatim.

### Change

**[TankorentPage.cpp:860-882](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L860-L882)** — `renderResults` dedup block. Three-tier key selection:

```cpp
for (const auto& r : sorted) {
    QString key;
    if (!r.infoHash.isEmpty()) {
        key = r.infoHash;                                    // tier 1: canonical 40-hex from Batch 2.1
    } else {
        auto m = btihRe.match(r.magnetUri);
        key = m.hasMatch() ? m.captured(1).toLower()         // tier 2: regex (v2 64-hex + base32)
                           : r.magnetUri.toLower();          // tier 3: whole magnet string
    }
    if (seen.contains(key)) continue;
    seen.insert(key);
    deduped.append(r);
}
```

The existing `btihRe` (`[a-fA-F0-9]{40}(?:[a-fA-F0-9]{24})?|[A-Z2-7]{32}`) is kept as the tier-2 fallback intentionally — `canonicalizeInfoHash` is 40-hex-strict, so v2 (64-hex) magnets and base32 magnets populate `r.infoHash` as empty; tier 2 catches them. Tier 3 preserves the pre-fix behavior byte-for-byte for whatever the regex misses.

Sort block above ([:852-858](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L852-L858)) and seeder-filter + soft-cap block below ([:884-911]) are untouched per TODO 2.2 scope.

### Why three tiers instead of two

TODO 2.2 spec lists three: primary/fallback/last-resort. Two would work for 90% of real-world cases (PirateBay/YTS/TorrentsCSV have direct hash fields; canonical path covers them), but tier 3's whole-magnet-string key is the invariant that made pre-fix dedup non-trivial — without it, genuinely broken magnets (missing `btih:` prefix, non-standard encodings from a scraper that drifts) would dedup against each other incorrectly or fail to collapse at all. Keeping it means a Batch 2.1 indexer regression (say, 1337x starts emitting malformed magnets) degrades to pre-fix dedup behavior rather than a new class of duplicate leaks.

### Convergence against pre-fix behavior

- Indexer emits clean v1 magnet + populates `r.infoHash` → tier 1 fires. **Better dedup** than before: subtly-different magnets (different tracker orders, added `&dn=`) collapse via canonical hash instead of magnet-string mismatch.
- Indexer emits clean magnet but leaves `r.infoHash` empty (Batch 2.1 parse miss or HTML-scraper with base32/v2 hash) → tier 2 fires, same regex as before. **Identical** to pre-fix.
- Indexer emits malformed magnet → tier 3 fires, whole-string lowercase. **Identical** to pre-fix.

No regression surface relative to pre-fix behavior under any indexer path.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 2.2]: Swap renderResults dedup from regex-primary to canonical-infoHash-primary with btih-regex fallback + whole-magnet last-resort. Tier-2/3 fallbacks preserve pre-fix behavior under any Batch 2.1 parse miss. ~14 LOC touched in one block. | files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

### Phase 2 exit — READY FOR REVIEW

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Phase 2]: TorrentResult canonical infoHash + schema expansion + canonical dedup | Objective: Phase 2 per TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/tankorent_2026-04-16.md SEARCH-P1-1. Files: src/core/TorrentResult.h, src/core/indexers/NyaaIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/PirateBayIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/YtsIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/ExtTorrentsIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/TorrentsCsvIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp.

### Smoke (Phase 2 end-to-end)

1. Query a well-indexed popular torrent (e.g. `"linux mint iso"`, `"big buck bunny"`) — run across All Sources.
2. Expected: result list shows ONE row per logical torrent even when 3+ indexers carried it. Pre-fix: duplicates survived when tracker lists or `&dn=` drifted between indexer-emitted magnets.
3. Verify obscure-source torrents still appear (tier 2/3 fallback path). A query that only EZTV carries should still render — EZTV has infoHash populated this batch but verifying the fallback path matters.
4. Debug log: very few `[<Indexer>] infoHash missing for:` lines expected on the JSON-API indexers; occasional ones on HTML scrapers when anti-bot intercepts.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 3 Batch 3.1 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:154-184](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L154-L184). TorrentIndexer base contract expanded with health + telemetry + credentials. All 7 indexers wired. EZTV cookie migrated from hardcoded header to user-supplied credential (with the old hardcoded string preserved as fallback until the user sets one). No UI yet — that's Batch 3.2.

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

One additive source entry at [CMakeLists.txt:69](CMakeLists.txt#L69) (next to other `src/core/*.cpp` in the torrent block):

```
    src/core/TorrentIndexer.cpp
```

No other `add_executable` / `find_package` / `-D` / target-link changes. Build order unaffected.

### Base contract

**[src/core/TorrentIndexer.h](src/core/TorrentIndexer.h)** — full rewrite (38 → 80 LOC). Adds:

- `enum class IndexerHealth { Unknown, Ok, Disabled, AuthRequired, CloudflareBlocked, RateLimited, Unreachable }` — 7 states per TODO spec.
- 4 pure-virtual getters: `health()`, `lastSuccess()`, `lastError()`, `lastResponseMs()`. Each indexer overrides with one-liner returning the protected state member.
- 4 credential virtuals: `requiresCredentials()` (default false), `credentialKeys()` (default `{}`), `setCredential()` / `credential()` (default no-op / empty). EZTV is the one that overrides; structure is ready for Phase 4's 1337x `cf_clearance`.
- Protected state members shared across all subclasses: `m_health`, `m_lastSuccess`, `m_lastError`, `m_lastResponseMs`, `m_requestTimer` (`QElapsedTimer`).
- Protected helpers: `startRequestTimer()`, `markSuccess()`, `markError(QNetworkReply*)`, `loadPersistedHealth()`, `savePersistedHealth()` — implemented in the new **[TorrentIndexer.cpp](src/core/TorrentIndexer.cpp)**.

**[src/core/TorrentIndexer.cpp](src/core/TorrentIndexer.cpp)** NEW — 70 LOC:

- `markError()` classifier per TODO spec: `TimeoutError` → `Unreachable`; HTTP 429 → `RateLimited`; HTTP 403 + Cloudflare fingerprint (Server header contains "cloudflare" OR CF-Ray header present) → `CloudflareBlocked`; HTTP 401 or 403-without-CF → `AuthRequired`; else → `Unreachable`. Null-reply defensive path included.
- `markSuccess()` records elapsed ms, sets `Ok`, clears `lastError`, persists.
- Persistence at QSettings key `tankorent/indexers/<id>/health/{lastSuccess,lastError,lastResponseMs}`. `m_health` is intentionally NOT persisted — it's Unknown on boot until the first live query (Batch 3.2 panel will show "Unknown (last success X ago)" for stale state).

### Per-indexer wiring (identical pattern in all 7)

Each indexer got:
1. Header: 4 override one-liners under the existing `search()` declaration.
2. Constructor: `loadPersistedHealth()` call.
3. Just before each outbound request: `startRequestTimer()`.
4. On QNetworkReply error path: `markError(reply)` before existing error emit.
5. On parse-error path (for JSON indexers — PirateBay / YTS / TorrentsCSV): `markError(reply)` before existing parse-error emit.
6. On successful parse: `markSuccess()` before result-emitting branch.

**Per-indexer call-counts (grep verified):**
- NyaaIndexer: 4 | PirateBayIndexer: 5 (includes parse-error path) | YtsIndexer: 5 | EztvIndexer: 4 | ExtTorrentsIndexer: 4 | TorrentsCsvIndexer: 5 | X1337xIndexer: 4.

Multi-reply indexers (EZTV mirror-failover, ExtTorrents URL-candidate-failover, 1337x detail fetches) only wire telemetry to the list-page request, not detail fetches — detail failures shouldn't flip indexer health after the list page succeeded. EZTV/ExtTorrents also call `markError` on each failed mirror/URL attempt so the last failure's classification persists if ALL mirrors fail.

### EZTV credentials migration

[EztvIndexer.h](src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.h#L20-L27) now declares:

```cpp
bool        requiresCredentials() const override { return true; }
QStringList credentialKeys() const override      { return { "cookie" }; }
void        setCredential(const QString& key, const QString& value) override;
QString     credential(const QString& key) const override;
```

[EztvIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.cpp) — `setCredential`/`credential` impls use QSettings path `tankorent/indexers/eztv/credentials/cookie`. The request-build path in `tryNextMirror` now reads `credential("cookie")` and falls back to a file-scope `kEztvDefaultCookie` string (the exact bytes the code used to hardcode — `sort_no=100; q_filter=all; ...`). This means:

- Pre-batch behavior is preserved byte-for-byte until the user sets a credential via Batch 3.2's panel.
- Once the user supplies their own cookie, it overrides the default — which matters when Cloudflare eventually rotates the default filter scheme or adds new q_filter_* toggles.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 3.1]: TorrentIndexer health + telemetry + credentials contract expansion. New enum IndexerHealth (7 states). New TorrentIndexer.cpp with markSuccess/markError classifier (TimeoutError/429/403+CF/401+403/other) + QSettings-persisted lastSuccess/lastError/lastResponseMs. All 7 indexers wired with state overrides + startRequestTimer/markSuccess/markError calls. EZTV cookie migrated to QSettings-backed credential (hardcoded default preserved as fallback). No UI change this batch — Batch 3.2 ships IndexerStatusPanel. | files: CMakeLists.txt, src/core/TorrentIndexer.h, src/core/TorrentIndexer.cpp (NEW), src/core/indexers/NyaaIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/NyaaIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/PirateBayIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/PirateBayIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/YtsIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/YtsIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/EztvIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/ExtTorrentsIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/ExtTorrentsIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/TorrentsCsvIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/TorrentsCsvIndexer.cpp, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp

- EZTV specifically: the hardcoded cookie is still applied by default, so EZTV's q_filter/sort/layout behavior is unchanged for any user who hasn't touched credentials.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 3 Batch 3.2 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.2 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:186-198](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L186-L198). NEW [IndexerStatusPanel](src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.h) — `QDialog` with a 7-column `QTableWidget` surfacing per-indexer health, enable-state persistence, and credential management. Batch 3.3 will wire the Sources button in TankorentPage to open this.

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

Two additive entries (one SOURCES, one HEADERS) grouped next to the other `src/ui/pages/*` entries:

```
    src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.cpp
    src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.h
```

Added at [CMakeLists.txt:69](CMakeLists.txt#L69) and [CMakeLists.txt:174](CMakeLists.txt#L174). No other build flag / target-link / find_package changes.

### Base-class API addition

**[TorrentIndexer.h](src/core/TorrentIndexer.h)** — one new public inline method:

```cpp
void reloadPersistedState() { loadPersistedHealth(); }
```

Exposes the existing protected `loadPersistedHealth()` for the panel's refresh path. Sentinel indexers can re-read QSettings after a real search from TankorentPage has updated the store. No behavior change for existing callers.

### Panel shell

**NEW [src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.h](src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.h)** — 40 LOC; inherits `QDialog` per TODO "dedicated QDialog host." Public API: `refresh()` slot + `configurationChanged()` signal for Batch 3.3 to wire.

**NEW [src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.cpp](src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.cpp)** — 260 LOC. Structure:

- **Sentinel indexers**: the ctor instantiates one of each of the 7 concrete indexers as dialog-owned children. Each one's ctor invokes `loadPersistedHealth()` (Batch 3.1 plumbing), so the table reflects the current QSettings state on open. Sentinels never call `search()` themselves — they're for metadata access (`displayName`, `requiresCredentials`, `credentialKeys`) and reading persisted state. `refresh()` calls `reloadPersistedState()` on each.
- **7 columns** per TODO spec: Name | Health | Last Success | Last Error | Response | Enabled | Credentials. Last-Success formatted as relative ("just now" / "N min ago" / "N h ago" / "N d ago"). Response time as "420 ms" or "1.2 s" or "—". Health as plain text label ("Unknown" / "Ok" / "Auth required" / "Cloudflare" / "Rate limited" / "Unreachable" / "Disabled") — no colored chips per `feedback_no_color_no_emoji`.
- **Enabled column**: `QCheckBox` cell widget reading `QSettings tankorent/indexers/<id>/enabled` (default true). Toggle writes to QSettings + emits `configurationChanged`. Batch 3.3 will read the same key in `dispatchIndexers` and AND it with the allowlist.
- **Credentials column**: `QPushButton` "Configure" only for indexers where `requiresCredentials() == true`; otherwise shows em-dash. Click opens an in-cpp anonymous-namespace `CredentialsDialog` — QFormLayout with one `QLineEdit` per `credentialKey()`, Save/Cancel buttons. Save calls `indexer->setCredential(key, value)` for each entry and emits `configurationChanged`.
- **Styling**: same grayscale palette as [TorrentFilesDialog](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.cpp) — `rgba(12,12,12,0.98)` bg, `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` border, 12px radius. Table uses the same `#1a1a1a`/`#888` header + `rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` body pattern used across the rest of the Tankorent UI.
- **Manual Refresh button** at the bottom. Batch 3.3 will also emit `refresh()` after each search from TankorentPage, but users can trigger it explicitly here too.

### Design call: sentinel pattern vs. live signal wiring

TODO literal reading ("connect all 7 at panel construction") could be read as subscribing the panel to the LIVE search-indexer signals. That would require TankorentPage to expose its transient `m_activeIndexers` list — a coupling between TankorentPage and IndexerStatusPanel. Rejected because:

1. TankorentPage's indexers are destroyed on every `cancelSearch` — connections would need re-wiring per search.
2. The panel only needs to reflect persisted state; it doesn't need real-time progress bar behavior.
3. Sentinels are cheap (7 `new`s scoped to the dialog's lifetime; dialog is modal so lifetime is short).

Batch 3.3 keeps the coupling one-way: TankorentPage calls `panel->refresh()` after each search completes. Panel emits `configurationChanged` when user toggles — TankorentPage can ignore during active search and reapply on next `startSearch`.

Enable checkbox writes QSettings tankorent/indexers/<id>/enabled. Credentials Configure button opens sub-dialog with QLineEdit per credentialKey. Grayscale palette per feedback_no_color_no_emoji. One new public inline method on TorrentIndexer: reloadPersistedState() exposing the protected loadPersistedHealth. configurationChanged signal emitted on toggle/save for Batch 3.3 to consume. Batch 3.3 will give the real smoke surface.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 3 Batch 3.3 SHIPPED (Phase 3 complete)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 3 Batch 3.3 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:200-209](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L200-L209). Sources button wired into TankorentPage toolbar; `dispatchIndexers` now ANDs QSettings enabled-state with the Phase 1 allowlist. Phase 3 exit criteria met — SEARCH-P0-1 in-scope portion + SEARCH-P0-4 both closed via the contract expansion (3.1) + panel (3.2) + wire-up (3.3).

### Changes

**[TankorentPage.h](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h)** — two additions:

```cpp
void onSourcesClicked();                    // private slot, under the other search/transfer slots
QPushButton* m_sourcesBtn = nullptr;        // next to m_refreshBtn in member block
```

**[TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp)** — three changes:

1. `#include "ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.h"` alongside the other UI page includes.
2. Sources button added between Refresh and More in the search controls row — same `QPushButton` pattern + 30px height + pointing cursor + `&TankorentPage::onSourcesClicked` connect. Grouped with search-side controls (More is reserved for global/bulk actions: speed limits, seeding rules, pause-all, history).
3. `dispatchIndexers::wanted()` lambda now reads `QSettings.value("tankorent/indexers/<id>/enabled", true)` and returns false when disabled. Refactored from the ternary-return form to explicit if-returns for readability once a third condition landed:

```cpp
QSettings settings;
auto wanted = [&](const QString& id) -> bool {
    if (hasAllowlist && !allowed.contains(id))
        return false;
    if (sourceFilter != "all" && sourceFilter != id)
        return false;
    return settings.value(
        QStringLiteral("tankorent/indexers/%1/enabled").arg(id), true).toBool();
};
```

4. `onSourcesClicked` impl — constructs `IndexerStatusPanel` modal and `exec()`s it. No connection to `configurationChanged` needed: `dispatchIndexers` reads `QSettings` on every call, so toggling an enabled-state or saving a credential in the panel automatically takes effect on the next `startSearch`. Preserves TODO's "ignores during active search, reapplies on next startSearch" contract for free.

### Why no signal handling for `configurationChanged`

`IndexerStatusPanel::configurationChanged` is emitted in Batch 3.2 but Batch 3.3 doesn't need to wire it. The mechanism is implicit: `dispatchIndexers` re-reads `QSettings` at every search. A user toggle during the modal dialog persists to disk; the next click of the Search button reads the updated value. The signal is still useful for future cases where a page needs to react live (e.g. refreshing a per-indexer status badge elsewhere in the UI) — leaving it in place as a seam, not wired for now.

### `populateSourceCombo` stays unchanged

Per TODO: the source combo (Nyaa / PirateBay / YTS / EZTV / ExtraTorrents / Torrents-CSV) is orthogonal to the enabled-state check. Source-combo is a user-picked narrowing filter; enabled-state is a user-configured availability flag. Both gates must pass (`sourceFilter matches id` AND `enabled == true`). A user can temporarily filter to Nyaa while Nyaa is disabled in Sources → zero results, same as picking any source that's disabled.

### Rule 11

Toggle/credential changes take effect on next startSearch via QSettings re-read — no explicit configurationChanged wiring needed. | files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Phase 3]: TorrentIndexer health contract + runtime config UI + search dispatch honors enabled state | Objective: Phase 3 per TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/tankorent_2026-04-16.md SEARCH-P0-1 (in-scope portion) + SEARCH-P0-4. Files: src/core/TorrentIndexer.h, src/core/TorrentIndexer.cpp, CMakeLists.txt, all 7 indexer .h/.cpp pairs in src/core/indexers/, src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.h, src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.cpp, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp.

### Smoke (Phase 3 end-to-end)

1. Open Tankorent page. Sources button is visible next to Refresh. Click → IndexerStatusPanel opens with 7 rows (one per indexer), all checkboxes checked, Health = "Unknown" for all on fresh start.
2. Run a search (e.g. `"linux mint"`, Videos). Panel closes. Run search → results populate from YTS/EZTV/PirateBay/ExtraTorrents per Phase 1's allowlist. Open Sources panel again: Health column should now read "Ok" for indexers that returned results; Last Success should show "just now"; Response time should show the measured latency (tens to hundreds of ms per indexer).
3. Uncheck Nyaa → close panel → search "one piece" Comics. Nyaa dispatch is skipped. Open Sources again: Nyaa's enabled is still unchecked (persisted).
4. Click "Configure" on EZTV → enter a test cookie string → Save. Credential is persisted under `tankorent/indexers/eztv/credentials/cookie`. Next EZTV search uses the new cookie instead of the default.
5. Kill the app, restart, open Sources: Last Success / Last Error / Response persist across restart. Health resets to "Unknown" (as designed — fresh state until next query).
6. Simulate a failure to validate classifier: disable internet, search, open panel. Affected indexers should show "Unreachable" (timeout path) with non-empty Last Error.
7. Cloudflare path: only reachable once Phase 4 un-disables 1337x in dispatch. Until then CF classification is latent (exercised via unit test or manual reply injection if desired).

Will need to check CMakeLists.txt for existing QWebEngine usage (video player has one) + plan cookie-extract timing.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 4 Batch 4.1 SHIPPED (isolate-commit)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 4 Batch 4.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:227-250](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L227-L250). NEW `CloudflareCookieHarvester` — singleton QObject wrapping a hidden `QWebEngineView` + isolated `QWebEngineProfile` that navigates to a Cloudflare-gated URL, waits for `cf_clearance` to land in the cookie store, persists it + the UA + a 7-day expiry to QSettings, and fires one of two signals. Flagged `Isolate-commit: yes` in the TODO — no caller yet; Batch 4.2 consumes in X1337xIndexer.

### CMake audit (pre-coding check per TODO)

[CMakeLists.txt:25-26](CMakeLists.txt#L25-L26) already has `find_package(Qt6 QUIET OPTIONAL_COMPONENTS WebEngineWidgets)` + `WebChannel` from the Foliate book reader work. [CMakeLists.txt:286-289](CMakeLists.txt#L286-L289) conditionally links `Qt6::WebEngineWidgets`/`Qt6::WebChannel` and defines `HAS_WEBENGINE=1`. **No new module dep needed** — the harvester piggybacks on the book reader's existing QWebEngine linkage.

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

Two additive entries (one SOURCES, one HEADERS) in the indexer block at [:75-76](CMakeLists.txt#L75) and [:181-182](CMakeLists.txt#L181):

```
    src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.cpp
    src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.h
```

No link-library / target changes. WebEngineWidgets link already in place.

### Harvester API

**NEW [src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.h](src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.h)** — 55 LOC. Singleton exposing:

```cpp
static CloudflareCookieHarvester* instance();
void harvest(const QUrl& target, const QString& indexerId);
signals:
    void cookieHarvested(indexerId, cfClearance, userAgent);
    void harvestFailed(indexerId, reason);
static QString   cachedClearance(const QString& indexerId);
static QString   cachedUserAgent(const QString& indexerId);
static QDateTime clearanceExpires(const QString& indexerId);
```

Three static accessors on top of the TODO spec so Batch 4.2 can check cached state cleanly before deciding whether to harvest-then-search or search-with-cache.

### Harvester impl

**NEW [src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.cpp](src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.cpp)** — 130 LOC. Key behaviors:

- **Singleton**: Meyer's-style `static` inside `instance()`, parented to `QCoreApplication::instance()` so Qt's normal shutdown cleanup handles it. Safe post-main — all Tankorent construction happens well after `QCoreApplication` exists.
- **Lazy construction**: the hidden `QWebEngineView` + isolated `QWebEngineProfile` are not created until the first `harvest()` call. Profiles are off-the-record by default (no-arg ctor or parent-only ctor), so cookies stay in-memory and don't pollute the book-reader's profile storage.
- **Cookie listen**: connects to `QWebEngineCookieStore::cookieAdded` with a lambda that filters for `cf_clearance` and calls `finishSuccess(value)` as soon as it shows up.
- **Page load**: `loadFinished(bool)` only triggers `finishFailure("page load failed")` on `ok == false`. On `ok == true` we DON'T finish — some CF flows set the cookie only after a JS challenge redirect, so we wait for the cookie signal or timeout.
- **Timeout**: single-shot 30s `QTimer` per TODO spec. Fires `harvestFailed("Cloudflare harvest timed out after 30s")`.
- **Persistence**: on success, writes three keys under `tankorent/indexers/<id>/`:
  - `cf_clearance` — the cookie value
  - `cf_user_agent` — the profile's `httpUserAgent()` at harvest time (so future requests match)
  - `cf_clearance_expires` — `QDateTime::currentDateTime().addDays(7)` per TODO's 7-day TTL
- **Concurrent calls**: second `harvest()` while `m_busy` is true logs a debug line and returns without firing either signal. Callers (Batch 4.2 X1337xIndexer) retry on their own if the first request fires `harvestFailed`.
- **Offscreen mode**: `m_view->setAttribute(Qt::WA_DontShowOnScreen, true)`. Combined with never calling `show()`, the view never reaches the screen — exactly the TODO's "hidden QWebEngineView (never added to widget tree; run in offscreen mode)" intent.

### HAS_WEBENGINE guard

The entire QWebEngine-using code path is `#ifdef HAS_WEBENGINE` gated. Without WebEngine, the class still compiles and still fires a signal — `harvest()` emits `harvestFailed(id, "Qt WebEngine not available in this build")` synchronously. Downstream code (Batch 4.2) can treat both "missing module" and "harvest timeout" uniformly through the same failure signal, falling through to the manual-credential path in the Sources panel.

### Lifetime call-out — m_view parentless delete

`QWebEngineView` is a `QWidget`, and Qt won't let a `QWidget` parent be a non-`QWidget` `QObject` (the harvester). So `m_view` is constructed parentless and the harvester's destructor explicitly `delete m_view;`s it. `m_profile` and `m_timeout` are `QObject`s (not `QWidget`s) and are parented to `this` — Qt auto-cleans on destruction. Destructor body is in the `#ifdef` block; stub build has a defaulted dtor.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 4.1]: NEW CloudflareCookieHarvester singleton (hidden QWebEngineView + isolated QWebEngineProfile). harvest(target, indexerId) emits cookieHarvested or harvestFailed. 30s timeout. On success persists cf_clearance + UA + 7-day expiry to QSettings tankorent/indexers/<id>/. HAS_WEBENGINE-gated with sync-failure stub fallback. Piggybacks on book reader's existing Qt6::WebEngineWidgets linkage — no new module dep. CMakeLists.txt additive (SOURCES + HEADERS). | files: CMakeLists.txt, src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.h (NEW), src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.cpp (NEW)

### Smoke notes (Batch 4.1 standalone — isolate-commit)

Nothing calls `harvest()` yet, so runtime smoke is limited to:

- Build succeeds on a WebEngine-enabled build (HAS_WEBENGINE=1). New moc-generated file for the singleton.
- Build succeeds on a WebEngine-less build (unusual for this project but should not regress). The `#else` branch of `harvest()` is the only code path compiled.
- No runtime exercise of the harvester until Batch 4.2. Isolate-commit discipline per TODO — any link/runtime issues with QtWebEngine surface here in isolation before the 1337x indexer starts depending on it.

### Next

Phase 4 Batch 4.2 — X1337xIndexer Cloudflare integration. Modifies [X1337xIndexer.h](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.h) + [.cpp](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp). Check cached `cf_clearance` on each search; if valid, attach `Cookie:` + matching UA to the HTTP request; if stale/missing, invoke harvester + queue the search until `cookieHarvested` fires; retry-once on server-side expiry (HTTP 503 / CF response body). Also overrides `credentialKeys()` to expose `cf_clearance` as a manual-paste fallback in the Sources panel.

Standing by on smoke.
