## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 4 Batches 4.2 + 4.3 SHIPPED (Phase 4 complete)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 4 Batches 4.2 + 4.3 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:252-278](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L252-L278). SEARCH-P0-2 closed — 1337x is now first-class across the indexer stack with automatic Cloudflare challenge handling + manual-paste fallback + server-side-expiry retry.

### Scope call-out — two batches in one turn

Hemanth directed "next phase" (not "next batch") — interpreted as "finish Phase 4." Per `feedback_one_fix_per_rebuild` I normally ship one batch per rebuild, but 4.2 and 4.3 are genuinely interdependent:

- 4.2 wires cookie handling into `X1337xIndexer` — meaningless without 4.3 un-disabling it in dispatch.
- 4.3 un-disables 1337x dispatch — broken without 4.2's cookie handling (hits Cloudflare, indexer falls over).

Deploying them as a single unit closes SEARCH-P0-2. Flagging the deviation from one-fix-per-rebuild explicitly for Agent 6's objective check.

### Batch 4.2 — X1337xIndexer Cloudflare integration

**[X1337xIndexer.h](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.h)** additions:
- Credential overrides: `requiresCredentials() = true`, `credentialKeys() = { "cf_clearance" }`, + `setCredential` / `credential` impls in the .cpp.
- Two private slots: `onCookieHarvested` / `onHarvestFailed` — filter on `indexerId == "1337x"` and `m_pendingSearch` to avoid false-triggering from other subscribers.
- Private helpers: `performSearch` (HTTP-GET portion factored out of the old monolithic `search`), `haveValidClearance`, `invalidateClearance`, `kickOffHarvest`.
- Pending-search state members: `m_pendingQuery` / `m_pendingCategoryId` / `m_pendingLimit` / `m_pendingSearch` / `m_retryAttempted`.

**[X1337xIndexer.cpp](src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp)** behavior:

1. **Ctor**: `loadPersistedHealth()` (from 3.1) + connect to singleton harvester's `cookieHarvested` / `harvestFailed` signals.
2. **`search()` flow** — stash pending params always; then branch:
   - **Fast path** (valid cached `cf_clearance` + non-expired `cf_clearance_expires`): call `performSearch(...)` immediately with the cached cookie + matching UA on the `Cookie:` / `User-Agent:` request headers.
   - **Slow path** (missing/stale clearance): set `m_health = CloudflareBlocked` + save, set `m_pendingSearch = true`, call `CloudflareCookieHarvester::instance()->harvest(QUrl("https://1337x.to"), "1337x")`.
3. **`onCookieHarvested`**: clears pending flag, replays `performSearch(stashed params)` with fresh cookie.
4. **`onHarvestFailed`**: clears pending flag, writes `m_lastError = "Cloudflare challenge unsolved: <reason>"`, emits `searchError`. Manual fallback: user pastes `cf_clearance` via Sources panel → `setCredential` writes to the same QSettings path the harvester uses + renews the 7-day expiry → next `search()` takes the fast path.
5. **503 retry-once** in `onListPageFetched`: Cloudflare can server-invalidate a TTL-valid cookie. On `HttpStatusCodeAttribute == 503` + `!m_retryAttempted`: set `m_retryAttempted = true`, invalidate the cached clearance, kick off harvester, replay search on success. Second 503 in a single search falls through to the normal `markError` / `emit searchError` path.
6. **`setCredential("cf_clearance", value)`** writes to `tankorent/indexers/1337x/cf_clearance` + sets `cf_clearance_expires = now + 7 days`. Single source of truth — harvester-populated and user-pasted cookies are indistinguishable to the reader side.

### Batch 4.3 — Un-disable 1337x in dispatch

**[TankorentPage.cpp:656-663](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L656-L663)** — `populateSourceCombo` gets one additive line:

```cpp
m_sourceCombo->addItem("1337x", "1337x");
```

Placed between PirateBay and YTS for alphabetical-ish ordering within the combo.

**[TankorentPage.cpp:740-748](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L740-L748)** — `dispatchIndexers` un-comments the 1337x `addIf` call; the `// 1337x disabled — Cloudflare JS challenge` comment is removed entirely per TODO.

Phase 1's `kMediaTypeIndexers` allowlist already has `"1337x"` in Videos / Books / Audiobooks / Comics (verified at [:708-712](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp#L708-L712)) — no change needed there. Phase 3's Sources panel already instantiates X1337xIndexer as a sentinel so health reporting works without further wiring.

### One intentional polish item deferred

TODO 4.2 mentioned: "During Phase 3 Sources panel's row rebuild, when 1337x row shows 'Solving Cloudflare challenge...' (health == CloudflareBlocked AND harvester is running), label changes to active-state indicator (spinner or 'Harvesting...' text)."

Shipped: health transitions to `CloudflareBlocked` while harvesting — panel displays "Cloudflare" per Batch 3.2's `healthLabel` mapping. That's the distinguishable state the user needs.

Deferred: the "Harvesting..." in-progress label / spinner. Would require either a new enum state (expanding `IndexerHealth` beyond TODO's 7) or an orthogonal "is a harvest running" boolean exposed through the base class. Both are bigger scope changes than the stated Phase 4 goal. Flagging for polish follow-up, not blocking.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batches 4.2 + 4.3]: X1337xIndexer Cloudflare integration (cached-cookie fast path, harvester fallback on stale/missing clearance, 503 retry-once with invalidate + re-harvest, cf_clearance manual-paste credential override) + un-disable 1337x in populateSourceCombo and dispatchIndexers. 4.2+4.3 bundled because they're meaningless apart (4.2 is unreachable without 4.3 un-disabling dispatch; 4.3 is broken without 4.2's cookie handling). | files: src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

### Phase 4 exit — READY FOR REVIEW

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Phase 4]: 1337x Cloudflare QWebEngineView auto-harvest | Objective: Phase 4 per TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/tankorent_2026-04-16.md SEARCH-P0-2 + identity #2 all-7-first-class. Files: CMakeLists.txt, src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.h, src/core/indexers/CloudflareCookieHarvester.cpp, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.h, src/core/indexers/X1337xIndexer.cpp, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp.

### Smoke (Phase 4 end-to-end)

1. **Fresh install + first 1337x query**: select 1337x in source combo (or leave as All Sources under Videos), search `"interstellar 2014"`. Expect: indexer health flips to "Cloudflare" briefly → hidden WebEngineView navigates to 1337x.to → cf_clearance cookie is harvested within 30s → indexer retries with cookie → results populate normally. Panel on next open shows 1337x health = "Ok", Last Success = "just now".
2. **Second query same session**: fast path hits cached cookie, no harvester. Response time is just the scrape — no 30s pause.
3. **Manual paste fallback**: simulate harvester failure by disabling internet → `harvestFailed` fires → Sources panel Configure button for 1337x → paste a `cf_clearance` from Firefox devtools → save → back to Tankorent → next 1337x query takes fast path with the manually-pasted cookie.
4. **7-day TTL rollover**: wait 7 days (or manually set `cf_clearance_expires` to a past datetime via QSettings) → next query re-harvests silently.
5. **Server-side invalidation (503)**: rare but possible — CF rotates challenge during a long session, next HTTP request returns 503. Indexer invalidates + re-harvests + retries once. User sees a slightly longer response time but results still land.
6. **No-WebEngine build** (if ever tested): harvester emits `harvestFailed("Qt WebEngine not available in this build")` synchronously; X1337xIndexer emits `searchError` with that reason; user falls back to manual Configure. 1337x row in panel reads "Cloudflare" with the reason as Last Error.

### Identity #2 status

All 7 indexers now first-class per Hemanth 2026-04-16: Nyaa, PirateBay, 1337x, YTS, EZTV, ExtraTorrents, Torrents-CSV. No disabled-with-comment sources. 1337x's Cloudflare solved as per-indexer-specific machinery (QWebEngineView embedded), NOT a general Cardigann-style runtime pipeline — matches identity #1's middle-ground scope.

### What's open

Phase 5 (DOWNLOADER-P0-1 — Add flows expansion) is next. Three batches per TODO: 5.1 AddFromUrlDialog, 5.2 drag-drop on TankorentPage, 5.3 clipboard-paste + File menu. Smaller phase, no new module deps.

Standing by on smoke + Agent 6 review.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Stream-mode tile sizing parity with Videos mode

Stream library grid now matches Videos-mode chrome. Explored both configs side-by-side to pinpoint the actual differences — most tile sizing was already identical (both modes share `TileStrip`/`TileCard`).

### What was already identical (no change needed)

- **Density-level pixel mappings** ([TileStrip.cpp:67-77](src/ui/pages/TileStrip.cpp#L67-L77)) — widths 150/200/240 and gaps 10/16/20 — shared.
- **Default card width** 200px, **image height** 308px — shared.
- **Corner radius** 8px — shared.
- **Vertical row spacing** 20px (`TILE_SPACING_V`) — shared.
- **Density slider** 100×20px, range 0..2, default 1 — identical wiring in both modes.

### What actually differed (3 fixes shipped)

| Setting | Videos mode | Stream pre-fix | Stream post-fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| Root `contentsMargins` | `(20, 0, 20, 20)` | `(0, 0, 0, 0)` | `(20, 0, 20, 20)` ✓ |
| Root `spacing` | 24px | 8px | 24px ✓ |
| Sort combo `fixedWidth` | 150px | 170px | 150px ✓ |

All three in [StreamLibraryLayout.cpp::buildUI](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp).

### Why this was the root cause

Tiles themselves were the same size in both modes (same `TileCard` defaults, same density mapping). The visual discrepancy the user saw was the *container chrome* around the grid: stream had no horizontal padding and tight row spacing, so tiles sat flush against the left edge and crowded the section header. Adding 20px side margins + 24px internal spacing matches the Videos-mode breathing room.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp) — 3 value changes + rationale comments

No other file touched. Zero change to shared tile/strip classes — those were already correct.

### Re-verification

1. Stream mode library grid → tiles now sit with 20px padding from left/right edges of the scroll area, matching Videos mode.
2. Section header + sort combo + density slider have 24px breathing room from the tile strip below (was 8px).
3. Sort combo visually matches Videos mode's width.
4. Density slider still works; tile widths still 150/200/240 at the three levels.
5. Continue Watching strip above the library section unaffected — it's part of StreamHomeBoard, not StreamLibraryLayout.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Stream tile parity with Videos]: StreamLibraryLayout container chrome aligned with VideosPage (root margins 20,0,20,20; spacing 24; sort combo 150px). Shared TileStrip/TileCard already matched — only container-level values diverged. | files: src/ui/pages/stream/StreamLibraryLayout.cpp

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 5 Batch 5.1 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:289-302](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L289-L302). NEW [AddFromUrlDialog](src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h) — multi-magnet entry with clipboard preload. "Add URL" button in TankorentPage toolbar between Sources and More. Opens dialog; on accept, iterates magnets through `resolveMetadata` + `startDownload` with a minimal AddTorrentConfig keyed off the dialog's category combo.

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

Two additive entries (one SOURCES, one HEADERS) in the dialogs block at [:101-103](CMakeLists.txt#L101-L103) and [:204-206](CMakeLists.txt#L204-L206):

```
    src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.cpp
    src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h
```

No link / target / find_package changes.

### Dialog design

**NEW [AddFromUrlDialog.h](src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h)** — 48 LOC. Public API:

```cpp
explicit AddFromUrlDialog(QWidget* parent, const QString& seedText = QString());
QStringList magnets() const;
QString     category() const;   // "videos" / "books" / "audiobooks" / "comics"
bool        startImmediately() const;
```

`seedText` seam is forward-looking for Batch 5.2's drag-drop — drop handler can pre-fill the URL list before showing the dialog.

**NEW [AddFromUrlDialog.cpp](src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.cpp)** — 125 LOC. Behavior:

- **Clipboard preload** when `seedText` is empty: if clipboard content starts with `magnet:` (case-insensitive), textedit is pre-filled. `.torrent` URL clipboard content intentionally NOT pre-filled — the HTTP-to-file add path isn't built yet (see Scope below).
- **QTextEdit** with monospace font, plain-text mode, placeholder showing example magnet URIs.
- **Category combo** (Videos / Books / Audiobooks / Comics — matches [AddTorrentDialog.cpp:147-150](src/ui/dialogs/AddTorrentDialog.cpp#L147-L150)).
- **"Start each immediately" checkbox**, default checked. Inverse of `AddTorrentConfig::startPaused`.
- **Live validation** via `textChanged` signal — parses each non-empty line, counts magnets vs skipped, updates a status label ("3 magnet link(s) ready." / "2 magnet(s) ready, 1 line(s) skipped." / "1 line(s) skipped (only magnet: links supported).").
- **Accept button** disabled until at least one valid magnet parsed. On accept, magnets list is already cached in `m_parsedMagnets`.

Styling follows the existing dark/grayscale pattern ([TorrentFilesDialog](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.cpp) + [IndexerStatusPanel](src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.cpp)) — `rgba(12,12,12,0.98)` bg, `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` border, 12px radius, monospace textedit.

### TankorentPage wiring

**[TankorentPage.h](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h)** — `m_addUrlBtn` member + `onAddFromUrlClicked` slot declaration.

**[TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp)**:
- `#include "ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h"` alongside the other dialogs.
- "Add URL" toolbar button placed between Sources and More, same 30px height / pointing cursor pattern as siblings.
- `onAddFromUrlClicked` impl — opens dialog, on accept iterates `magnets()`:
  1. `m_client->isDuplicate(magnet)` → skip if already added.
  2. `m_client->resolveMetadata(magnet)` → empty hash means invalid/unparseable → skip.
  3. Build minimal `AddTorrentConfig`: `category = dialog.category()`, `destinationPath = defaultPaths[category]`, `contentLayout = "original"`, `sequential = false`, `startPaused = !dialog.startImmediately()`, empty priorities (libtorrent default = Normal for all files).
  4. `m_client->startDownload(hash, config)`.
- After the loop: switch to Transfers tab if any adds succeeded + surface count in `m_searchStatus` ("Added 3, skipped 1" / "Added 5 torrent(s)" / "All 3 skipped (duplicates or invalid)").

### Scope call-out — .torrent URL support deferred

TODO 5.1 said: "For `.torrent` URLs, download the file to temp + invoke add-torrent-file path (TorrentClient likely already has this — verify during implementation)."

Verified during implementation: **no such path exists.** [TorrentEngine](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h) has `addMagnet` (wraps `lt::parse_magnet_uri`) and `read_resume_data` for fast-resume files, but no `lt::torrent_info` / `add_torrent_file` path. Building it would need: a new `TorrentEngine::addTorrentFile(const QString& path, const QString& savePath, bool paused) → QString` plumbed through to libtorrent + a temp-file download step via `QNetworkAccessManager`. Non-trivial — scope-expands this batch significantly.

**Call:** ship magnet-URI support this batch (closes the realistic-usage portion of DOWNLOADER-P0-1 — users paste magnets from Stremio / trackers / chat). Flag `.torrent` URL + local `.torrent` file support as follow-up work that needs a new `TorrentEngine::addTorrentFile` API. Documented in the dialog itself: placeholder text and hint label both say "Only magnet: links supported in this build." Batch 5.2 (drag-drop) will need the same API before it can route local `.torrent` files — worth batching the engine work there if Hemanth wants it.

Not a silent deviation — called out for Agent 6's objective check. Honest trade-off: ship a usable feature now, wire up the file path when there's a concrete need.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 5.1]: NEW AddFromUrlDialog (multi-magnet entry, clipboard preload when clipboard is a magnet, live validation + count, category combo, start-immediately checkbox). "Add URL" button in TankorentPage toolbar. On accept: iterate magnets through resolveMetadata + startDownload with default AddTorrentConfig (category from combo, default destination path for that category, original layout, non-sequential, user-toggled start-paused flag). Duplicates skipped via isDuplicate. Switches to Transfers tab + surfaces count in status label. .torrent URL support deferred to follow-up — documented in dialog placeholder and hint. | files: CMakeLists.txt, src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h (NEW), src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.cpp (NEW), src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

### Smoke notes (Batch 5.1 standalone)

1. "Add URL" button visible in TankorentPage toolbar between Sources and More.
2. Click with empty clipboard → dialog opens empty. Type 3 magnet URIs → status shows "3 magnet link(s) ready." → Accept → Transfers tab opens with 3 new torrents queued.
3. Copy a magnet to clipboard, click "Add URL" → dialog opens with clipboard content pre-filled.
4. Paste a mix of magnets + garbage text → status shows "N magnet(s) ready, M line(s) skipped." → Accept adds only the valid ones.
5. Paste all-invalid content → Accept button disabled, status shows "1 line(s) skipped (only magnet: links supported)."
6. Paste a magnet already in the transfers list → status after Accept shows "Added N, skipped 1."
7. Existing search-result add flow via row context menu / row double-click unchanged — still opens AddTorrentDialog with per-file priority UI.

### Next

Phase 5 Batch 5.2 — drag-drop on TankorentPage. `setAcceptDrops(true)` + `dragEnterEvent` + `dropEvent` overrides. Magnet URIs + URL-list drops route through AddFromUrlDialog via its `seedText` seam. `.torrent` file drops — gated on the same engine API as 5.1's deferred scope; I'll surface the question to Hemanth before coding 5.2 to decide: (a) build `TorrentEngine::addTorrentFile` now as part of 5.2, or (b) scope 5.2 to URL/magnet drops only and leave `.torrent` file support for a later dedicated batch.

Standing by on smoke + Agent 6 review.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 5 Batch 5.2 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.2 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:304-316](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L304-L316). Drag-drop on TankorentPage — magnet drops route through AddFromUrlDialog (pre-filled via the `seedText` seam from Batch 5.1); `.torrent` file drops surface an informative Toast. Hemanth signaled "next batch" without answering my open question re: building `TorrentEngine::addTorrentFile` — interpreted as "proceed with scoped deferral," consistent with 5.1's trade-off.

### Scope

- **Shipped**: magnet URIs (from `text/plain` and `text/uri-list` / `magnet:` scheme URLs), drag-enter gating, drop-time partitioning, user-facing Toast feedback via existing [Toast](src/ui/widgets/Toast.h) helper.
- **Deferred** (same reason as Batch 5.1): local `.torrent` file drops and `.torrent` HTTP URL drops. Needs `TorrentEngine::addTorrentFile` + temp-file download path. Detected in `dropEvent` → counted as `torrentFileCount` → surfaced in Toast (".torrent files aren't supported yet — use magnet links."). User knows what happened.

### Changes

**[TankorentPage.h](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h)**:
- Forward-declared `QDragEnterEvent` and `QDropEvent`.
- New `protected:` section: `dragEnterEvent(QDragEnterEvent*) override` + `dropEvent(QDropEvent*) override`.
- New private helper: `QPair<int, int> addMagnetBatch(magnets, category, startImmediately)` — factored from Batch 5.1's inline loop so both the toolbar click path and the drop path share the same add logic.

**[TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp)**:
- `#include "ui/widgets/Toast.h"` alongside the other UI includes; also `<QMimeData>`, `<QDragEnterEvent>`, `<QDropEvent>`, `<QFileInfo>`.
- Constructor: `setAcceptDrops(true)` after `m_nam` init.
- **`addMagnetBatch` helper**: the inline loop from `onAddFromUrlClicked` (5.1) moved here verbatim — iterates magnets, calls `isDuplicate` / `resolveMetadata` / `startDownload`, returns `{added, skipped}`. `onAddFromUrlClicked` now calls the helper and handles status-label surfacing.
- **`dragEnterEvent`**: accepts drop if MIME has URLs, OR if MIME text starts with `magnet:` (case-insensitive). Rejection for anything else is automatic (no `acceptProposedAction`).
- **`dropEvent`** flow:
  1. Parse mime: walk `urls()` (magnet-scheme → magnets list; `file://` or other → passed to `classify()` lambda), then walk `text()` split by newlines through `classify()`.
  2. `classify()` lambda: `magnet:` prefix → magnets list; `.torrent` suffix → `torrentFileCount++` (deferred); everything else ignored.
  3. De-duplicate collected magnets.
  4. If no magnets: Toast with appropriate message depending on whether `.torrent` files were present.
  5. If magnets present: open `AddFromUrlDialog(this, magnets.joined("\n"))` — the user picks category + start-immediately for the whole batch. On accept, reuse `addMagnetBatch`. Toast on completion with "Added N torrent(s), skipped M (K .torrent file(s) ignored)" formatted based on what happened.
- All drop paths call `event->acceptProposedAction()` at the end so the OS cursor resolves correctly.

### Dialog reuse call-out

TODO spec says "Multiple items → open AddFromUrlDialog pre-filled with the URL/magnet list." My impl opens AddFromUrlDialog for **both** single and multi-magnet drops — uniform path, user always gets a chance to pick category + confirm. TODO also says "Single `.torrent` file → open existing AddTorrentDialog preloaded with file path," but `.torrent` files fall into the deferred-scope bucket here. If/when the engine path lands, `.torrent` drops can route to AddTorrentDialog per spec without disturbing the magnet flow.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 5.2]: Drag-drop on TankorentPage. setAcceptDrops(true); dragEnterEvent accepts URLs + magnet-prefixed text; dropEvent partitions URLs/text into magnets vs .torrent files (deferred-scope, surfaced via Toast), opens AddFromUrlDialog with magnets as seedText for category + start-immediately confirmation. Factored the 5.1 inline add loop into addMagnetBatch helper so both the toolbar button path and the drop path share single-sourced add logic. Toast notifies on drop completion with "Added N, skipped M (K .torrent ignored)" formatting. | files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

### Smoke notes (Batch 5.2 standalone)

1. **Drag a magnet URL from a browser tab** (Firefox: right-click → Copy Link → drag from URL bar; or drag a `magnet:` link element) onto TankorentPage → dialog opens pre-filled → Accept → Transfers tab opens with the new torrent → Toast: "Added 1 torrent(s)".
2. **Drag text containing magnets** from a text editor or chat → dialog opens pre-filled with every line; non-magnet lines show as "skipped" in the dialog status.
3. **Drag a `.torrent` file from Explorer** → Toast: ".torrent files aren't supported yet — use magnet links." Nothing added (expected per deferred scope).
4. **Drag multiple magnets simultaneously** (select-multi in a text editor, drag onto page) → dialog opens with all as seed text → Accept → Transfers opens with each added.
5. **Drag unrelated content** (image, non-magnet text) → dragEnterEvent rejects, cursor is "no-drop" icon, no action taken.
6. **Regression**: existing "Add URL" toolbar button + search-result double-click add unchanged.

### Next

Phase 5 Batch 5.3 — Ctrl+V keybind + optional File menu entry. Intercept `Ctrl+V` in TankorentPage's `keyPressEvent` when focus is NOT in the search field (or any QLineEdit / QTextEdit child). Opens AddFromUrlDialog with clipboard preload. Also need to check [MainWindow](src/ui/MainWindow.cpp) for an existing QMenuBar — per TODO: if present, add "Add Torrent → From URL...\tCtrl+V" entry; if not (likely — app uses `WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE` style), skip the menu and rely on the page-level shortcut.

Standing by on smoke + Agent 6 review.

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 5 Batch 5.3 SHIPPED (Phase 5 complete)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 5 Batch 5.3 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:318-326](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L318-L326). `Ctrl+V` on TankorentPage opens [AddFromUrlDialog](src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h) with clipboard preload — unless focus is in a text input, in which case the paste passes through normally. Menu entry skipped per TODO fallback — MainWindow has no QMenuBar.

### MainWindow audit

Grepped [MainWindow.cpp](src/ui/MainWindow.cpp) for `QMenuBar` / `menuBar()` / `setMenuBar` — **zero matches**. App is menu-less (confirmed Tankoban's `WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE` pattern). Per TODO: "If no menu bar exists... skip the menu entry. Ctrl+V keybind inside TankorentPage suffices." Menu entry skipped; keybind alone is the feature.

### Changes

**[TankorentPage.h](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h)** — forward-declared `QKeyEvent`, added `keyPressEvent(QKeyEvent*) override` to the protected section next to the drag-drop overrides from 5.2.

**[TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp)**:
- New includes: `<QKeyEvent>`, `<QKeySequence>`, `<QTextEdit>` (needed for the qobject_cast check).
- Impl placed right after `dropEvent`:

```cpp
void TankorentPage::keyPressEvent(QKeyEvent* event)
{
    if (event->matches(QKeySequence::Paste)) {
        QWidget* focused = QApplication::focusWidget();
        const bool inTextInput = qobject_cast<QLineEdit*>(focused)
                              || qobject_cast<QTextEdit*>(focused);
        if (!inTextInput) {
            onAddFromUrlClicked();
            event->accept();
            return;
        }
    }
    QWidget::keyPressEvent(event);
}
```

Reuses the same `onAddFromUrlClicked` slot the toolbar button wires to — same `AddFromUrlDialog` with the same clipboard-preload behavior, same `addMagnetBatch` call path, same status-label feedback. Consistent UX.

### Why `event->matches(QKeySequence::Paste)` and not manual `Ctrl+V` check

`QKeySequence::Paste` resolves to Ctrl+V on Windows/Linux, Cmd+V on macOS. Tankoban is Windows-first today but the sequence abstraction is idiomatic Qt and costs nothing. `event->matches()` also handles Shift+Insert (legacy Windows paste) for free, which some users hit reflexively.

### Focus-through semantics

- Focus in `m_queryEdit` (search bar): QLineEdit handles Ctrl+V natively → text pastes into search. TankorentPage's `keyPressEvent` is never reached (QLineEdit consumes). Safe — no regression.
- Focus in a QLineEdit inside a modal dialog (AddTorrentDialog's destination edit, etc.): modal dialog captures the event, never bubbles to TankorentPage.
- Focus on `m_resultsTable` or `m_transfersTable`: both have `setFocusPolicy(Qt::NoFocus)` from createResults/createTransfers, so they don't take focus; bubbled Ctrl+V reaches TankorentPage. Opens dialog.
- Focus on a toolbar button (Search / Sources / Add URL / etc.): Ctrl+V reaches TankorentPage via bubble; `focusWidget` is the button (not a QLineEdit/QTextEdit). Opens dialog.
- Focus on a nested QTextEdit anywhere in the hierarchy (not currently present but future-proofed): pass-through.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 5.3]: Ctrl+V keybind on TankorentPage opens AddFromUrlDialog with clipboard preload. Uses QKeySequence::Paste for cross-platform correctness + Shift+Insert coverage. Focus check skips when QLineEdit / QTextEdit has focus so in-field paste passes through. Menu entry skipped — MainWindow has no QMenuBar. | files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

### Phase 5 exit — READY FOR REVIEW

READY FOR REVIEW — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Phase 5]: Downloader add flows expansion | Objective: Phase 5 per TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md + agents/audits/tankorent_2026-04-16.md DOWNLOADER-P0-1. Files: CMakeLists.txt, src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.h, src/ui/dialogs/AddFromUrlDialog.cpp, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp.

**Known deferral, surfaced for the review**: `.torrent` local file support + `.torrent` HTTP URL support. Needs a `TorrentEngine::addTorrentFile` path that doesn't exist today. Documented in the dialog UI (placeholder + hint label both say "Only magnet: links supported in this build"), in the drop handler (Toast: ".torrent files aren't supported yet — use magnet links."), and in this chat across Batches 5.1/5.2/5.3. Not blocking DOWNLOADER-P0-1 per realistic usage patterns — magnet URIs cover the overwhelming majority of torrent-source handoffs (Stremio, 1337x, PirateBay, YTS, Nyaa all emit magnets directly).

### Smoke (Phase 5 end-to-end)

1. **Toolbar**: click "Add URL" → dialog opens (clipboard-preloaded if clipboard was a magnet) → paste magnets → Accept → Transfers tab.
2. **Keybind**: click anywhere on TankorentPage except the search box → Ctrl+V → dialog opens with clipboard. Regression check: focus the search box → Ctrl+V → pastes into search, no dialog.
3. **Drag-drop (magnets)**: drag a magnet URL from a browser or a text file → dialog opens pre-filled → Accept → Transfers + Toast.
4. **Drag-drop (.torrent files)**: drag a .torrent from Explorer → Toast ".torrent files aren't supported yet" → no action.
5. **Mixed drop**: magnet + .torrent → only the magnet lands in the dialog; Toast formats as "Added N (K .torrent file(s) ignored)".
6. **Duplicate**: paste an already-added magnet → counted as skipped in status line / Toast.
7. **Status feedback is route-appropriate**: toolbar/keybind use the status label (persistent); drops use Toast (transient overlay). Deliberate UX split — drops happen in flow, status label stays for later glance.

### What's open

Phase 6 (DOWNLOADER-P0-2 + P0-3 + P0-4 merged — Unified TorrentPropertiesWidget). 5 batches per TODO: 6.1 shell widget, 6.2 Files tab (DOWNLOADER-P0-4), 6.3 Trackers tab (DOWNLOADER-P0-2), 6.4 Peers tab (DOWNLOADER-P0-3), 6.5 General tab. Biggest phase by surface area — tabbed per-torrent detail view.

Standing by on smoke + Agent 6 review.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 StreamDetailView layout rebalance

Per Hemanth: the detail view's hero was dominating the page; episodes + sources ended up in a tiny corner. My first-attempt fix (wrapping in a QScrollArea) was the wrong direction — user wants episode/source surfaces to be bigger, not scrollable.

### What I reverted (from this turn, wrong direction)

- `QScrollArea` wrapping `contentRow` — **removed**; went back to `root->addLayout(contentRow, 1)`.
- `setVerticalScrollBarPolicy(Qt::ScrollBarAlwaysOff)` on episode table — **removed**; table's internal scrollbar is back (user confirmed they like scrolling inside the table, just wants the table to be bigger).
- `setMinimum/MaximumHeight` on episode table after populate — **removed**; table grows naturally with stretch=1 again.
- `<QScrollArea>` + `<QFrame>` includes — stripped.

### What I actually changed (correct direction)

**1. Hero height 240 → 140.** [StreamDetailView.cpp buildUI / renderHeroPixmap](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp) — hero still visually present but no longer eats ~240px of vertical budget. Frees ~100px for episodes + sources.

**2. Removed `m_infoLabel` entirely.** [StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h) + [StreamDetailView.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp) — the year · type · IMDB rating line was redundant with the chip row immediately below. Deleted from buildUI + showEntry + applyChips (removed the hide/show coordination since there's nothing to coordinate). ~20px more vertical budget.

**3. Episode table and sources pane get those ~120px back** via the existing `stretch=1` / `stretch=2` weighting in contentRow. No code change to the stretch logic — the space just wasn't available before.

### Space budget (on a 768px window)

| Row | Pre-fix | Post-fix |
|---|---|---|
| Top bar | 40 | 40 |
| Hero | 240 | 140 |
| Title + chips + info + desc + cast + season | ~180 | ~155 (info line gone) |
| Remaining for episodes + status | ~308 | ~433 |

Episode table at 64px/row: ~4 rows → ~6+ rows visible at once. Sources pane on the right grows proportionally.

### What's explicitly still true

- Episode table keeps its internal vertical scrollbar for when a season has more rows than the available space. User confirmed this behavior is desired.
- Page-level scroll is **not** added — the detail view fits in the window with room to spare for most titles post-rebalance.
- Hero still fades with the gradient overlay (`renderHeroPixmap`). Art still shows, just smaller.
- Chip row (year · runtime · genres · IMDb · type) unchanged — that's the metadata surface now.

### Files touched

- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h) — `m_infoLabel` member removed.
- [src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp) — hero height + `m_infoLabel` construction + setText + hide/show coordination all removed; scroll-area revert from earlier this turn.

No other files. No sidecar change.

### Re-verification

1. Stream mode → Open any series detail → hero shows at 140px (was 240); title + chip row + description + cast/director + season combo all visible; episode table now shows ~5-6 rows at once depending on window size, with internal scrollbar for longer seasons.
2. Sources pane (right column) gets more vertical space, matching the taller episode column.
3. Regression: hero image still paints with gradient fade. Chips still populate on meta arrival. Description show-more still works. Cast/director row still shows. Trailer button still in top bar.
4. Regression: movie detail views (no episode table) still render — title + chips + desc + cast + sources pane, no season combo, no episode table. Sources pane fills the right column.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, StreamDetailView layout rebalance]: Hero shrunk 240px→140px; redundant m_infoLabel removed (chips row already conveys year/type/rating). ~125px of vertical budget returned to episode table + sources pane via existing stretch weights. Reverted this-turn's QScrollArea + table-size-clamp experiments that were the wrong direction. Episode table keeps its internal scrollbar for long seasons. | files: src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.h, src/ui/pages/stream/StreamDetailView.cpp

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 6 Batch 6.1 SHIPPED

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 6 Batch 6.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:344-355](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L344-L355). NEW `src/ui/pages/tankorent/` subdirectory. NEW [TorrentPropertiesWidget](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h) — `QDialog` host for the tabbed properties view. 4 placeholder tabs (General / Trackers / Peers / Files) with "arriving in Batch 6.X" labels; 6.2-6.5 swap each placeholder for a concrete tab class. Double-click + Info-column click on the transfers table now open this widget; "Properties..." context menu entry added.

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

Two additive entries (one SOURCES, one HEADERS), placed with the other `src/ui/pages/*` block:

```
    src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp
    src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h
```

No link / target / find_package changes. New subdirectory `src/ui/pages/tankorent/` is implicitly recognized by CMake's AUTOMOC since the individual files are listed.

### Shell widget

**NEW [TorrentPropertiesWidget.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h)** — 36 LOC. Public API:

```cpp
explicit TorrentPropertiesWidget(TorrentClient* client, QWidget* parent = nullptr);
void showTorrent(const QString& infoHash);
```

`showTorrent` stashes the hash, resolves the torrent name via `m_client->listActive()`, sets the window title ("Torrent Properties — <name>" or "... — <hash-prefix>" fallback), starts the 1 Hz refresh timer. Call before `show()` / `exec()`.

**NEW [TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp)** — 95 LOC. Structure:

- `QDialog` host with `QTabWidget` child.
- **4 placeholder tabs** constructed via a local `buildPlaceholderTab(label)` helper that returns a `QWidget` with a centered `QLabel`. Each placeholder states which batch will populate it. Order matches TODO: General / Trackers / Peers / Files. General is the default first tab per 6.5's spec.
- **Styling** mirrors [IndexerStatusPanel](src/ui/pages/IndexerStatusPanel.cpp) + [TorrentFilesDialog](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.cpp) — dialog `rgba(12,12,12,0.98)` bg with `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)` border + 12px radius. QTabWidget has `#TorrentPropertiesTabs` objectName for scoped QSS — tab bar uses `#1a1a1a`/`#ccc` default, `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)`/`#eee` selected, `rgba(255,255,255,0.05)` hover. Matches the rest of the Tankorent chrome.
- **1 Hz refresh timer** (`m_refreshTimer`, 1000 ms). Constructed but NOT started in ctor — `showTorrent()` starts it after stashing the infoHash. Slot `refresh()` is a no-op today; Batches 6.2-6.5 will give each concrete tab its own refresh hook that this slot forwards to.

Dialog lifetime: opens as top-level via `show()` + `WA_DeleteOnClose` (see TankorentPage wiring below). Not blocking — user can keep scrolling the transfers table while it's open.

### TankorentPage wiring

**[TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp)**:
- `#include "ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h"`.
- Renamed the transfers-table `openFilesFor` lambda to `openPropertiesFor`. It now constructs `TorrentPropertiesWidget` + calls `showTorrent(hash)` + `show()`. Applied to both `cellDoubleClicked` and `cellClicked(col==11)` (the Info column) — matches the old dual-entry behavior but points at the new widget.
- Transfers context menu gains a new **"Properties..."** action next to the existing "View Files..." entry. For now both entries work: "View Files..." still opens the legacy [TorrentFilesDialog](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.cpp) (deleted in Batch 6.2), "Properties..." opens the new widget.

`WA_DeleteOnClose` on every launch: the widget cleans itself up when the user dismisses it. No member-pointer tracking needed in TankorentPage.

### Transition state note

Between 6.1 and 6.2, both the old read-only `TorrentFilesDialog` AND the new tabbed widget coexist. Users who hit "View Files..." see the old dialog; users who double-click or pick "Properties..." see the new widget. Visually obvious which is which — the new widget is tabbed and much larger. 6.2's first act is to delete [TorrentFilesDialog.h](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.h) + `.cpp`, remove them from CMakeLists.txt, and redirect the "View Files..." action to `TorrentPropertiesWidget::showTorrent` with the Files tab pre-selected.

### Rule 11

QTabWidget with 4 placeholder tabs (General / Trackers / Peers / Files — each "arriving in Batch 6.X"). showTorrent(infoHash) resolves torrent name via TorrentClient::listActive, sets title, starts 1Hz refresh timer (slot is no-op until 6.2-6.5 give tabs refresh hooks). TankorentPage transfers table double-click + Info-column click + new "Properties..." context menu entry all route here. ## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Search bar alignment with video mode

User shipped screenshots — stream mode search bar + chrome visibly tighter than video mode's. Exploration confirmed: tiles themselves already matched, but the search bar's horizontal margins + height + top padding differed.

### Three fixes on [StreamPage::buildSearchBar](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)

| Setting | Pre-fix | Post-fix | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| Search frame `contentsMargins` (left/right) | 16 | **20** | Align with gridLayout's `(20,0,20,20)` so search bar edges match tile grid edges |
| Search frame `contentsMargins` (top) | 8 | **20** | Provide the same "breathing room from page top" video mode gets via its `addSpacing(12) + searchLayout(0,12,0,0)` stack |
| Search frame `contentsMargins` (bottom) | 8 | **8** | Unchanged |
| Search input `setFixedHeight` | 32 | **36** | Match VideosPage.cpp:119's 36px |
| Search button `setFixedHeight` | 32 | **36** | Row consistency with 36px input |
| Addons / Calendar / Catalog buttons | 32 | **36** | Same |

Done via one `replace_all` on the button heights + an explicit edit on the search frame margins + input height.

### What's already aligned (verified no-op via exploration)

- Scroll layout margins `(20,0,20,20)` + spacing 24 in both [VideosPage](src/ui/pages/VideosPage.cpp) and [StreamPage browse layer](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp).
- Library section header font + color + letter-spacing.
- `CONTINUE WATCHING` header font + padding.
- Tile density mappings + sort combo 150px + density slider 100×20.

### What intentionally remains different

- **Search bar lives outside the scroll area in stream mode** (sticky at page top), whereas video mode's search bar is inside `gridLayout` and scrolls with content. This is an architectural UX choice — sticky-search is arguably better for stream mode since users search far more often there. Making them identical would require moving the search bar into the scroll area, which is a larger restructure. Keeping as-is; the horizontal/vertical padding fixes above close the *visual* alignment gap without needing the structural move.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — 3 contentsMargins values + 1 input height + 4 button heights.

No other files touched.

### Re-verification

1. Open stream mode. Compare screenshot vs video mode screenshot — search bar should now sit the same distance from the page top + left edge.
2. Search input height matches the Search / Addons / Calendar / Catalog button heights (all 36px) — no vertical jaggedness.
3. Search input left edge aligns with the "CONTINUE WATCHING" header + tile grid left edge (all at x=20 from the scroll area edge).
4. Regression: search-history dropdown positioning (Batch 4.2's `positionSearchHistoryDropdown`) uses `mapTo(this, QPoint(0, h+2))` — still anchors correctly under the (now taller) input.
5. Regression: search-bar spinner + Catalog button clicks still work; just taller by 4px.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Stream search bar alignment with video mode]: Horizontal margins 16→20, top margin 8→20 on m_searchBarFrame; input + 4 buttons 32→36px height. Matches VideosPage.cpp:119 + gridLayout chrome. Search bar remains outside the scroll area by design (sticky UX); only padding + sizing aligned. | files: src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 6 Batch 6.2 SHIPPED (isolate-commit)

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

**Added:**
```
    src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentFilesTab.cpp

```

**Removed:**

Net +2 entries in SOURCES, +2 in HEADERS (2 added, 2 removed on each side). No link / target changes.

### Engine additions (bounded)

Agent 4B flag #1 held: DOWNLOADER-P0-4 is UI-only since `TorrentEngine::setFilePriorities` already existed. Two small engine additions this batch:

- **[TorrentEngine::torrentFiles](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp)** — now also emits a `priority` integer per file (read via libtorrent `handle.get_file_priorities()`). Existing Stream-engine callers only read `index`/`name`/`size`/`progress` and are unaffected.
- **[TorrentEngine::renameFile(infoHash, fileIndex, newName)](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp)** — new method wrapping libtorrent `handle.rename_file(lt::file_index_t, std::string)`. Stub provided in the non-libtorrent build.

Header updated at [TorrentEngine.h:64-65](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h#L64-L65) with `renameFile` declaration.

### Files tab shipped

**NEW [TorrentFilesTab.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentFilesTab.h)** — 56 LOC. QWidget with public `setInfoHash(QString)` + public slot `refresh()`. Parent widget ([TorrentPropertiesWidget](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp)) drives the 1 Hz `refresh()` call.

**NEW [TorrentFilesTab.cpp](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentFilesTab.cpp)** — 270 LOC. Features:

- **QTreeWidget** with columns Name / Size / Progress / Priority. Directory grouping via `/` split of the JSON `name` field. Folders are `ROLE_FILE_INDEX = -1`; leaves carry their libtorrent file index.
- **Per-row Priority combo** (`QComboBox` in cell via `setItemWidget`, 5 items: Skip / Low / Normal / High / Maximum). Mapped to libtorrent priority scale 0/1/4/6/7. On change, rebuilds the full priority vector from current combo state and calls `TorrentEngine::setFilePriorities(infoHash, vector)`.
- **Context menu** on tree rows: Skip/Low/Normal/High/Maximum shortcuts + Open File + Open Containing Folder + Rename. Folder rows cascade priority changes to all descendant leaves. File rows only.
- **Select All / Deselect All buttons** below the tree — set every priority to Normal / Skip respectively via the same combo path. Pattern matches [AddTorrentDialog.cpp:548-589](src/ui/dialogs/AddTorrentDialog.cpp#L548-L589).
- **Open File / Open Folder** via `QDesktopServices::openUrl(QUrl::fromLocalFile(...))`. Absolute path resolved as `QDir(torrent.savePath).filePath(file.fullPath)`.
- **Rename** via `QInputDialog::getText` + `TorrentEngine::renameFile`. Path relative portion preserved (rename only the leaf name). libtorrent handles internal state updates (file_storage + resume data) — no QFile manual work.
- **Live progress refresh** via public `refresh()` slot: re-fetches `torrentFiles`, updates Progress text in-place. Priority column is NOT refreshed (user-driven — don't overwrite mid-edit).

### Priority scale call-out for Agent 6 review

TODO specified 5 priority levels (Skip / Low / Normal / High / Maximum) while [AddTorrentDialog.h:90-93](src/ui/dialogs/AddTorrentDialog.h#L90-L93) uses 4 levels (Skip=0 / Normal=1 / High=6 / Maximum=7). Pre-existing quirk: AddTorrentDialog's `PRIORITY_NORMAL = 1` is actually libtorrent's "Low" value — its "Normal" files are de-prioritized vs a correct Normal (libtorrent default = 4).

My call in 6.2: use correct libtorrent semantics (0/1/4/6/7) in the Files tab. Reading logic maps {0→Skip, 1-3→Low, 4-5→Normal, 6→High, 7+→Maximum} — so torrents added via AddTorrentDialog with its "Normal=1" will display as "Low" in this tab until the user edits or AddTorrentDialog is updated in a follow-up polish batch.

Not a regression — current behavior was just mislabeled in the existing dialog. Surfacing it honestly for Agent 6.

### Transition cleanup

- [TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp) — removed `#include "ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.h"`. "View Files..." context menu entry now opens [TorrentPropertiesWidget](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h) with the new `TabFiles` index pre-selected (`showTorrent(hash, TorrentPropertiesWidget::TabFiles)`). "Properties..." entry and double-click still open with the default General tab.
- [TorrentPropertiesWidget.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h) gained `enum Tab { TabGeneral, TabTrackers, TabPeers, TabFiles }` + `showTorrent(hash, tabIndex = TabGeneral)` overload.
- Old [TorrentFilesDialog.h](src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.h) + `.cpp` files deleted via `rm`.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 6.2]: DOWNLOADER-P0-4 closed. NEW TorrentFilesTab replaces deleted TorrentFilesDialog. Per-file priority combo writes to live torrent via TorrentEngine::setFilePriorities (already existed). Context menu with Skip/Low/Normal/High/Maximum + Open File + Open Containing Folder + Rename; folder rows cascade priority to descendants. Select-All/Deselect-All buttons. Live progress refresh via parent's 1Hz timer. Engine additions: torrentFiles() now emits priority per file; new renameFile() wraps libtorrent handle.rename_file. "View Files..." context menu entry redirects to TorrentPropertiesWidget with TabFiles pre-selected. Legacy dialog deleted + CMakeLists.txt cleaned up. | files: CMakeLists.txt, src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h, src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp, src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h, src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp, src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentFilesTab.h (NEW), src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentFilesTab.cpp (NEW), src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp | DELETED: src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.h, src/ui/dialogs/TorrentFilesDialog.cpp

### Smoke notes (Batch 6.2 standalone — isolate-commit)

1. Double-click a transfer → properties widget opens → Files tab shows the torrent's file tree with current priorities.
2. Change a file's priority combo from Normal to Skip → download stops on that file (Progress stops incrementing). Change to Maximum → download accelerates on that file (progress gains on it first vs Normal peers).
3. Right-click a file row → context menu shows all 5 priority levels + Open File + Open Containing Folder + Rename.
4. Right-click a folder row → context menu shows 5 priority levels only (no open/rename on folders). Priority cascades to all children.
5. Select All button → all files set to Normal. Deselect All → all to Skip.
6. Rename a file → QInputDialog appears → new name → libtorrent renames. File now shows in new name.
7. Open File on a completed file → OS opens it. Open Containing Folder → Explorer opens at parent dir.
8. Legacy "View Files..." context menu entry → opens properties widget with Files tab pre-selected (regression check — old dialog is gone).
9. Live progress refresh: watch a downloading file; progress percentage updates every second.

New engine API: `trackersFor`, `addTracker`, `removeTracker`, `editTrackerUrl` + `TrackerInfo` struct. Context menu: Force reannounce (existing API), Copy URL, Edit URL, Remove. ## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 6 Batches 6.3 + 6.4 + 6.5 SHIPPED (Phase 6 complete)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 6 closed. Hemanth directed "next phase" so all three remaining batches shipped together — DOWNLOADER-P0-2 (trackers) + DOWNLOADER-P0-3 (peers) + TorrentDetails General tab landing in one bundle. Explicit deviation from `feedback_one_fix_per_rebuild` per directive; batches are interdependent — no meaningful rebuild gate between them since all three wire into the same `TorrentPropertiesWidget`.

### Rule 7 announcement — CMakeLists.txt touched

Six additive entries (three SOURCES, three HEADERS) in the tankorent/ block:

```
    src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentTrackersTab.cpp  (+ .h)
    src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPeersTab.cpp     (+ .h)
    src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentGeneralTab.cpp   (+ .h)
```

No link / target / find_package changes.

### Engine additions (bounded per TODO ceiling)

**[TorrentEngine.h](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.h)** gained three data structs at file scope and three clusters of methods:

```cpp
struct TrackerInfo { url, tier, status, nextAnnounce, minAnnounce, peers, seeds, leeches, downloaded, message };
struct PeerInfo    { address, port, client, country, flags, connection, progress, downSpeed, upSpeed, downloaded, uploaded, relevance };
struct TorrentDetails { name, totalSize, pieceCount, pieceSize, created, createdBy, comment, infoHash, savePath, currentTracker, availability, shareRatio, nextReannounce };

// 6.3
QList<TrackerInfo> trackersFor(infoHash) const;
void    addTracker(infoHash, url, tier);       // tier 0-indexed internally
void    removeTracker(infoHash, url);          // via replace_trackers rebuild
void    editTrackerUrl(infoHash, oldUrl, newUrl);

// 6.4
QList<PeerInfo> peersFor(infoHash) const;
void    banPeer(ipAddr);                       // persists to QSettings + applies to session ip_filter
void    addPeer(infoHash, "ip:port");
QStringList bannedPeers() const;

// 6.5
TorrentDetails torrentDetails(infoHash) const;
```

**[TorrentEngine.cpp](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp)** impls + new libtorrent includes (`announce_entry.hpp`, `peer_info.hpp`, `ip_filter.hpp`):
- `trackersFor` aggregates across `announce_entry.endpoints[*].info_hashes[*]` — takes the first populated info_hash state per endpoint. Status derived from `updating`/`last_error`/`fails`/`start_sent` flags. Times computed via `std::chrono::duration_cast` on `next_announce - lt::clock_type::now()`.
- `addTracker` uses `lt::announce_entry(url)` ctor + `tier` field + `handle.add_tracker(ae)`.
- `removeTracker` / `editTrackerUrl` both rebuild the tracker vector via `handle.trackers()` → filter/mutate → `handle.replace_trackers(vec)`.
- `peersFor` wraps `handle.get_peer_info(vec)`. Flags compact: "I" (remote_interested) / "E" (rc4_encrypted) / "uTP" (utp_socket). Country is `"--"` — GeoIP is out-of-scope per identity ceiling.
- `banPeer` writes to QSettings `tankorent/bannedPeers` (deduped append) then updates `session::ip_filter` with `blocked` rule for that address range.
- `addPeer` parses `ip:port`, calls `handle.connect_peer({addr, port})`.
- **Ban list persistence**: [start() :372-385](src/core/torrent/TorrentEngine.cpp) now loads QSettings `tankorent/bannedPeers` and applies every address to the session ip_filter before the alert worker thread starts. Banned peers stay blocked across restart.
- `torrentDetails` combines `torrent_handle::status()` + `torrent_file()` + first-announce-entry into the single `TorrentDetails` struct. Fields: name/save_path/total_wanted from status; piece count/piece size/creator/comment/creation_date from torrent_info; share ratio = `all_time_upload / all_time_download` (0 when nothing downloaded yet); next reannounce via `status.next_announce` chrono duration; availability = `status.distributed_copies` (libtorrent's swarm availability proxy).
- Stubs for all new methods in the `#else !HAS_LIBTORRENT` block.

### Batch 6.3 — Trackers tab

**[TorrentTrackersTab.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentTrackersTab.h) + [.cpp](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentTrackersTab.cpp)** — `QWidget` with 10-column `QTableWidget` (URL / Tier / Status / Next Announce / Min Announce / Peers / Seeds / Leeches / Downloaded / Message). Above the table: "Add Tracker..." button. On the table: context menu per row with Force Reannounce (existing `TorrentClient::forceReannounce`), Copy URL (clipboard), Edit URL... (QInputDialog), Remove. **Tier is rendered 1-indexed in the UI** (libtorrent's 0-indexed internal) — converted at the boundary: display `tier + 1`, input `tier - 1`. Next/Min-announce columns format as relative "15s" / "2m 30s" / "1h 15m" rather than absolute timestamps.

### Batch 6.4 — Peers tab

**[TorrentPeersTab.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPeersTab.h) + [.cpp](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPeersTab.cpp)** — `QWidget` with 11-column `QTableWidget` (Country / IP:Port / Client / Flags / Connection / Progress / Down / Up / Downloaded / Uploaded / Relevance). Above: "Add Peer..." button (QInputDialog for "ip:port"). Per-row context menu: Copy IP:Port, Ban Peer Permanently (confirm dialog before applying — "persists across app restart"). Speeds formatted via existing `humanSize(bytes)+"/s"` helper; sizes via `humanSize`. Progress/Relevance as "NN.N%".

### Batch 6.5 — General tab

**[TorrentGeneralTab.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentGeneralTab.h) + [.cpp](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentGeneralTab.cpp)** — `QWidget` with a `QFormLayout` of 13 read-only rows + an "Open save folder" button. Fields: Name / Size / Pieces / Piece size / Created / Created by / Comment / Info hash (monospace font, selectable) / Save path / Current tracker / Availability / Share ratio / Next reannounce. Static fields (name/size/pieces/piece-size/created/creator/comment/hash/save-path) populate once per `setInfoHash` call; `m_staticPopulated` flag guards against re-writing them every refresh tick. Dynamic fields (current tracker, availability, share ratio, next reannounce) refresh every 1 Hz. Info-hash `QLabel` has `Qt::TextSelectableByMouse` for copy-paste.

### TorrentPropertiesWidget wiring

**[TorrentPropertiesWidget.h](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.h)** — added forward decls + member pointers for all three new tabs.

**[TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp](src/ui/pages/tankorent/TorrentPropertiesWidget.cpp)**:
- All 4 placeholder tabs replaced with real concrete tab instances.
- `showTorrent(infoHash, tabIndex)` now calls `setInfoHash()` on all four (General / Trackers / Peers / Files) before activating the requested tab.
- `refresh()` slot (driven by the 1 Hz timer) forwards to `refresh()` on each tab — each decides what to re-fetch vs leave alone.

The `buildPlaceholderTab` helper is no longer used but kept in the file for quick restoration if a tab ever needs staging again.

### libtorrent API caveat for Agent 6

Several libtorrent 2.x API touchpoints in the new engine methods weren't previously exercised by Tankorent:
- `announce_entry::endpoints[*].info_hashes[*]` state access (introduced in 2.x; 1.x had direct fields on `announce_entry`).
- `peer_info::peer_flags_t` bitmask names (`remote_interested` / `rc4_encrypted` / `utp_socket`).
- `session::get_ip_filter()` / `set_ip_filter(ip_filter)`.
- `lt::make_address(std::string, error_code&)` for IP parsing.
- `torrent_handle::connect_peer(tcp::endpoint)`.

I wrote the code against libtorrent 2.x's published API shape. If Hemanth's local libtorrent headers at `C:/tools/libtorrent-2.0-msvc/include` expose slightly different names (e.g. deprecated-in-favor-of renames), there may be one-line fixes on build. Worth a build pass before declaring Phase 6 green.

## Agent 4 (Stream & Sources) -- 2026-04-15 Streaming regression fix — orphan seek-retry bug

User reported "video streaming has gone awry again" with blank player. Diagnosed from `_player_debug.txt` trace — **evidence-first, not analysis-first this time**.

### Evidence from the log (21:24 session)

```
21:24:25.879 [VideoPlayer] openFile: http://127.0.0.1:7069/stream/.../0
21:24:25.892 [VideoPlayer] starting sidecar...
21:24:25.898 [Sidecar] started OK, pid=19336
21:24:26.031 [Sidecar] SEND: set_audio_speed (from SyncClock ticking)
21:24:26.233 [VideoPlayer] openFile: http://127.0.0.1:7069/stream/.../0   ← SECOND openFile, 354ms later
21:24:26.234 [Sidecar] SEND: stop
21:24:26.235 [Sidecar] SEND: shutdown
21:24:26.236 [VideoPlayer] sidecar already running, sending open directly
21:24:26.237 [Sidecar] SEND: open (seq 160, session d66fe430)
21:24:26.516 [Sidecar] RECV: ready
21:24:26.517 [Sidecar] SEND: open (seq 161, session 83fe4bdc — new session!)
21:24:28.678 [Sidecar] RECV: closed
21:24:28.917 [Sidecar] process finished: exit=0 status=normal intentional=yes
```

Two `openFile` calls for the same URL, 354ms apart. The second fires `stopPlayback()` (which sends stop+shutdown) on a still-booting sidecar. Sidecar dies clean before `probe_complete` → blank player, no frame.

The 354ms interval ≈ my Batch 2.4 seek-pre-gate retry's 300ms QTimer interval.

### Root cause

[StreamPage.cpp onReadyToPlay](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) Batch 2.4 seek-pre-gate logic creates a `QObject* retryState = new QObject(this)` + a `QTimer::singleShot(300, ...)` when `prepareSeekTarget` returns false. The retry state is parented to `StreamPage` so it survives across playback sessions. If the user cancels / closes before the 300ms timer fires, the retry state + pending singleShot lambda stay alive on the heap.

When the user re-opens the same stream:
1. New `onReadyToPlay` → `prepareSeekTarget` returns true this time (head + target windows already downloaded) → synchronous `launchPlayer()` call #1.
2. 300ms later, orphan retry from the PRIOR session fires. Its guard `m_playerController->currentInfoHash() != infoHash` **passes** (same stream, same infoHash) → `launchPlayer()` call #2.
3. `VideoPlayer::openFile()` at [line 283](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L283) unconditionally calls `stopPlayback()` which sends stop+shutdown on the sidecar that just booted for call #1.
4. Sidecar exits cleanly before processing the open command → blank player.

### Fix

**1. `m_seekRetryState` QObject* member on StreamPage ([StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h)).** Single-instance pointer tracks the one currently-live retry state.

**2. Generation check inside the retry lambda** ([StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)): `if (retryState != m_seekRetryState) return;` — orphan retry objects bail silently when a newer onReadyToPlay has replaced the pointer.

**3. Top-of-`onReadyToPlay` cancel** ([StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp)): before any launch decision, `deleteLater()` any prior `m_seekRetryState` and null it. Covers every exit path (synchronous launch on zero-resume, no-infoHash, or immediate prepareSeekTarget-true).

**4. Teardown paths cancel too** — `showBrowse` and `onStreamFailed` both `deleteLater()` + null the state. Navigation-away no longer leaves retries pending. (`onStreamStopped` is reached via the player's existing cleanup which routes through `showBrowse` in most paths; `onStreamFailed` is the independent path.)

### Why the `infoHash` guard alone wasn't enough

The original guard: `m_playerController->currentInfoHash() != infoHash`. This catches "user switched to a different stream" but **passes through** "user re-opened the same stream." Same-stream reopen is the exact case that triggered this bug.

### Files

- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h) — `m_seekRetryState` member added.
- [src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp) — top-of-onReadyToPlay cancel + in-retry generation check + cancel on `showBrowse` + `onStreamFailed`.

No sidecar change, no other files.

### Re-verification

1. Close the player on a partially-watched stream (e.g. close at 15s into One Piece E1). Immediately reopen the same source. Expected: sidecar boots once, probe completes, playback starts at the saved offset. Log shows a single `openFile` call per session; no orphan 300ms-later openFile.
2. Open a stream where `prepareSeekTarget` returns false (fresh torrent, resume offset mid-file). Let the retry loop run. Close the player before the retry succeeds. Expected: retry aborts silently; log shows `deleteLater` on `m_seekRetryState`.
3. Regression: zero-resume cold start → no pre-gate retry path runs; launchPlayer fires synchronously once.
4. Regression: cross-stream reopen (One Piece → close → Jujutsu Kaisen). Orphan retry's infoHash guard still fires; new session creates its own fresh retryState.
5. Log check: `grep "openFile:" _player_debug.txt | uniq -c` — should show one `openFile` per session attempt, never two.

### Self-critique

This bug was shipped in Batch 2.4 of STREAM_PLAYBACK_FIX. The orphan-retry failure mode wasn't covered by the re-verification plan I wrote for that batch. The specific failure pattern (close-then-reopen same stream within 300ms-9s window) wasn't in my test matrix. Filing as a reminder: **any retry state / pending timer / async closure that persists across user sessions needs an explicit cancel hook at every session-teardown path, not just the happy-path completion**.

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4, Streaming orphan-seek-retry fix]: Batch 2.4's retry state could outlive its session and fire launchPlayer against a newer session, causing double openFile → stop+shutdown race on booting sidecar → blank player. Fix: promote retryState to m_seekRetryState member, add generation check in retry lambda, cancel at top of onReadyToPlay + on showBrowse + onStreamFailed. | files: src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h, src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 7 Batch 7.1 SHIPPED (isolate-commit)

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 7 Batch 7.1 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:463-474](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L463-L474). Closes the first of two inherited-debt items from Agent 4's Congress 4 observer position: `torrentCompleted → CoreBridge::rootFoldersChanged` rescan signal wiring.

### Rule 7 heads-up — CoreBridge edit

Touching [src/core/CoreBridge.h](src/core/CoreBridge.h) + [.cpp](src/core/CoreBridge.cpp) with a purely **additive** public method (new `notifyRootFoldersChanged(domain)`). No existing CoreBridge API changed, no signal signature changed, no private structure changed. Agent 0 territory per GOVERNANCE.md Level 2 architecture ownership — flagging per Batch 7.1's explicit "heads-up" note. If Agent 0 wants this method renamed or relocated, trivial to adjust; functionally a one-line emitter.

### Pre-audit findings

The audit the TODO asked for found that **both signals already existed**, which made this batch pure wiring:
- [CoreBridge::rootFoldersChanged(domain)](src/core/CoreBridge.h#L38) — already emitted at [CoreBridge.cpp:99](src/core/CoreBridge.cpp#L99) (addRootFolder) and [:120](src/core/CoreBridge.cpp#L120) (removeRootFolder).
- [TorrentClient::torrentCompleted(infoHash)](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.h#L90) — emitted at [TorrentClient.cpp:397](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp#L397) inside `onTorrentFinished`.

Neither existed with the exact signature TODO suggested (`torrentCompleted(infoHash, savePath)`), so I kept the existing `infoHash`-only signature and derived savePath from `m_records` instead. Smaller blast radius.

### Changes

**[CoreBridge.h](src/core/CoreBridge.h)** — one new public method declaration with a 3-line explanatory comment (only explanation the method needed: "emit signal without mutating list" — the existing addRootFolder/removeRootFolder already covers the mutation case, so the new method's existence vs not-existing is non-obvious).

```cpp
void notifyRootFoldersChanged(const QString& domain);
```

**[CoreBridge.cpp](src/core/CoreBridge.cpp)** — single-line emitter body placed between `removeRootFolder` and the Shell prefs block:

```cpp
void CoreBridge::notifyRootFoldersChanged(const QString& domain) { emit rootFoldersChanged(domain); }
```

(Actual impl is the standard 3-line form with the separate `emit` line for readability parity with neighbors.)

**[TorrentClient.cpp](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp)** — two changes:

1. Added `#include <QDir>`.
2. `onTorrentFinished` now captures `category` + `savePath` from the record before emitting `torrentCompleted`, then iterates `m_bridge->rootFolders(category)` and checks if the completed save path prefix-matches any tracked root via `QDir::absolutePath()` normalization + `Qt::CaseInsensitive` match (Windows FS behavior). On first match: `m_bridge->notifyRootFoldersChanged(category)`. No-match → no emission.

Category values are "comics" / "books" / "audiobooks" / "videos" per [AddTorrentConfig::category](src/ui/dialogs/AddTorrentDialog.h#L17) and [ROOTS_FILES](src/core/CoreBridge.cpp#L10-L15) — direct 1:1 mapping to CoreBridge's domain string.

### Agent 5 integration — zero code change needed on their side

Per `feedback_agent5_scope` + Congress 4 architecture: Agent 5's scanners (LibraryScanner / BooksScanner / VideosScanner) already listen on `rootFoldersChanged` to re-run on list mutation. Since this signal is being re-used as a generic "content in a tracked root changed, please re-scan" notification, no Agent 5 code change is required. The existing scanner pipeline handles it.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, TANKORENT_FIX Batch 7.1]: torrentCompleted → rootFoldersChanged wiring. Added CoreBridge::notifyRootFoldersChanged(domain) public emitter (additive — no API break). TorrentClient::onTorrentFinished captures category+savePath from m_records, prefix-matches savePath against m_bridge->rootFolders(category) via QDir::absolutePath + case-insensitive startsWith, calls notifyRootFoldersChanged on match. Agent 5 scanners re-trigger without Agent 5 code change. | files: src/core/CoreBridge.h, src/core/CoreBridge.cpp, src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp

### Smoke notes (Batch 7.1 standalone)

1. Set up a videos root folder (e.g. `C:\Users\Suprabha\Videos\`). Start a torrent downloading into that folder with category=Videos.
2. When the torrent completes → `onTorrentFinished` fires → `notifyRootFoldersChanged("videos")` emits → VideosPage re-scans → new file appears in the grid within ~5s.
3. Regression: start a torrent downloading into an UNtracked folder (e.g. `C:\Temp\`) → completion → `notifyRootFoldersChanged` NOT called (prefix match fails) → no spurious rescan, library pages unchanged.
4. Regression: add a root folder via the UI (existing addRootFolder path) → still emits rootFoldersChanged exactly once per the unchanged path.

### Next

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Phase 7 Batch 7.2 SHIPPED — TANKORENT_FIX TODO COMPLETE

TANKORENT_FIX Phase 7 Batch 7.2 per [TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md:476-492](TANKORENT_FIX_TODO.md#L476-L492). Closes the second inherited-debt item. **Phase 7 complete. All 7 phases of TANKORENT_FIX_TODO shipped.**

### Change — single file pair, pure additive API

**[TorrentClient.h](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.h)** — new public query:

```cpp
// Aggregate progress [0..1] across all active torrents whose save path
// is under `folderPath`. Weighted by torrent size so a big mostly-done
// download and a tiny just-started one aggregate sensibly. Returns 0.0
// when no matching active torrents. Case-insensitive prefix match on
// Windows-style paths.
float downloadProgress(const QString& folderPath) const;
```

**[TorrentClient.cpp](src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp)** — impl placed just above the existing `// ── Query ──` banner:

```cpp
float TorrentClient::downloadProgress(const QString& folderPath) const
{
    if (folderPath.isEmpty()) return 0.0f;
    const QString normFolder = QDir(folderPath).absolutePath();

    qint64 totalSize = 0;
    double totalDone = 0.0;
    for (const auto& info : listActive()) {
        if (info.savePath.isEmpty() || info.totalWanted <= 0) continue;
        const QString normSave = QDir(info.savePath).absolutePath();
        if (!normSave.startsWith(normFolder, Qt::CaseInsensitive)) continue;
        totalSize += info.totalWanted;
        totalDone += static_cast<double>(info.totalWanted) * info.progress;
    }
    if (totalSize <= 0) return 0.0f;
    return static_cast<float>(totalDone / static_cast<double>(totalSize));
}
```

Same path-normalization discipline as Batch 7.1: `QDir::absolutePath` + `Qt::CaseInsensitive` `startsWith` for Windows FS parity. Uses the existing `listActive()` path which already merges `m_records` + engine live status — no separate live/persisted state reconciliation needed.

Size-weighted aggregation means the returned float behaves intuitively when multiple torrents save into the same folder — a 10 GB torrent at 50% and a 100 MB torrent at 10% aggregate to ~49.6%, reflecting what's actually on disk rather than a naive average (30%).

### Agent 5 consumer — unwired by design

Per TODO: "Agent 5 wires on their own timeline via HELP request." This batch just makes the API available. When Agent 5 builds the list-view "Download" column (Congress 4 Track A Batch 11/12 deferral per chat.md history), they call `m_client->downloadProgress(folder)` and render.

### Rule 11

Iterates listActive(), prefix-matches savePath against folderPath (QDir::absolutePath + Qt::CaseInsensitive startsWith), aggregates size-weighted progress. Returns 0.0 when no matches, 1.0 when all matches complete, [0, 1] otherwise. Pure API addition — no cross-subsystem edits, no signal wiring. Agent 5 consumes on their own timeline. | files: src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.h, src/core/torrent/TorrentClient.cpp

## Agent 4B (Sources — Tankorent + Tankoyomi) -- 2026-04-16 — Transfers Progress column cleanup

Per Hemanth: remove the progress bar in the Transfers table's Progress column, keep the percentage text only.

### Change

Removed the custom `ProgressBarDelegate` that painted a rounded track + fill behind the percentage in column 2 of the transfers table. Column now uses Qt's default item delegate — existing `progItem->setText("NN.N%")` + `setTextAlignment(Qt::AlignCenter)` already written in `refreshTransfers` render as plain centered text. Sorting continues to work via the `Qt::UserRole` float that `refreshTransfers` still stores.

### Deletions (dead code cleanup)

With the delegate assignment gone, `ProgressBarDelegate` was unused:

- [TankorentPage.h](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h) — removed class declaration + unused `<QStyledItemDelegate>` / `<QPainter>` includes.
- [TankorentPage.cpp](src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp) — removed `paint()` + `sizeHint()` impls (~45 LOC). Removed the `setItemDelegateForColumn(2, new ProgressBarDelegate(table))` call in `createTransfersTable`.

Net change: ~60 LOC deleted, 0 added. Column behavior becomes "render the item's text" — which is "100.0%" / "42.3%" / etc. as before, minus the bar.

### Rule 11

READY TO COMMIT — [Agent 4B, Transfers Progress bar removal]: Removed custom ProgressBarDelegate + its assignment on column 2 of the transfers table. Percentage text already set via progItem->setText in refreshTransfers — default delegate renders it as centered text. Sorting still works via Qt::UserRole float. Removed unused QStyledItemDelegate/QPainter includes. | files: src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.h, src/ui/pages/TankorentPage.cpp

Standing by.
