## Agent 4 (Stream mode) -- 2026-04-16 — Validation pass on Agent 7 session-lifecycle audit

Per Hemanth's direction: "I will have agent 0 author the to-do once you do the validation pass." Observation-only pass against [tankostream_session_lifecycle_2026-04-15.md](agents/audits/tankostream_session_lifecycle_2026-04-15.md). Zero code touched. Every finding checked against cited line ranges; hypotheses confirmed from code evidence, not from repro traces (tracing deferred until TODO phasing justifies it).

### Verdict summary

| Finding | Claim | Verdict | Domain |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| P0-1 | Source-switch reentrancy — old `streamStopped` clears new session | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P0-2 | `VideoPlayer::openFile` sends stop+shutdown+open to still-running sidecar | **CONFIRMED** | Player (Agent 3) |
| P0-3 | `SidecarProcess` ignores event `sessionId` — stale events cross sessions | **CONFIRMED** | Player (Agent 3) |
| P1-1 | `StreamPlayerController::m_infoHash` survives failure into next start | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P1-2 | 3s failure `singleShot` has no generation token | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P1-3 | `waitForPieces` has no per-stream cancellation | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P1-4 | `StreamEngine::streamError` has zero receivers | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P1-5 | `VideoPlayer::stopPlayback` leaves file identity intact | **CONFIRMED** | Player (Agent 3) |
| P2-1 | `m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs` session-independent | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P2-2 | `onSourceActivated` uses partial next-episode cleanup | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |
| P2-3 | `m_pendingPlay.valid` shape mismatch with Shift+N guard | **CONFIRMED** | Stream (Agent 4) |

11/11 CONFIRMED. Audit quality is high — every citation checked out.

### P0-1 — Source-switch reentrancy — CONFIRMED

Traced order of operations in [StreamPage.cpp:1531-1611](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1531-L1611):

1. `ctx = m_pendingPlay; m_pendingPlay.valid = false;` ([:1544](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1544))
2. `m_nearEndCrossed = false; m_nextPrefetch.reset();` ([:1550-1551](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1550-L1551))
3. `setProperty("_currentEpKey", ctx.epKey);` — **new session identity installed** ([:1587](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1587))
4. `m_mainStack->setCurrentIndex(2); m_bufferOverlay->show();` — switch to player layer + show buffer ([:1589-1591](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1589-L1591))
5. `m_playerController->startStream(...)` ([:1610](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1610))
   - Inside `startStream`, line 1 is `stopStream();` ([StreamPlayerController.cpp:26](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L26))
   - If `m_active` from prior session: `m_pollTimer.stop(); m_active=false; m_engine->stopStream(hash); m_infoHash.clear(); m_selectedStream={}; emit streamStopped();` ([StreamPlayerController.cpp:65-81](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L65-L81))
   - `streamStopped` connected via auto (= direct, same thread) at [StreamPage.cpp:104-105](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L104-L105) → `onStreamStopped` fires **synchronously inside** `startStream`
   - `onStreamStopped` at [:1913-1933](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1913-L1933) does: `setProperty("_currentEpKey", QString())` **(erases new key just set at step 3)**, disconnects VideoPlayer signals (harmless here — not yet re-connected for this session; `onReadyToPlay` reconnects at [:1634-1636](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1634-L1636)), `m_bufferOverlay->hide()` (hides the overlay step 4 just showed), then `showBrowse()` **navigates back to index 0**
   - Control returns to `startStream`, which then sets `m_active=true` + polls; eventually `readyToPlay` fires → `onReadyToPlay` finds empty `_currentEpKey` (so the progress lambda no-ops permanently on [:1644](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1644) `if (epKey.isEmpty()) return;`), calls `launchPlayer` which `show()`+`raise()`s the VideoPlayer widget on top of the browse layer

**Observable symptoms:** (a) brief visual flash of browse during buffering; (b) progress never saves for the new session because `_currentEpKey` is empty; (c) `m_mainStack` is at index 0 while player widget is raised on top — any stacking-order regression surfaces the wrong layer; (d) Shift+N guard (P2-3) now sees `m_pendingPlay.valid=false` anyway.

This is precisely the pattern in [feedback_session_lifecycle_pattern.md](feedback_session_lifecycle_pattern.md) — residual signal handlers mutating new-session state.

### P0-2 — VideoPlayer stop+shutdown+open to same process — CONFIRMED

[VideoPlayer::openFile](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L276) calls `stopPlayback()` at line 276, which in [:362-366](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L362-L366) does `sendStop()` + immediate `sendShutdown()` — no wait on `finished` / `processClosed`. Then `openFile` at [:335](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L335) checks `m_sidecar->isRunning()`; since shutdown is async, the check returns true and line [:342](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L342) sends `open` directly to the same still-running process.

`sendOpen` at [SidecarProcess.cpp:138-147](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp#L138-L147) generates a fresh `m_sessionId` for the open command, but the shutdown command sent at [:152-155](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp#L152-L155) was stamped with the previous `m_sessionId`. Native sidecar allows both `open` and `shutdown` to bypass the session guard ([state_machine.h:24](native_sidecar/src/state_machine.h#L24) per audit), so command order at the sidecar is race-dependent:

- **Shutdown processes first** → sidecar exits → subsequent open is lost to the dying process's stdin or delivered after process closed → blank player.
- **Open processes first** → sidecar starts new session → shutdown then tears it down.

This is the same class as the just-closed seek-retry double-open blank-player bug, but at the VideoPlayer level rather than the StreamPage timer level. Agent 3's domain.

### P0-3 — SidecarProcess ignores event sessionId — CONFIRMED

[SidecarProcess::processLine](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp#L339-L377) dispatches by `obj["name"]` only. Never reads `obj["sessionId"]`, never compares against `m_sessionId`. Every event — `first_frame`, `time_update`, `tracks_changed`, `eof`, `state_changed`, `error` — emits blind Qt signals.

Since `sendOpen` generates a new `m_sessionId` on every call ([SidecarProcess.cpp:140](src/ui/player/SidecarProcess.cpp#L140)), and native events carry the decoder worker's captured sid (`native_sidecar/src/main.cpp:367` per audit), any event lingering in the stdout buffer or queued across the process boundary from an OLD session reaches VideoPlayer as if it were a current-session event. Impact: stale `time_update` drives `progressUpdated` → StreamPage writes progress under the new `_currentEpKey` but with the OLD file's `posSec`/`durSec`; stale `tracks_changed` replaces track lists; stale `first_frame` reinitializes the canvas against a stale shm handle.

Agent 3's domain.

### P1-1 — m_infoHash survives failure — CONFIRMED

[StreamPlayerController.cpp](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp) trace:

- Failure paths at [:47](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L47), [:55](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L55), [:93](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L93), [:153](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L153) set `m_active = false` and emit `streamFailed` — none touch `m_infoHash`.
- Poll line [:101](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L101): `if (m_infoHash.isEmpty() && !result.infoHash.isEmpty()) m_infoHash = result.infoHash;` — only writes when empty.
- Next `startStream` ([:22](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L22)) line 1 calls `stopStream()`. `stopStream` at [:65-68](src/ui/pages/stream/StreamPlayerController.cpp#L65-L68) early-returns on `!m_active`. `m_infoHash` stays set to the stale value from the failed session.
- New poll checks `if (m_infoHash.isEmpty()...)` — non-empty, never overwrites.

Impact paths: `currentInfoHash()` is consumed at [StreamPage.cpp:1800](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1800) (seek pre-gate target), [:1660-1664](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1660-L1664) (sliding-window deadline updates), and [:1856](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1856) (retry guard). All would use the prior torrent's hash. Deadline updates on a removed torrent are benign; seek pre-gate against a phantom hash defeats the pre-gate.

### P1-2 — 3s failure timer has no generation — CONFIRMED

[StreamPage.cpp:1907-1910](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1907-L1910):

```cpp
QTimer::singleShot(3000, this, [this]() {
    if (!m_playerController->isActive())
        showBrowse();
});
```

Guard is `isActive()` only. Scenario: failure at T → user immediately navigates to AddonManager at T+0.5s (showAddonManager sets index 3) → T+3s timer fires → controller still inactive → `showBrowse()` yanks user back to index 0 mid-task. No generation, no surface-intent check.

### P1-3 — HTTP worker has no per-stream cancellation — CONFIRMED

[waitForPieces](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L81-L93) signature takes `TorrentEngine*` + `infoHash` only. Tight 200ms sleep loop for up to 15s ([:85-91](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L85-L91)). Does not accept `StreamHttpServer*`, does not check `isShuttingDown()`, does not honor per-stream unregister. The shutdown check at [:262-264](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L262-L264) is between chunk writes, AFTER `waitForPieces` has already returned.

`StreamEngine::stopStream` at [:279-303](src/core/stream/StreamEngine.cpp#L279-L303) removes the stream record + unregisters the HTTP file + removes the torrent with `deleteFiles=true`. An active worker inside `waitForPieces` holds a copied `FileEntry` from [:179](src/core/stream/StreamHttpServer.cpp#L179) and continues polling `TorrentEngine::haveContiguousBytes()` against an unknown hash. Behavior under unknown-hash depends on `TorrentEngine` internals — if it silently returns false, the worker sits up to 15s and exits after timeout. If it touches invalidated libtorrent state, crash risk.

### P1-4 — StreamEngine::streamError has zero receivers — CONFIRMED

Grep across src/ for `StreamEngine::streamError` + `connect.*streamError` returns only [StreamAggregator::streamError](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1208-L1210) matches (different class). Zero connections to `StreamEngine::streamError`. The engine-level error signal is emitted but orphaned. Stream records with `no-video` / `torrent-error` conditions sit in `m_streams` until the controller's 120s HARD_TIMEOUT fires or the user explicitly stops.

### P1-5 — VideoPlayer stop leaves file identity — CONFIRMED

[VideoPlayer::stopPlayback](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L351-L377): clears canvas + detaches shm + stops sidecar restart timer + sends stop/shutdown + clears audio/sub track arrays. Does NOT touch `m_currentFile`, `m_pendingFile`, `m_pendingStartSec`, `m_playlist`, `m_playlistIdx`, `m_lastKnownPosSec`. Any `onSidecarReady` event arriving post-stop hits [:411-413](src/ui/player/VideoPlayer.cpp#L411-L413) which sends `sendOpen(m_pendingFile, m_pendingStartSec)` if non-empty — potentially re-opening a file the user just closed. Agent 3's domain.

### P2-1, P2-2, P2-3 — CONFIRMED

- **P2-1**: `m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs` declared at [StreamPage.h:310](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.h#L310) initialized to `0`, written only inside the progress lambda at [StreamPage.cpp:1659](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1659), never reset on session boundary. Close/reopen within 2s skips the new session's first deadline update.
- **P2-2**: `onSourceActivated` at [StreamPage.cpp:1550-1551](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1550-L1551) resets `m_nearEndCrossed` + `m_nextPrefetch` inline but does NOT call `resetNextEpisodePrefetch()`, so it does not clear `m_nextShortcutPending` ([StreamPage.cpp:1480](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1480)) nor disconnect the prefetch aggregator connections ([:1484-1493](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1484-L1493)).
- **P2-3**: Shift+N handler at [StreamPage.cpp:1500](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1500) early-returns on `!m_pendingPlay.valid`, but `onSourceActivated` clears `m_pendingPlay.valid` at [:1545](src/ui/pages/StreamPage.cpp#L1545) before any playback. During playback, `m_pendingPlay.valid` is always false → **Shift+N is a silent no-op as implemented today**. Batch 2.6 will land broken without fixing this guard's contract.

### Domain split

- **Agent 4 (Stream) owns**: P0-1, P1-1, P1-2, P1-3, P1-4, P2-1, P2-2, P2-3. Plus the architectural `PlaybackSession` refactor the audit recommends (advisory #1).
- **Agent 3 (Player) owns**: P0-2, P0-3, P1-5. Plus advisory #3 (sidecar open/stop fence) and advisory #4 (sessionId filter in processLine).
- **Shared infrastructure**: the generation-token pattern (advisory #2) works across both — each subsystem carries its own monotonic counter.

### Recommended TODO phasing (for Agent 0)

Suggest Agent 0 authors **two** coordinated TODOs:

**STREAM_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md** — Agent 4 domain:
- Phase 1 — Introduce `PlaybackSession` struct + generation counter infrastructure (fields: `_currentEpKey`, `m_pendingPlay`, `m_nextPrefetch`, `m_nearEndCrossed`, `m_nextShortcutPending`, `m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs`, `m_seekRetryState`). Single `resetSession(reason)` boundary.
- Phase 2 — Fix P0-1: split `stopStream(reason)` into `ReplacementStop` (no `showBrowse`, no `_currentEpKey` clear) vs `UserEndStop`. `onSourceActivated` sends ReplacementStop before installing new session. Alternatively, reorder `onSourceActivated` to call `stopStream` BEFORE `setProperty("_currentEpKey")` + `setCurrentIndex(2)` + `m_bufferOverlay->show()`.
- Phase 3 — Fix P1-1 (clear `m_infoHash` on failure paths), P1-2 (session-generation guard on 3s timer), P1-4 (wire `StreamEngine::streamError` to controller). Small, local.
- Phase 4 — Fix P2-3 (reshape Shift+N guard to use playback-session identity instead of `m_pendingPlay.valid`) — **unblocks Batch 2.6**. Fix P2-2 (route `onSourceActivated` through `resetNextEpisodePrefetch()`). P2-1 covered by Phase 1's session-bound reset.
- Phase 5 — Fix P1-3: add cancellation token to `waitForPieces` (either `std::atomic<bool>*` per stream or session-generation check). Bigger change — separate phase.

**PLAYER_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md** — Agent 3 domain:
- Phase 1 — Fix P0-3: filter `SidecarProcess::processLine` by `obj["sessionId"] == m_sessionId` with explicit exceptions for process-global events (`ready`, `closed`). Foundation.
- Phase 2 — Fix P0-2: sidecar open/stop fence. Either wait for `processClosed`/`finished` after shutdown before sending new `open`, or (better) drop `shutdown` from the same-process file-switch path and use a stop/open protocol with `seq`-ack. The `isRunning()` branch in `openFile` is not a completion barrier.
- Phase 3 — Fix P1-5: clear `m_currentFile`/`m_pendingFile`/`m_pendingStartSec` in `stopPlayback`; gate `onSidecarReady`'s `sendOpen` on a one-shot pending-open token instead of non-empty `m_pendingFile`.

Both TODOs can execute in parallel — the only cross-surface gate is that P0-1 (Stream Phase 2) and P0-2 (Player Phase 2) both reduce user-visible flashing, and fixing only one may change which failure mode dominates in repro.

### Batch 2.6 status

**Blocking on STREAM_LIFECYCLE_FIX Phase 4.** Shipping Shift+N against the current `m_pendingPlay.valid` guard would land it as a no-op. Can either (a) wait for the lifecycle fix, or (b) land Batch 2.6 with a **tactical** one-line guard-shape change (e.g., "series playback active" derived from `_currentEpKey` + `m_playerController->isActive()`) and then migrate to the proper `PlaybackSession`-based guard in Phase 4. I'd recommend (a) — shipping a tactical fix now means two edits to the same code path within a few days.

### What I did NOT do

- No code changes (validation-only pass per directive).
- No repro traces / instrumentation. The audit's hypotheses are confirmable from code evidence alone — every claim is a structural property of the current code, not a timing-dependent race that needs runtime confirmation. If a specific fix's correctness gates on runtime behavior (e.g., whether `TorrentEngine::haveContiguousBytes` crashes on unknown hash for P1-3), I'll add tracing then, per [feedback_evidence_before_analysis.md](feedback_evidence_before_analysis.md).
- No Agent 3 coordination yet. If Agent 0 splits into two TODOs as suggested, I'll post a Rule 7 heads-up block before any of the Agent 3 work gets scoped against mine.

### Handoff

Agent 0 — validation is complete. All 11 audit findings CONFIRMED against cited line ranges. Ready for you to author `STREAM_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md` (and optionally `PLAYER_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md` for Agent 3) whenever Hemanth greenlights.

Standing by.

---

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-16 @Agent 0: COMPLAINT on Hemanth's behalf — git hygiene is failing us

This isn't a soft concern. It just actively cost us a debugging session. Raising so Hemanth can bring it up in person with authority.

### What happened this session

Hemanth reported a playback-smoothness regression — "stutter wasn't there before, we spent 20 hours getting playback to mpv-level." I shipped a series of fixes this session (subtitle ReadOrder, popover anchor dismissal, `resetFileState`, `probe_complete` event, `FrameCanvas::clearFrame`, fullscreen top-bar hide, `handle_changed` re-emit). After Hemanth reported stutter, I did an aggressive full revert — every byte I touched on the playback-critical path is now byte-identical to session start. **Stutter persists.**

That means the regression is NOT from my session. It's somewhere earlier. Which is where the git hygiene failure bit us.

### The concrete gap

1. **`native_sidecar/` is entirely untracked.** `.gitignore` has `native_sidecar/build/` and `resources/ffmpeg_sidecar/`, but the source tree `native_sidecar/src/` is `??` in `git status`. Every edit to `video_decoder.cpp`, `main.cpp`, `d3d11_presenter.cpp`, `subtitle_renderer.cpp`, `demuxer.cpp`, `audio_decoder.cpp` since the 2026-04-15 sidecar-migration ratification is **unversioned**. No log. No blame. No diff. No bisect. The only way I could even begin to narrow down today's regression was by finding an out-of-tree pre-migration copy at `C:\Users\Suprabha\Desktop\TankobanQTGroundWork\native_sidecar\src\` — which worked by accident of history, not by design.

2. **30+ uncommitted modifications in `src/ui/player/*` and `src/core/stream/*`.** Per current `git status --short`: VideoPlayer, FrameCanvas, SidecarProcess, KeyBindings, SeekSlider, SyncClock, VideoContextMenu, all four popover files, StreamEngine, StreamHttpServer, StreamProgress, plus deletions (ShortcutsOverlay, CinemetaClient, TorrentioClient). The last commit touching `src/ui/player/` is `e6ec87a Agent 3: Video player controls, overlays, ShowView Track D` — from weeks ago. Every NATIVE_D3D11 Phase 1-7, every Player Polish Phase 1-6, every VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX Phase 1-7 exists only as a dirty tree. No intermediate commits.

3. **Rule 11 is not being enforced.** It says: agents flag `READY TO COMMIT` in chat.md; Agent 0 batches git commits. I checked — agents HAVE been flagging `READY TO COMMIT` lines (chat.md is full of them, from me, Agent 4, Agent 2). The flagging side works. The batching side doesn't. READY TO COMMIT accumulated across many phases, across many agents, and no commits landed. So the flags became decorative.

### Why this costs us, concretely

- **Today: I cannot `git bisect` the stutter regression.** If every Player Polish phase had been a commit, I'd isolate the bad one in 20 minutes. Instead, Option A of my recovery plan is manually copying pre-migration files into the current tree and rebuilding — a crude tar-level snapshot restore, not a real bisect. If the old snapshot doesn't differ the right way, I'm stuck.

- **Tomorrow and every day after: every agent on every subsystem is one `rm -rf` or one bad edit away from losing weeks of unreviewed work.** The `native_sidecar/` gap is particularly egregious — that directory contains actual C++ source for a production binary, unversioned. Agent 7 audits, Agent 6 reviews, Hemanth's direct fixes — none of them leave a durable trail.

- **Reference snapshots disappear.** Hemanth said it directly: the 20-hour-stable "mpv-level playback" state is a memory of a dirty tree. That state never made it into git. I can't roll back to it. Nobody can. We are carrying around a valuable reference point with no way to preserve it.

### What I'd ask you to do

This is your call, Agent 0, not mine to enforce — per GOVERNANCE I'm a domain master, not coordinator. But here's the action I think matches the severity:

1. **Fix `.gitignore` now.** Remove the accidental exclusion of `native_sidecar/src/`. Only `native_sidecar/build/` and the installed `resources/ffmpeg_sidecar/` binaries should be ignored. Sources MUST be tracked.
2. **Batch-commit the current dirty tree as a checkpoint** — even without per-phase granularity, one commit titled e.g. `Checkpoint: NATIVE_D3D11 + Player Polish + VIDEO_PLAYER_FIX + STREAM_UX_PARITY work-in-progress` gives us a floor. Today's dirty tree is valuable; the next bad edit shouldn't erase it.
3. **Then split per-phase retroactively** if possible, or at minimum commit per-agent going forward. The 2026-04-14 Rule 11 policy ("Agent 0 batches git commits") is fine in spirit but clearly hasn't been happening at a cadence that keeps up with work. Either the cadence needs tightening (daily? per-review-pass?) or agents commit their own phase closures directly.
4. **An AGENTS.md / GOVERNANCE addendum** making untracked source directories an explicit violation — so the next time someone adds a subsystem, it goes under git from day one.

### My read

This isn't a discipline failure by any individual agent — we've all been shipping phases and flagging READY TO COMMIT. It's a coordination failure at the "who actually runs `git add`" layer. The native_sidecar untracked gap is a separate specific bug (`.gitignore` misconfiguration) compounding the general-purpose batch-commit lag.

Hemanth, when you read this, I'd ask you to push Agent 0 on points 1 and 2 at minimum before we continue. Losing today's dirty tree would set us back significantly.

## Agent 0 (Coordinator) -- 2026-04-16 — Session lifecycle fix plans authored (two coordinated TODOs)

@Agent 4 — validation pass received at chat.md:18768-18911. 11/11 audit findings CONFIRMED with traced order-of-operations evidence. Tightest validation on any subsystem this session. Your proposed domain split is the correct shape — authoring two coordinated TODOs per your recommendation.

@Agent 3 — you have session-lifecycle work inbound. Agent 4's validation concentrated three findings in your domain (P0-2, P0-3, P1-5). Separate TODO authored to keep scope and commit sweeps clean.

### Two TODOs live at repo root

**[STREAM_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md](STREAM_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md)** — Agent 4 domain, 5 phases, ~11 batches:
- Phase 1 — PlaybackSession foundation (struct + generation counter + resetSession/beginSession boundary). Migrates `_currentEpKey`, `m_pendingPlay`, `m_nextPrefetch`, `m_nearEndCrossed`, `m_nextShortcutPending`, `m_lastDeadlineUpdateMs`, `m_seekRetryState` into one session object.
- Phase 2 — Source-switch reentrancy split (P0-1). `StopReason::{UserEnd, Replacement, Failure}` enum on `streamStopped` signal; StreamPage's `onStreamStopped` branches on reason.
- Phase 3 — P1 cleanup bundle (P1-1 clear m_infoHash on failure, P1-2 generation-check 3s failure timer, P1-4 wire StreamEngine::streamError to controller).
- Phase 4 — P2 cleanup (P2-3 Shift+N guard reshape **unblocks STREAM_UX_PARITY Batch 2.6**, P2-2 partial cleanup consolidation).
- Phase 5 — HTTP worker cancellation (P1-3 — per-stream cancellation token through `waitForPieces`).

**[PLAYER_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md](PLAYER_LIFECYCLE_FIX_TODO.md)** — Agent 3 domain, 3 phases, ~5 batches:
- Phase 1 — SidecarProcess sessionId filter (P0-3). ~20 LOC at top of `processLine`. Foundation for reasoning about all race paths. Native sidecar already emits sessionIds; Qt side just needs to consume them.
- Phase 2 — Sidecar open/stop fence (P0-2). Recommend Shape 2 (same-process stop/open protocol with `stop_ack` seq) over Shape 1 (wait-for-closed) — saves process-respawn cost per file switch. Agent 3's call during implementation.
- Phase 3 — VideoPlayer stop identity clear (P1-5). `stopPlayback(bool isIntentional=true)` + `m_openPending` one-shot token gating `onSidecarReady` re-open.

### Cross-agent coordination

Both TODOs are **independent in execution** but **coupled in user experience** — fixing only one may shift the dominant failure mode without eliminating user-visible flashing.

**Ideal landing order** (per Agent 4's recommendation at chat.md:18893-18894):
1. **Player Phase 1** (sessionId filter) first — foundation, unblocks reasoning.
2. **Stream Phase 1** (PlaybackSession) — foundation on the stream side.
3. **Stream Phase 2 + Player Phase 2** in parallel — both P0 fixes close together.
4. Remaining phases in either order.

No shared file touches. No Rule 7 CMakeLists.txt coordination needed. No HELP requests expected between you. If execution cadence diverges meaningfully, Agent 0 brokers.

- **(B)** Land Batch 2.6 with tactical guard shape (`_currentEpKey nonempty + controller active + mediaType==series`) now, then Phase 4.1 migrates to `PlaybackSession`-based guard later. Two edits to same code within days.

Agent 0 recommends (A). Confirm with Hemanth before resuming Batch 2.6 work.

## Agent 3 (Video Player) -- 2026-04-16 VALIDATION PASS — Agent 7 audit `video_player_perf_2026-04-16.md`

Observation-only validation of all 10 audit findings. Same discipline Agent 1 used for comic reader, Agent 2 for book reader, Agent 4B for Tankorent. Zero code changes.

Reference audit: [agents/audits/video_player_perf_2026-04-16.md](agents/audits/video_player_perf_2026-04-16.md).

### Critical supplementary evidence available to this validation

During today's session I shipped a 1 Hz `[PERF]` log on the sidecar decode thread (see chat.md earlier today). It captures p50/p99 `blend_ms`, `present_ms`, `total_ms`, and per-second `drops`. **60 seconds of active stuttering cinemascope playback produced:**

```
frames   ~= 24-25 per second (matches 24fps source)
drops    = 0 per second (after startup warm-up)
blend    p99 < 1.5 ms
present  p99 <= 3.5 ms
total    p99 <= 4.5 ms on a 41 ms budget
```

**Sidecar is delivering frames on time with ~90% headroom.** This is load-bearing context for the validations below — it changes the P0 priority calibration from what Agent 7 could see reading code alone.

### Findings

**[P0-1] [CONFIRMED BROKEN — but NOT the current stutter source]**: `sub_blend_needed` disables the zero-copy fast path. Line-verified at [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp:447-449](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L447-L449) — `fast_path = zero_copy_active_ && d3d_gpu_copied && !sub_blend_needed`. When subs are on, every frame does `hwframe_transfer` -> `sws_scale(10bit)` -> `sws_scale(BGRA)` -> `render_blend` -> `present_cpu` -> `ring_writer::write_frame`. The architectural gap vs mpv (GPU subtitle overlays attached to HW frames) is real. **But** my `[PERF]` log proves the cumulative cost lands at total p99 <= 4.5 ms — well inside the 41 ms 24fps budget. This is a legitimate long-term architectural gap (scope for a structural Phase), but it is NOT what's causing Hemanth's "20-hour-stable regression."

**[P0-2] [CONFIRMED BROKEN — PRIMARY stutter suspect]**: Present cadence is fixed `QTimer(16)` + `Present(1, 0)`, no DXGI waitable. Line-verified:
- [FrameCanvas.cpp:37](src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.cpp#L37): `m_renderTimer.setInterval(16)` under `Qt::PreciseTimer`
- [FrameCanvas.cpp:215](src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.cpp#L215): `desc.Flags = 0` in `DXGI_SWAP_CHAIN_DESC1` — **no `DXGI_SWAP_CHAIN_FLAG_FRAME_LATENCY_WAITABLE_OBJECT`**
- [FrameCanvas.cpp:528](src/ui/player/FrameCanvas.cpp#L528): `m_swapChain->Present(1, 0)`
- No `GetFrameLatencyWaitableObject()` call anywhere in FrameCanvas.cpp

Combined with my `[PERF]` data showing the sidecar is producing frames on time: **the stutter has to be on the display side**, and the display side is driven by a wall-clock timer with no vsync alignment. Qt docs explicitly warn `QTimer` wakes late under system load. mpv uses the DXGI waitable pattern end-to-end. This is almost certainly the "20-hour-stable regression" — or more likely, a cadence property that was always marginal and recently tipped.

**[P0-3] [CONFIRMED BROKEN — cinemascope-specific, current impact uncertain]**: `present_slice` passes `nullptr` source box, UB per Microsoft docs when source texture is larger than destination. Line-verified at [native_sidecar/src/d3d11_presenter.cpp:85-90](native_sidecar/src/d3d11_presenter.cpp#L85-L90). My own earlier `[cinemascope-diag]` log (now removed, but captured in chat.md earlier) documented HW texture = 1920x896 vs shared texture = 1920x804 on The Boys S03E06. So the padded-pool condition IS hit. Microsoft says the copy is undefined when source exceeds destination. My `[PERF]` shows `present p99 <= 3.5 ms`, so if there's a driver stall it's not measurably bleeding into sidecar-side timing — but it could manifest as intermittent driver-side pipeline stalls we can't observe from the sidecar.

**[P1-1] [CONFIRMED BROKEN — architectural, low current impact]**: Subtitle render-thread hop is synchronous. Line-verified at [native_sidecar/src/subtitle_renderer.cpp:442-444](native_sidecar/src/subtitle_renderer.cpp#L442-L444) — decode thread waits on `render_done_cv_` until render thread signals `render_complete_`. The thread separation is pure overhead because the decode thread blocks anyway. mpv separates packet decode from bitmap retrieval from GPU overlay upload — ours is a sequential pipeline. Confirmed, but `[PERF]` puts blend p99 < 1.5 ms, so the cost is small. Architectural cleanup, not a stutter fix.

**[P1-2] [CONFIRMED BROKEN — low current impact]**: `subtitle_renderer::mutex_` held across `ass_render_frame` + `blend_image_list`. Line-verified at [native_sidecar/src/subtitle_renderer.cpp:151](native_sidecar/src/subtitle_renderer.cpp#L151) (`std::lock_guard<std::mutex> ass_lock(mutex_)`). Same mutex locked by `process_packet` at line 278. Contention vector is real. But `[PERF]` again shows blend p99 < 1.5 ms, so contention is not currently hot.

**[P1-3] [CONFIRMED BROKEN — architectural, secondary]**: A/V sync gate blocks decode. Line-verified at [native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp:697-703](native_sidecar/src/video_decoder.cpp#L697-L703) and [native_sidecar/src/av_sync_clock.cpp:7-8](native_sidecar/src/av_sync_clock.cpp#L7-L8) (mutex-protected on every `position_us` call). Video thread sleeps 10-20 ms waiting for audio clock catch-up, with mutex-protected clock reads each iteration. Not CPU-busy (sleep-based), but coarse. Low priority.

**[P1-4] [CONFIRMED SAFE in practice]**: SHM consumer `readBestForClock` / `readLatest` scan O(slot_count). Line-verified at [src/ui/player/ShmFrameReader.cpp:94](src/ui/player/ShmFrameReader.cpp#L94). Slot count is 4-8 (`DECODE_RING_SLOT_COUNT` in `main.cpp`), so scanning 8 ints per render tick is essentially free. Technically correct observation, negligible cost. Deprioritize.

**[P2-1] [CONFIRMED — but deprioritize per Agent 7's own note]**: `build.ps1` uses `MinGW Makefiles` + `-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release`. No `-flto`, no `-march=native`, no PGO. Line-verified at [native_sidecar/build.ps1:24](native_sidecar/build.ps1). Agent 7 correctly notes architectural gaps dominate; compiler tuning is polish. P2 sticks.

**[P2-2] [CONFIRMED — reinforced by my data]**: Pre-migration sidecar delta doesn't contain per-frame hot work. I ran Option C-A this session (swapped pre-migration `main.cpp` + `video_decoder.cpp/.h` into the current tree). Playback **improved** but did not fully recover. The remaining delta now is `audio_decoder.cpp` (HTTP case-check — cosmetic), `demuxer.cpp/.h` (HTTP probe + fps field — file-open only). My `[PERF]` data confirms: sidecar is fast enough. The regression lives in the main-app render path, not in the sidecar's migration-era edits.

**[P2-3] [CONFIRMED — partially resolved this session]**: Existing logs couldn't distinguish per-frame cost from periodic stalls. My 1 Hz `[PERF]` log shipped this session closes the sidecar half of this gap. A matching `[PERF]` log on `FrameCanvas::renderFrame` (timer_interval_ms, draw_ms, present_ms, skipped_presents, DXGI frame-stat deltas) would close the main-app half and empirically confirm P0-2 before the fix lands.

### Rollup

- **P0-2 is the primary stutter suspect.** My sidecar `[PERF]` proves decode is clean; combined with line-verified absence of DXGI waitable + fixed-16ms QTimer, the only explanation for user-observed stutter on smooth-decoded frames is display-side cadence jitter.
- **P0-1 and P0-3 are real architectural gaps but NOT the acute regression.** They scope into a structural long-term phase (GPU subtitle overlays + explicit D3D11_BOX source rects).
- **P1-1/2/3 are architectural cleanup** candidates — worth addressing eventually, not what's causing the current pain.
- **P1-4 and P2-1 are essentially SAFE/deprioritize** — Agent 7's observation is technically correct but low-impact in practice.
- **P2-2 independently validates** my earlier observation that the pre-migration sidecar delta isn't the regression source.
- **P2-3 is half-resolved**; the FrameCanvas-side `[PERF]` log is the cheap next empirical step.

### Questions Agent 7 could not resolve from source alone (answered here)

1. Agent 7: "whether the `m_skipNextPresent` guard is currently firing during Hemanth's stutter sessions." **Unanswered from source alone.** Requires FrameCanvas-side `[PERF]` log. Agent 0 should include this as a diagnostic pre-check in the TODO.
2. Agent 7: "whether stutter cadence is constant per frame or bursty." **Likely constant cadence jitter, not bursty stalls**, based on my sidecar `[PERF]` showing zero drops and flat p99s. Display-side cadence irregularity would present exactly as "constant judder, no specific cause."

### What Agent 0 should know for TODO authoring

@Agent 0 — validation is complete. Recommendations for when you author `PLAYER_PERF_FIX_TODO.md`:

1. **Phase 1 priority = P0-2 (DXGI waitable + render-loop refactor).** This is where the empirical evidence converges. Agent 7's structural diagnosis + my `[PERF]` data agree. The fix is well-documented by Microsoft and mpv. ~40-60 LOC in `FrameCanvas.cpp`. Suggest ordering:
   - Batch 1.1: FrameCanvas `[PERF]` log (timer_interval_ms, present_ms, skipped_presents, DXGI frame-stat deltas). Confirms the diagnosis pre-fix.
   - Batch 1.2: Add `DXGI_SWAP_CHAIN_FLAG_FRAME_LATENCY_WAITABLE_OBJECT` to swap chain desc.
   - Batch 1.3: Grab `GetFrameLatencyWaitableObject()` post-creation; replace `QTimer(16)` tick with a `WaitForSingleObject` on the waitable handle.
   - Batch 1.4: Audit `m_skipNextPresent` guard for redundancy — with vsync-aligned presents, the overrun guard may be unnecessary or need recalibration.

2. **Phase 2 priority = P0-3 (explicit `D3D11_BOX` in `present_slice`).** Targeted cinemascope fix. ~10 LOC in `d3d11_presenter.cpp`. Small but surgical — eliminates one UB path Microsoft documents explicitly.

3. **Phase 3 priority = P0-1 (GPU subtitle overlays).** Structural. Biggest change — replace `render_blend` CPU path with a GPU overlay texture attached to the D3D11 shared texture as a separate overlay draw. mpv's `vo_gpu_next.c:313-408` is the pattern reference. Probably 200-400 LOC. Would close the last big mpv gap. **Hemanth should ratify scope here** — this is a real re-architecture of the subtitle path, and the user's immediate pain is cadence, not blend cost.

4. **Phase 4+ deprioritized**: P1-1/2/3 cleanup (CV hop removal, finer mutex granularity, async A/V gate) — worth doing in a polish pass, not urgent.

5. **Out of scope**: P1-4, P2-1, P2-2. P1-4 is negligible in practice; P2-1 is not the primary gap per Agent 7; P2-2 is now confirmed irrelevant.

### Identity direction needed from Hemanth

Before Phase 3 scoping, Hemanth should decide whether the video player's identity is:
- **mpv-clone target** (go all-in on GPU subtitle overlays — Phase 3 is in scope, big work).
- **"good enough" target** (Phase 1 + Phase 2 only, accept P0-1's CPU blend cost because `[PERF]` says it's fine within budget).

My read: the 20-hour-stable memory is about **cadence**, not **subtitle rendering cost**. Phase 1 alone may fully recover the subjective smoothness. Phase 2 is cheap and worth including. Phase 3 is optional scope for "beyond mpv" territory.

### What I did NOT do

- No code changes (validation-only pass per the ratified flow).
- No new instrumentation beyond what I shipped earlier this session (the sidecar `[PERF]` log).
- No speculation about the sidecar being the root cause — `[PERF]` data rules that out empirically, not by theory.

Standing by for `PLAYER_PERF_FIX_TODO.md` authoring. Will execute phased per Rule 6 + Rule 11 with Agent 6 review per phase gate once the TODO lands.

It EMPIRICALLY re-ranked Agent 7's P0 priorities. Without that data, the audit would have pointed primarily at subtitle blend (P0-1); your traces proved the sidecar is clean and redirected the primary suspect to cadence (P0-2).

### Identity direction locked

Hemanth 2026-04-16 via AskUserQuestion: **mpv-clone (full arch).** Phase 3 GPU subtitle overlays IN scope despite your `[PERF]` data showing current CPU blend is within budget. Commitment to architectural parity, not just "good enough." Your own read ("Phase 1 alone may fully recover subjective smoothness, Phase 3 is optional scope for 'beyond mpv'") pushed for good-enough — Hemanth chose beyond.

### PLAYER_PERF_FIX_TODO.md is live at repo root

Structure: **4 phases, ~13 batches.**

- **Phase 1 — DXGI waitable swap chain + vsync-aligned render loop** (P0-2, PRIMARY stutter fix). 4 batches: FrameCanvas `[PERF]` diagnostic (Batch 1.1, isolate-commit per `feedback_evidence_before_analysis`), add `DXGI_SWAP_CHAIN_FLAG_FRAME_LATENCY_WAITABLE_OBJECT`, replace `QTimer(16)` with `WaitForSingleObject` on waitable handle (Batch 1.3, isolate-commit), audit `m_skipNextPresent` redundancy. Main-app only, no sidecar rebuild.
- **Phase 2 — Explicit D3D11_BOX source rect in present_slice** (P0-3, cinemascope correctness). ~10 LOC, 1 batch. Sidecar rebuild required.
- **Phase 3 — GPU subtitle overlays** (P0-1, mpv-clone architectural). 5 batches: libass-to-bitmap pipeline split, D3D11 overlay texture resource (Batch 3.2, isolate-commit), wire sidecar video_decoder, FrameCanvas overlay integration, smoke+retirement. 200-400 LOC. Sidecar rebuild.
- **Phase 4 — P1 cleanup** (CV hop + mutex split + A/V gate decoupling). 3 batches, capacity-gated. Ships when Phase 3 lands + bandwidth opens. Sidecar rebuild.

### Key design decisions baked in from your validation

Your recommendations shaped the TODO verbatim in several places:

1. **Batch 1.1 isolate-commits ahead of Batch 1.2.** Your own recommendation — diagnostic `[PERF]` log first, confirm diagnosis empirically, then land the fix.
2. **Batch 1.3 isolate-commit for the DXGI waitable switch.** Clean pre/post trace comparison; one-commit rollback if the waitable proves incompatible with our Qt/D3D11 combo.
3. **`m_skipNextPresent` disposition is a data-driven decision in Batch 1.4.** Three options documented; you choose based on post-fix `[PERF]` trace.
4. **Phase 4 batch granularity matches your flagging.** CV hop + mutex split + A/V gate are separate batches; 4.3 may split further if queue introduction proves nontrivial.

- P1-4 (SHM scan) — your validation confirmed SAFE in practice.

- P2-2 (pre-migration sidecar delta) — your validation confirmed irrelevant after Option C-A swap-in.

Phase 3 is the big one. PLAYER_LIFECYCLE can run in parallel since those changes touch different files (SidecarProcess vs FrameCanvas/presenter). If scope pressure forces triage, Agent 0 brokers.
